abstract: The paper introduces a profound reinforcement learning-based data privacy system that can be applied to real-time industrial sensor networks with very rigid performance and confidentiality requirements. The framework uses policy-optimization mechanisms to control the exposure of data whilst preserving the analytical utility of dynamic industrial processes. The analysis on the experiment shows that the average reward of 0.87, privacy leakage reduction of 42% and utility preservation of 91 percent are notable when the network loads vary. The system will be able to accommodate variation in sensors, different patterns of threats and high frequency data streams without compromising operational information. A stability index analysis also indicates the reliability of convergence in updated policies, making it possible to have similar behavior on long term industrial loads. The suggested solution will provide a fair trade-off between data safeguarding and operational efficiency, which is a crucial aspect of Industry 4.0 settings and secure and intelligent automation. The findings confirm the practicability of reinforcement learning as an online privacy controller of industrial complex ecosystems.

title: Enhancing Lung Cancer Detection Accuracy with CrossViT-Lung: A Vision Transformer-Based Approach
authors: Khandakar Rabbi Ahmed, Zinat Tasneem
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Lung cancer (LC) is the leading cause of death globally. Early detection of lung tumors is essential in reducing the number of LC cases and increasing the survival rate. CrossViT-Lung is a model that uses a Cross-Attention MultiScale Vision Transformer (ViT) that is specifically designed to increase the accuracy of LC diagnosis while being computationally efficient. It uses two parallel branches operating at different spatial scales, enabling the model to recognise both large and small features that are essential for diagnosing LC. In addition to the original feature extraction of the ViT, the model uses EfficientNet-B7, which is known for its global context understanding using self-attention. The accuracy of the CrossViT-Lung model is 99.41 %, which is higher than the accuracy of EfficientNet-B7, which is 94.53 %, in the detection of small and scattered patterns in LC images. This proves that the model is efficient in the early detection of LC and is great advancement in the field. The results demonstrate the potential of multi-scale transformer designs and cross-attention in creating trustworthy computer-aided diagnostic systems for early detection of LC.

title: LLM-TDA: A LLM-based Tabular Data Augmentation Framework
authors: Rui Lin, Zhiwei Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Tabular data augmentation is a critical technique for improving machine learning performance in data-limited scenarios. However, existing methods face inherent limitations in balancing quality and efficiency. Filter-based approaches tend to introduce low-quality or irrelevant features due to the lack of task-specific feedback, while wrapper-based methods incur prohibitive time overheads necessitated by massive downstream model training. Fundamentally, both paradigms rely solely on statistical properties, neglecting the rich semantic information in tabular data. To address these limitations, this paper proposes LLM-TDA, a novel framework that leverages the semantic reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) to optimize the feature augmentation process. LLM-TDA employs a coarse-to-fine pipeline: first, a table-level analysis rapidly prunes semantically irrelevant tables based on business logic; second, a fine-grained feature selection mechanism fuses semantic understanding with statistical assessment to identify high-value features. Extensive experiments on real-world datasets demonstrate that LLM-TDA significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in downstream task performance. Notably, it achieves this accuracy while delivering a 10-fold to 14-fold improvement in computational efficiency compared to wrapper-based methods, providing a robust and scalable solution for automated feature engineering.

title: A User-Centric Approach to Fitness Tracking and Nutrition Guidance Using Machine Learning Classification and Clustering
authors: Shiela David, Varsha Veronica A, Vandhana R
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The importance of health and fitness management in contemporary society has been risen significantly, with sedentary lifestyles and unhealthy eating patterns contributing to overall health concerns. Traditional means of assessing fitness and nutrition generally fail to take into consideration individual differences when creating a plan for managing one's health and fitness. Rather, they provide users with static, pre-generated recommendations based on a specific criterion that inhibit users from continuing to use the service for an extended period of time and do not provide users with the most accurate information about their health and fitness. In order to help solve the problem of traditional assessment methods, we will provide users with a web-based fitness tracking and nutrition guidance application that uses multiple users, machine learning classification and clustering models, and principles of gamification. Users will be able to input their health goals into the application and track their individual activity levels, including their daily steps taken, calories expended, exercise sessions completed, hours of sleep, heart rate and BMI. The classification models used to classify users based on their level of activity are Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Logistic Regression. All three classification models will classify users into levels of low, moderate and high activity levels, with Random Forest achieving at least 99.5 % accuracy. The Random Forest classification model has outperformed the other two models, as it accounts for nonlinear relationships that exist between data sets, and because it accounts for participants that have very similar physiological make-ups. Besides, the KMeans clustering system allows for participants who demonstrate similar patterns of fitness and health behaviours to receive simultaneous personalized prescriptions and to engage with other K-Means community members. The gamification principles include streaks, achievements, leaderboards, and points to increase long-term adherence. The fitness tracker system also incorporates a dashboard reporting a range of visualizations for users, which assists users with their workouts, alongside peer comparisons.

title: A Neural Network Approach for Speech to Text Translation of Low Resource Language
authors: Kumaresh Sheelavant, Preksha G P, Sakshi Shanbhag, Shreya B C, Sneha T, Shilpa Patil
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Developing speech translation systems for low-resource languages remains a major issue because lack of annotated datasets and linguistic resources. This work introduces a neural speech-to-text translation framework designed for low-resource Indian languages, with Konkani as a case study. The proposed system converts spoken Konkani into English text through Hindi as a pivot language, integrating Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) and Neural Machine Translation (NMT) models within a unified architecture. The methodology involves audio preprocessing, feature extraction, and pivot-based translation using pretrained models such as IndicConformer and Transformer-based NMT to ensure fluency and adaptability. By leveraging transfer learning and multilingual modeling, the approach effectively addresses issues of data scarcity, accent variation, and linguistic diversity. In comparison, our proposed pipeline attains 29.6% WER and 28.1 BLEU, demonstrating competitive performance despite operating in a low-resource setting. These results show that our model performs on par with, and in some cases surpasses, existing benchmarks for similar multilingual pipelines.

title: FreqHDR: Frequency-Enhanced HDR Video Reconstruction
authors: Tianxin Huang, Yun Zhou, Tian He, Zhixin Zheng, Aidong Men, Zhuqing Jiang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: Reconstructing high dynamic range (HDR) video from alternating exposure sequences remains highly challenging. Existing methods primarily rely on optical flow or attention mechanisms for low dynamic range (LDR) sequence alignment and ghosting artifact suppression. However, optical flow integration alone proves insufficient for consistent performance improvement in dynamic/static scenes, exhibiting limited noise suppression and suboptimal detail recovery. To address these limitations, a novel exposure fusion framework, FreqHDR, is proposed, incorporating a dedicated Spatial-Frequency Integration Block (SFIB). The encoder adopts a stage-wise cascaded architecture for progressive multi-scale feature extraction, with each stage enhanced by the SFIB module. The SFIB follows a dual-path design: the spatial path employs convolutions and channel attention to capture local structural details, while the frequency path utilizes Fourier transforms for global semantic context. Features from both domains are dynamically fused and refined via a gated residual connection, significantly strengthening representation learning. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior or competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art approaches on benchmark datasets.

title: Soft-Switching ZCS Commutated Current-Fed DAB DC/DC Converter with New Modulation Scheme
authors: Nil Patel, Sheron Figardo, Akshay Kumar Rathore
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: This paper analyzes a soft-switching ZCS currentfed dual active bridge (DAB) isolated DC/DC converter. Through a novel secondary-modulation technique, the topology achieves natural voltage clamping across the primary-side devices and resolves the long-standing issue of voltage spikes across devices during turn-off, this solution eliminates unnecessary snubbers or clamping circuits. As a result, the converter is snubber-free, characterized by reduced hardware complexity, enhanced compactness, and improved costeffectiveness. By enabling zero-current switching (ZCS) on the primary side and zero-current turn-on switching on the secondary side, the converter effectively reduces switching losses. The feasibility of the proposed modulation scheme is verified through the hardware results of a 1.5 kW lab prototype.

title: The Concept of Constructing Normalized Scales for Diagnostic Results of Complex Objects
authors: Artem A. Geleta, Elena S. Suloeva
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: The report is devoted to issues of metrological assurance of diagnostics. Diagnostic methods aimed at assessing the state of complex objects are presented. Within the framework of the metrological approach, the problem of low informativeness of results reducible to a binary choice is investigated. A transition to the use of normalized scales for quantitative assessment is proposed as a solution. The result of the work is implementation variants for constructing scales based on the principles of metrological assurance, allowing for an increase in the informativeness of diagnostics.

title: Improvement on optical signal bit rate recovery algorithm based on multiple ultrashort optical pulse samplings
authors: Lin Zuo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: In software-synchronized optical sampling technology, the original optical signal is sampled twice using sampling pulses with different repetition frequencies. By performing fast Fourier transform and chirp Z transform on the sampled signals, the equivalent sampling bit number is obtained, and the rate of the original optical signal is recovered, which is called the signal rate recovery algorithm. However, when the equivalent sampling bit numbers of the two sampled signals satisfy specific conditions, the signal rate recovery algorithm fails, and the recovered rate is always less than the true rate, which is called the blind zone of the signal rate recovery algorithm. Through theoretical analysis and numerical simulation, the conditions for the existence of the blind zone are confirmed. An improved scheme is proposed, which involves using another sampling pulse with different repetition frequency besides the existing two sampling pulses for sampling. The equivalent sampling bit numbers of all the three samplings are obtained through the software synchronization algorithm, and three rates are recovered, the maximum value among which is taken as the correct original signal rate. The feasibility of this scheme is verified through numerical simulation and experimental investigation, which shows the blind zone is eliminated.

title: GRACE-RAG: Graph Retrieval with Adaptive Chunk Extraction for Long-Context Question Answering
authors: Tianwei Huang, Shuai Lei, Askar Hamdulla, Chunxiao Gao, Huaping Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) improves factuality by grounding large language models (LLMs) on external corpora, but it still struggles with multi-hop reasoning and long-context overload. We propose GRACE-RAG, a two-stage framework that (i) builds a fine-grained chunk–sentence–entity graph via dynamic chunking during offline indexing, (ii) performs query-aware entity activation and personalized PageRank for associative retrieval online, and (iii) adaptively compresses retrieved evidence under a token budget before answer generation. Experiments on seven QA benchmarks show consistent gains over strong graph-based RAG baselines.

title: Modeling a Reduced Hybrid Quantum-Inspired Framework for Optimized Speckle Denoising Filters
authors: M Su-Ada, S Shailesh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Computing and Applications (AICAPS)
abstract: This paper examines a hybrid classical-quantum workflow for speckle-oriented image denoising, in which denoising is formulated as a discrete filter-parameter selection problem over image batches. Candidate convolution kernels are evaluated using batch-averaged peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) and structural similarity (SSIM), and the best-performing kernel is deployed within a variance-guided adaptive filtering stage. The selected kernel configuration is then encoded into a quantum register and marked using a Grover oracle, followed by amplitude amplification to increase the likelihood of retrieving the target configuration upon measurement. In this formulation, the quantum circuit serves to demonstrate parameter encoding, oraclebased marking, and probabilistic retrieval consistent with the classically selected optimum, rather than replacing classical fitness evaluation. Experiments conducted on speckle-contaminated image batches from multiple imaging modalities show that the optimized kernel provides stable denoising performance, while the quantum subroutine reproduces the target configuration with high probability under practical circuit-depth constraints.

title: Machine Learning Models and Power BI Dashboards for Predicting Employee Attrition in a Manufacturing Company in Bahrain
authors: Hawra Nasser, Dana Alshoala, Mariam Alshaikh, Noor Hassan, Minwir Al-Shammari
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Sustainable Islamic Business and Finance (SIBF)
abstract: Employee attrition is emerging as a growing concern for organizations, particularly in industrial sectors where skilled labor retention is critical to productivity and continuity. This research aims to investigate the determinants of employee attrition at Aluminum Bahrain (ALBA) using a combination of secondary data and primary sources. The study uses the IBM Human Resources (HR) Analytics Attrition dataset to identify key drivers of turnover with Python-based machine learning (ML) models, specifically logistic regression, decision trees, and random forests. Additionally, we directly asked ALBA employees what was on their minds by sending out a well-organized survey to gather their thoughts on their jobs, work-life balance, communication, and opportunities for advancement. Results were presented visually in Power BI dashboards to derive actionable insights. The findings recognize overtime, non-promotion, and job stress as key causes of attrition. The study concludes by offering strategic recommendations to reduce turnover and enhance employee engagement through fact-based decisionmaking.

title: Multi-Modal Longitudinal Framework for Alzheimer's Disease Diagnosis Using DPTPSAN and SHAP-Based Interpretability
authors: Virendra V. Bagade, Yashwant V. Dongre, Laxmi Bewoor
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: This paper is addressing AD diagnosis on pre-clinical stage by considering the longitudinal multimodal biomarker studies which reveal that the continuum of Alzheimer's disease (AD) includes a long latent phase, referred to as preclinical AD, which precedes the onset of symptoms by decades. Diagnosis treatment during the preclinical AD phase offers an optimal opportunity for slowing the progression of disease. Despite significant advancements in the field of Alzheimer's disease (AD) diagnosis, current diagnostic models still face several challenges that hinder their clinical applicability. Most existing methods focus either on neuroimaging or clinical data in isolation, without effectively leveraging the complementary strengths of multi-modal data. Moreover, many diagnostic frameworks rely on cross-sectional data, ignoring the temporal dynamics that are crucial for understanding the progression of AD. The integration of longitudinal clinical data with imaging information remains underexplored, and synthetic adjustment of such heterogeneous data is rarely addressed in the literature. Furthermore, issues like class imbalance, suboptimal feature fusion, limited interpretability, and lack of real-world deployment mechanisms-such as mobile-based tools-further exacerbate the challenge.

title: Achieving Secure and Reliable URLLC in Cell-Free Massive MIMO via Self-Supervised Learning
authors: Donggen Li, Shuo Sun, Xinxin Ma, Hengjie Ren, Chong Huang, Wenjiang Feng
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: The stringent requirements of ultra-reliable low-latency communication (URLLC) contribute to an enhanced service experience for user equipments (UEs). However, it also poses a challenge to the design of security strategies when UEs require multiple businesses while the communication resources are constrained. This letter focuses on the security and reliability of URLLC for uplink transmission in cell-free massive multiple-input multiple-output (CF-mMIMO) systems. We define a new metric, secure and reliable probability (SRP), to characterize the trade-off between secure and reliable services in the system. Furthermore, we propose a power control approach based on self-supervised learning, which adjusts the user's power allocation strategy to meet maximum security or maximum reliability requirements of different UEs and improve the performance of the whole system. Finally, extensive simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: DQN-Based Resource Allocation and Offloading (DQN-RAO) Algorithm for Multi-User Multi-Server MEC Network
authors: Hoa Tran-Dang, Dong-Seong Kim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: This paper proposes a DQN-based resource allocation and offloading (DQN-RAO) Algorithm designed to minimize the average task delay in Multi-User Multi-Server Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) networks. We address the inherent complexity of the joint task-server assignment and resource allocation problem through a two-phase decomposition. First, a Deep Q-Network (DQN) learns the optimal global strategy for the discrete task offloading assignment. Second, a convex optimization model efficiently calculates the optimal CPU frequency allocation for the determined assignment. Comparative simulation results demonstrate that the DQN-RAO algorithm consistently achieves the lowest average task delay, significantly surpassing both the random offloading algorithm (ROA) and the greedy offloading algorithm (GOA) across varying server capacities and task complexities.

title: Automated Malaria Diagnosis Using a Fine-Tuned EfficientNetB3 Model on Blood Smear Images
authors: Mukesh Kumar, Sandeep Kumar, Gaurav Sharma, G Santhosh Kumar, Zaid Alsalami, Anurag Shrivastava, Deepak Banerjee
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Malaria is a major global health issue with great death especially in tropical and subtropical regions. Correct and quick diagnosis is essential for good prevention and control of diseases. Historically, the diagnosis of malaria has hinged on hand microscopic inspection of blood smears, a technique prone to human error as well as time-consuming and labour-intensive character. By building an automated malaria detection system using an upgraded EfficientNetB3 model, our work seeks to address these problems. Designed to split blood smear images into parasitised and uninfected groups, the EfficientNetB3 design celebrated their harmonic scaling of depth, width, and resolution. With an F1-score of 0.97 the model proved dependability and durability by showing amazing general accuracy of 97% for both classes. Particularly in environments with limited resources, these findings underline the promise of the customised EfficientNetB3 model to greatly increase malaria diagnosis, hence improving patient outcomes and supporting more efficient disease control.

title: Deep Learning-Based Bird Species Classification Using ResNet-50
authors: Girish S, Yukesh S, Balaji Kannan K, Vigneshwar B, Vishnu Sekhar R
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Taxonomic classification of biological species plays a vital role in biodiversity monitoring, ecological research, and conservation efforts. Recent advances in deep learning have enabled automated species identification using visual data, reducing reliance on manual expertise. In this work, we investigate the effectiveness of transfer learning-based convolutional neural networks for bird species classification using a curated dataset of Indian birds. A systematic experimental study is conducted using ResNet architectures pretrained on ImageNet, focusing on the impact of feature extraction strategies, partial fine-tuning, and data augmentation. We compare a frozen backbone baseline against fine-tuned models with varying degrees of trainable layers and evaluate their performance across multiple training configurations. Additionally, a comparison between ResNet-50 and ResNet-152 is performed to analyze the trade-off between network depth and classification accuracy. To further assess the influence of dataset complexity, experiments are conducted on a reduced subset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5}$</tex> visually distinctive bird species. The results demonstrate that partial fine-tuning of higher network layers combined with data augmentation significantly improves classification performance, achieving high validation accuracy while maintaining stable training behavior. This study highlights the effectiveness of controlled fine-tuning strategies for fine-grained taxonomic classification tasks.

title: Early detection method for high-risk diabetic foot based on infrared thermal imaging
authors: Xiao Wu, Zhen Ma, Xi Chen, Diming Liu, Huarui Liu, Yang Xu, Xianjun Yang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Diabetic foot ulcers represent a severe complication of diabetes, associated with high rates of amputation and mortality risk. Consequently, early identification of high-risk foot lesions is crucial for prevention. Since abnormal plantar temperature changes often precede ulcer formation, infrared thermography has emerged as a promising screening tool. However, its clinical application is limited by uneven temperature distribution and a primary focus on established ulcers. This study integrates the deep features from infrared images, and plantar temperature characteristics to construct a machine learning-based classification model for high-risk diabetic foot lesions. After evaluating multiple classifiers and feature fusion strategies, the XGBoost model trained using fused ResNet-101 Global Average Pooling features and temperature features demonstrated optimal performance. This confirms the potential of multimodal infrared thermography models for clinical early screening and preventive management.

title: A Hybrid Machine Learning Framework for Real-Time Student and Tutor Matching: A Design Science Implementation Through FocusDesk
authors: MAVKT Muramudali, Nirosha Wedasinghe
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: The demand for timely, topic-specific academic support is increasing among university students in Sri Lanka, while existing digital learning platforms provide limited mechanisms for real-time, micro-level tutoring. In response, this study adopts a Design Science Research (DSR) methodology to design, implement, and evaluate FocusDesk, a web-based micro-tutoring platform that integrates real-time peer-to-peer interaction with a hybrid recommendation mechanism. The recommendation model combines content-based similarity modeling using TF-IDF with collaborative filtering to support tutor matching under sparse interaction conditions. Experimental evaluation was conducted using a combination of pre-deployment model initialization and interaction data collected during system usage, complemented by a controlled pilot study to assess usability. The results indicate stable matching behavior under early-stage deployment conditions and a positive user experience for short, topic-focused tutoring sessions. This study demonstrates the technical feasibility and practical viability of integrating hybrid machine-learning based matching with real-time micro-tutoring in a resource constrained higher education context. The results highlight the potential of such systems to support timely, topic-focused academic assistance and provide a foundation for future investigations into learning outcomes, fairness-aware recommendation, and scalable peerlearning platforms.

title: A Novel Multi-Factor Authentication Technique Using DNA Cryptography to Safeguard Oil Companies' Data
authors: Amal Khaleel Hamad, Mishall Al-Zubaidie
publish: 2025 15th International Conference on Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Computer Science and Statistics (MACS)
abstract: Cyber transaction protection is one of the largest concerns to oil firms due to the increasing security and privacy threats. Rivest Cipher 4 (RC4), and ElGamal cryptographies are only two among the many frameworks and measures that are already being implemented to secure transactions in the cyber world. Nevertheless, they possess their drawbacks like slow performance, breakability, obsolescence, and susceptibility to the whale-phishing attack. This paper introduces an efficient means of guarding Internet of Things (IoT) communications and oil industry cyber transactions depending on zero trust architecture (ZTA). It is an amalgamation of advanced practices such as tokenization (TK), data integration/DNA cryptography, GPS (global positioning system)-based authentication, password salting and hashing. This strategy will enhance the security of online transactions in the oil industry by enhancing the degree of privacy and reliability of cyber transactions through incorporation of extensive, robust, and effective verification frameworks that can withstand complex cybers attacks. The proposed framework is assessed theoretically to prove the security and performance of the system. In this study, we have been able to provide a theoretical security analysis against various threats and comparisons to show that our proposal would be better than the past research.

title: AI-Driven Dual CNN System for Automated Tomato Ripeness Classification and Defect Detection
authors: L.P.G.K.G. Lankawarna, K.A.P.M. Perera, S.L.D.P. Pramodya, A.A. Lakshitha, E.R.J. Samarakoon
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Tomato is one of the most perishable food commodities, and post-harvest handling of tomato is very crucial. In most commercial supply chains, tomato sorting and grading are done manually using manpower. Manual sorting of tomatoes is usually subjective, inconsistent, and laborintensive, leading to misclassification and economic losses. This research study was conducted to address the above challenges by developing a dual Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) system that simultaneously classifies tomatoes based on their ripening stages and outer surface defects. Images of Gowri F1 hybrid tomatoes were used for this study. The tomato grading criteria used in this study align with those of the Keells supermarket chain. The images were captured under diverse lighting and background conditions. At the end of the study, the ripening stage classification model achieved 97% accuracy, while the defects detection model achieved 55% accuracy. This dual-output model processes each tomato image once and predicts both ripening stage and defect status in a single pass. Findings of this study demonstrate that deep learning can improve post-harvest quality management in tomato supply chains, though challenges remain in detecting subtle defects.

title: Delay Tolerant Precaching Scheme Based on Proximal Policy Optimization in Content-Centric Vehicular Networks
authors: Jeongtak Na, Jongpil Youn, Irina Em, Euisin Lee, Youngju Nam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: The surge in multimedia demand from autonomous driving and infotainment stresses RSUs; mobility and limited wireless capacity cause delays and QoS degradation. While CCVN precaching has been studied, delay-tolerant services remain underexplored. We present a PPO-based delay-tolerant precaching scheme featuring path-aware RSU discovery, proportional precaching decisions, and opportunistic transmission within deadlines. A tailored reward balances delivery success, backhaul reduction, and traffic efficiency. Simulation results in Large-scale SUMO experiments show that our scheme outperforms a heuristic scheme in content delivery success ratio and traffic overhead, demonstrating scalable and adaptive learning for delay-tolerant vehicular content delivery.

title: Fast Quasi-Optimal Control of On-Board Energy Storage Systems for Regenerative Braking Energy Recovery Based on Area-Equivalence Principle
authors: Jiayu Mi, Zhongping Yang, Yajie Zhao, Zhongbei Tian, Fei Lin, Zhihong Zhong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Existing threshold-based control strategies for on-board energy storage systems (OESSs) often fail to achieve optimal energy-saving performance, while AI-based strategies typically suffer from low computational efficiency. To address these limitations, this article proposes a fast quasi-optimal control strategy for OESSs by leveraging the electrical characteristics of supercapacitors and the area-equivalence principle. Specifically, during train braking, OESSs should absorb as much regenerative braking energy as possible. By applying the area-equivalence principle, this goal is reformulated as a geometric problem-maximizing the area bounded by the train power curve and the OESS power curve. Thus, the optimal charging current and initial charging voltage of OESSs can be determined. Correspondingly, a discharging strategy is developed to ensure that the OESS voltage at the end of train traction reaches the previously obtained optimal initial charging voltage. Case studies reveal that the proposed area-equivalent strategy can achieve 98% of the optimal energy-saving performance while maintaining low root-mean-square (rms) current and average di/dt. Meanwhile, its computational time is only 1/92 of that of dynamic programming (DP) method. With a well-balanced trade-off among energy recovery performance, computational efficiency, and OESS lifetime, the superiority of the area-equivalence-based control strategy is verified.

title: IP-UNet: Intensity Projection UNet Architecture for IoMT-enabled 3D Medical Volume Segmentation
authors: Nyothiri Aung, Tahar Kechadi, Liming Chen, Sahraoui Dhelim
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Areas in Sensors
abstract: The proliferation of high-resolution medical imaging data, increasingly generated and transmitted via Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) infrastructure, presents significant computational challenges. Limited memory capacity remains a common drawback when processing these high-resolution 3D volumetric datasets, as direct cropping or down-sizing often results in a loss of critical diagnostic detail, exacerbates class imbalance, and degrades segmentation performance. 3D volumes are usually cropped or downsized first before processing, which can result in a loss of resolution, increase class imbalance, and affect the performance of the segmentation algorithms. In this paper, we propose an end-to-end deep learning approach called IP-UNet. IP-UNet is a UNet-based model that performs multi-class segmentation on Intensity Projection (IP) of 3D volumetric data instead of the memory-consuming 3D volumes. IP-UNet uses limited memory capability for training without losing the original 3D image resolution. We compare the performance of three models in terms of segmentation accuracy and computational cost: 1) Slice-by-slice 2D segmentation of the CT scan images using a conventional 2D UNet model. 2) IP-UNet that operates on data obtained by merging the extracted Maximum Intensity Projection (MIP), Closest Vessel Projection (CVP), and Average Intensity Projection (AvgIP) representations of the source 3D volumes, then applying the UNet model on the output IP images. 3) 3D-UNet model directly reads the 3D volumes constructed from a series of CT scan images and outputs the 3D volume of the predicted segmentation. We test the performance of these methods on 3D volumetric images. Experimental results show that IP-UNet can achieve similar segmentation accuracy with 3D-UNet but with much better performance. It reduces the training time by 70% and memory consumption by 92%.

title: Enhancing Elastic Full-Waveform Inversion with Envelope Cosine Similarity: A Multiscale Frequency Strategy for Walkaway VSP Data
authors: Huiyuan Quan, Xiaobo Liu, Jun Lu, Ran Zhang, Zhe Yang, Zhonglin Cao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Conventional elastic full-waveform inversion (EFWI) using the L2-norm objective function is highly sensitive to phase errors and prone to local minima, which often results in cycle skipping when low-frequency components are absent in field data. Although Walkaway vertical seismic profiling (VSP) offers higher-resolution constraints than surface seismic data, the lack of low-frequency energy in VSP recordings still limits the stability and accuracy of conventional EFWI. To address these challenges, we propose a novel multiscale inversion framework that employs envelope cosine similarity (ECS) as the misfit function for EFWI of Walkaway VSP data. Compared to the L2-norm, the ECS is less sensitive to phase mismatches and emphasizes structural similarity of signal envelopes via cosine similarity rather than absolute envelope differences, thereby mitigating cycle skipping and enhancing low-wavenumber recovery. Synthetic and field experiments confirm that the proposed method is effective, yielding stabilized convergence and improved velocity reconstruction accuracy in scenarios with low-frequency data deficiency, thus offering a robust framework for multiparameter VSP inversion.

title: A Gate Drive Strategy for Full Output Voltage Regulation of a Zero-Inductor-Voltage Converter
authors: Sina Salehi Dobakhshari, Aamna Nasir Hameed, Binghui He, Mojtaba Forouzesh, Yan Fei Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: The Zero-Inductor-Voltage (ZIV) converter is a step-down capacitive solution which conventionally has a fixed input to output voltage conversion ratio. It has zero output current ripple that has led to high power density and high efficiency at high output current applications. However, the fixed conversion ratio has limited the converter's utilization. Nonetheless, in this paper a new pulse pattern for the 7-Switch is proposed by which the output voltage can be regulated from 0V to Vin. Unlike the other control methods introduced in the literature, this method does not need any auxiliary components, and the regulation range is from zero to unity. At the same time, the output current ripple is kept at lower value as compared with conventional Buck converter or three-level Buck converter. The benefit of a lower current ripple is that a smaller inductor can be used which improves the power density and reduces the cost significantly. In the paper, the performance of the proposed control method is investigated through theoretical analysis, and it has been validated by a prototype with 20-60V input voltage, 12V output voltage, 21A output current, with 97.12% average efficiency at full load, and 1025W/in3 power density.

title: IEEE Transactions on Sustainable Computing
authors: N/A
publish: Computer

title: Spectrum and Power Resource Allocation for C-RAN Enabled Dual-Connectivity Networks
authors: Jixing Liu, Xinwei Yue, Jinhe Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: In 6G wireless networks, the substantial increase in user equipment (UE) density and wireless resource utilization has made spectrum and power resource allocation challenges. Currently, limited research exists on spectrum and power resource allocation when UE employs dual-connectivity mode within the cloud radio access network architecture, particularly in scenarios where unmanned aerial vehicle base station is rapidly deployed to enhance network performance during peak traffic periods. Therefore, we propose a spectrum and power resource allocation scheme for this scenario. The scheme formulates the problem as two separate optimization problems to maximize utility functions, allocating spectrum and power resources based on system throughput, UE service priority, and operator's revenue. Simulation results show the proposed scheme's effectiveness in improving system throughput, spectrum utilization, and operator's revenue, especially under high-load conditions, while ensuring quality of service requirements for high-priority UEs.

title: Spectral-Routed Mixture-of-Experts for High-Fidelity Complex-Field Measurement in Single-Frame Lens-Free On-Chip Microscopy_supp1-3677977.pdf
authors: Zhengjun Liu
publish: N/A
abstract: Single-frame lens-free on-chip microscopy (LFOM) offers a compact and cost-effective solution for biomedical diagnostics and micro/nanoscale inspection but suffers from severe twin-image artifacts and frequency-dependent degradation due to numerical aperture (NA) limitations. Existing reconstruction methods often neglect the heterogeneous frequency sensitivity of LFOM and fail to explicitly suppress artifacts, leading to suboptimal complex-field measurement quality. To address these challenges, we propose SEMNet, a spectral-expert measurement network that introduces frequency-aware modeling into single-frame LFOM reconstruction. Specifically, SEMNet transforms captured holograms into polar Fourier space, partitions them into directional subbands, and adaptively routes each subband via a soft top-k gating mechanism to lightweight expert networks specialized for low-, mid-, and high-frequency restoration. A physics-consistent loss jointly enforces complex-field fidelity, Fresnel-likelihood consistency, twin-image suppression, and spectral load balancing, thereby aligning the reconstruction objective with physical imaging principles. Extensive experiments on 404 high-resolution hologram–complex field pairs from seven types of biological tissues demonstrate that SEMNet improves peak signal-to-noise ratio by up to 1.7 dB over strong single-frame baselines, with statistically significant gains in structural similarity indices (p < 0.001). SEMNet restores high-frequency textures while suppressing NA-boundary artifacts. It delivers 47.1 FPS end-to-end inference with 100× fewer parameters than a representative CNN baseline and 5× fewer MACs than a representative Transformer baseline, highlighting a frequency-structured, physics-aligned design for efficient LFOM.

title: Enhancing Performance of Doubly Fed Induction Generator Under Unbalanced Voltage
authors: Jitendra Kumar Mahawar, Saikat Chakrabarti
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Unbalanced voltage at the terminals of the doubly fed induction generator (DFIG) leads to double grid frequency oscillation (DGFO) in its operating quantities. The operating quantities include stator and rotor current, electromagnetic torque, DC-link voltage, and DFIG power. These oscillations de grade the performance and lifespan of the DFIG. The magnitude of DGFO in these operating quantities can be effectively reduced by regulating the negative-sequence (NS) current and the stator terminal voltage of the DFIG through coordinated control be tween the rotor-side converter (RSC) and the grid-side converter (GSC), rather than independent NS control of each converter. This paper presents a comprehensive investigation of several NS coordinated control strategies derived from the individual NS control of RSC and GSC for various operating conditions of the DFIG. Along with it, the impacts of NS-coordinated control on voltages at the PCC and stator terminal, as well as on the magnitude of DGFO under weak grid conditions are analyzed, which have not been addressed in the existing literature. Further, the suitability of the evaluated strategies for DFIG is suggested based on operating conditions, DGFO magnitude, and converter current-handling capability. The effectiveness of the proposed strategies is verified through real-time digital simulator (RTDS) testing and validated using hardware-in-loop (HIL) experiments, demonstrating their practical applicability in real-world DFIG based wind energy systems.

title: Robust Self-Localization of Wireless Acoustic Sensor Networks in the Presence of TDoA Outliers_supp1-3676429.pdf
authors: De Hu
publish: N/A
abstract: Today, we are surrounded by numerous intelligent terminals (e.g., smartphones, tablets, and laptops) that can collectively form wireless acoustic sensor networks (WASNs), which are expected to be the next-generation platform for sound processing. Since the geometric structure of WASNs is critical for tasks like source localization and acoustic beamforming, automatic self-localization of WASNs (SL-WASNs) is indispensable. Although several SL-WASN methods have been developed, most of them fail to account for the impact of time-difference-of-arrival (TDoA) outliers induced by noise and reverberation. To address this issue, we first propose a centralized-robust self-localization (CRSL) method for WASNs with a centralized network topology. Specifically, we develop a novel probabilistic model leveraging the zero-sum property of TDoAs to derive the probability of a TDoA measurement being an inlier (or outlier). Then, we construct a weighted cost function based on TDoA measurements, in which the weights are controlled by the computed inlier probabilities. Afterward, the Broyden-Fletcher-Goldfarb-Shanno (BFGS) algorithm is employed to simultaneously estimate the positions of nodes and sources, as well as the inter-node time offsets. In addition, we extend the CRSL to a distributed framework, termed distributed-robust self-localization (DRSL), which incorporates distributed computation of inlier probabilities, distributed cost function construction, and distributed optimization. Furthermore, the computational complexities of both CRSL and DRSL are analyzed in detail. The main contributions of this work can be summarized as follows: a novel TDoA outlier recognition model, the CRSL method, the DRSL method, and their complexity analysis. In real-world experiments with M = 10 nodes and N = 8 sources, the proposed methods achieved over 8% higher localization accuracy than state-of-the-art approaches.

title: Design of Power Allocation and Device Grouping in PD-NOMA Communication Systems_supp1-3678581.pdf
authors: Min Hua
publish: N/A
abstract: Power domain non-orthogonal multiple access (PD-NOMA) is considered an effective means of enabling massive connectivity and improving spectral efficiency in next-generation (6G) wireless communication networks, particularly for the massive Internet of Things (mIoT). This paper optimizes power allocation and device grouping to address energy efficiency and resource scarcity challenges. We investigate optimal power allocation under two distinct successive interference cancellation (SIC) decoding orders at the receiver, incorporating constraints on limited transmit power, quality of service (QoS), and SIC decoding requirements. Using Karush-Kuhn-Tucker (KKT) conditions, we derive rigorous criteria for grouping IoT devices on shared subchannels and determine their optimal power coefficients. Analytical results prove that channel-gain-ascending SIC maximizes downlink subchannel capacity, while channel-gain-descending SIC optimizes uplink performance. Furthermore, we propose a resource-efficient device grouping scheme that reduces the required number of orthogonal subchannels compared to conventional orthogonal multiple access (OMA). Simulations demonstrate that the proposed scheme not only conserves the number of orthogonal subchannels compared to the OMA system, but also exhibits superior overall system capacity. This work directly contributes to energy-efficient resource management in sustainable IoT communications by minimizing spectral resources and enhancing network longevity.

title: Cycle Time Optimization in Scheduling Automated Bench Cleaning Tool for Semiconductor Manufacturing
authors: Luetao Li, Siwei Zhang, Yan Qiao, Naiqi Wu, Jie Li, Bai Yang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In the semiconductor wafer manufacturing process, wafer cleaning operation is responsible for removing the residues on the surface of wafers after processing. The cleaning time accounts for 30% of the wafer fabrication work, which is significant as other wafer production processes. Wafer cleaning equipment consists of a single-arm robot for transportation and multiple processing tanks that are organized in a linear topology structure. Unlike cluster tools with its processing modules being arranged in a radial structure, the moving time of the robot between the tanks in a cleaning tool cannot be treated as the same, making its scheduling problem more difficult. The purpose of this paper is to solve the scheduling problem of automatic bench cleaning tools to make the wafer residency time same among the tanks for a processing step while maximizing the tool productivity. In order to achieve this goal, a backward strategy for wafer cleaning is proposed and its characteristics under steady state are studied. Based on the analysis results, scheduling algorithms aiming at minimizing the wafer cleaning cycle are established. Finally, the practicability of the algorithm is proved by case analysis.

title: Adaptive Game-Based Learning for Artistic Anatomy: Effects on Learning Achievement and Creative Self-Efficacy among First-Year University Art Students
authors: Guo Xinming, Chau Kien Tsong, Wan Ahmad Jaafar Wan Yahaya
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This study investigates whether interpretable adaptive difficulty regulation in game-based learning enhances learning achievement and creative self-efficacy in artistic anatomy education. An Adaptive Game-Based Learning (AGBL) system was developed using the ADDIE model and grounded in constructivist theory, the adaptive learning framework, and social cognitive theory. The system implements a rule-based, transparent difficulty adjustment mechanism driven by a performance index derived from accuracy and response time. A quasi-experimental design was conducted with 109 first-year university art students, who were assigned to either the AGBL condition or a Non-Adaptive Game-Based Learning (N-AGBL) condition. Analysis of covariance (ANCOVA) controlling for pretest scores revealed that the AGBL group significantly outperformed the N-AGBL group in both learning achievement (F(1,106) = 42.56, p < .001, ηp² = .286) and creative self-efficacy (F(1,106) = 56.90, p < .001, ηp² = .349). Furthermore, creative self-efficacy was positively correlated with sketching test scores (r = .587), and the association remained significant after controlling for group assignment (partial r = .370), providing convergent validity evidence. Offline benchmarking against fixed, random, and Bayesian knowledge tracing scheduling strategies demonstrated that the rule-based mechanism achieved more stable difficulty trajectories while maintaining comparable accuracy. The findings suggest that structured and interpretable adaptive control is associated with improvements in cognitive mastery and motivational belief. This study contributes empirical evidence for rule-based adaptive game-based learning in visual-spatial domains and offers replicable design principles for integrating adaptive regulation into creative learning environments.

title: Decoupling Energy Stability and Thermal Response in Thermoelectric Atmospheric Water Generators
authors: María C. Salgado-Gutiérrez, José L. López-Moreno, Perla Y. Sevilla-Camacho, Gilbert F. Pérez-García, Rubén Martínez-Revuelta, Horacio I. Solís-Cisneros
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This work experimentally evaluates the dynamic reproducibility of a thermoelectric atmospheric water generation (AWG) prototype operated under six control strategies. Three independent trials were conducted per strategy, and time-series data of cold-side temperature, hot-side temperature, energy consumption, and coefficient of performance were analyzed. Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) was used to quantify temporal similarity and capture non-linear deviations across repetitions. Results show that energy consumption remains highly reproducible, with average DTW distances below 1.15 Wh in most cases. In contrast, thermal variables exhibit significant dispersion, with DTW values reaching up to 113.53 for the Thermal Lift Coefficient. These findings demonstrate a clear dynamic decoupling between energy stability and thermal response, indicating that stable electrical input does not guarantee consistent thermal behavior. The observed variability is attributed to the intrinsic thermal inertia and non-linear dynamics of the Peltier effect, which amplify minor operational fluctuations. The results highlight the limitations of average performance metrics and emphasize the need for time-resolved analysis and advanced control strategies to ensure reliable operation of thermoelectric AWG systems.

title: EnCANet: A Network with Entropy-Guided Saliency and Cross-Spatial Attention for Urban Building Change Detection
authors: Tiankai Ge, Xianglong Qiao, Yanyan Zhang
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Remote sensing change detection (RSCD) is essential for urban building monitoring, yet accurate building change extraction is often limited by the structural complexity of buildings and spatial heterogeneity. To address these challenges, we propose EnCANet, a supervised RSCD framework for urban building change detection that explicitly models spatial information density and cross-spatial dependencies. The framework comprises three key components: (1) an Entropy-guided Saliency Prior (ESP) module that computes local channel-wise entropy to quantify spatial information density, generating adaptive weights to guide early-stage feature modulation; (2) an Entropy-Focused Cross-Spatial Attention (EnFocSA) mechanism that integrates entropy priors into attention calculation to suppress pseudo-change responses while enhancing cross-region contextual discrimination; and (3) a Uni-FiRE decoder that employs a unified fusion-and-refinement strategy to restore multi-scale details and ensure structural consistency. Extensive experiments on the LEVIR-CD and WHU-CD building change detection datasets demonstrate that EnCANet achieves F1 scores of 92.25% and 94.27%, surpassing the closest state-of-the-art competitors by 0.46% and 1.01%, respectively. These results quantitatively demonstrate the effectiveness of integrating entropy-driven spatial priors and cross-spatial attention for accurate and robust urban building change detection in complex heterogeneous environments. Our code is available at https://github.com/SkyeGe04/EnCANet.

title: Fine-Grained Features in Any-Shot Learning: A Survey
authors: Qiangchang Wang, Wenhao Li, Jinglin Zhang, Zheyun Qin, Linhao Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence
abstract: Deep learning has achieved remarkable success across a wide range of domains and applications over the past few decades. However, the high performance of most deep learning models is heavily dependent on large volumes of labeled data, which is often labor-intensive, time-consuming, and sometimes impractical to collect. To address this challenge, anyshot learning (ASL)—such as few-shot and zero-shot learning—has been proposed to recognize novel classes with only a few labeled examples or class descriptions. The human visual system excels at identifying discriminative regions in complex scenes, and inspired by this capability, fine-grained features are extracted from input data to emphasize key regions, advancing the field of ASL. Despite the success of these methods, there is a lack of comprehensive surveys on the use of fine-grained features in ASL. This paper provides a detailed survey of recent research on fine-grained features in few-shot and zero-shot learning, offering summaries of various approaches, discussing their strengths and limitations, and identifying key challenges, future directions, and research opportunities to drive further advancements in the field. We also explore the potential of ASL in semi-supervised learning, weakly supervised learning, and domain adaptation and highlight its application

title: Wireless Channel Randomness Integrated Spread Spectrum Sequence Generation
authors: Dongming Li, Yuting Lai, Dong Wei, Meng Zhang, Dawei Wang, Xianglin Fan, Linchao Yang, Lin Bai
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Spread spectrum communication plays a vital role in safeguarding the Internet of Things systems, due to its inherently low probability of interception and anti-jamming capability. However, conventional spread spectrum systems based on pseudorandom sequences are disadvantageous in limited sequence length and deterministic periodicity, making them vulnerable to brute-force attacks. To address these limitations and enhance the randomness of the spread spectrum sequences, a novel wireless channel randomness integrated spread spectrum sequence generation method is proposed in this work. Taking advantages of the intrinsic randomness, temporal variations, and unpredictability of wireless channel fadings, the proposed approach converts the extracted channel features into ordered sequences, which are then used to control the selection of irreducible generating polynomials for spread spectrum sequence generation. The proposed method improves the randomness and secrecy of the integrated spread spectrum sequence. Theoretical analysis and simulation results demonstrate that the proposed sequences not only achieve higher randomness entropy compared to the traditional m-sequence, but also pass the National Institute of Standards and Technology randomness tests. Furthermore, performance evaluations under various signal-to-interference ratio conditions show improved autocorrelation properties and largely lower bit error rates, validating the effectiveness of the proposed method in improving the anti-jamming capability.

title: Exploring Continuous Modes-Based Nonlinear Embedding Models for GaN HEMT Power Amplifiers
authors: Ahmad Zakaria Ahmad, Mohammad Abdul Shukoor, Patrick Roblin, Karun Rawat
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This work presents a nonlinear embedding model approach for designing continuous class E (CCE) power amplifiers (PAs) based on gallium nitride (GaN)-HEMT packaged devices with harmonics above 6 GHz. This approach extends the embedding model for broadband PAs by enabling direct access to the current generator plane (CP), as well as the accurate projection of loads at the package plane (PP). By exploiting the embedding model’s ability to enforce Foster behavior at PP, this method circumvents non-Foster load challenges and simplifies the design of wideband passive matching networks. CCE design parameter (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\phi $</tex-math> </inline-formula>) possibility is investigated over the operating frequency range to achieve the wideband Foster behavior. For validation, two CCE PA designs with CGHV1F025S (plastic DFN) and CGH40025F (ceramic) devices are implemented using the advancements in the extracted embedding model. These PAs deliver 43–44.5 dBm of output power and 51.3%–69.1% drain efficiency (DE) in the S-band. This work provides comprehensive, systematic guidelines for parameter extraction, and the results establish embedding model-assisted CCE design as a scalable solution for high-efficiency broadband RF transmitters.

title: Reliability-Based Design and Control of Crystallization Systems Under Parametric and Process Uncertainties_supp1-3670402.pdf
authors: Zoltan Nagy
publish: N/A
abstract: Population balance model-based approaches are widely employed in crystallization process design to meet the industry-specific critical quality attributes (CQAs). However, their reliability is often limited by parameter uncertainties arising from noisy experimental data, which if, unaccounted for, can lead to suboptimal or failed designs. This study introduces a novel reliability-based design optimization (RBDO) framework for open-loop design of crystallization systems under uncertainty. The framework formulates CQA constraints as probabilistic constraints with user-defined reliability thresholds, enabling quantified, risk-informed process design. To solve the resulting RBDO problems efficiently, a surrogate modeling-based, nested simulation-optimization workflow is developed. The proposed methodology is applied to both batch and continuous pharmaceutical crystallization systems, demonstrating its ability to generate robust design trajectories with significantly higher probabilities of meeting CQAs under both parametric and operational uncertainties compared to the deterministic designs. The computational efficiency and accuracy of the approach are further validated through benchmarking against back-mapping-based solutions of the underlying chance constrained formulations.

title: Background-Noise-Driven Detection of Diffusion-Generated Images_supp1-3678476.pdf
authors: ou weihua
publish: N/A
abstract: Diffusion-based image generation has proliferated, making robust detection of synthetic imagery critical. We propose a background-noise-driven detector motivated by the observation that real camera images preserve physical noise traces, whereas model-generated images tend to exhibit algorithm-induced residual statistics. Starting from sRGB, we apply a deterministic inverse ISP to obtain an approximate Bayer RAW representation, train a lightweight RAW-domain denoiser, and extract a noise residual by subtracting the denoised reconstruction from the inverse-ISP signal. A detector trained only on ADM residuals generalizes in a zero-shot manner to a wide range of unseen diffusion generators. By emphasizing noise-domain cues rather than semantic content, the proposed method offers a simple and practical approach to diffusion-generated image detection.

title: Improved Discrete Spider Monkey Optimization Using a Dynamic Penalty Function with Partial Constraint Violation Acceptance for Vending Machine Column Optimization Considering Sales and a Replenishment Cycle
authors: Riko Hasegawa, Yoshikazu Fukuyama, Takuya Watanabe, Naoto Ishibashi, Tatsuya Iizaka
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: This paper proposes an improved discrete spider monkey optimization based method using a dynamic penalty function with partial constraint violation acceptance for vending machine column optimization considering sales and a replenishment cycle. The conventional method has a challenge that it only searches for feasible regions. To address the challenge, the proposed method added a dynamic penalty function in order to search for infeasible regions. Moreover, partial constraint violation acceptance rate is introduced to obtain high quality solutions. Effectiveness of the proposed method is verified by comparing with the conventional method, a method with only a static penalty function, a method with only a dynamic penalty function with actual vending machine data.

title: A DBN-Based Reliability Analysis of Aircraft Braking Systems
authors: Jihui Xu, Rong Liu, Wenjie Tian
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computing, Robotics and System Sciences (ICRSS)
abstract: A reliability analysis and fault diagnosis method for aircraft braking systems based on dynamic Bayesian networks (DBN) is proposed to address the reliability and fault diagnosis issues of such systems. First, conditional probability tables and marginal probability tables for each node are given by integrating failure rates and repair rates, based on the dynamic logic gates of DBN and combined with probability table modeling methods. Second, a multi-level DBN model for typical fault modules of the braking system is constructed by considering standby components and historical fault data, in accordance with the dynamic operational characteristics of the aircraft braking system. The reliability functions of components in each module are derived through the inference algorithm of DBN. Finally, laboratory simulation data are used for verification. The simulation results provide the reliability curves of each subsystem module, achieve system fault assessment and positioning of weak components, and validate the practicality of this method.

title: Automated Snow Depth Measurement and Its Transmission
authors: Praveen Kumar Mishra, Sachin Kumar, Rajesh Kumar Garg
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: Accurate snow depth measurement is critical for avalanche forecasting and for safeguarding communities and infrastructure in the snow bound regions. Monitoring snow depth enables assessment of snowpack stability and supports timely issuance of warnings and advisories to mitigate risks. This paper presents an automated method for snow depth measurement using images of snow stakes captured remotely by a fixed-position camera. The system employs a cost-effective yet efficient combination of a fixed camera and a processing unit. While largely autonomous, the method requires minimal user input for initializing a reference image patch. The approach integrates image processing and machine learning techniques, including image patch similarity analysis and optical character recognition (OCR), to determine snow depth. After detection of snow depth, measured snow depth is transmitted through a GSM modem using protocol description unit (PDU) mode over short message service. The proposed method offers a low-cost solution for image transmission in bandwidth constraint environment and in remote areas where there are no operators available for manual measurement.

title: A Novel Automatic Modulation Recognition Methodology based on Attention Mechanism
authors: Yue Hu, Yu Long, Jinchao Hu, Yongyang Hu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cognitive and Intelligent Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Automatic modulation recognition is a key technology in cognitive intelligence, which can be used to distinguish signal modulation modes, especially for non-cooperative signals. The development of deep learning empowers automatic modulation recognition to be more intelligent and demonstrates better performance than traditional methods. However, it should be noted that automatic modulation recognition based on deep learning still has shortcomings. Firstly, the existing methods still cannot fully extract signal features. Secondly, the extracted features are directly used for brute force classification in most methods, which ignores the deep processing of features. These issues limit the further improvement of recognition accuracy. To solve the problems mentioned above, a novel automatic modulation recognition methodology based on attention mechanism is proposed. Firstly, a feature extraction enhancement module is designed to optimize feature extraction of signal. Then, a feature cross fusion module is proposed to fuse the enhanced features extracted from In-phase signal and Quadrature-phase signal respectively. The experiment is conducted to prove the effectiveness of our proposed methodology based on the benchmark dataset RadioML2016.10A. Compared to the existing popular deep learning methods, our methodology exhibits better performance, with recognition accuracy increased by 1.82% to 14.40%.

title: Investigating Slow Multiplicative Noise and its Influence on ECG Signals
authors: Gokul Prakash, Libya Thomas
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: The influence of slow multiplicative noise (MN) on signal quality has been studied mainly from a theoretical perspective. Earlier work derived closed-form expressions for the energy spectrum of the noise modulation function (NMF), showing that such noise introduces both discrete and continuous spectral components. In this paper, we build on that foundation and move toward practical validation. Using synthetic electrocardiogram (ECG) signals created with NeuroKit2, we simulate amplitude and phase distortions modeled as Gaussian processes. The time and frequency domain behavior of the ensuing noise-modulated signals are investigated with particular focus on energy spectra, pulse fluctuations, and phase changes. To emphasize the distinctive role played by MN, we also contrast its action with that of additive noise. The overall goal is to introduce insight into the way MN deforms biomedical signals like ECG, and why its action has to be taken into account in clinical and wearable monitoring scenarios.

title: Research on Fault Traveling Wave Location of Medium-Voltage Cables Based on DWT-VMD and Time-Domain Cross-Correlation
authors: Aoyu Liu, Xiaotong Jing, Xiangjun Zeng, Jian Huang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Medium-voltage cables are the core equipment of 35kV collection lines in new energy power stations, and their fault location accuracy directly affects the operation and maintenance efficiency and power supply stability of the stations. To address the problems of traditional location methods being affected by noise interference and inaccurate time delay calculation, this paper proposes a traveling wave location method based on DWT-VMD decomposition and time-domain cross-correlation. Through wavelet denoising and VMD feature extraction, the optimal decomposition layer is selected by combining a dynamic weight scoring system, and the traveling wave time delay is accurately calculated using time-domain cross-correlation. Verified by simulation and experimental platforms, the method effectively suppresses noise and refraction-reflection interference, featuring high positioning accuracy and strong robustness, which provides reliable technical support for the rapid fault location of cables in new energy power stations.

title: Dual-Phase Graph Refinement: Pruning Noisy Edges and Augmenting Tail Links for Recommendation
authors: Ziyuan Zhu, Wentao Huang, Jinhui Zhang, Haibo Ye
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyberworlds (CW）
abstract: Implicit feedback recommender systems face significant performance challenges due to noisy interactions and sparse data from long-tail users. Noisy edges propagate errors through graph convolutions, while sparse interactions of long-tail users bias recommendations toward popular items, compromising fairness and accuracy. This paper proposes a novel Dual-Phase Graph Refinement (DPGR) framework, built upon GNN, to optimize the interaction graph structure through synergistic denoising and long-tail link augmentation. In the first phase, DPGR employs node neighborhood similarity with a dynamic thresholding mechanism to accurately identify and remove unreliable edges, constructing a high-quality subgraph to enhance embedding robustness. In the second phase, targeting longtail users-defined as the bottom 80% by interaction count-DPGR selectively adds high-confidence links via a probabilistic sampling model, enriching their neighborhood representations and mitigating sparsity. Comprehensive empirical studies on three diverse datasets demonstrate that DPGR outperforms other methods in both overall and long-tail performance, achieving average improvements of 7.98% and 6.53% over the baseline, respectively, across Recall and NDCG metrics. Ablation studies confirm the necessity of the dual-phase design, and noise injection experiments further highlight the method's robustness. By jointly optimizing noise reduction and long-tail augmentation, DPGR offers an efficient and equitable solution for graph-based collaborative filtering.

title: Smart Assistance System for Alzheimer's Patients Using the Internet of Things
authors: Anita Dombale, Ishika Mahadar, Heramb Mahadik, Dhruv Madderlawar, Amruta Magar, Aditya Loya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease leads to memory decline and creates significant challenges for caregivers. This paper introduces a Smart Assistance System that uses IoT and AI to bring together various support modules into one mobile platform. The system combines real-time GPS tracking with geofencing, fall detection, medication reminders, appointment reminders, behavioral logging, and a digital family tree. It uses dual Firebase databases for better data handling. A notable feature is the AI caregiver chatbot, which offers guidance and helps reduce the burden on caregivers, an area that has not been fully explored in previous research. We validated the hardware implementation with ESP32, NEO-6M GPS, and MPU6050 sensors in controlled trials. These trials showed 95.6% accuracy in geofencing and 92% accuracy in fall detection. Caregiver alerts were sent out within 3 to 5 seconds. Unlike existing systems that focus on single functions, NeuroCare provides a unified, affordable, and scalable solution for Alzheimer's care. Future work will include predictive analytics, biosensor integration, and improved data security.

title: Adaptive Channel Switching Algorithm for Enhanced Resilience in Hybrid OCC-RF Drone Systems
authors: Yukang Kim, Ki-Hyung Kim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) communications face dual challenges: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R F}$</tex> channels are vulnerable to interception and jamming attacks [1], while Optical Camera Communication (OCC) offers security but suffers from physical disruptions [2] [3]. This paper proposes a Hybrid Adaptive Channel Switching Algorithm that designates OCC as the main secure channel with RF as emergency backup. The algorithm employs hierarchical defense: (1) cryptographic filtering of logical attacks (replay, tampering) via nonce and MAC verification without channel switching [7]; (2) adaptive FEC (LDPC/Reed-Solomon) redundancy adjustment based on the experimentally derived physical collapse threshold of BER 0.02 and (3) autonomous RF switching upon physical collapse detection. Simulation across six attack scenarios demonstrates successful logical attack filtering <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(<1 ~\text{ms}$</tex> Response Time), adaptive resilience under degraded conditions, and reliable failover during jamming/blockage <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{(}\boldsymbol{\sim}\mathbf{500ms}$</tex> switching response time), ensuring continuous drone control even under severe cyber-physical threats.

title: Structured Prompt Optimization for Few-Shot Text Classification via Semantic Alignment in Latent Space
authors: Jiasen Zheng, Zijun Zhou, Huajun Zhang, Junjiang Lin, Jingyun Jia, Qi Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: This study addresses the issues of semantic entanglement, unclear label structure, and insufficient feature representation in few-shot text classification, and proposes an optimization framework based on structured prompts to enhance semantic understanding and task adaptation under low-resource conditions. The framework first uses a pretrained language model to encode the input text and obtain basic semantic representations. It then introduces structured prompts composed of multi-dimensional semantic factors and integrates them with text features through a learnable combination mechanism, which forms task-related representations with clear boundaries in the latent space. To further strengthen the consistency between text representations and label semantics, the method constructs a structured label embedding matrix and employs a cross-space alignment mechanism to ensure stable matching between textual features and label attributes. In addition, the model applies prompt orthogonality constraints and a joint optimization objective to maintain independence across different semantic factors in the prompts, allowing the structured prompts to provide transparent and controllable guidance for classification decisions. Three types of sensitivity experiments, including learning rate sensitivity, prompt length sensitivity, and data scale sensitivity, are designed to evaluate the stability and robustness of the framework under different conditions. Experimental results show that the proposed structured prompt optimization framework effectively alleviates semantic conflicts and label ambiguity in few-shot text classification. It significantly improves performance on accuracy, precision, recall, and AUC, and demonstrates strong cross-task applicability. Overall, the method achieves stable semantic modeling under limited data and offers an efficient and interpretable optimization pathway for few-shot text classification.

title: Embedding-Only Federated Edge Learning for Privacy-Preserving eHealth
authors: Bharathwaj Vijayakumar, Samyukta Alapati, Sahana Varadaraju
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: The next wave of digital health innovation lies in combining federated learning and edge intelligence for secure, low-latency physiological monitoring. This paper introduces a federated self-supervised edge framework for wearable devices that track physiological transitions such as heart-rate variability, skin temperature, and galvanic skin response. The system enables decentralized learning directly on devices without transmitting sensitive data, addressing key challenges in privacy and communication efficiency. Each client employs a lightweight one-dimensional convolutional encoder trained via contrastive self-supervised learning to capture temporal patterns in local signals. Instead of sharing raw data or model weights, each device uploads a compressed embedding centroid to a central aggregator that computes a global reference embedding for anomaly scoring. A prototype simulation across five edge clients achieved Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic (AUROC) <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.86$</tex> while reducing communication overhead by 82 %. Visualization via t-SNE revealed separable manifolds of normal and anomalous physiology, demonstrating a novel, communication-efficient paradigm for privacy-preserving, federated IoT-based healthcare.

title: A General Framework for Neural Adaptive Sensing of Dynamical Fields
authors: Felix Köster, Atsushi Uchida
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Physical dynamical systems can be considered as natural information processors, where their trajectories encode past inputs in high-dimensional continuous states. Reservoir computing exploits this property by driving a fixed nonlinear dynamical system and learning only a linear output mapping, however traditional approaches assume fixed measurement locations and weights, which limits robustness across different regimes and substrates. This work introduces a general framework for adaptive measurement of continuous physical dynamics using a partial-differential-equation-based reservoir, where both the spatial positions of the measurements and the output weighting are learned jointly. The method dynamically determines how and where to observe the state of the system and optimizes sensor placement end-to-end. Experiments on forecasting tasks involving multiple chaotic systems demonstrate substantial improvements in accuracy compared to fixed-measurement reservoir computing approaches, indicating that adaptive measurement provides an effective interface for extracting task-relevant information from continuous dynamical systems.

title: Cross-view UAV Geo-localization via Wavelet-based Local Feature Enhancement
authors: Chen Zhang, Rui Zhao, Zhiping Lin, Zhenguo Gao, Qing an Peng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Cross-view image matching serves as a pivotal technology for UAV autonomous localization and navigation.
