abstract: Stock market forecasting remains a challenging task due to the nonlinear, volatile, and stochastic nature of financial time- series data. This study aims to (i) comparatively evaluate statistical and machine learning approaches for stock price prediction, (ii) assess the impact of technical feature engineering on forecasting accuracy, and (iii) analyze the suitability of different models for volatile technology-driven stocks. A comparative framework is developed using ARIMA, Prophet, and Random Forest regression to forecast the daily closing prices of Tesla and Google stocks. Historical price data are preprocessed and enriched with technical indicators, including moving averages, momentum indicators, and volatility measures. Model performance is evaluated using Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE), and Coefficient of Determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>). Experimental results demonstrate that the Random Forest model consistently outperforms ARIMA and Prophet, achieving lower prediction errors due to its ability to capture nonlinear relationships through engineered features. ARIMA and Prophet serve as effective statistical baselines for trend and seasonal analysis. The findings confirm that feature-driven ensemble learning significantly enhances predictive accuracy, highlighting its applicability in algorithmic trading and decision-support systems.

title: Multimodal Representation Learning for Modeling New Quality Productivity in Complex Systems
authors: Runhan Fang, Rui Sun, Jingchong Yang, Yulin Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: In response to the growing need for intelligent analysis of productivity dynamics, this paper presents a computational framework for modeling and predicting new quality productivity using machine learning and data-driven techniques. A multi-dimensional productivity index is constructed by integrating heterogeneous features from economic, social, and environmental datasets. Advanced algorithms, including gradient boosting machines, graph neural networks, and attention-based temporal models, are employed to capture the complex nonlinear relationships and temporal dependencies. Feature selection, model interpretability, and sensitivity analysis are conducted to identify the key digital transformation and human-capital-related factors contributing to productivity evolution. The proposed framework demonstrates high predictive performance and offers an interpretable basis for understanding how digital systems and human-centric variables interact within productivity models. This research bridges the gap between data science and productivity intelligence, offering scalable tools for automatic productivity assessment and real-time decision support in digital ecosystems.

title: Towards Deploying VLA Without Fine-Tuning: Plug-and-Play Inference-Time VLA Policy Steering Via Embodied Evolutionary Diffusion_supp1-3678455.mp4
authors: Zhuo Li
publish: N/A
abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have demonstrated significant potential in real-world robotic manipulation. However, pre-trained VLA policies still suffer from substantial performance degradation during downstream deployment. Although fine-tuning can mitigate this issue, its reliance on costly demonstration collection and intensive computation makes it impractical in real-world settings. In this work, we introduce VLA-Pilot, a plug-and-play inference-time policy steering method for zero-shot deployment of pre-trained VLA without any additional fine-tuning or data collection. We evaluate VLA-Pilot on both simulation and real-world experiments across distinct robotic embodiments. Experimental results demonstrate that VLA-Pilot substantially boosts the success rates of off-the-shelf pre-trained VLA policies, enabling robust zero-shot generalization to diverse downstream tasks and embodiments. Experimental videos and code are available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://rip4kobe.github.io/vla-pilot/</uri>.

title: Noncommutative Quantum Dynamics for Feature Representation Learning
authors: Hirokazu Shimauchi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: We propose an explicit feature representation for supervised learning derived from short quantum dynamical trajectories composed of generally noncommuting operations, conceptually inspired by reservoir computing in that fixed, untrained dynamics are coupled to a simple trained readout. For each input, a fixed-depth circuit generates a sequence of prefix states, and features are formed by concatenating expectation values of a predetermined set of single- and two-qubit Pauli observables recorded across depths; multiple randomized but fixed circuit instantiations are aggregated to improve stability. A classical l2- regularized logistic-regression readout is trained on the resulting explicit features. To isolate representational effects, all methods are evaluated under identical preprocessing, repeated stratified holdout splits, and a unified model-selection protocol on a controlled subset of 11 tabular classification datasets, and are compared against classical baselines, including random Fourier features and second-degree polynomial expansions, as well as reservoir-style quantum baselines, with feature dimensionality matched when feasible. We further examine controlled variants targeting commutativity, entangling structure, history concatenation, and observable choice. A staged evaluation contrasts an idealized exact-expectation regime with finite-shot estimation under fixed and larger measurement budgets, thereby assessing sensitivity to sampling noise after re-tuning regularization. Beyond predictive performance, we analyze the induced geometry via empirical kernel spectra and effective-dimension diagnostics to contextualize the representations from a regularization-aware capacity perspective. Empirically, the strongest support concerns noncommutativity and history concatenation, whereas the contribution of the entangling block is mixed; the trajectory-based features induce empirically distinct kernel geometries, while finite-shot robustness remains strongly task-and configuration-dependent.

title: Improving the Restore Performance of Fine-Grained Deduplication on Docker Container Image Storage
authors: Haoliang Tan, Wenhao Ou, Xiangyu Zou, Lisha Qin, Cai Deng, Zhaoquan Gu, Wen Xia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems
abstract: The rapid growth of container images leads to heavy storage pressures on image registries. Fine-grained deduplication, such as at the file and chunk levels, is a promising technique for reducing the storage space in image registries, compared to Docker's native coarse-grained image layer deduplication. However, fine-grained deduplication often incurs significant image restoration latency due to fragmented I/Os, resulting in up to 8× restoration I/O slowdowns. Moreover, existing restore-optimized deduplication techniques are not tailored to the characteristics of the container image, resulting in low deduplication ratios of images. Consequently, restoring performance remains a critical barrier to the practical adoption of fine-grained deduplication in container image registries. To address this challenge, we propose MiDedup, a restore-friendly fine-grained deduplication approach tailored for container image registries. MiDedup is built on an underexplored observation: the redundancy across layers of images follows a non-uniform distribution. Based on this insight, MiDedup introduces three core techniques: ① Across-layer-aware reorganization, which reorganizes deduplicated data into a compact, sequential layout, significantly reduces fragmented I/Os during image restore. ② Popularity-aware rewriting, which selectively rewrites hot image layers to further improve the balance between deduplication ratio and restore performance. ③ Hybrid-granularity deduplication, which combines file-level and chunk-level deduplication to reduce metadata and fragmented data. Experiments on the real-world Docker image dataset show that MiDedup reduces the 50–80% restore I/O overhead of fine-grained deduplication, which is comparable to the restore performance of native layer-level deduplication, while achieving 1.2–7.3× higher deduplication ratios.

title: Miniaturized Wideband Filtering Antenna on Glass Substrate for mm-Wave AiP
authors: Sha Xu, Hongsheng Cai, Mankai Jiang, Qiongsen Wu
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This letter presents a miniaturized wideband millimeter-wave (mm-wave) filtering antenna with high out-of-band suppression, implemented on a glass substrate using through-glass via (TGV) technology. The antenna topology integrates a primary radiating patch with parasitic elements and a set of planar inverted-F antennas (PIFAs). The PIFA structures, enabled by metallized vias, are key to achieving both a wide impedance bandwidth and a sharp gain roll-off in the lower stopband. Concurrently, two sets of slots etched onto the main patch introduce two additional radiation nulls to ensure strong suppression in the upper stopband. The single antenna element maintains a compact footprint of 0.7 λ0 × 0.56 λ0 × 0.065 λ0 (@ 24 GHz). For experimental validation, a 1 4 linear array was fabricated and measured. The measured results demonstrate a 10-dB bandwidth of 21.8 - 30 GHz ( 31.7%) and a peak gain of 10.7 dBi. The three well-defined radiation nulls collectively provide over 13 dB of out-of-band suppression. The combination of wideband filtering performance, compact footprint, and a silicon-compatible glass-based platform make the proposed antenna an excellent candidate for highly integrated Antenna-in-Package (AiP) solutions for 5G and beyond mm-wave communication systems.

title: Event-based Facial Keypoint Alignment via Cross-Modal Fusion Attention and Self-Supervised Multi-Event Representation Learning
authors: Donghwa Kang, Junho Kim, Dongwoo Kang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Event cameras offer unique advantages for facial keypoint alignment under challenging conditions, such as low light and rapid motion, due to their high temporal resolution and robustness to varying illumination. However, existing RGB facial keypoint alignment methods do not perform well on event data, and training solely on event data often leads to suboptimal performance because of its limited spatial information. Moreover, the lack of comprehensive labeled event datasets further hinders progress in this area. To address these issues, we propose a novel framework based on cross-modal fusion attention (CMFA) and self-supervised multi-event representation learning (SSMER) for event-based facial keypoint alignment. Our framework employs CMFA to integrate corresponding RGB data, guiding the model to extract robust facial features from event input images. In parallel, SSMER enables effective feature learning from unlabeled event data, overcoming spatial limitations. Extensive experiments on our real‑event E‑SIE dataset and a synthetic‑event version of the public WFLW‑V benchmark show that our approach consistently surpasses state‑of‑the‑art methods across multiple evaluation metrics.

title: Bearing-Only Formation Control of Nonholonomic Unicycles: A Conditional Disturbance Utilization Method
authors: Qin Wang, Hanyu Yin, Yanmeng Zhang, Yang Yi, Wenbing Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: Maintaining a stable formation among nonholonomic unicycles presents a significant challenge, particularly when subjected to real-world disturbances such as wheel slipping and motor noise. To tackle this issue, we propose a conditional disturbance utilization (CDU) framework that selectively uses favorable disturbances rather than simply rejecting all. First, the disturbance observer (DO) is employed to establish the informational foundation for subsequent conditional utilization decisions. And, the CDU mechanism dynamically decides whether the disturbance aligns with the desired control objective. Moreover, incorporating bearing rigidity achieves global stability with fast convergence. Specifically, the proposed potential function, constructed from bearing information, mitigates disturbance driven collision risks and ensures safety. Finally, simulations and experiments validate the proposed CDU-based formation control. The results demonstrate substantial improvements in responsiveness, and efficiency when compared to disturbance suppression method, highlighting the superiority of the proposed framework.

title: Towards Generalized and Explainable Face Anti-Spoofing via Decomposition–Fusion Networks
authors: Dhimas Arief Dharmawan, Anto Satriyo Nugroho, Seviko Attalarik Pramayudha Handoko
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biometrics, Behavior, and Identity Science
abstract: This paper presents DFNet, a decomposition–fusion network for generalized and interpretable face anti-spoofing. DFNet comprises two explicitly designed branches—a decomposition branch and a fusion branch—that jointly enhance robustness and interpretability in presentation attack detection. The decomposition branch extracts multi-level representations through a two-stage process, where coarse features and saliency maps are first computed to emphasize prominent regions, followed by the suppression of these regions to facilitate fine-grained feature extraction from less dominant areas. The fusion branch integrates the coarse and fine-grained streams using a residual architecture augmented with convolutional block attention modules, while a dynamic weighting mechanism adaptively balances their contributions during feature fusion. DFNet is extensively evaluated on multiple public benchmarks under both intra- and cross-dataset protocols, and its robustness is further assessed under strong Projected Gradient Descent (PGD) adversarial attacks. Experimental results demonstrate that DFNet achieves competitive or superior performance across diverse datasets and attack scenarios, while maintaining improved resilience to adversarial perturbations. Moreover, the modular, attention-driven design of DFNet provides saliency-based insights into the learned spoof traces, supporting its interpretability. Overall, this work high-lights the effectiveness of explicitly incorporating generalization, robustness, and interpretability into architectural design for real-world face anti-spoofing, with DFNet offering a concrete step toward this goal. For reproducibility, the source code is publicly available at https://github.com/dhimasarief/DFNet.

title: Proficient implementation of Resource Allocation in New Radio based Vertical Network Slicing
authors: Kalpana Naidu, Narasimha Rao Banavathu, Vanlin Sathya, Kota Nageswara Rao, Sunkaraboina Sreenu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: This paper addresses the channel and power allocation (CAP) problem in a downlink vertical network slicing (VNS) system. In this downlink, a Terrestrial Base Station and an Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) cooperatively serve multiple ground users. Besides, UAV exploits both licensed and unlicensed spectrum resources to enhance the network capacity. Thus, an optimization framework for CAP is developed to maximize the aggregate downlink throughput under interference and minimum-data rate constraints. In order to resolve CAP, conventional iterative algorithms incur high computational complexity. In contrast, the proposed analytical approach provides predominantly closed-form solutions for the CAP problem. Further, the proposed analysis establishes the uniqueness of the obtained solution by deriving closed-form expressions for the required number of channels (based on channel conditions). Moreover, simulation results confirm the effectiveness of the proposed framework. Besides, simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves lower computational complexity, while maintaining performance comparable to that of iterative algorithms.

title: 50 & 25 YEARS AGO
authors: Erich Neuhold
publish: Computer
abstract: Summaries of articles presented in this issue of the publication.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence

title: ELA-FPM: An Energy-Efficient LUT-based Approximate Floating-Point Multiplier for Neural Network Acceleration
authors: Mahla Salehi Sheikhali Kelayeh, Asma Naserirad, Sahand Divsalar, Omid Salehinia, Shaghayegh Vahdat
publish: IEEE Micro
abstract: As artificial intelligence applications grow in practicality and complexity, efficient hardware implementation of these algorithms turns into a critical issue. Floating-point multiplication is one of the most fundamental, yet power-hungry, operations in modern artificial intelligence algorithms. This paper proposes a novel floating-point approximate multiplier, called ELA-FPM, which uses a combination of static segmentation of input operands and an energy-efficient lookup table-based method to significantly alleviate the overhead of mantissa multiplication, which is the most power-consuming operation in a floating-point multiplier. The superiority of ELA-FPM manifests itself as delay, power, and area improvements of up to 68.3%, 95.3%, and 92.1%, compared to an exact floating-point multiplier and improvements of up to 65.6%, 76%, and 71%, compared to other state-of-the-art approximate multipliers. Furthermore, ELA-FPM is tested on some of the most famous convolutional neural network models, demonstrating a maximum accuracy drop of 0.67% compared to a model that uses exact multipliers.

title: Active Coupling Control-Based Transient Frequency–Voltage Coordinated Support for Grid-Following Inverters
authors: Jingrong Yu, Linyi Ju, Yijun Wang, Yan Li, Jiaqi Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: The modern power systems with high proportion of renewable energy features “weak frequency” and “weak voltage” characteristics, imposing stringent requirement on the transient frequency and voltage support of grid-following (GFL) inverters in renewable energy generation systems. Existing grid support methods for GFL inverters are unable to simultaneously address both rate of change of frequency (ROCOF) and rate of change of voltage (ROCOV) under grid disturbances such as voltage sags. To overcome this limitation, the proposed method investigates the coupling mechanism between transient frequency and voltage when the grid impedance is of resistive-inductive characteristics. On the basis of this coupling mechanism, a frequency–voltage coordinated support method is proposed by employ an active coupling gain between the active power and reactive power. Compared with the existing method, the proposed method employs the active coupling rather than decouple ideas to enable the joint optimization of transient rate of change of ROCOF and ROCOV, yielding better grid compatibility with wide variation range of short-circuit ratio (SCR) and resistance-to-inductance(X/R) ratio, and smaller extrema of ROCOF and ROCOV. Both simulation and experimental results verified the effectiveness and feasibility of the proposed method.

title: Finite-Time Disturbance Observer-Based Adaptive Second-Order Sliding Mode Control for Parallel Connected DC–DC Buck Converters With Nonideal Switches
authors: Cong Wang, Li Li, Minghui Yao, Yan Niu, Qiliang Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Parallel connected DC–DC buck converters (PCBCs) are inevitably subjected to the disturbances caused by nonideal active switches and uncertain passive components, which poses a significant challenge for achieving both precise current sharing and robust voltage regulation. To address the coupling between inductor current and output voltage, a multilayer control framework equipped with a slow voltage controller and fast current controllers is developed, which can automatically deal with the load variations, and be easily extended to PCBC with <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$N$</tex-math> </inline-formula> modules. Subsequently, the nonideal characteristics in PCBC are modeled as disturbances. The constructed finite-time disturbance observer (FTDO) can effectively estimate the disturbances, and then a feedforward compensation is made by using the obtained estimates. To mitigate chattering with the further aim of enhancing control accuracy of output voltage, a finite-time stable adaptive second-order sliding mode (SOSM) control is proposed, in which the adaptation algorithm achieves rapid reaching of sliding manifold, and avoids excessive control action. The presented control strategy can obtain fast and accurate tracking of output voltage, as well as balanced current sharing. Experimental results are presented to verify the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Sparse SAR Separated Iterative Imaging for Stationary and Moving Targets
authors: Yufan Song, Fuxuan Cai, Cuijun Liu, Jingjing Zhang, Liping Wang, Wen Hong, Hui Bi
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: In synthetic aperture radar (SAR) imaging, moving targets often appear along with the stationary scene in the surveillance regions. The imaging results of moving targets are defocused by using traditional imaging methods since the velocities of them are unknown. Most ground moving target imaging (GMTIm) methods ignore the stationary targets when analyzing the signal of moving target. Therefore, effective imaging methods for both stationary and moving targets focusing in the same surveillance region are worth investigating. In this paper, the stationary and moving targets sparse SAR separated iterative imaging model and methods are proposed and used for the real data processing. Firstly, the two-dimensional (2-D) stationary and moving targets separated imaging model is constructed based on approximate observation. Then a novel sparse iterative reweighted algorithm (SIRA) is proposed to solve the constructed model and achieve the focusing of stationary and moving targets simultaneously. Furthermore, on the basis of SIRA, we improve the constraints on the stationary targets to enhance the recovery accuracy about them, and use alternating direction multiplier method (ADMM) to solve the novel optimization problem. Finally, the normalized relative speed estimation (NRSE)-based imaging method for moving target is introduced. Compared with traditional matched filtering (MF)-based and sparse-driven imaging methods, the proposed methods achieve separated reconstruction of moving and stationary targets, respectively while improving the imaging quality. Experiments based on simulated and real Fucheng-1 SAR dataset verify the proposed methods.

title: LISK: A High-Performance In-Memory Learned Index for Variable-Length String Keys
authors: Zhaole Chu, Yigui Yuan, Junli Wang, Xiaoliang Wang, Peiquan Jin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computers
abstract: Learned index has emerged as a new indexing technique that leverages machine learning to accelerate in-memory data processing. However, current learned indexes are primarily designed to index numeric keys and lack robust support for variable-length string keys. In this paper, we propose a novel in-memory learned index called LISK (<underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">L</u>earned <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">I</u>ndex for <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">S</u>tring <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">K</u>eys) to support string keys. The novelty of LISK is two-fold. First, we propose a trie-like structure to address the limitations of linear models in fitting string keys. Each trie node indexes 8-byte key slices, which are organized as learned sub-indexes or B+-trees. Second, we present a new structure for learned sub-indexes, namely TLS (<underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</u>wo-layer <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">L</u>earned <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">S</u>ubindex), which is tailored to handle the complex distribution of string keys. TLS utilizes three key designs to improve the overall performance: (1) a two-phase hybrid index construction, (2) a second-derivative-based data partitioning, and (3) a cachefriendly overflow node design. We conduct extensive experiments on five datasets and six workloads to compare LISK with seven existing indexes, including five trie-based indexes and the state-of-the-art learned index LITS. The experimental results show that LISK achieves an average 1.99× (up to 7.87×) higher throughput across the six workloads on real-world datasets. Specifically, compared with LITS, LISK achieves an average 1.42× (up to 1.91× ) higher throughput. The source code of LISK is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/suibianll/LISK/tree/master</uri>.

title: Virtual Reality Laboratory for Teaching Power Systems in Electrical Engineering Undergraduate Courses
authors: Daniel Rebouças Jaguaribe, Gustavo Alves, Fernando Lessa Tofoli, Ruth Pastôra Saraiva Leão, Lucas Silveira Melo, Raimundo Furtado Sampaio, Antônio José Melo Leite Júnior, Giovanni Cordeiro Barroso
publish: IEEE Transactions on Education
abstract: The study of power substations is fundamental to the training of electrical engineers, as these facilities are crucial to power system operation, ensuring the reliability and efficiency of energy distribution. However, traditional teaching methods often face limitations in offering practical and immersive experiences. Given this gap, this work presents a Virtual Reality Substation Laboratory (VRSL), developed based on a 69/13.8-kV substation at the Federal University of Ceará, Fortaleza, Brazil. The VRSL provides a free-to-use educational platform that allows teachers and students to explore the structural and operational aspects of power substations, including equipment operation and fault simulations. Its integration into two substation-related courses over a three-year period engaged more than 120 students by combining virtual reality (VR) with conventional lectures. The triangulation of quantitative and qualitative analyses demonstrates substantial improvements in academic performance, student engagement, and comprehension of complex substation concepts. By bridging theoretical knowledge and practical simulation, the VRSL enhances engineering education and prepares students for real-world challenges in power systems.

title: Identification of Critical Frequencies in Wideband Near-Field Spatial Scans
authors: Tomas Monopoli, Xinglong Wu, Cheng Yang, Christian Schuster, Sergio Amedeo Pignari, Johannes Wolf, Flavia Grassi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electromagnetic Compatibility
abstract: Identifying critical frequencies of a device under test (DUT) from its near-field scans is an essential step in integrating near-field scanning into electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) testing. Indeed, real electronic devices may exhibit radiated emissions at multiple frequencies, which are unknown a priori. To this end, three computational methods are introduced to identify the frequencies at which the DUT is radiating: the space-filled spectrogram analysis, the grey level co-occurrence matrix approach, and spatial fast Fourier transform approach. Although a single general solution is not provided, each method leverages different properties of the measurements to extract significant features and identify frequencies of interest. Therefore, the choice of which method to apply depends on the characteristics of the DUT and on the requirements of the specific measurement campaign. The proposed methods are evaluated on a common dataset, assessing their computational efficiency, sensitivity to patterns, and robustness against noise. Case studies demonstrate their successful application in real-world scenarios and their comparative analysis provides insights into selecting the most appropriate approach for a specific application.

title: Intelligent flame monitoring system on the robotic dog platform
authors: Yao Wang, Zengwen Zhang, Yiyi Zhou, Baorong Zhan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Fire is a major hidden danger that threatens building safety. There are about 300000 fire disasters in China every year, among which high-rise buildings have particularly serious consequences due to the difficulty of extinguishing fires and the rapid spread of fire. The traditional fixed fire monitoring system relies on a single sensor, which has bottlenecks such as limited monitoring range, poor environmental adaptability, lagging early warning, and insufficient reliability of wired deployment. To overcome the above limitations and combine the development trends of the Internet of Things and robotics technology, an intelligent flame monitoring system based on quadruped robotic dogs is proposed. The system takes the robot dog as the mobile carrier, integrates the multi-mode perception module and edge computing unit composed of infrared flame sensor, multispectral imaging device, temperature and humidity sensor and combustible gas sensor, and relies on the robot dog's excellent ability to surmount obstacles and extreme environmental adaptability to achieve all-round dynamic monitoring of building fire hazards. By adopting the technology path of "multi-sensor fusion+deep learning algorithm", a fire risk assessment model is constructed by collecting real-time data such as flame spectral characteristics, temperature distribution, and gas concentration, to achieve accurate identification and grading warning in the early stage of fire smoldering. Experimental verification shows that the system achieves a flame recognition accuracy of 98.3% in complex building environments, with an alarm response time of less than 2 seconds; Compared to traditional fixed monitoring systems, the monitoring coverage has increased by 40%, and it has outstanding environmental adaptability and dynamic operation performance such as smoke penetration resistance and high temperature resistance. This study provides a scientifically feasible intelligent solution for building fire inspection and complex scene fire warning. Its technical framework can be further extended to fields such as forest fires and industrial site fires, and has important scientific value and engineering application significance.

title: A Method for Locating Single-phase Ground Fault Sections in Distribution Networks Based on a Waveform Similarity Algorithm Using Hausdorff Distance
authors: Yushan Zhou, Mengru Zhang, Yubo Song, Haibo Zhang, Wanying Qi, Jinrui Tang
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: Fault section location constitutes the critical component in single-phase grounding fault diagnosis for distribution networks. Addressing the issues of low accuracy, slow speed and poor adaptability which existing fault section localization methods when dealing with single-phase ground faults in complex distribution networks with multiple nodes, this paper proposes an enhanced approach for fault section localization with the Hausdorff distance as fundation algorithm. Initially, the principle of the waveform similarity algorithm with the Hausdorff distance algorithm as the foundation is analyzed; Second, by leveraging the correlation of the three zero-sequence currents near each node of the distribution line, the relationship between the fault location and the node positions of the distribution line is determined. Further, by means of the Hausdorff distance algorithm, the fault position is identified via analyzing the similarity of current waveforms between the upstream and downstream areas of the fault point. A fault localization method with the Hausdorff distance algorithm as the foundation is established; Finally, a simulation model of a 110kV/10kV non-effectively grounding system was established in MATLAB software. Through theoretical analysis and experimental verification, it was demonstrated that the proposed method can efficiently and reliably complete fault section localization in complex distribution networks with multiple nodes and single-phase ground faults. The proposed methodology demonstrates efficient and reliable fault section identification with computationally efficient operations, strong adaptability to varying network topologies and straightforward engineering implementation.

title: Deep Learning in Sign Language: A Comprehensive Systematic Review of Recent Progress
authors: Ashwini Nikhil Shinde, Rajeswari Kannan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: Advances in recent past years studies focusing on the language of sign recognition and translation have grown, which in turn has improved access to interaction with people who have hearing impairments. We present a survey of recent work in the field and we also report current research trends and the methods are primarily used. We did a systematic literature review which included 32 research articles published between 2019 and 2023 which we looked at in detail. The review is mainly of approaches based on deep neural networks used for SL recognition and translation. We look at what the main models, data sets and support technologies are which we also look at in terms of their pros and cons. We found out that video-based sign to text translation is a key element in the enablement of continuous and effective communication.

title: Consolidated Competence Assessment of Seafarers Using Eye-Tracking, Speech Analysis, and EEG-Based Stress Evaluation
authors: Kan Hon Wong, Yisi Liu, Vishakha Lall, Jia Da Lim, Chee Onn Tham, Ashwin Madhav Khandke
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyberworlds (CW）
abstract: Seafarer competence in visual attention, communication, and stress management is critical to maritime safety. While assessment methods for these individual areas have evolved with simulators, sensor data, and artificial intelligence, there remains no established approach to systematically integrate these outputs into a practical competence measure to support more efficient and standardised debriefing and to enable reliable comparison between trainees. In this paper, we present a multimodal assessment report that fuses analysed results from eyetracking, audio recordings, and Electroencephalogram (EEG) signals collected during full-mission bridge simulator exercises. The report provides an integrated, instructor-friendly summary of each trainee's performance across key competence pillars, along with an executive summary. This approach offers maritime instructors a more comprehensive and objective tool to identify individual strengths, weaknesses, and development needs.

title: Ray Tracing and Full-Wave Simulation Integrated Channel Simulation Method
authors: Yixiao Li, Fei Wang, Le Zhang
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: The traditional statistical channel model has significant limitations, as it can only handle regular tunnels, fails to consider the details of tunnel walls, and does not account for the impact of antenna deployment environments. On the other hand, the physics-based channel model based on ray tracing still has limitations, such as ignoring the influence of scatterers when the antenna is located in the induction field or near-field region, and not supporting high-precision simulations that consider the shielding effect of the train car. This paper proposes a simulation method that integrates ray tracing with full-wave simulation. For the antenna installation environment and the interior of the train, a full-wave simulation method is employed. For the tunnel environment outside the train, the ray tracing method is used. This approach allows for precise full-process evaluation and enhances the end-to-end transmission performance within the channel. Test results have verified the feasibility and accuracy of this method.

title: Joint Hardware–Waveform Co-Design of Semantic RF Transceivers for 6G Communications
authors: Francois Rivet, Mohamed Amine Hamoura, Sami Ouabrk, Nour Hello, Emilio Calvanese Strinati
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: The transition to sixth-generation (6G) wireless networks represents a paradigm shift from bit-centric transmission toward semantic and goal-oriented communication. Traditional modulation schemes, such as Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (OFDM), are designed to maximize bit-level reliability but overlook the semantic relevance of transmitted information. With the advent of Artificial Intelligence (AI) as native component of future communication systems, this limitation creates inefficiencies for emerging 6G AI assisted applications. This paper introduces a joint hardware–waveform co-design framework for semantic communication (SemCom) transceivers. At its core lies a novel semantic-aware waveform, termed Orthogonal Semantic Sequency Division Multiplexing (OSSDM), which adaptively shapes physical signals carrying semantic messages. OSSDM is designed to maximize communication effectiveness while ensuring spectral compliance with RF signal masks imposed by standards. The experimental validation with an USRP X310 exhibits the associated trade-offs of SemCom compression with resilience to impairments due to quantization and synchronization, and compliance with emission masks. These results highlight OSSDM-enabled semantic RF transceivers as a promising candidate for future 6G networks.

title: LSTM - Greedy Algorithm Integration Framework for Timing Prediction and Multi-Objective Optimisation Research
authors: Zezheng Wei, Hongyu Du
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: In this study, the integration framework of Long Short-Term Memory Network (LSTM) and greedy algorithm is constructed for the problem of time-series data prediction and multi-objective optimisation. Firstly, the historical data and predictive features are analysed by time-series modelling, and the LSTM model is constructed to mine the time-dependent features of the data to achieve fine-grained prediction of the future time period, which provides the basic data support for the subsequent optimisation. Secondly, the greedy algorithm is introduced to dynamically group the time series, and the constraints control the group size and the sum of sequence values, so that the optimisation objective takes into account multi-dimensional indicators and maximises the utilisation rate. Further, capacity adjustment variables are introduced to solve the mixed-integer programming problem by enumeration method to find the optimal solution between processing time and capacity constraints; for the prediction uncertainty, a robust optimisation model is constructed, and the worst-case sequence values are used as the constraints to validate the stability of the model within the perturbation range. The framework significantly improves the accuracy of prediction and the robustness of scheduling strategies through multi-level data processing and model synergy, and demonstrates excellent performance in time-series prediction and multi-factor scheduling problems, providing innovative analysis methods and technical paths for dynamic management scenarios.

title: SHAP-Based Data-Driven Analysis of an Offset-Canceling Sense Amplifier in DRAMs
authors: Hyerin Lee, Geon Kim, Dongyeong Kim, Suyeon Kim, Jewon Park, Shinwook Kim, Ryun Kang, Sowon Kim, Chaehyuk Lim, Hyeona Seo, Jeonghyeon Yun, Juwon Lee, Ujin Choi, Eojin Kim, Minwoo Jeong, Myoungjin Lee
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: This paper presents an optimization method for the Offset-Canceling Sense Amplifier (OCSA) using a Support Vector Regression (SVR) model. The proposed approach quantitatively analyzes the influence of key design parameters through SHAP (Shapley Additive Explanations) and interprets the trade-off relationships among sensing accuracy, sensing speed, and power efficiency. The analysis results show that structural parameters mainly affect sensing sensitivity and speed, while timing parameters have a dominant impact on stability and power efficiency. Through this quantitative and data-driven analysis, the complex performance characteristics of the offset cancelling circuit are clarified, and a design framework is proposed that enables optimal performance balancing during the design stage.

title: Multimodal Clustering-Driven Accurate Recommendation and Optimization System for Smart Health and Elderly Care Service
authors: Fang Guo, Xiaoli Gong, Min Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: With the accelerating aging population, traditional health care services face challenges such as mismatched supply and demand and insufficient personalization. This study proposes a smart health care service system driven by multimodal clustering to achieve precise service matching and dynamic optimization. The system integrates physiological, behavioral, and semantic data through a hierarchical architecture, including modules for multimodal data collection, clustering analysis, precise matching, and feedback optimization. Key technologies involve improved multimodal clustering algorithms (e.g., adaptive weight multimodal clustering, AWMC) that embed geriatric domain knowledge, coupled with a dual-loop feedback mechanism for real-time adjustment of matching strategies. Experimental verification in community and institutional care scenarios demonstrates that the system significantly enhances matching accuracy (92.3% precision vs. 78.5% in the control group) and reduces response time by 52%, compared to traditional rulebased methods. User satisfaction reaches 89.7%, reflecting the effectiveness of personalized service packages generated by multimodal profiling. The research provides a novel framework for integrating data-driven intelligence into health care services, offering practical insights for optimizing resource allocation and improving elderly care experiences.

title: Distribution Network Energy Loss Cost Minimization by Network Reconfiguration
authors: Prashant Singh, Ajay Singh, Subho Paul
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: Minimizing energy losses in distribution systems is a critical objective for enhancing operational efficiency and economic viability. This paper proposes a comprehensive mixed-integer nonlinear programming (MINLP) framework for Distribution System Reconfiguration (DSR), integrating YALMIP as the modeling interface and Gurobi as the optimization solver. By incorporating time-varying load profiles and detailed network constraints, the proposed model accurately captures real-world distribution system behavior. The method is valid on IEEE 33-bus and 136-bus test systems, Displaying meaningful energy loss cost reductions and operational improvements.

title: Transfer Learning for Early Diabetic Retinopathy Detection Using Efficientnet with Explainable AI
authors: Rahul Burkul, Sandhya Waghere
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: This research proposes a novel approach for early diabetic retinopathy detection using transfer learning techniques on EfficientNet combined with explainable AI methods. The novelty lies in optimizing EfficientNet architectures specifically for early-stage DR detection while providing clinically interpretable visualizations through Grad-CAM integration. It employs compound scaling to balance network depth, width, and resolution, achieving superior performance with fewer parameters compared to conventional CNN architectures [13]. Diabetic retinopathy accounts for significant vision loss in diabetic patients and is better managed if detected in the early stages. Our model uses feature extraction on pre-trained EfficientNet architectures which are fine-tuned on retinal image datasets, integrating explainability schemes to provide interpretable outcomes for clinicians. Among the tested architectures (B0-B5), EfficientNet-B4 achieved the best performance, reaching 83.86% testing accuracy, showing stronger generalization ability compared to the smaller variants The explainability components enhance confidence in the automated detection system, facilitating its integration into clinical workflows for early intervention and treatment planning.

title: Deep Learning-Based Multi-Class Road Surface Classification Using CNN and Random Forest
authors: Parveen Sharma, Bhavna Thakur, Gaurav Sharma, Amandeep Kaur, Deepak Banerjee, Gourav Verma
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: In this paper, a road surface classification model based on a deep-learning approach is presented that classifies four types of roads ( Dry, Wet, Snowy, and Muddy roads) accurately. It has a data set of 9,880 images where the model was trained and tested and it has an outstanding performance on the four categories. In the case of Dry Road, it presents Precision (98.46%), Recall (98.65%), and F1 Score (98.55%); Wet Road (98.68%, 98.49%, 98.58%); Snowy Road (98.42%, 98.61, 98.52); and Muddy Road (98.35%, 98.11, 98.23). The support of each class includes 2,585 Dry, 2,650 Wet, 2,525 Snowy, and 2,120 Muddy road pictures that comprise 26, 27, 26 and 21 percent of the entire dataset respectively. The model is robust and consistent in the sense that the model has a uniform accuracy of 99 percent and an overall accuracy of 98.48 percent. Therefore, the findings demonstrate that the model works well in the correct identification and categorization of the type of road surface and can be applied to a big extent in autonomous vehicles navigation, intelligent transportation and automated road surface surveillance.

title: A Low-Cost, SDR-Based Testbed for Multi-Domain Characterization of 5G NR Amplifier Chains
authors: Rodrigo Apaza Huanca, Renan Garcia Escarzo, Juan Carlos Paredes Condori, Esteban Gironda Lagrava, Karel Walter Gomez Orellana, Hugo Orlando Condori Quispe
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: The characterization of 5G New Radio (NR) power amplifiers is critical but traditionally relies on cost-prohibitive equipment. This paper presents a low-cost, reconfigurable testbed using a single Software-Defined Radio (SDR) for both 5G compliant waveform generation and analysis. We introduce a multi-domain methodology integrating frequency (ACPR), time (power envelope), and modulation (EVM) analysis. Validated on an amplifier chain, the testbed captures the EVM “bathtub” curve, determining the linear dynamic range for a 256-QAM 5G NR signal. This work establishes the platform as an accessible tool for practical RF engineering, education, and research.

title: Dual-Transformer-Based Zero-Voltage Switching of Dual Active Bridge Converter
authors: Anuj Kumar, Mayank Kumar, Madhusudan Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: The dual active bridge (DAB) topology is increasingly used as an isolated and bidirectional dc-dc converter topology for medium to high voltage and medium to high power applications. A dual-transformer-based (DT-DAB) with active H-bridge with H6 structure (i.e., three-semiconductor switches in each leg) for the primary side and active H-bridge with H4 structure (i.e., two-semiconductor switches in each leg) for the secondary side is proposed in this article with bidirectional power transfer capability. The proposed dual-transformer-based zero-voltage switching of dual active bridge (DT-ZVSDAB) topology reduces current stress, better power distribution, and improved efficiency. A strategy of double frequency switching for auxiliary switches is presented. The proposed converter operated on different charging state threshold point as compared to conventional DAB. Simulation results with single-phase shift modulation technique are presented for characterization of the proposed converter at a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$200 ~\mathrm{V} / 100 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex> system under the buck mode of operation.

title: Research and Analysis of Intelligent Traffic Flow Prediction Based on Deep Learning and Multi-Source Data Fusion
authors: Junming Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Accuracy prediction of traffic flow plays a vital role in intelligent transportation system, which directly affects the performances of traffic signal control and route planning. In this article, we propose a traffic flow prediction model based on deep learning with multi-source data fusion. It learns to predict short- and medium-term traffic flow by integrating historical traffic flow, weather information, time cycles, and spatial topology features. The proposed model adopts a weighted ensemble approach to exploit the strengths of Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), Transformer, and graph neural network models. The experimentation for the work is validated using the Kaggle and METR-LA datasets. The MAE of the fusion model is decreased by about 12% compared to that of the best single model, and the RMSE is dropped by about 15%. Further analysis of feature importance reveals spatial and weather features contribute the most to the performance improvement. The proposed approach demonstrates superior accuracy and robustness, which can allow engineers to better optimize signals and ease traffic jams in smart transportation.

title: Smart Nutrient Management for Enhancing Sugarcane and Paddy Production
authors: Ms I NITHYA ME, LAKSHANA S, VARUNIKA R
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Fertilizers need to be utilized in the most efficient way possible to ensure that the farming process is more productive and less damaging to the environment. Traditional systems of fertilizer recommendations often focus on human experience and fixed recommendations, which cannot consider changing environmental and soil situations. The proposed system concerning the fertilizer recommendations applied with the use of machine learning is much more efficient in the reduction of fresh fertilizer application and thus more environmentally friendly. Based on a database of 9,999 farming records, comprising eight key parameters: temperature, humidity, moisture, crop type, soil type, nitrogen, potassium, and phosphorus, the presented system selects the most efficient among the seven possible types of fertilizers. The three models constructed and tested by the researchers were the Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM), the Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) and an ensemble of Decision Tree, Random Forest and LightGBM classifiers. The study of feature importance has shown that soil nutrients and climatic conditions were the most important factors in predicting fertilizers with an accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 4. 2 \%}$</tex>. LightGBM has been found to be more effective than XGBoost (89.4) and the ensemble model (84.9) with the greater accuracy. This is because the results indicate that application of boosting strategies may significantly improve making of decisions regarding the use of fertilizers using sustainable management. The developed system, by offering a scalable, intelligent, and easy-to-use framework, which can be incorporated with the real agricultural systems, allows the farmers the power to apply the fertilizers accurately.

title: Enhanced Age and Gender Classification in Dental Radiographs Through a Synergistic Deep Learning Framework Integrating Convolutional and Recurrent Neural Networks
authors: V. S. Chithra, K. S. Charishma, A. K. Hemanth, P Tejas
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: Forensic dentistry plays an important role in human identification, especially anthropology. Audit of dental features is time-consuming, subjective, and prone to bias. To overcome this, we would like to propose a hybrid deep learning framework that is novel in that it synergizes ResNet50, MobileNetV2, and VGG16 with Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) layers and improves the spatial feature extraction analysis of dental structures. Our method differs from previous studies that either used conventional CNNbased feature extraction or classifiers, as we benefit from GRUs in our work and exploit brightness transitions in the images over time for improving classification accuracy. Yet we have made efforts in improving model interpretability through explainable AI (X-AI) techniques, covering the aspects of reliability and transparency in predictions, which are often neglected by forensic AI models. The proposed model had been trained on a dataset divided into two gender classes and four age groups, yielding results of 90% and 92.5% accuracy in gender and age classification against existing methodologies, respectively. By increasing precision in the automation of forensic identification, this research not only opens new horizons in forensic dentistry but also paves the way for a scalable, interpretable, and efficient AI solution for post-disaster victim identification and anthropological studies.

title: Implementation of YOLO-Based Deep Learning Models in the Development of Autonomous Traffic Accident Identification and Communication System
authors: Janice C. Sombillo, Jonardo R. Asor
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cognitive and Intelligent Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The main objective is to develop an accident detection system that integrates seamlessly with the existing network of closed-circuit television (CCTV) to automatically identify and notify authorities of traffic incidents as they occur. This approach leverages the current infrastructure of the Municipality of Los Banos, making the system both practical and cost-efficient for local government implementation. The dataset used for model training is sourced from RoboFlow, with additional non-accident data obtained from municipal CCTV footage. The system employs YOLO11, to detect traffic accidents in real-time. The model was trained using a curated dataset of annotated images covering a variety of traffic conditions and accident scenarios. Data augmentation techniques enhanced the model's adaptability to lighting and environmental factors. To ensure smooth streaming and timely detection, system-level optimizations were implemented to reduce latency, frame drops, and CPU load. A 20% portion of the dataset was reserved for testing, where the model achieved a precision, recall and Fl-score of 94.0%, indicating high accuracy and reliability.

title: Safe Vision: AI Surveillance and Alert System using YOLOv8 for Robbery Prevention
authors: Satheesh Kumar M, Pula Akash, Guntupalli Balashowreelu, Rajendran Thanikachalam, R. Saranya, Shahul Hameed C
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Robbery and gun related violence create serious public safety issues that need quick detection and action. Traditional CCTV systems are common, but they depend a lot on human operators. This reliance can cause problems like fatigue, distraction, and delays in response. To address these issues, this study introduces a smart, real time surveillance system that uses the YOLOv8 deep learning architecture. Unlike older methods that focus only on static object detection, this new approach combines object identification specifically for firearms, knives, and facial disguises with behavior assessment to identify high risk situations accurately. The model is trained on a well-organized, unified dataset, which helps it work well under different conditions, such as changing light, severe obstruction, and varying crowd sizes. When the system detects a possible threat, it sends an automatic alert to security personnel or law enforcement right away to reduce response time. Tested for use in crucial places like banks, ATMs, and retail stores, this solution changes surveillance from merely collecting evidence to actively enhancing public safety. By greatly cutting the time between detection and response, this framework provides a practical technological solution for preventing and reducing violent crime in cities.

title: Optimizing DistilBERT for Low-Resource Sentiment Analysis: A Study on Global Unstructured L1-Norm Pruning and Accuracy-Efficiency Trade-Offs
authors: W.M.N.W.B. Manike, R.A. Wijesinghe, A.M.T.N Adasuriya
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Transformer models are not always deployed to low-resource devices due to the computational intensity of the model. Although some distilled models, such as DistilBERT, provide a minimum level of efficiency, additional compression is required to apply to any edge application. This paper offers a systematic evaluation of L1-norm unstructured pruning globally on a DistilBERT model that has been fine-tuned on binary sentiment analysis on the Stanford Sentiment Treebank (SST-2) dataset. The approach consists of pruning the model sequentially by 10 % to 60 % to check its performance using the accuracy of a stratified validation set and inference latency on a CPU. These findings indicate that model accuracy is very robust, and it still has more than 99% of the baseline performance until 40% sparsity. Only at this stage major degradation has taken place, and the accuracy retention was found to be 97.25 % and 94.22% at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 \%}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 0 \%}$</tex> sparsity, respectively. An important conclusion here is that this unstructured sparsity fails to bring inference speedups in conventional CPU hardware, highlighting a lack of a connection between theory and practical efficiency improvements. As a result, the paper has found the best operational point to be <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 \%}$</tex> sparsity, which is an evidence-based guide to developers. The research also concludes that L1-norm unstructured pruning is a very effective method of maximizing model compression without significant accuracy impairment and thus can be used to achieve efficient deployment of NLP models in resource-constrained settings.

title: DeepVerify: Advanced AI Framework for Deepfake Detection and Media Authenticity Validation
authors: SV Shivani, Surabi C S, J. Merlin Mary Jenitha, L.Mary Gladence
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Deepfakes are becoming more realistic in nature. As a result, the authenticity associated with digital media is being questioned. In fact, most current detection systems are based on single-modal or loosely-coupled multi-modal analysis models. As a result, the generalization capability is poor in realistic scenarios. Moreover, there is also a lack of interpretability in the role of different modality components. In this paper, an end-to-end fully automated deepfake detection method is proposed. It is based on the fusion of deep learning-based face analysis and audio verification systems. The analysis of the video frames is conducted using TensorFlow-based CNN models. The models are designed to detect the visual manipulations in the form of texture artifacts, peculiar eye blinking signals, and inconsistencies in the face structure. The audio signals are analyzed using Librosa- based feature extraction models that consist of RNN- based models. Experimental analysis carried out using public deepfake databases confirms that the multimodal framework outperforms both audio and video-based models in accuracy and robustness. Also, with regard to the practical applicability and challenges faced while deploying such models, the proposed architecture considers delays and scalability. The system is built on the Django web framework, which is a communication platform for providing online access (via an online forms) and issuing instantaneous forensic analysis to verify the effectiveness of multimodal deep learning to assist in preventing the proliferation of deepfake technology.

title: A Review of Proximal Policy Optimization for Uplink Multi-User SIMO-RSMA Systems
authors: Huy Dang Mac, Kiet Nguyen Tuan Tran, Dongwook Won, Sungrae Cho
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Rate-Splitting Multiple Access (RSMA) has emerged as a powerful multiple access technique for interference management in next-generation wireless networks. In uplink multi-user single-input multiple-output (SIMO) systems, RSMA enables a base station to decode multi-user transmissions more flexibly by splitting messages and partially decoding interference. Optimizing such RSMA systems is challenging due to the complex resource allocation and decoding order decisions required, especially under dynamic channel conditions. Recently, deep reinforcement learning (DRL) approaches, particularly Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO), have been applied to efficiently learn near-optimal policies for uplink RSMA resource allocation. This paper provides a comprehensive review of PPO-based techniques for uplink multi-user SIMO-RSMA systems. We outline the fundamentals of SIMO-RSMA, describe the PPO approach for solving the sum-rate and fairness optimization problems in these systems, and survey state-of-the-art PPO-based solutions from 2022 to 2025. A comparative analysis of recent studies is presented, and open challenges as well as future research directions are discussed.

title: Federated Learning: State-of-the-art, Challenges and Research Opportunities
authors: Ton That Tam Dinh, Manh Cuong Ho, Ayalneh Bitew Wondmagegn, Juyoung Kim, Sungrae Cho
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) is an emerging paradigm in machine learning that enables collaborative model training without centralizing data, thereby addressing privacy and security concerns. Unlike traditional centralized approaches, FL keeps data on local devices and only exchanges model updates, making it highly relevant in the era of strict data governance regulations. This survey provides a comprehensive overview of FL, covering its foundational concepts, related works, and major categories including horizontal, vertical, and transfer learning. We highlight practical applications across healthcare, finance, mobile devices, IoT, autonomous systems, and wireless communications. Furthermore, we examine the challenges faced by FL, such as data heterogeneity, system scalability, communication efficiency, and security vulnerabilities. We also explore solutions proposed in recent literature, ranging from differential privacy and secure aggregation to personalization and hierarchical architectures. The survey discusses future research directions that integrate FL with edge computing, blockchain, and next-generation wireless networks. By synthesizing these insights, we emphasize the transformative potential of FL in shaping decentralized, privacy-preserving artificial intelligence.

title: FindMe.AI: A Multi-Modal Framework for Real-Time Missing Person Detection Using YOLOv5 and BERT
authors: P S Neha, Athira K Jagadeesh, Nandhana Lalu, Tn NavaneethKrishna, Abhinav Gosh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Computing and Applications (AICAPS)
abstract: FindMe.AI is a novel, real-time AI system for missing person identification. Our framework offers a truly end-to-end multi-modal fusion pipeline, in contrast to previous works that treat text and vision as distinct modalities. Three essential modules are synergistically integrated into FindMe.AI: (3) a predictive movement model that uses spatiotemporal data to predict likely trajectories; (2) BERT-based natural language processing to extract geo-temporal clues from unstructured text; and (3) YOLOv5-based real-time object detection for facial and vehicle recognition from video streams. These modules contribute to a single dashboard that gives law enforcement access to realtime, actionable intelligence. We demonstrate that our system achieves a 92% precision rate in facial recognition and an 89% F1-score for location extraction, outperforming traditional investigative methods with a 68% reduction in average search time during field deployments. Our system showcases a significant real-world benefit for public safety by transitioning investigations from a reactive to a proactive, data-driven paradigm while adhering to strict privacy and ethical guidelines.

title: Current Status and Prospects of Noise Reduction Materials for Power Transformers
authors: Fengju Shang, Shixiang Liu, Jiaqing Zhang, Yi Guo, Fei Tang
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: The development of ultra-high voltage transmission is an important measure to optimize the allocation of energy resources, ensure national energy security and realize the national dual carbon strategic goal. The use of noise reduction materials in substations (converter stations) is of great significance for meeting the noise standards of substations and achieving the goals of the dual-carbon strategy. This article analyzes the principles and classifications of sound-absorbing and sound-insulating materials used in power transformers in substations, and elaborates on the current development status, advantages and disadvantages of commonly used noise reduction materials. By combining the operating environment characteristics of substations, it is pointed out that the noise reduction materials for power transformers should have wide-frequency sound absorption (insulation) characteristics, excellent weather resistance, reliable mechanical properties, and ease of construction. At the same time, it is pointed out that to further improve the noise reduction performance, weather resistance, mechanical performance and environmental protection performance of noise reduction materials for power transformers, research on the preparation of noise reduction materials using natural sand particles or solid waste particles treated for environmental protection should be strengthened. These materials possess the functions of noise reduction under normal operating conditions and high-temperature melting in fire conditions, and also have the characteristics of low price, easy processing, environmental protection and dust resistance. They are ideal materials for noise reduction treatment in substations (converter stations), but their resistance to rain exposure and aging under high and low temperatures still need to be further verified through engineering practice.

title: A Review of Multi-Resource Frequency Regulation Control Strategies in New Power Systems
authors: Cong Wang, Wenying Wu, Wei Cui, Wei Wang, Chongtao Li, Chao Duan
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: With the rapid growth of renewable energy, new power systems are experiencing an increasing share of wind and solar generation. This shift reduces the system’s overall inertia and introduces greater variability, posing significant challenges for maintaining frequency stability. Traditional synchronous generators offer strong but sluggish frequency support, while renewables and energy storage systems respond more rapidly but often with constrained regulation capacity. These differences can lead to serious frequency fluctuations, especially under high renewable penetration. To address these challenges, this paper comprehensively reviews the frequency response characteristics of multiple energy resources, including thermal, hydro, wind, photovoltaic, load, and energy storage units. Moreover, the paper explores key control strategies such as deadband tuning and coordinated regulation, and discusses optimization approaches for system-level frequency support. The objective is to propose effective technical paths and parameter settings that support stable operation of power systems with high renewable integration. The findings aim to guide the development of frequency regulation frameworks for future renew-able-dominated power systems.

title: Deep Neural Networks-Powered Medical Image Classification Of X-Rays
authors: Kannaram Meghana, Vajrapu Sai Anurag, Parinitha Chilakala, Dr.M Purushotham Reddy
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Medical imaging is essential to healthcare because it makes internal body structures visible without causing harm, which aids in diagnosis and treatment. Because chest X-rays are affordable, easily accessible, and rapidly provide crucial information for identifying respiratory illnesses like COVID-19, pneumonia, and tuberculosis, they are frequently used among imaging techniques. But it’s difficult to interpret chest X-rays correctly; it takes specific knowledge. Human error and delays in diagnosis can result from even slight image variations. This study uses the Vision Transformer (ViT) architecture to propose an automated chest X-ray classification system in order to address this problem. In contrast to conventional convolutional neural networks (CNNs), which primarily concentrate on local image features, To understand global relationships across the image, ViTs segment images into patches and use self-attention mechanisms. Better pattern recognition is made possible by this. To increase model robustness, the approach involves preprocessing a labeled chest X-ray dataset and applying data augmentation techniques. The system seeks to improve diagnostic accuracy, decrease reliance on manual interpretation, minimize human error, and expedite decision-making by leveraging the ViT model. In turn, this enables medical professionals to treat patients in clinical settings more quickly and consistently.

title: Conditional Implicit Neural Representations via Cross-Attention for Multivariate Time-Series Imputation
authors: Eunho Shin, Janghun Hyeon, Yunho Jeon
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Existing Transformer-based approaches for time-series imputation typically rely on full-sequence reconstruction, which leads to computational inefficiency. To address this, we propose Conditional Implicit Neural Representation via Cross-Attention (CINR-CA), a novel framework designed for high-efficiency imputation. We formulate this task as a Conditional Implicit Neural Representation (INR) problem. By adopting a coordinate-based query mechanism, our model selectively computes outputs only for missing timestamps. This strategy significantly reduces inference costs by avoiding unnecessary computations on observed data. Experiments on the ETTh1 dataset demonstrate that CINR-CA reduces computational complexity (MFLOPs) by approximately 43 % (Large settings) compared to the state-of-the-art SAITS model, offering a practical solution for resource-constrained environments where efficiency is important.

title: Ceylonmate - Smart Tourism Companion for Foreign Travelers in Sri Lanka
authors: Thathsarani K K T, Pathiraja J.W.P. D, Moragaspitiya M.R.K. A, DE Soysa J C, Buddika Harshanath, N.C.P. Kumarapperuma
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: We present an AI Travel Agent by an ecosystem which is more than a set of static, digital or physical catalogues. It consists of four innovative modules: context-aware dynamic itinerary planning, intelligent safety management, community cultural experience marketplace, and spatio-semantic AR discovery. The planner will use Hybrid Filtering and MultiObjective Genetic Algorithms to optimize the itineraries according to current weather and user fatigue. It is secured using a Spatio-Temporal Risk Analysis Engine and offline-first kinematic anomaly detection. The platform connects tourists with villagers through a 360° VR “Virtual Experience Viewer” and an AI “Moments” social content engine to support rural empowerment. Lastly, the system integrates with a Visual Positioning System (VPS) for markerless AR reconstruction by providing significant implications in terms of personalization, rural safety and historical engagement.

title: Design of a Multimodal Fusion Architecture for Anomaly Detection in Smart Greenhouse Environments
authors: JooWon Jeong, KiWoong Song, Hyun Yoe
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: This paper presents a multimodal fusion architecture for anomaly detection in smart greenhouse environments. The framework integrates image streams and environmental sensor time-series to address the limitations of single-modality systems. The design includes an image encoder, sensor encoder, and fusion layer, enabling scalable and real-time operation. A simulation-based evaluation, derived from structural module properties, suggests improved detection sensitivity and reduced false-alarm rates. Although theoretical, the findings indicate the potential of the proposed architecture as a foundation for future implementation and validation in next-generation smart agriculture monitoring systems.

title: CNN-BiLSTM Based Channel Estimation in RIS-NOMA System for 5G and Beyond
authors: Aman Kanwar, Aryan Mittal, Akash, Sandeep Kumar Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) is one of the key enabler along with Non-Orthogonal Multiple Access (NOMA) for beyond 5G (B5G) and 6G networks, offering enhanced spectral efficiency and massive connectivity. The estimation of RIS-NOMA channels is particularly challenging due to the cascaded nature of the BS-RIS-user transmission path combined with non-linear distortion introduced by NOMA-based signal superposition. In this study, a composite deep learning approach is designed that leverages convolutional neural processing for spatial feature learning and bidirectional recurrent memory structures to effectively characterize temporal correlations in the observed signals. A synthetic RIS-NOMA dataset is generated to capture user mobility, power imbalance, and varying RIS configurations. The model predicts the amplitude and phase of cascaded channels and is evaluated under multiple SNR levels using NRMSE and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> score. Simulation results show that the proposed CNN-BiLSTM model outperforms a baseline CNNLSTM, particularly in low-SNR and asymmetric power scenarios, demonstrating its potential for efficient channel estimation in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 G}$</tex> RIS-NOMA networks.

title: Bell Slotted Circular Patch Antenna for 5G and Beyond
authors: Anslin Star R, Anitha G
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: A new circular microstrip patch antenna featuring a unique bell-shaped slot specifically for millimeter wave usage. The antenna is fabricated on a Rogers RT/duroid 5870 substrate with circular patch radius of 0.933 mm. The design resonates at 59.75 GHz that achievs excellent impedance matching. A return loss of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-\mathbf{6 0. 5 ~ d B}$</tex> and a peak gain of 7.66 dBi is achieved when the bell-shaped slot is incorporated to the patch. A 1.4 GHz bandwidth is obtained which provides the reliable operation across the target spectrum. The bell slot geometry allows the antenna to remain compact without sacrificing performance making it an ideal fit for the next generation of 5 G and beyond wireless communication technologies.

title: Real-Time Traffic Sign Detection and Recognition for Smart Vehicles
authors: Zahraa Shihab Al Hakeem, Hiba J. Aleqabie
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: This paper proposes a real-time traffic sign detection and recognition framework tailored for smart vehicles in dynamic road environments. The system integrates convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for deep feature extraction with support vector machines (SVMs) for reliable classification. A robust preprocessing pipeline—comprising normalization, noise filtering, and color space transformation—ensures effective operation under variable lighting and weather conditions. Evaluated on a hybrid dataset of $\mathbf{1 8, 8 2 6}$ annotated traffic signs and real-time video frames, the system achieved an accuracy of $99.06 \%$, with a precision of $98.94 \%$ and a recall of $98.81 \%$. The false alarm rate remained low at $1.3 \%$, validating its practical reliability. Comparative assessments against state-of-the-art models such as YOLOv4-SPP and MobileViT demonstrate that the proposed system offers superior accuracy while maintaining real-time performance on modest hardware. Minor degradation was noted under conditions such as occlusion and low-light scenarios. Future enhancements will explore multi-frame tracking, sensor fusion (e.g., LiDAR and infrared), and lightweight model optimization through pruning and quantization. Overall, the proposed system provides an efficient, scalable, and high-performance solution for real-world deployment in intelligent transportation systems and autonomous driving platforms.

title: A Decentralized Cloud Computing Enhancement Framework Using Blockchain for Resource Allocation
authors: Ganji Ramanjaiah, Balajee Maram, Tummala Srinivasa Ravi Kiran, Komanduri Venkata Sesha Sai Ramakrishna
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
