title: Semantic fusion of dual perspectives on genomic sequences and quorum sensing for bacteriophage lifestyle prediction
authors: Zhen Xiao, Ankang Wei, Yi Jia, Weizhong Zhao, Xingpeng Jiang
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: As the most ubiquitous and abundant viral community, bacteriophages (phages for short) play a vital role in regulating the ecological balance by infecting bacteria and archaea. Phages can be classified into two types based on their lifestyles: virulent phages and temperate phages, which are closely related to their functional characteristics and influence their interaction patterns with hosts. Therefore, identifying phage lifestyle is critical for understanding the mechanisms by which phages infect bacteria and represents a key step in mastering their functions and potential applications. In this paper, we propose a novel method for phage lifestyle identification by considering two perspectives. One perspective is based on the genomic sequences of phages, in which both local and global semantic features are integrated. The other perspective focuses on the host quorum sensing phenomena that influence phage lysogen-lysis decisions. Specifically, we first capture local sequence variation patterns by extracting the relative positional information of nucleotide fragments at different intervals, which enables robust representation of local genomic semantics. Secondly, the pretrained nucleotide language model DNABERT is applied to capture the semantics of genome sequences by considering the global contextual information. Finally, combined with quorum sensing signals from the bacterial host, a final fusion representation is obtained, which is fed into a predictive model to identify the phage's lifestyle. Experimental results show that our method has excellent and stable performance in both phage complete genome and short contigs from metagenomic data. We also investigate early-life viral colonization in the human gut metagenome, further validating the model's generalizability and real-world applicability.

title: Federated Learning Framework for Privacy-Preserving Explainable AI-Driven Clinical Decision-Making
authors: Emad-ul-Haq Qazi, Waleed Khalid AL-Ghanem, Muhammad Hamza Faheem, Hanif Ullah
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: The application of artificial intelligence (AI) in clinical diagnostics has shown substantial potential; however, conventional centralized learning frameworks often encounter critical limitations related to patient data privacy, data heterogeneity, and limited generalizability. To address these challenges, we propose a novel Federated Deep Learning (FDL) framework tailored for privacy-preserving, AI-driven clinical decision support. The proposed architecture integrates Vision Transformers (ViT) with DINOv2-based self-supervised learning to enable effective representation learning in the absence of extensive labeled datasets. Furthermore, personalized model updates are facilitated using Federated Self-Supervised Learning (FedSSL) in conjunction with FedProx, ensuring client-specific adaptation in non-identically distributed (non-IID) data environments. Privacy preservation is ensured through the application of differential privacy mechanisms at the model update level, coupled with Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC) for secure communication. To enhance clinical transparency and interpretability, the framework incorporates Grad-CAM and LIME for sample-level explainability. The proposed system is evaluated on three publicly available medical imaging datasets encompassing Tuberculosis (TB) detection from chest X-rays, Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) from fundus images, and Brain Tumor (BT) classification from MRI scans. The federated model achieved an accuracy and F1-score of 99.80% for Tuberculosis, 89.0% for Diabetic Retinopathy (DR), and 97.1% for Brain Tumors (BTs), reflecting high overall diagnostic performance across all tasks. These findings validate the efficacy, scalability, and privacy-resilience of the proposed method, positioning it as a robust candidate for real-world clinical deployment in distributed healthcare environments.

title: Characterization of FDSOI Technology for Energy-Efficient Cryo-CMOS Circuits
authors: Arpit Rao, David R. Daughton, Benjamin Chacko, Jason Chonko, Yukang Kuang, Sarah Guenther, Subhanshu Gupta
publish: IEEE Journal of the Electron Devices Society
abstract: High-performance cryo-CMOS circuits are the foundation for quantum computing hardware. The energy efficiency of cryo-CMOS circuits is limited by the changing operating point of the device as the temperature drops. Operating point calibration becomes imperative to achieve energy-efficient designs, yet accurate threshold-voltage calibration at deep-cryogenic temperatures proves challenging. At cryogenic temperatures, conventional threshold-voltage extraction methods fail as the device operating point exhibits a nonlinear shift with temperature, rendering standard threshold voltage extraction methods unreliable, particularly in the low-VGS regime where cryo-CMOS circuits are typically biased for energy efficiency. This work characterizes flip-well 22 nm FDSOI technology and demonstrates a robust methodology for operating point calibration using back-gate control across a variety of VDS. Established extraction methods, including constant current, maximum transconductance, second-derivative, third-derivative, linear extraction, and transconductance-to-current ratio, are performed to assess their applicability under cryogenic conditions. In doing so, we demonstrate that back-gate tuning provides an effective and scalable mechanism for compensating operating-point shifts without emphasis on the absolute value of the threshold voltage. Calibrated threshold voltage results across temperature are presented for a 22 nm FDSOI super-low-VT (SLVT) transistor using body bias sweeps from 0 to +2 V. To enable circuit-level calibration in the low-VGS regime, this work identifies the body-coefficient η as a relevant metric for biasing, capturing the effective sensitivity of gate threshold voltage to back-gate bias. The extracted η exhibits nonlinearity with respect to both temperature and bias conditions, providing a physically meaningful and design-relevant parameter for energy-efficient cryo-CMOS calibration.

title: Novel Switching Frequency Injection Sensorless Control for SPMSM in Finite-Control-Set Deadbeat Predictive Current Control
authors: Yonglin Cui, Siqi Wang, Shanmei Cheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: When switching frequency injection (SFI) is implemented in finite-control-set (FCS) model predictive control (MPC) frame, position error signal will deteriorate due to inaccurate voltage injection. This paper establishes novel switching frequency injection sensorless control with proposed FCS deadbeat predictive current control (DPCC) based on novel angular domain iterative optimization method with extended control set (ECS). The inherent limitations of the FCS method inevitably introduce injection errors, which degrade current control performance. Compensating for these errors requires significantly extended execution time to achieve acceptable results. To address these issues, this paper further proposes a novel injection-time based SFI method, realizing precise injection with greatly reduced execution time. With these contributions, the proposed SFI sensorless control strategy based on d-axis current offset is successfully implemented on a target surface-mounted PMSM (SPMSM). Moreover, a simple but effective initial position detection method is proposed. And speed oscillation induced by current offset is studied. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed methods and validate the theoretical analysis.

title: A Three-layer AI-driven Image Filtering for Efficient LEO Satellite Remote Sensing
authors: Nourhen Sboui, Hakim Ghazzai, Sameh Najeh, Lokman Sboui
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Low-earth orbit (LEO) satellites are considered essential tools for remote sensing (RS) and Earth observation (EO) applications thanks to their high spatial resolution images.However, limited onboard resources (storage, downlink throughput, and bandwidth) make it challenging to manage the large volume of data collected. Real-time data management is needed to avoid wasting on-board compute and downlink budget, especially when dealing with a significant influx of anomalous data images resulting from factors such as redundancy, seasonal changes, sensor properties, and satellite orbit dynamics. This paper introduces a novel three-layer filtering solution designed to optimize satellite image transmission for EO. Incorporating a fusion of machine learning and deep learning methodologies, our data management solution mitigates critical challenges associated with LEO satellite resource constraints. The proposed solution includes a no-reference deep image quality assessment (NoR-DIQA) model using a convolutional neural network architecture to identify and filter out distorted images. Then, it employs a perceptual hashing redundancy detection approach to eliminate duplicated images, and finally, it employs a classification model that categorizes suitable RS and EO applications for each image set before transmission. This framework effectively maximizes data utility and optimizes resource allocation for RS and EO satellites. The proposed framework was tested on different RS and EO datasets, where each layer was benchmarked against established models and consistently achieved superior results (e.g., higher correlation and lower error in image quality assessment, improved redundancy detection, and classification accuracy up to 99.4%), demonstrating the robustness and reliability of the three-layer filtering solution.

title: Purpose-Driven Data Sanitization for Secure AI/ML Applications in Next Generation Mobile Networks
authors: Chaitanya Aggarwal, Divya G. Nair, Huanzhuo Wu, Christian Mannweiler, Emrah Tomur, Jörg Ott
publish: IEEE Communications Standards Magazine
abstract: The integration of Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AI/ML) into 5G and forthcoming 6G networks critically relies upon User Equipment (UE) collected data. However, this dependency creates a vulnerability to data poisoning, as current defenses on UEs lack contextual understanding of the collected data’s intended use by data consumers. This paper proposes SANITGUARD-UE, a novel data consumer purpose-driven solution designed to prevent the ingress of poisoned data. It seamlessly integrates with the 3rd Generation Partnership Project (3GPP) architecture by enabling UE sanitization capability signaling, Data Collection Configuration Function (DCCF)-configured purpose-driven data sanitization and an enhanced L2 header. Leveraging the use case of cell load estimation, empirical experiments demonstrated that SANITGUARD-UE significantly improves AI/ML model accuracy, outperforming purpose-agnostic sanitization by a factor of 3.73 to 7.66. This research establishes the essential role for purpose-driven data sanitization to build the trustworthiness and to optimize the performance of AI/ML services in next-generation mobile networks.

title: Data-Driven Motion Planning for Uncertain Nonlinear Systems
authors: Babak Esmaeili, Hamidreza Modares, Stefano Di Cairano
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: This article presents a data-driven motion-planning framework for nonlinear systems based on constructing a sequence of overlapping invariant polytopes. The planner randomly generates waypoints, and around each sampled waypoint, it learns a locally invariant polytopic safe set together with a corresponding local state-feedback gain. Each invariant polytope is obtained as the convex hull of a collection of invariant ellipsoids induced by a piecewise-affine controller. Safe and smooth transitions between waypoints are ensured by introducing an intermediate waypoint. Control gains are interpolated in real time via simplex-based interpolation, keeping the state inside the invariant polytopes throughout the motion. Unlike traditional approaches that rely on system dynamics models, our method requires only data to compute safe regions and design state-feedback controllers. The approach is validated through simulations by comparing against a containment-based invariant-ellipsoid baseline and reporting planning time, sampling effort, and trajectory quality, demonstrating safe and dynamically feasible navigation in complex environments.

title: AI-Driven Feature-Enhanced Stacking Ensemble with Global-Context Vision Transformers for Breast Cancer Classification in Ultrasound Images
authors: Nghia Trong Vo, Hoang Phi Yen Duong, Tuan Thanh Nguyen, Nhan Duc Le, Trung Q. Duong
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Breast cancer remains a leading cause of death among women worldwide. Early detection of breast cancer is a crucial step towards improving survival rates for patients affected by the disease and is typically performed with the help of ultrasound imaging. Current rapid advancements in artificial intelligence (AI) research have produced a plethora of machine learning methods that aid in building automated diagnostic assistance systems for early cancer detection, including breast cancer detection. While deep learning has shown promise in medical image analysis, most existing approaches rely on single models or simple ensemble methods that fail to fully exploit complementary feature representations across architectures. This paper introduces a novel feature-enhanced stacking ensemble framework that combines state-of-the-art global context vision transformer (GCViT) with well-established convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures (ResNet-50V2, ConvNeXt-Tiny, and EfficientNetV2-B3) for automated breast cancer classification from ultrasound images. Unlike conventional ensembles that aggregate only prediction probabilities, our approach extracts deep feature embeddings from a dedicated CNN branch and concatenates them with base model predictions as input to a meta-learner, a multi-layer perceptron (MLP), enabling the ensemble to leverage both decision-level and feature-level information. When incorporating a meta model with feature representations from a CNN-based feature extractor, we are able to produce superior performance across multiple metrics compared to prior works. We accomplish top performance of 94.23% accuracy, 95.47% AUC-ROC. To further evaluate the robustness and generalizability of our approach, we conduct additional experiments on the melanoma cancer image dataset and achieve 95.4% accuracy. We provide comprehensive explainability analysis through shapley additive explanations (SHAP) values for feature attribution, permutation importance for model contribution quantification, and saliency maps for visual interpretation from base models and the end-to-end ensemble model to explain their contributions to final predictions.

title: Multifocal metalens for photodetector arrays based on material library fitting
authors: Junjing Huang, Kai Wang
publish: IEEE Photonics Technology Letters
abstract: In this paper we proposed a metalens design scheme based on material library fitting, which utilizes mathematical models, functional relationships, and a small amount of database resources to quickly predict the properties of the materials without the need to scan each sample one by one for simulation. The calculation time is about 3/13 of the traditional method. This greatly reduces the simulation time and improves the efficiency. In addition, we used this method to design a high performance quadfocal metalens with a focusing efficiency of 64% at 1310nm, an average transmittance of 70% and a maximum transmittance of 82% at 1000nm-2000nm. This is a significant advantage over the work of other researchers. The proposed work will significantly improve the design efficiency of metalens, thereby facilitating the advancement of the technology.

title: Quantifying Computational Reliability of Task Assignment in Extreme Edge Computing
authors: MHD Saria Allahham, Hossam S. Hassanein
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: This paper presents a reliability analysis framework for distributed computing in extreme edge computing (XEC) with limited information availability. XEC pushes computation to the outermost boundaries of networks by leveraging consumer-owned devices, known as Extreme Edge Devices (XEDs). Unlike traditional distributed systems with defined computational resource states, XEC operates under uncertainty due to consumer device usage patterns, varying computational capacities, and local scheduling algorithms. In this work, we address discrete task assignment particularly. The framework analyzes scenarios for computational reliability assessments with minimal knowledge of XED capabilities and service requirements. The framework adapts to different levels of available information, from operational limits to historical performance data, providing refined reliability estimates. The aim of this work is to provide generalized reliability models for distributed computing in XEC that allow decision-makers (e.g. service orchestrators) to make informed decisions about task allocation, service placement, and resource allocation under uncertainty in XEC. Simulations and experimental analysis demonstrate the framework's effectiveness in estimating reliability under various system conditions.

title: PointRL: Reinforcement Learning-Based Approach for Air-Ground Communications Using Multi-Dimensional Target Sensing Point Cloud
authors: Leyan Chen, Kai Liu, Peng Yang, Zehui Xiong, Tony Q. S. Quek, Jisi Fang, Zhibo Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are increasingly used in smart city communications for air-ground communications due to their flexibility, low cost, and independence from ground conditions, enabling high data rates for future networks. This paper explores UAV-to-vehicle (U2V) mmWave integrated sensing and communication (ISAC), where vehicles are represented as rigid shapes in a 3D radar point cloud. Considering maximizing channel capacity with multi-user interference and radar performance, two adaptive optimization problems are proposed, incorporating vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V) communication for interference mitigation. The radar point cloud-driven reinforcement learning (PointRL) algorithm is designed to solve these problems. It includes a point cloud-based deep neural network (PDNN) for extracting action spaces from 3D radar data and a decision network that reduces network complexity through segmentation and connection. A linear weighted sliding window reward mechanism is also designed to enhance decision-making in dynamic environments. Simulation results show that the proposed PointRL outperforms benchmark methods.

title: High-Resolution Spatial Downscaling of Multi-Frequency Brightness Temperature in Global Mare Basalts on the Moon
authors: Shuangshuang Wu, Jianping Chen
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Research on lunar maria offers key constraints on the Moon’s endogenic evolution, yet thermal characterization of mare basalts remains limited by the low spatial resolution of Chang’e-2 microwave radiometer (MRM) data. To address this issue, we develop a SHAP-assisted downscaling framework for lunar difference brightness temperature (dTB). Using SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP), we quantify the contributions of candidate kernel-driven factors and identify FeO, TiO₂, and the digital elevation model (DEM) as the most influential kernels. Two regression models (Ordinary Least Squares, OLS; Geographically Weighted Regression, GWR) combined with two kernel sets (full and SHAP-selected) are compared to evaluate the effects of regression strategy and feature configuration. The SHAP-screened GWR model (GWR-3) delivers the best performance, attaining R² values of 0.954, 0.956, 0.951, and 0.950 at 3.0, 7.8, 19.35, and 37.0 GHz, respectively. The resulting ~2 km dTB products represent the highest-resolution multi-frequency dTB datasets to date. This SHAP-assisted framework offers an effective tool for enhancing lunar thermal analyses and supports refined investigations into the thermal heterogeneity and geological evolution of mare basalts.

title: Period-Two Dynamics of an Optically Injected Semiconductor Laser for Range-Sensitive Resonance Detection
authors: Wen-Hao Jiang, Sze-Chun Chan
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: Period-doubling nonlinear dynamics of a laser is experimentally found to be highly sensitive for ranging the distance of an optoelectronic feedback loop. The semiconductor laser is first optically injected for period-one (P1) dynamics at a fundamental frequency <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$f_{0}$</tex-math></inline-formula>. The optoelectronic feedback then induces period-doubling bifurcation in yielding period-two (P2) dynamics at the subharmonic frequency <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$f_{0}/2$</tex-math></inline-formula>. By adopting current modulation, the periodic dynamics adiabatically exhibits frequency-modulated continuous-wave (FMCW) behavior in general, but a resonance is unveiled when the modulation frequency <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$f_{\mathrm{{m}}}$</tex-math></inline-formula> matches the reciprocal of the feedback delay <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\tau$</tex-math></inline-formula>. Such a resonance is manifested through broadening the FMCW comb around the fundamental and concurrently suppressing the P2 dynamics at the subharmonic. It can be detected by simply monitoring the subharmonic power without frequency mixing. It allows for measuring <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\tau$</tex-math></inline-formula> to determine the effective absolute distance of the feedback loop with a microwave path included. It is also highly sensitive to fine changes in distance without being limited by the FMCW microwave coherence length. For the laser operating at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$f_{0} = 15$</tex-math></inline-formula> GHz with feedback of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1/\tau = 17$</tex-math></inline-formula> MHz, the resonance is sensitive to fractional changes of the order of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{-4}$</tex-math></inline-formula> in <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\tau$</tex-math></inline-formula>. Ranging at 8.8 m is demonstrated with the capability to resolve down to 1 mm, where the required bandwidth is no more than 3 GHz. The novel form of resonance as detected through the P2 suppression enables the applications of range-sensitive nonlinear dynamics.

title: Closing the UV-Induced Photodegradation Gap Through Global Scale Modeling of Fixed Tilt and Tracking Photovoltaic Systems
authors: S. Poddar, S. Liu, P. Hamer, M. Kay, B. Hoex
publish: IEEE Journal of Photovoltaics
abstract: Ultraviolet (UV) radiation accelerates the degradation of photovoltaic (PV) modules, both at the cell and module levels, leading to reduced efficiency and a shorter lifespan. While reliability testing has advanced, current standards often fail to reflect real-world UV exposure, especially across diverse climates. Global UV irradiance varies widely, from <30 W/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> in high-latitude regions to >80 W/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> in arid zones, while standard tests such as IEC-61215 only test up to a dose of 15 kWh /m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> that can be reached in less than 50 days in some locations. This study develops a high-precision model to estimate UV radiation on tilted surfaces and assesses the influence on UV photodegradation. The model shows minimum bias (<±3.87 W/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, i.e., <±4.28%) when compared with observations. Results show notable global variation in UV irradiance on tilted surfaces between single-axis tracking (SAT) and fixed-tilt systems, hence affecting degradation rates. SAT systems experience approximately twice the degradation in arid and semi-arid climates compared to fixed-tilt systems, where UV exposure is most intense. These findings highlight the critical need for climate-specific degradation assessments even for identical module technologies. To ensure outdoor durability and long-term performance, advanced regionally adaptive reliability testing thresholds should be adapted for better-informed material selection and manufacturing.

title: Front Cover
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Technology and Society Magazine

title: Enhanced Dynamic Representation via Gradient-Motion Modeling for Micro-Expression Recognition
authors: Tong Zhang, Shiting Xu, C. L. Philip Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Affective Computing
abstract: Micro-expressions (MEs) are brief, involuntary facial expressions revealing inner emotions. The unique occurrence mechanism of ME brings challenges for micro-expression recognition (MER). However, existing methods often suffer from task-irrelevant and overly localized representations, which lead to the loss of crucial discriminative information and thus limit recognition performance. In this paper, an enhanced dynamic representation via gradient-motion modeling (EDR) is proposed to obtain a complete and discriminative representation of the dynamic variations in ME. We provide theoretical support for EDR based on the complementarity of gradient and motion information in the computational rules of traditional optical flow. Specifically, a self-attentive gradient module (SAGM) is designed to enhance the sensitivity and localization of subtle motions. Inspired by the analytical solution of optical flow, we design a cross-attentive motion module (CAMM). Besides, dual-stage training is proposed to enhance fine-grained perception and generalization by first learning local-global correlations through a mask modeling strategy, followed by optimization under a standard recognition objective. Extensive experiments show that our method outperforms state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods. Ablation studies also demonstrate the effectiveness of each component.

title: Follow the Signs or the Crowd? Effects of Environmental Load and Crowd Dynamics in VR Evacuation
authors: Zheng Wei, Jingchen Gao, Zhe Yan, Yihan Liu, Linjie Qiu, Xian Xu, Yun Huang, Huamin Qu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Emergency evacuation in VR must balance realism with clear guidance. However, most prior studies strengthen either sensory or social factors in isolation, leaving equal-geometry causal estimates of load versus crowd still lacking. We present SAFE-VR, a controlled testbed that orthogonally varies Environmental Load (low vs. high) and Crowd Dynamics (orderly vs. chaotic) while keeping layout, signage, and spawn constant. In a preregistered 2×2 between-subjects experiment (N = 80), we analyzed time-to-exit, frame-coded behavior, presence, and workload to distangle sensory from social effects. Both factors impaired egress, with the High×Chaotic condition performing worst overall. For time-to-exit, effects were additive (no reliable Load×Crowd interaction); in contrast, Temporal demand showed a crossed interaction. High load increased effort, frustration, and object contacts; while chaotic f low increased route deviations, human contacts, and slowed exits. These patterns align with reliability-weighted cueing: as guidance becomes harder to perceive, participants may shift toward crowd-following, especially when flow is unstable. SAFE-VR thus delineates how load and crowd structure jointly shape route fidelity, collisions, and evacuation time, and highlights conditions where subjective time pressure diverges from objective delay.

title: A Vibration Suppression Method Based on Anisotropic Wedge for FSCW-PMSMs
authors: Siyao Huang, Wei Wang, Zheng Xu, Wenxiang Zhao, Ming Cheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Fractional-slot concentrated-winding (FSCW) permanent magnet (PM) synchronous machines (PMSMs) exhibit numerous low-spatial-order radial force waves (RFWs), which are a dominant source of vibration and noise. This article investigates the influence of slotting structures on flux density harmonics, RFWs, and vibration and proposes a new vibration suppression method tailored for FSCW-PMSMs. First, analytical and numerical studies reveal that the closed-slot structure can effectively mitigate the non-integer-pole-pair vibrations induced by slotting effects, but it increases flux leakage, leading to torque loss. To address the torque deficiency of the closed-slot structure design, an anisotropic wedge-based vibration suppression method was proposed to achieve simultaneous torque retention and vibration attenuation. Next, the effectiveness of the proposed method for the FSCW-PMSMs with different pole–slot combinations is validated via finite element analysis (FEA) on two groups of FSCW-PMSMs, where each group comprises three machines—employing open slot, anisotropic wedge, and closed slot, respectively—while maintaining identical geometries. Finally, experimental verification using three prototyped machines confirms that the proposed method can significantly reduce the vibration acceleration without compromising electromagnetic torque for FSCW-PMSMs.

title: The Standards Landscape in the IEEE Computer Society
authors: Darren Galpin
publish: Computer
abstract: Here, we highlight the work done by IEEE Computer Society’s various standards committees by showcasing the standards and project authorization requests approved in the December 2025 New Standards Committee (NesCom), Standards Review Committee (RevCom), and IEEE Standards Association Standards Board (SASB) meeting series.

title: Voltage-dependent Capacitance as a Measure of Pore Accessibility of Supercapacitor Electrodes_supp1-3677937.pdf
authors: Nandini Gupta
publish: N/A
abstract: Despite extensive research over the past two decades, achieving simultaneous high energy and power density in supercapacitors (SCs) remains a challenge. The key lies in designing nanoporous networks that offer a very high surface area while also maximizing electrolyte-ion accessibility of the pores. However, experimentally measuring pore accessibility in supercapacitor electrodes is challenging. This study employs a Particle-In-Cell-based computational approach to simulate charge transport, governed by the Poisson-Nernst-Planck equation, from the bulk electrolyte into porous electrodes. The influence of applied potential and pore morphology on charge dynamics of the SC is investigated. Results reveal that highly tortuous pores, along with low porosity, significantly reduce the charging rate. However, this adverse effect is mitigated by applying higher voltages, making initially inaccessible pores accessible, and thereby enhancing capacitance. Hence, the extent of voltage-dependence of capacitance is introduced as a measurable index for quantifying the pore accessibility in SC electrodes. Experimental validation confirms that this metric effectively distinguishes pore networks of various SC electrodes in terms of pore accessibility, using simple capacitance measurements. The proposed metric thus serves as a practical tool for designing high-performance SC electrodes for energy storage in electric vehicles and renewable energy systems.

title: Digital Twin-Based Motion Planning and Virtual-Physical Mapping Control for an OMR Through Narrow Corners
authors: Hongjiu Yang, Guowei Zhao, Yuanqing Xia, Peng Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: In this paper, a digital twin system is presented to enable static motion planning, reliable digital simulation and dynamic state monitoring for an omnidirectional mobile robot (OMR) through narrow corners. A virtual robot is constructed to realize precise collision detection and real-time motion visualization for the OMR which is taken for a physical robot. A precise configuration space is obtained based on the virtual robot in a virtual environment which is used to map the real environment. A six-neighborhood three-dimensional <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$A^{*}$</tex-math></inline-formula> algorithm is designed to find the optimal path from an initial configuration to a target configuration. A virtual-physical mapping control strategy is designed to realize trajectory tracking and dynamic monitoring of the physical robot and the virtual robot. Experimental results show effectiveness of the proposed motion planning and virtual-physical mapping control for the OMR through narrow corners.

title: Message from the General and Program Chairs: CW 2025
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyberworlds (CW）
abstract: Welcome to the 24th International Conference on Cyberworlds (CW 2025), hosted by Taiyuan University of Technology (TYUT) in Jinzhong, Shanxi Province, China, from October 14 to 16, 2025. As one of China's century-old and prestigious universities, TYUT takes great pride in organizing this international event. Shanxi, the venue of CW 2025, is renowned as a “treasure house of cultural relics,” offering a unique blend of rich historical heritage and vibrant modern development, which provides an inspiring backdrop for academic exchange and innovation.

title: Blockchain-Based Auditable Power Data Sharing Scheme
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: To facilitate the intelligent and digital transformation of the power grid, Grid Corporations have undertaken the construction of a data center-oriented infrastructure. This architecture enables the real-time acquisition, analysis, and sharing of massive volumes of electricity data, thereby significantly enhancing the efficiency of grid dispatching and the utilization of energy resources. However, the existing power data-sharing systems suffer from a lack of comprehensive auditing mechanisms, giving rise to several critical risks—including difficulty in tracking access behaviors, opacity in data transmission processes, and challenges in tracing the source of data leakage. These issues collectively compromise the trustworthiness and operational stability of the overall system. In response to these concerns, this paper proposes a blockchain-based auditable data-sharing scheme, which integrates smart contracts and cryptographic primitives to implement decentralized access control and behavioral auditing. The proposed solution ensures not only secure and traceable data interactions but also maintains favorable performance and scalability, as validated through experimental evaluation.

title: Standardized Approach for Assessing Wireless IoT Devices in Millimeter-Wave Industrial Work-Cell Channels
authors: Mohamed Kashef, Kate A. Remley, Paritosh Manurkar, Joshua M. Kast, Iyemeh E. Uchendu, Robert D. Horansky, Dylan Williams, Matthew Simons, Michael Frey, Lucas Koepke, Richard Candell
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: We present a standardized, repeatable test approach that replicates key features of measured industrial environments in a fully-characterized hybrid test chamber. The hybrid chamber consists of an anechoic chamber along with movable metallic spheres that provide discrete reconfigurable multipath components. The chamber is characterized by a synthetic-aperture system providing a reference measurement that captures the non-idealities of the chamber along with the intended spatial multipath, completing characterization of the test environment. A key feature of our approach is the propagation of correlated uncertainties through to the final system response – including uncertainties specific to synthetic apertures such as cable bending due to scanning and inexact positioner locations. The uncertainty analysis provides statistically accurate knowledge of the environment, allowing separation of measurement errors from errors related to the device or system under test. We have also extracted three exemplar channels that capture key features of a work-cell-sized industrial environment from measurements made with the same synthetic aperture. Each exemplar channel presents different, realistic angular-diverse multipath challenges to a millimeter-wave internet-of-things device, subsystem, or active antenna under test. This allows engineers to evaluate their hardware designs in a physical, repeatable test environment and also facilitates future standardization of test methods for these and other millimeter-wave wireless devices.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A

title: Bias-Compensated Maximum Quaternion Correntropy for Adaptive Filtering
authors: Minyong Wei, Qiangqiang Zhang, Zhengwen Tu, Xueqin Yang, Jianfeng Liu, Dongyuan Lin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Quaternion adaptive filters have been widely used in processing three-dimensional (3-D) and 4-D signals. To improve the filtering performance and estimation accuracy in the quaternion errors-in-variables (QEIV) model where the output signal is disturbed by non-Gaussian impulsive noises, this paper addresses the issue of quaternion parameter estimation. First, a novel bias-compensated maximum quaternion correntropy (BCMQC) criterion is proposed to resist impulsive outliers and mitigate the bias induced by noisy inputs, simultaneously. Then, based on an alternating optimization strategy involving explicit input compensation, the BCMQC algorithm is derived using the quaternion generalized Hamilton-real (GHR) calculus. Moreover, the Sherman-Morrison formula is employed to derive a computationally efficient update rule. Finally, simulations demonstrate the improved filtering performance and robustness of the proposed algorithm in the QEIV model.

title: ADODroid: A Static Detection Model for Android Malware Based on GCN and BiLSTM
authors: Shuoyuan Zhang, Xinyou Zhang, Zonghai Zhu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: This article proposes a new Android malware detection model ADODroid. The model extracts three types of software features through static analysis: API call graph, data flow, and opcode sequence. The API call graph provides spatial semantic information of the software, the opcode sequence provides contextual semantic information of the software, and the data flow can enhance the integrity of spatial semantic features. Abstracting the API call graph to enhance the model's anti-obfuscation ability and reduce computational complexity, then embedding the data flow into the graph to enrich the spatial semantics of features, while converting the opcode sequence into embedding vectors. Finally, inject the graph data and embedding vectors into a fusion neural network composed of GCN and BiLSTM, and output the classification results. The experimental results on the AndroZoo and VirusShare datasets show that the detection precision of the trained ADODroid can reach 96%, while having stronger robustness than other similar methods.

title: E-GPT: A Multi-Agent LLM Framework for Intelligent Educational Assistance
authors: Tabassum Ara, Shreyansu Panda, Jason Samuel Das, Amit Ranjan Das, Sidharth Vivek Prabhugoankar
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Recent developments in Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Natural Language Processing (NLP) have significantly influenced the education sector, mainly through Large Language Models (LLMs) and Multi-Agent Systems (MAS). Educational chatbots such as GPTutor use Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and transformer-based models to offer more intelligent tutoring and automated assessment support. Still, most existing systems depend on centralized APIs and monolithic designs, which restrict scalability, adaptability to different contexts, and data privacy.In this paper, we introduce E-GPT, a locally deployable multi-agent educational chatbot framework designed to divide learning tasks among independent, specialized agents. These include a PDF-to-Quiz Generator, PDF-to-Technical Article Writer, RAG-based Chat Assistant, Question Paper Generator, and an OCR-enabled PDF Analyzer. The system integrates fine-tuned versions of LLaMA3 and Mistral models with a MongoDB-FAISS vector database, allowing efficient context retrieval and smooth collaboration between agents.Experimental results show that E-GPT improves contextual accuracy, modular scalability, and response time while maintaining user data privacy. By distributing different cognitive functions across coordinated agents, the system takes a step toward building a more adaptive, transparent, and scalable AI-driven learning environment.

title: AI-based Emergency Help Service
authors: Afraim Uzzaman Romeo, Maisha Rahman Diya, Ataur Rahman, Mahady Hasan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: In today's world, timely response during emergencies is a matter of life and death. This paper highlights the critical need for a fast and reliable emergency help system that is capable of alerting emergency contacts, authorities, and first aid services immediately. The goal of this system is to build a safe and efficient platform capable of sending emergency notifications, providing real-time location, establishing secure communication channels, offline communication, and assisting users with AIdriven first aid assistance. In this paper, we conducted a survey and an interview. In our survey, we wanted to find out if people actually needed the mobile app in emergency situations. After developing a user interface design, we conducted an interview for user satisfaction to understand how well it worked and what could be improved. Based on their feedback, we made some changes. Their insights helped us refine the design further, were reliable, and easy to use. The system was created following Agile methodology, with iterative design and regular user feedback. In short, the Emergency Help Service system adequately improves emergency response. Future improvements will aim to integrate IoT devices, predictive AI, and multiple communication channels to make the platform stronger and smarter.

title: A Transient Stability Assessment Model Based on the Broad Learning System
authors: Shenghong Liu, Bo Wei, Jinghao Zhang, Zhengdan Wang, Kuiping Ding, Xiyuan Zhang, Junyi Bian, Jun Liu
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)
abstract: Accurate online assessment of transient stability remains a key challenge in power system analysis. To overcome the limitations of time-domain simulation and direct methods in practical use, this paper proposes a data-driven model based on the Broad Learning System (BLS). A data-driven framework for online transient stability assessment is established, and feature augmentation is achieved using the algorithmic principles of BLS. An online incremental learning model is developed for transient stability assessment. Simulation studies on the IEEE 39-bus system demonstrate that the proposed BLS-based model achieves superior accuracy and shorter training time compared to several benchmark models, highlighting its potential for practical engineering applications.

title: A Hybrid LSTM-VGG16 and Fuzzy Logic Framework for Illegal Land Occupation and Flood Damage Assessment from Satellite Imagery
authors: S. Madhavan, V. Logeshwar, G. Sivagurunathan
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Under this work, a combined scheme of tracking down cases of illegal land occupation and measuring flood damages through satellite images are also presented. The method of analyzing the pattern of space and time land use uses a deep learning and fuzzy logic method. High-level spatial features are extracted in images using a pre-trained VGG16 convolutional neural network. A Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network is used to sequence and analyze these features to display dynamic variations and discover illegal land occupation. In flood damage assessment, fuzzy logic system is applied to data uncertainties occurring with pre and post event imagery to facilitate subtle damage assessment. The framework creates real time notifications to the authorities automatically to aid quick response to disasters and sound decision making in land management. The system, written in Python and powered by geospatial libraries, shows potential to improve the urban planning and environmental agency in terms of providing accurate and timely understanding of land use dynamics and the effects of disasters.

title: MultiMotion: A Scalable Multi-Person Interactive Motion Generation Framework
authors: Shuaibo Li, Ke Lv, Kun Dong, Jian Xue
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Multi-person motion generation is a fundamental problem in computer vision, with broad applications in gaming, animation, and virtual environments. This task entails the joint modeling of multi-person motion synthesis and interactive dynamics—an inherently challenging problem due to the complex spatiotemporal dependencies among participants. Existing approaches are largely confined to single-person or dyadic (two-person) settings and struggle to generate coherent, scalable multi-person motion sequences. In this paper, we rethinks the formulation of multi-person motion generation and proposes MultiMotion, a conditional collaborative motion generation framework. MultiMotion comprises two key components: a generation module and an extension module. The generation module synthesizes realistic and diverse single-person motion sequences. The extension module coordinates and guides the motion generation process during diffusion by conditioning on the motion sequences of other agents, thereby enabling seamless scalability from individual motions to multi-person collaborative and interactive behaviors. furthermore, our framework utilizes a collaborative diffusion mechanism that exchanges noisy motion representations among participants at every diffusion step. By leveraging these shared intermediate signals, the extension module dynamically extends single-person actions into multi-person collaborative interactions throughout the denoising process. Comprehensive evaluations confirm that our approach is effective and capable of simultaneously inferring motions involving either individual or multiple persons within a unified generative framework.

title: Stock Trading Prediction Based on the DQN Series Algorithm
authors: Yichuan Lu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Stock trading decisions hold significant importance in financial trading. Traditional stock trading decisions rely on fundamental analysis, technical analysis, or expert experience, which have shortcomings such as strong subjectivity. As technology advances, reinforcement learning techniques are also being applied to stock trading decisions. Based on the reinforcement learning theory, agents are allowed to autonomously learn stock trading strategies in the stock market environment to achieve maximum trading returns. To overcome the limitations of traditional methods, this paper uses three reinforcement learning algorithms: DQN, Double DQN (DDQN), and Dueling Double DQN (DDDQN) for stock trading decisions, and carry out tests and comparative analysis. The experimental results show that all three methods can achieve certain returns in the training set and the test set. Among them, the Dueling Double DQN algorithm performs the best overall. The Double DQN algorithm performs well, and the stability of the DQN algorithm needs to be improved. The effectiveness of the algorithm is verified, and the reproducible solution of stock trading decision is provided in this study.

title: Explainable Hybrid PSO-SVM Framework for EEG-Based Cognitive Confusion Detection
authors: Ravichander Janapati, Vasupalli Manoj, Usha Desai, Viswaprakash Babu, Ghousia Sanober Sabreen
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Cognitive confusion is an urgent affective condition that affects learning effectiveness and decision-making performance. Identification of such states with the help of electroencephalography (EEG) helps in human cognitive processes and allow the adaptation of educational or neurofeedback systems. This study proposes an explainable hybrid approach to Particle Swarm Optimization-Support Vector Machine (PSO-SVM) to detect the presence of confusion states in a dataset of publicly available Wanghaohan Confused Student EEG Brainwave Dataset with sustainable accuracy and interpretability. In this study, EEG data recorded through a single-channel NeuroSky MindSet device, which has power spectral features, such as delta, theta, alpha, beta, and gamma, as well as the indices of attention and meditation during the e-learning activities. PSO is used in the proposed framework to optimize the SVM hyperparameters and subsets of features, which guarantees better generalization and less overfitting. The model attained high classification rates in identifying the confounded and non-confounded cognitive states compared to baseline machine learning classifiers. Moreover, Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) models, such as SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) and feature importance analysis, were combined to understand the value of each EEG feature in the model decision. The findings prove that high-frequency gamma and attention scores are the prevailing signs of confusion. The proposed PSO-SVM-XAI system is accurate as well as transparent, providing scalability of intelligent human-computer interaction and adaptive learning systems based on monitoring the cognitive state.

title: A Deep Learning-Based Method for Separating Overlapping ADS-B Signals in Continuous Single-Antenna Reception
authors: Chenchen Wang, Wenyi Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Automatic Dependent Surveillance-Broadcast (ADS-B) signals are prone to in-air overlapping, which leads to decoding failures. Most existing deep learning-based methods are trained and evaluated on pre-segmented overlapping signals, which differs from real reception scenarios. In practice, continuous signals received by a single antenna are dominated by noise and interference, with only a few randomly occurring ADS-B signal segments. To address this issue, an end-to-end time-domain ADS-B signal separation method is proposed to process continuous observation signals directly. This work is the first to introduce the overlap-add (OLA) strategy into this task, enabling direct recovery of overlapping signals. Considering the low proportion of effective signals in continuous data and the insufficient constraints of conventional scale-invariant source-to-noise ratio (SI-SNR) loss, a log-envelope region-weighted SI-SNR (LERW-SNR) loss is designed. The proposed loss enhances the emphasis on effective signal regions during training while suppressing the influence of background noise. Experimental results on real-world continuous observation signals show that, compared with the standard SI-SNR loss, the proposed method improves cyclic redundancy check (CRC) pass rates by 3.27% and 11.24% on Dual-Path Recurrent Neural Network (DPRNN) and Separation Transformer (Sepformer) networks, respectively. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method for continuous signals received by a single antenna.

title: Bengali Speech Based Medical Assistant Using Retrieval Augmented Generation for Symptom-Based Diagnosis and Treatment
authors: Dhruba Chowdhury, Saklain Abdullah, Riad Hossain, Mohammad Shamsul Arefin, M. M. Musharaf Hussain
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Thousands of rural and marginalized people in Bangladesh still face serious barriers to accessing basic healthcare due to language limitations, low literacy, and social prejudice. Women and adolescent girls are especially affected, as they often lack a safe and private way to ask health-related questions in their native language. Since most digital health systems are designed primarily in English, a large number of Bengali speakers remain excluded from these services. To address this challenge, this paper presents a Bengali voice-based medical assistant developed using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). The system understands spoken Bengali, retrieves trusted medical information from a verified knowledge base, and generates clear and reliable healthcare advice. The proposed framework integrates Speech-to-Text, hybrid information retrieval (fuzzy matching for direct disease queries and semantic search using FAISS for symptombased queries), and a large language model to provide an end-to-end speech-to-speech healthcare assistant. The system was evaluated on a doctor-verified dataset of 100 diseases and symptom profiles, achieving a macro F1-score of 0.93. This performance is significantly higher than approaches relying only on keyword search, semantic retrieval, or a language model without retrieval support. The results demonstrate that combining knowledge-based retrieval with generative AI can deliver safe, accurate, and understandable medical guidance in Bengali.

title: Numerical Modeling of Radiosonde-Assisted Compact Ranges for Electromagnetic Propagation under Atmospheric Variability
authors: Sujit Kumar Chakravarty, Amul Batra, Mohd Imran Ansari, Vijay Kumar Soni
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: Conventional compact-range electromagnetic (EM) characterization assumes homogeneous atmospheric conditions and ignores thermodynamic gradients that distort propagation. In this paper, we propose a RadiosondeAssisted Compact Range (RACR) framework to integrate real-time temperature, pressure, and humidity profiles into Maxwell-based full-wave simulations using continuous altitude-dependent refractivity mapping. RACR preserves vertical dielectric gradients while enabling closed-loop validation through S-parameter analysis, which is not possible with discrete-layer or ITU-based models. The proposed method reduces transmission prediction RMSE by 61 % across <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0-5 \text{GHz}$</tex>, enhances correlation with measurements to 0.97, and limits phase-delay deviation to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq 1.2 \%$</tex>. The results demonstrate enhanced beam coherence and transmission fidelity in stratified and humid atmospheres, paving the way for weather-adaptive electromagnetic calibration.

title: Strengthening Password Security in Virtual Learning Environments: An Analysis of Protocols, Compliance, and User Behavior
authors: Bin Hu, Ifrah Malik, Fanyu Zeng, Noman Sohail
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Technology, Communication Ecosystem and Management (ITCEM)
abstract: This study evaluates password security protocols in virtual learning environments (VLEs), addressing critical gaps in current practices and proposing enhancements to improve security and user satisfaction. Using hypothetical four platforms (A, B, C, and D), the study examines the effectiveness of various security measures, including minimum password length, special character requirements, multi-factor authentication (MFA), and password expiry periods. The findings reveal significant disparities in security practices across platforms, with Platform D, which implements stringent password policies and MFA, demonstrating the highest user compliance, fewest security incidents, and greatest user satisfaction. Conversely, Platform B, with the least stringent measures and no MFA, shows the highest incidence of security breaches. The study highlights the crucial role of MFA in enhancing security, as platforms with MFA report significantly fewer incidents. A strong inverse correlation between password strength and security incidents underscores the need for robust password policies. User behavior, such as password reuse and poor password hygiene, remains a challenge, emphasizing the importance of user education programs. Additionally, the study advocates for standardized security practices across VLEs to ensure consistent protection. Implications of this research suggest that educational institutions must adopt comprehensive security measures, including stringent password policies, MFA, and user education, to safeguard sensitive information and improve user satisfaction. The findings provide a framework for developing more secure VLEs and highlight the critical role of cybersecurity in supporting the future of digital education. This study contributes to the broader effort of enhancing VLE security and fostering a safer online learning environment.

title: Blue Finance Policy for Sustainable Development Goals: An Evidence-Based Approach
authors: Naglaa Fathy El Dessouky
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Sustainable Islamic Business and Finance (SIBF)
abstract: Blue finance has emerged as an instrumental policy tool for directing funds to associated ocean activities. It supports the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), specifically SDG 14. Maritime ecologies face increasing challenges, ranging from climate change to risky pollution and unsustainable resource utilization, which underscores the need for rational financial mechanisms to support sustainable ocean policies. Given the scarcity of research on this topic and its novelty, the paper aimed to deepen understanding and address that gap. The research methodology is framed in a qualitative, evidence-based approach that utilizes critical case study analysis to investigate marine sustainability and blue finance initiatives across selected MENA and GCC countries. The paper examined the conceptual foundations of the blue finance notion, its policy instruments and tools, its governance and networking framework, and its policy elaboration within international contexts. Through the analysis of these leading initiatives, the study examined the intervention approach, policy instruments, stakeholder engagement, and governance modes of the blue finance policy design. The study emphasized that, despite contextual differences, countries consistently aligned their initiatives with international standards for the Sustainable Development Goals. The paper concluded with practical recommendations to strengthen public policy design, enhance policymakers' interventions in policy formulation and implementation, improve stakeholders' governance, and inform impact strategies.

title: Parameter-Efficient Federated Multimodal Alignment Tuning for Heterogeneous Clients
authors: Xu Tan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Large-scale vision-language models like CLIP achieve breakthrough performance in zero-shot classification and cross-modal retrieval by learning aligned representations from massive image-text pairs. However, their centralized training paradigm faces data sharing challenges in privacy-sensitive domains. Although federated learning enables collaborative training without sharing raw data, it encounters the cross-modal alignment challenge with heterogeneous client modalities. To address this challenge, a parameter-efficient federated multimodal alignment tuning framework Fed-MoAlign is proposed in this paper. In Fed-MoAlign, the model is decomposed into vision encoder, text encoder, and alignment layer. The backbone is frozen and Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) layers are inserted into encoders based on pretrained CLIP. Unimodal clients train corresponding encoder LoRA parameters independently, while multimodal clients additionally update the alignment layer. To enable unimodal clients alignment training, a dynamic prototype-based pseudo-modality generation algorithm is proposed where prototypes are maintained at the server from multimodal clients, and pseudo features are generated by unimodal clients via similarity-weighted prototype retrieval. Furthermore, a hierarchical selective aggregation mechanism based on cross-modal mutual information is introduced, designing differentiated strategies for different network layers according to their roles in multimodal alignment. Experiments on MS-COCO and Flickr30K demonstrate that Fed-MoAlign achieves 55.1% and 35.6% I2T R@1 respectively, outperforming state-of-the-art federated multimodal methods CreamFL by 3.0% and 3.3%. Ablation studies further validate the effectiveness of our proposed components.

title: Automated Brain Tumor Detection and Segmentation using Rob flow annotation & YOLOv11 Model
authors: Nivedita Gudigenavar, Manjunath Managuli, Nikhil Inamdar, Ramesh Koti, Jagadish S Jakati, Sharanappa P H
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: A brain tumor, which can be either benign or malignant, is an abnormal development of brain cells. For better treatment outcomes and increased survival rates, early identification is essential. Traditional analytical techniques such as CT and MRI provide valuable structural and locational information but rely heavily on manual interpretation. Biopsy remains a gold standard but is invasive and time-consuming. In this work, we employ Artificial Intelligence (AI) to enhance brain tumor detection using MRI scans. The dataset was annotated and preprocessed using Rob flow, incorporating data augmentation to improve model generalization. A YOLO-based deep learning framework was trained in Google Colab with GPU support, enabling efficient and instantaneous discovery of tumor regions. The proposed structure integrates Rob flow’s dataset management with YOLO’s high-speed object detection, adapted for tumor segmentation. Performance evaluation was carried out expending metrics such as mean Average Precision (mAP), Correctness, F1-score, Recall, and confusion matrix. Investigational results demonstrate the capability of the approach to support radiologists in early diagnosis, offering a reliable, automated, and non-invasive solution for brain tumor detection.

title: Problem Formalization and Benchmarking of Multimodal Learning for Text, Image, Audio, and Video Understanding to Promote Inclusive and Sustainable Knowledge Access
authors: K. Ramu Dev Burman, Moorthy Agoramoorthy
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The Multimodal learning, being a combination of text, image, audio and video information has come up as the essential paradigm of creating intelligent and context sensitive information system. They are also being used more in education, healthcare, governance, and media, where the successful combination of various types of data can be used to improve knowledge, decision-making, and accessibility. Although there has been much progress, there have been a number of challenges such as the absence of standard definitions of the problem, disparities between benchmarking protocols and the problem of equitable and inclusive knowledge access. The current paper will provide a detailed system toward formalizing and benchmarking of multimodal learning models, especially in terms of interpretability, fairness, and resource efficiency. Representative architectures are analyzed in a systematic manner, in a variety of modalities, to measure performance, robustness as well as computation requirements trade-offs. The results offer viable lessons to researchers and practitioners in order to create not only accurate and scalable multimodal systems but also socially accountable and able to promote inclusive knowledge sharing. Besides, the research points out the most influential areas where research should focus in the future to develop effective, accessible, and sustainable multimodal models.

title: Perceptual Quality Assessment of Compressed Vibrotactile Signals Based on Weber–Fechner Threshold Adjustment
authors: Yixuan Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Vibrotactile signal compression is essential for efficient transmission in the Tactile Internet. However, distortions introduced during compression often degrade perceptual quality, while conventional objective metrics based on signal-domain errors fail to accurately reflect human tactile perception. To address this issue, this paper proposes a biomimetic multi-channel vibrotactile perceptual quality assessment framework based on Weber–Fechner threshold adjustment. Inspired by the frequency selectivity of Fast-Adapting Type I (FA-I) and Fast-Adapting Type II (FA-II) mechanoreceptors, the proposed method decomposes vibrotactile signals into multiple perceptual channels and incorporates Just Noticeable Difference (JND) calibration within each physiological channel. Grounded in standardized psychophysical thresholds, the proposed model establishes a perception-consistent evaluation baseline in the signal domain. Experiments conducted on diverse subsets of the HapticGen dataset demonstrate that the proposed method maintains stable accuracy under low compression ratios, while achieving an average improvement of approximately 0.2–0.3 in Pearson Linear Correlation Coefficient (PLCC) compared to existing mainstream metrics in high-compression scenarios.

title: PC-CDS: Real-Time Secure Authentication and Intelligent Steering for Hybrid TN-NTN Networks
authors: Abdul Samim, Love Allen Chijioke Ahakonye, Jae Min Lee, Dong-Seong Kim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: The integration of Terrestrial Networks (TN) and Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTN) represents a cornerstone for 6G wireless systems, promising ubiquitous connectivity and enhanced quality of service. However, hybrid TN-NTN architectures introduce critical security vulnerabilities and complex network steering challenges. This paper proposes PureChainsecured Cognitive Dual-Steering (PC-CDS), integrating a custom blockchain authentication with Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO)-based intelligent network steering. PureChain achieves a mean authentication delay of 15 ms with a 99.8% success rate, compared to 100 ms and 98.5% for traditional blockchain. The PPO-based cognitive agent dynamically orchestrates user connections between terrestrial gNBs and Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) satellites. Extensive simulations with 80 users, 25 terrestrial gNBs, and 3 VLEO satellites over 200 episodes demonstrate superior performance: 87.5% VLEO connectivity, network switching latency under 20 ms versus 100 ms for traditional blockchain, stable throughput at 90 Mbps versus 85 Mbps degradation, and controlled packet loss at 11% versus 17%. PPO outperforms Soft Actor-Critic, achieving a median reward of 160 compared to 140. PC-CDS lays the foundation for secure, intelligent 6G hybrid networks.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning-Based Remaining Useful Life Prediction for Industrial Motors
authors: Debasis Jana, Suprakash Gupta, Sukomal Pal, Sandip Ghosh
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: Precise Remaining Useful Life (RUL) prediction of industrial machinery and its components is important for ensuring high operational availability, reducing unplanned downtime, and optimizing maintenance costs. Industrial machinery are operates in dynamic and harsh environmental condition leading to a complex, non-linear and non-stationary degradation. It is challenging to model such degradation pattern using the traditional statistical models and classical machine learning techniques. To overcome this issue, a deep learning based hybrid model comprises of a Bi-directional Long Short Term Memory (Bi-LSTM) network and a Transformer architecture, has been developed for predicting the RUL of industrial machinery in this study. The developed model is trained and tested using the multivariate time-series sensor data collected from a heavy duty industrial DC motor operated in a mining machinery. The developed model exhibits superior performance over the standalone model of Bi-LSTM and Transformer, achieving a Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of 231.77 and an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.94. the results demonstrate strong predictive capability of the hybrid model. The study highlights the interpretability through attention mechanisms, providing insights into critical degradation patterns of industrial motor for inform maintenance decisions.

title: Design and Implementation of a Bifacial Solar-Powered Water Pumping System Using Boost Converter for Off-Grid Homes
authors: Kiruthiga Devi V, Veena P, Jeyabharath R, Prabhakaran K, Loganath D, Pradeep M
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The aim of this research project is to design and implement a Gallium Nitride FET (GaN-FET) boost converter in conjunction with a bi-facial photovoltaic panel-based water pump to enhance the reliability of energy usage in off-grid homes and to improve the overall efficiency of the energy usage. In this research, there are two groups. Group 1 refers to the conventional monofacial solar panel + silicon-MOSFET converter configuration, and Group 2 refers to the proposed bifacial solar panel + GaN-FET converter configuration. The system performance was evaluated under 6 different irradiance levels using MATLAB/Simulink R2025a simulation and hardware prototype testing. Using the GaN-FET based converter, the system consistently generated a stable <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$48-\mathrm{V}$</tex> output with less than 0.5 V peak-to-peak ripple across all irradiances from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 ~\text{kW} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$200 ~\mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex>. With respect to the irradiance level, the bi-facial configuration produced between <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5}-\mathbf{2 5} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex> more power than the monofacial module. The average efficiency of the complete system was approximately 96.5 %. With regard to the battery backup, the transition from bright light conditions to dark light conditions resulted in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 8 - 3 2 \%}$</tex> more average power delivery and approximately 30 % more total daily volume of water production compared to the monofacial + Si-MOSFET system. Bifacial Solar-Powered Water Pump with a GaN-FET Converter Offers Improved Reliability and Efficiency of Energy Usage in Comparison to Monofacial Systems and is therefore suitable for residential off-grid water supplies.

title: Intelligent Resume Analysis and Skill Matching Using NLP
authors: Dontamsetti Nageswarrao, M Sai Kiran, V Sai Krishna, V Sree Hansitha, Bunga Sekhar, Sultana Saba
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Resume screening that is performed manually is also tedious, expensive, and can be biased and does not usually result in the rightful matching of job descriptions with the candidate. The proposed solution to these dilemmas is described in the paper that offers an intelligent resume analysis and skill-matching system based on the application of Natural Language Processing (NLP) and machine learning methods. The system uses SpaCy and PyResparser to get structured information like skills, education, and experience using unstructured resumes. The features extracted are then converted into a form of vectors and the cosine similarity is used to generate the relevance scores between resumes and job descriptions. On these scores, the candidates are ranked so as to help the recruiters in short listing the most appropriate candidates. They are experimentally tested and prove that the offered system is characterized by the high accuracy of resume parsing, generating rankings which are similar to the expectations of recruiters, causing a significant shortening of the screening process, as opposed to the traditional ones. Moreover, real-time rejection and selection messages are used to increase the transparency of the recruitment process. The suggested solution is a fair, efficient, and scalable method of automated hiring and enhances the efficiency of the contemporary recruitment system appropriately.

title: An ML Model to detect Cyber-attacks for Wireless Sensor Networks in Microgrids
authors: Harshitha H B, Pavithra A C, Manjunath K, Juslin F
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Microgrids' operating efficiency has been completely transformed by the development of smart energy systems and the incorporation of Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs). The stability, security, and dependability of microgrid infrastructures are seriously threatened by these technical advancements, which also increase susceptibility to cyberattacks. WSNs are especially vulnerable to a range of cyberattacks, such as Denial of Service (DoS), data integrity breaches, reconnaissance assaults, and more sophisticated persistent threats, because of their resource-constrained design, wireless communication protocols, and widespread deployment A thorough machine learning-based approach for identifying cyberattacks in WSNs installed in microgrid systems is presented in this study. The suggested system is intended to use intelligent classification algorithms to effectively categorize hostile actions, learn from past patterns, and proactively monitor network traffic and sensor data. To find the best method for real-time intrusion detection under microgrid-specific limitations, a number of machine learning approaches were assessed, including decision trees, random forests, support vector machines, and ensemble learning. Data collection and preprocessing were the first steps of a multi-phase methodology that included feature selection, model training, and a thorough performance evaluation. Both publicly accessible benchmark datasets designed for network intrusion detection and simulated cyberattack scenarios were used to train and evaluate the model. To guarantee a fair trade- off between detection efficacy and computing efficiency, key performance parameters including accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and detection latency were examined The study's findings show that the suggested model may effectively generalize to novel and changing assault patterns while achieving high detection accuracy and a low false positive rate. Additionally, the system is flexible and scalable, which allows it to be integrated into actual microgrid designs without sacrificing their functionality. To sum up, this work offers a fresh and useful approach to the increasing cybersecurity issues that contemporary energy systems face. The framework improves microgrid resilience and lays the groundwork for further study of safe, sustainable, and self-sufficient energy systems by utilizing machine learning for intelligent threat identification.

title: Research on Ceramic Product Classification and Localization Algorithm Based on Deep Learning
authors: Yihong Yuan, Yanke Jiang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Due to uncertainties in the manufacturing process and interference from human factors, various anomalies can occur in ceramic products during production. These anomalies not only affect the quality of the products but may also pose a threat to user safety. Therefore, using anomaly detection methods is crucial for improving the quality and safety of ceramic products. In light of this, this paper proposes a target detection algorithm based on YOLOv8 for the classification and localization of ceramic products, leveraging computer vision detection technology. The algorithm employs a lightweight model and incorporates an attention mechanism, ensuring high accuracy while simultaneously enhancing the inference speed. This makes the algorithm more accurate and efficient in collecting object category information and key point location information. The improved algorithm is then tested, and experimental results demonstrate a significant improvement in both accuracy and speed, achieving an average precision of 97.8% on the test set. Compared to manual inspection, this method improves efficiency and accuracy and addresses the long-tailed distribution problem of positive and negative samples encountered in actual industrial production.

title: MMS-DETR: Fusing State Space Model and Mixed Aggregation for Small Object Detection on USVs
authors: Jizhang Luo, Feng Liu
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: Unmanned Surface Vehicles(USVs) are playing an increasingly crucial role in key areas such as marine monitoring and maritime search and rescue. The accurate and realtime detection of small water-surface objects, such as persons overboard and small obstacles, is a core technical bottleneck for achieving autonomous navigation. Currently, this task faces severe challenges, including minute target scales, sparse features, and complex dynamic background interference from waves and reflections. To address these issues, this paper proposes MMS-DETR, an end-to-end network specifically designed for small object detection on the water surface.
