Results show that MSCVT delivers clearer feature representation, more stable predictions, and higher accuracy than standard CNN-based models, making it a stronger option for real-world turmeric disease detection. This improvement can help farmers catch infections earlier, reduce crop loss, and make disease monitoring more efficient in practical agricultural settings.

title: Integration of AI Chatbots in MSMEs: Factors Affecting Adoption and Their Effects on Organizational Performance
authors: Gabriella Kristiani Sandhya Dewanto, Okky Rizkia Yustian
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: AI Chatbot is currently growing rapidly to be implemented by various large companies, but many MSMEs still do not have the readiness and feel the impact of using AI Chatbot. To support digital transformation in Indonesia, it is important to know, how the impact of AI chatbot on organizational performance. However, research that discusses AI chatbot factors and their impact on organizational performance is still limited. This study aims to identify Organizational readiness and Competitive pressure factors on AI Chatbot Adoption and evaluate the impact of the chatbot on Organization Performance with a quantitative approach. Data was collected from 250 MSME owners/managers in Bandung City through a survey and analyzed using PLS-SEM. The results of the analysis show that AI Chatbot Adoption has a negative impact on organizational performance. Then Organizational Readiness has a significant effect on AI Chatbot Adoption, but the direction of the effect is negative and Organizational Readiness and Competitive Pressure have no significant effect on Organization Performance. Additional variables such as digital literacy, organizational culture, AI governance, and maturity implementation, as well as the use of mixed method for deeper understanding can be used for future research.

title: Deployment of SAR Target Recognition Model Based on Embedded Devices
authors: Jinxin Xia, Qian Luo, Zaiping Lin, Qian Yin, Miao Li, Weidong Sheng
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: With the continuous development of computer-related technologies, deep learning-based technologies are playing an increasingly important role in the field of Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) target recognition. The current deep learning algorithms have a large number of parameter models and rely on powerful computer equipment to achieve high-precision object detection results. However, due to the limited computing power of the radar terminal equipment, it is difficult for the model to run on the radar terminal equipment. In response to the above issues, this paper implements model deployment on domestic embedded devices by adopting lightweight neural networks while ensuring recognition accuracy. This paper choose domestic embedded development board Atlas200IDKA2 developer suite. Convert the pth file obtained under the PyTorch framework into a dedicated om model, and perform inference on the Pruned-ResNet18-SE and Pruned-MobileNetV3 models by calling the model inference interface provided by the developer suite. The experiment found that the converted model could ensure accuracy, and the average reasoning speed per SAR image was 2.28ms and 2.49ms respectively. Meanwhile, by comparing the ResNet18-SE and MobileNetV3 models before pruning, the experiment found that Pruned-ResNet18-SE increased the accuracy rate by $1.48 \%$ and the reasoning speed by $32.14 \%$ on the basis of a $95.69 \%$ reduction in the number of parameters. Pruned-MobileNetV3 has increased the accuracy by $2.24 \%$ and the inference speed by $8.46 \%$ on the basis of reducing the number of parameters by $36.79\%$

title: Hybrid CNN-Transformer Models for Automated Brain Tumor Detection in MRI Scans
authors: Swati Killikatt, Sanjay B. Ankali
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: One of the most accurate imaging techniques for identifying and diagnosing brain tumors is magnetic resonance imaging (MRI). Automated, precise, and scalable solutions are required since manual MRI scan interpretation is laborious and subject to inter-observer variability. This study introduces a hybrid deep learning system for automated brain tumor analysis that combines Transformer encoders and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). While the Transformer encoder represents long-range relationships and global contextual information, the CNN component efficiently extracts local spatial characteristics. When tested using publicly accessible brain tumor MRI datasets (BraTS), the suggested hybrid architecture outperformed traditional CNN and Transformer-only methods. The CNN layers are employed to capture fine-grained spatial features, while the Transformer component models long-range contextual dependencies across volumetric MRI data. The framework was evaluated on publicly available BraTS datasets and achieved superior results compared to CNN-only and Transformer-only baselines. Experimental findings demonstrate consistent improvements in accuracy, Dice coefficient, sensitivity, and specificity, confirming the robustness of the approach. These outcomes suggest that CNN–Transformer hybrids can serve as a strong foundation for computer-aided diagnosis systems in clinical practice.

title: Prioritizing Factors Influencing Cloud ERP Adoption in Sri Lankan Organizations: An Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP) Approach
authors: F. Mariyam, R. Wickramarachchi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: The rapid evolution of enterprise systems has positioned Cloud-based Enterprise Resource Planning (Cloud ERP) as a strategic solution for improving operational efficiency and scalability. However, enterprises, particularly in developing economies such as Sri Lanka, face challenges in identifying which factors most critically influence successful adoption. This study aims to prioritize the key factors affecting Cloud ERP adoption in Sri Lankan enterprises by applying the Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP) within the framework of the Technology-Organization-Environment (TOE) model. A comprehensive literature review identified fifteen critical factors across the three TOE dimensions. Ten domain experts representing academia, ERP vendors, and user organizations participated in structured pairwise comparisons to evaluate the relative importance of these factors. Their judgments were aggregated using the geometric mean method, and consistency was verified through the AHP consistency ratio. The resulting prioritization revealed that technological factors hold the greatest overall influence, with data security, system reliability, and integration capability emerging as top-ranked determinants. Top management support and organizational readiness were identified as dominant organizational factors, while vendor support and regulatory compliance led the environmental dimension. The findings provide empirical guidance for Sri Lankan enterprises and policymakers by highlighting which dimensions require strategic emphasis for effective Cloud ERP adoption. Moreover, the study demonstrates the applicability of AHP as a systematic decision support tool for prioritizing complex multi-criteria issues in enterprise technology adoption contexts.

title: AI-Augmented Legal Analytics for Assessing the Public Health Consequences of Corporate Carbon Emissions
authors: Shalini. R, Anju Mohan, U.Marimuthu
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: The increasing contribution of corporate carbon emissions to climate change and public health degradation necessitates a robust, interdisciplinary framework for legal accountability and impact assessment. The study introduces an AI-augmented legal analytics system designed to evaluate the public health consequences of corporate carbon emissions by integrating environmental, legal, and epidemiological datasets. The proposed model employs spatio-temporal alignment of satellite-based pollutant concentration data (Sentinel-5P, GHGsat) with public health metrics (hospitalization, mortality, morbidity), and incorporates legal compliance data extracted from databases like LexisNexis and Westlaw. Natural language processing techniques—such as Named Entity Recognition and transformer-based models (Legal-BERT, CaseLaw-BART)— enable extraction of obligations, violations, and litigation precedents. Graph Neural Networks and Double Machine Learning models are utilized to quantify the causal relationship between emissions and health outcomes, while a cross-domain knowledge graph links corporate entities, pollutants, legal norms, and medical conditions. Results show that the LSTM-Attention network achieved a predictive accuracy of 91.6% in estimating respiratory hospital admissions, while Bayesian Neural Networks reached 88.5% accuracy with uncertainty estimation. The Legal Environmental Non-Compliance Index (LENCI) accurately ranked corporations based on violation history and associated health impact. Knowledge graph inferences exposed latent legal-pollution-health associations, enhancing regulatory visibility. The AI-augmented pipeline offers a comprehensive, interpretable framework that facilitates policy-making, regulatory enforcement, and early health risk detection. By combining legal reasoning with machine learning and geospatial analytics, the study demonstrates a new frontier in environmental governance and public health protection.

title: A Metric for Evaluating Face Image Deep Learning Classification Models
authors: Fernando Quiroz, Vladimir Y. Mariano
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: This study proposes a multidimensional metric for evaluating deep learning models in face image classification, addressing long-standing limitations of accuracy-centered evaluation. While modern architectures such as convolutional neural networks and Vision Transformers demonstrate strong predictive performance, conventional metrics fail to capture deeper structural behaviors, including fairness, representational quality, interpretability, and computational viability. To address this gap, the proposed framework integrates six components: validation F 1 score, linear-probe embedding separability, fairness via skin-tone gap reduction, compute efficiency, anatomical interpretability, and embedding stability, yielding a holistic assessment of model performance. Applied across six benchmark architectures and four Baumann Skin Type tasks, the metric reveals consistent superiority of transformer-based models, which exhibit stronger fairness, stability, and interpretability compared to CNNs. Findings demonstrate that responsible facial model evaluation requires multidimensional criteria that move beyond accuracy to ensure equitable, transparent, and deployment-ready systems. This work contributes toward trustworthy and fairness-aware facial AI.

title: Light-ResKAN: A Parameter-Sharing Lightweight KAN With Gram Polynomials for Efficient SAR Image Recognition
authors: Pan Yi, Weijie Li, Xiaodong Chen, Jiehua Zhang, Li Liu, Yongxiang Liu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) image recognition is vital for disaster monitoring, military reconnaissance, and ocean observation. However, the large size of SAR images, a result of their unique imaging perspective, hinders the deployment of deep learning models on resource-constrained edge devices. Existing lightweight models often struggle to balance high-precision feature extraction with low computational requirements. The emerging Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (KAN) significantly enhances fitting capabilities by replacing fixed activation functions in traditional neural networks with learnable ones and theoretically requires only 2n+1 activation functions to approximate arbitrary functions, drastically reducing both parameter count and computational overhead. Inspired by KAN, we propose Light-ResKAN to achieve a better balance between high-precision feature extraction and low computational requirements. Firstly, Light-ResKAN fundamentally modifies ResNet by replacing convolutional layers with KAN convolutions, which allows for more intricate and adaptive feature extraction in SAR images. In addition, we leverage Gram Polynomials as activation functions, which are particularly well-suited for SAR data due to their ability to efficiently capture complex non-linear relationships. To further enhance computational efficiency, we innovatively employ a parameter-sharing strategy, where each convolutional kernel shares a set of parameters within the same channel, enabling each channel to learn unique features while significantly reducing the model's parameter count and Floating Point Operations (FLOPs). The model achieved a recognition accuracy of 99.09%, 93.01%, and 97.26% on the MSTAR, FUSAR-Ship, and SAR-ACD datasets, respectively. Experiments on MSTAR resized to 1024× 1024 show that compared to the traditional VGG16 model, our model achieves an 82.90- fold reduction in FLOPs and an 163.78- fold reduction in parameter count. In conclusion, this work establishes an efficient solution for SAR image recognition in edge computing scenarios.

title: Mapping Eucalyptus Plantations Age Using Phenology Knowledge-Aided Algorithms and GEE
authors: Hao Liu, Lifan Zhou, Pinghao Li, Kai Zhou, Kang Yang, Hanwen Yu, Wei Liu, Xiaolu Cheng
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Accurate and fast mapping Eucalyptus plantation age was vital for smart forestry management and economic development in Southern China. Traditional forest age detection approaches were focused on building regression relationships or allometric equations, limited by optical signal saturation. Current forest change detection approaches use long-term time series (LTSS) imagery to detect key forest disturbance and then generate age. However, Eucalyptus plantation suffered a short-rotation period and clear-cut phenology event dynamic, causing it a specific spectral trajectory. Hence, in this study, we proposed a novel Eucalyptus phenology knowledge-aided (EPKA) algorithm that considers specific phenology and detects the abrupt enhancement and rapid recovery of the integrated forest z-score (IFZ) index using Google Earth Engine (GEE). The spatial mapping of Eucalyptus plantation indicated that the EPKA algorithm achieved an overall accuracy (OA) of 93.33% by validation plots. The Eucalyptus age mapping indicated the spatial distribution of forest age, ranging from 0 to 13. Scatterplots between EPKA estimated and field surveyed disturbance year indicated that the EPKA achieved the accuracy of R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.96 and RMSE = 0.69 yr. The short-rotation pattern under different generations indicated that Generation 1 of Eucalyptus plantation was the dominant proportion in the study area. This study demonstrated the benefits of the EPKA algorithm for mapping Eucalyptus plantation age in combination with phenology knowledge and LTSS change detection, which provided a promising algorithm for sustainable forest monitoring.

title: Generative Surface: GenAI-Assisted Semantic Scaffolding for 3D Conceptual Design in Mixed Reality
authors: Yichen Chai, Qianyuan Zou, Zihang Liu, Yi Wang, Henry Been-Lirn Duh
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: The transition from conceptual sketch to 3D model poses a major challenge in engineering design. While Mixed Reality (MR) enables direct 3D creation, designers face two key challenges: the Initiation Challenge (lack of meaningful starting points) and the Expression Challenge (difficulty in precise mid-air sketching). We introduce Generative Surface, leveraging AI to translate natural language descriptions into interactive 3D surfaces that aid designers in 3D sketching, simultaneously addressing both challenges. Our system transforms the design workflow into a fluid “describe, generate, sketch, iterate” cycle. Comparative evaluation against primitive scaffolding and freehand mid-air sketching demonstrates that our approach effectively bridges the gap between designers' conceptual intent and 3D sketch expression in immersive environments.

title: Power Oscillation Suppression of Self-Synchronizing Voltage Source Inverter Based on Reactive Power Control Loop Shaping Method
authors: Youze Fu, Zili Wang, Yandong Chen, Yunfeng Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Self-synchronizing voltage source inverters (SSVSIs) are a promising technology for improving grid inertia and frequency stability. The virtual resistance introduced by SSVSI can suppress the synchronous frequency resonance (SFR), but it will inevitably deepen the coupling effect of the active power and reactive power control loop, which leads to power oscillation. To solve this contradiction, this paper establishes the small-signal model to analyze the influence mechanism of virtual resistance on SFR and power oscillation. Besides, based on the idea of eliminating the coupling effect of the reactive power control loop, a reactive power control loop shaping method is proposed in this paper. The proposed method can eliminate the negative damping characteristics of the reactive power control loop without affecting the suppression of SFR by the virtual resistance. Moreover, the proposed control method can enhance the damping of the system, which helps to suppress the active power oscillation when the system is disturbed. Finally, simulation and experimental results verify the correctness of the theoretical analysis and the validity of the control method.

title: Saliency-Guided Foveated Video Encoding for Low-Latency and Immersive Cloud VR
authors: Ze Wu, Ahmad Alhilal, Yuk Hang Tsui, Wen Jye Chai, Matti Siekkinen, Pan Hui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: In cloud virtual reality (VR), delivering high perceptual visual quality under constrained wireless bandwidth remains a pivotal challenge. Foveated video encoding (FVE) copes with this by human vision-driven quality allocation, reducing bandwidth demand while preserving high visual fidelity for immersive VR streaming. However, existing FVE methods are content-independent, relying exclusively on gaze position and predefined quality degradation profiles. Thus, they fall short in modeling the intricate mechanisms of human attention. This leads to suboptimal quality distribution for visual saliency stimuli, causing perceptible artifacts and degraded perceptual quality. In this paper, we introduce a novel AI-driven streaming framework that incorporates visual saliency cues, defined as the innate capacity of scene elements to attract attention, into the video encoding pipeline. To meet the stringent low-latency demands of immersive VR, we propose a lightweight deep neural network for saliency inference, reducing computational complexity (FLOPs) by 48× compared to prior models while maintaining comparable accuracy. We integrate our pipeline into an open-source cloud VR gaming platform and conduct comprehensive experiments. Evaluation results demonstrate that our approach enhances perceptual visual quality by 22.98% compared to SoTA systems. Our IRB-approved user study shows that the saliency-guided FVE pipeline achieves superior visual quality and spatial smoothness while significantly reducing noticeable artifacts introduced by gaze-exclusive FVE. The project source code is available at https://github.com/WuZemyp/SaliencyFov.

title: TransformKV: Optimizing Multi-Turn Conversational Services in LLMs via KV cache Transformation
authors: Jiahang Zhou, Zhiyuan Fang, Yusheng Qin, Wuhui Chen, Tao Zhang, Chuanfu Zhang, Zibin Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computers
abstract: Multi-turn conversational systems based on large language models are increasingly being integrated into web platforms and applied across a wide range of domains. However, these systems typically combine the userߣs current query with contextual information from previous interactions, resulting in continuously expanding input prompts. This leads to a significant increase in time-to-first-token (TTFT), causing intolerable delays in web response times. To address this issue, we introduce TransformKV, which maximizes the reuse of the KV cache from previous conversations rather than recomputing, thereby reducing TTFT latency. TransformKV first identifies the specific locations that require transformation to maximize the reuse of the KV cache with minimal operations. It then efficiently transforms the KV cache for a subset of tokens by recomputing only the KV cache that impact semantics. Additionally, TransformKV further reduces TTFT latency by performing only QKV computations in certain layers while skipping other computations that contribute less to overall performance. Experimental results demonstrate that in multi-turn conversation tasks, TransformKV can reduce inference latency by up to 30%, achieving up to a 1.8× improvement in performance compared to similar approaches. Notably, as the context window size increases, the performance gains become even more pronounced.

title: Interaction-Driven Edge Crisping for Underwater Salient Object Detection
authors: Zetian Mi, Shuaiyong Jiang, Yuanyuan Li, Guanxi Li, Jiqing Zhang, Huibing Wang, Xianping Fu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Underwater salient object detection (USOD) faces greater challenges than general scenes due to the edge blurring which is caused by light absorption and scattering in water. Existing methods employ unrefined edge feature to perform unidirectional guidance on saliency feature, resulting in the coarse edge of saliency map. To address this issue, we propose a novel interaction-driven edge crisping network (IDENet) for underwater salient object detection. IDENet facilitates the bidi-rectional modulation of inter-features and the self-refinement of intra-feature, generates crisp saliency map and edge map. In IDENet, the interaction-driven edge guidance module (IDEGM) is designed to utilize cross-feature interaction by leveraging their correlations, facilitating saliency feature’s awareness of edge information, mitigating the interference of non-salient objects in edge feature. To learn more accurate edge region of the salient object, the edge intersection-and-union loss function (EIUL) is introduced to restrict the intersection and union of predicted saliency maps and edge maps to prevent over-expansion or under-contraction. Experimental results on two latest underwater datasets demonstrate the superiority of the proposed method over the state-of-the-art models. The source code of our method will be made available at https://github.com/ UnderwaterVisionMZTdlmu/IDENet.

title: Location-Aware Fractional Numerology-use for Cross-Technology Interference Management in 4G–5G Coexistence
authors: Mujahid Ali Shah, Chiza M. Christophe, Salim M. Yahya, Ahmed Naeem, Hüseyin Arslan
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: The coexistence of 4G and 5G systems within shared spectrum bands has emerged as a practical pathway toward seamless evolution and efficient spectrum utilization. However, the coexistence of mixed numerologies, characterized by different subcarrier spacings and symbol durations, inherently breaks orthogonality and gives rise to inter-numerology interference (INI), which degrades throughput and spectral efficiency (SE). This paper presents a fractional numerology framework for cross-technology interference management between 4G and 5G. A comprehensive analytical model is developed to quantify the bidirectional INI considering pathloss, shadowing, and time-domain misalignment, capturing realistic coexistence conditions. Building upon this model, we introduce an adaptive numerology assignment strategy that partitions the 5G cell into inner and outer subregions. The outer region employs 4G-aligned numerologies to suppress cross-technology INI, while the inner region uses a flexible numerology assignment to better match the prevailing channel conditions. Extensive simulations demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior INI suppression and significantly improves both bit error rate (BER) and SE compared with conventional guard-band (GB) and windowing-based schemes, confirming its effectiveness for practical 4G–5G coexistence scenarios.

title: Bendable and Conformable 3D-Printed Dielectric Resonator Antenna Enabled by Structural Segmentation
authors: F. P. Chietera, L. Mescia, L. Catarinucci
publish: IEEE Journal on Flexible Electronics
abstract: The integration of flexible and conformable antennas is essential for devices that must operate on non-planar surfaces, particularly in avionics, automotive, and wearable systems where rigid solutions are often inadequate. This paper presents a novel bendable dielectric resonator antenna (DRA), designed for operating in the 2.4-GHz band for Wi-Fi/IoT applications. The key contribution lies in achieving mechanical adaptability through structural segmentation of the dielectric resonator, rather than employing intrinsically flexible dielectric materials, an approach not previously explored for DRAs. In combination with a 3D-printed flexible substrate, this method enables the antenna to preserve stable electromagnetic performance under deformation. Experimental validation confirms that the proposed design maintains a stable impedance bandwidth of approximately 28% across all tested bending configurations, with measured gain ranging from 1.82 dBi in convex bending to 4.13 dBi in concave bending. The complete design workflow, fabrication process, and measurement results demonstrate the feasibility of integrating mechanical flexibility into DRA systems through the use of additive manufacturing.

title: Leaderless Consensus Fuzzy Control For Uncertain Euler-Lagrange MASs With Digraphs
authors: Ruimei Zhang, Liang Liu, Deqiang Zeng, Ju H. Park, Hak-Keung Lam
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Euler-Lagrange multi-agent systems (MASs), which are typical IoT systems, have wide applications including autonomous underwater vehicles, mobile robots and robot manipulators. As a crucial aspect of Euler-Lagrange MASs, the consensus becomes a research priority. However, most of the existing works on consensus of Euler-Lagrange MASs are based on the assumption of linearized parameters, and the designed distributed event-triggered (ET) control protocols are only applicable to the leader-following consensus (LFC) of uncertain Euler-Lagrange MASs with undirected graphs. Hence, the Leaderless consensus (LLC) of uncertain Euler-Lagrange MASs with digraphs is studied in this paper. Based on fuzzy logic system (FLS), a fully distributed ET adaptive control protocol is designed, and new theoretical analysis results are proposed in Lemmas 3–5. By the proposed fully distributed ET fuzzy control protocol, the communication resources are effectively saved, and the LLC for uncertain Euler-Lagrange MASs without the assumption of linearized parameters is achieved. In the end, based on networked two-linked robot manipulators, simulations are given to show the validity of the proposed results. In comparison with the method of [28], our method with σi = 0.005 provides 263.48%, 245.87%, 237.15%, 210.46% and 210.72% less information exchange on manipulators 1-5, respectively.

title: Analyzing Feedback Mechanisms in Ai-Generated Mcqs: Insights Into Readability, Lexical Properties, and Levels of Challenge
authors: Antoun Yaacoub, Zainab Assaghir, Lionel Prevost, Jérôme Da-Rugna
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Computer, Software and Modeling (ICCSM)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI)-generated feedback in educational settings has garnered considerable attention due to its potential to enhance learning outcomes. However, a comprehensive understanding of the linguistic characteristics of AI-generated feedback, including readability, lexical richness, and adaptability across varying challenge levels, remains limited. This study delves into the linguistic and structural attributes of feedback generated by Google's Gemini 1.5-flash text model for computer science multiple-choice questions (MCQs). A dataset of over 1,200 MCQs was analyzed, considering three difficulty levels (easy, medium, hard) and three feedback tones (supportive, neutral, challenging). Key linguistic metrics, such as length, readability scores (Flesch-Kincaid Grade Level), vocabulary richness, and lexical density, were computed and examined. A fine-tuned RoBERTa-based multi-task learning (MTL) model was trained to predict these linguistic properties, achieving a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 2.0 for readability and 0.03 for vocabulary richness. The findings reveal significant interaction effects between feedback tone and question difficulty, demonstrating the dynamic adaptation of AI-generated feedback within diverse educational contexts. These insights contribute to the development of more personalized and effective AI-driven feedback mechanisms, highlighting the potential for improved learning outcomes while underscoring the importance of ethical considerations in their design and deployment.

title: Style Evolution Mechanism for Surreal Images Guided by Natural Language
authors: Tianhao Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Aiming at the problem of surreal image style evolution guided by natural language, this paper proposes an original semantic anchoring-diffusion co-evolution algorithm (SADCA). The algorithm extracts key semantics of natural language through a semantic parsing module, uses a semantic anchor mechanism to achieve accurate mapping of semantics and visual features, and combines an improved diffusion model to complete the evolution of surreal image style. Experimental simulation results show that on public datasets, the average CLIP matching score of the SADCA algorithm reaches 0.82, which is 7.9 % higher than DALL-E 2 and 12.3 % higher than Stable Diffusion; the average style similarity between the generated image and the training set image is 0.31, which is lower than the comparison algorithm; it takes an average of 2.3 seconds to generate a single image, which is comparable to Stable Diffusion and better than DALL-E 2. Ablation experiments verify the effectiveness of the semantic anchor and semantic feedback mechanism. Research shows that the SADCA algorithm can efficiently realize the style evolution of surreal images guided by natural language, and performs well in semantic consistency and style uniqueness.

title: Deep Learning Techniques for Differentiating Human-Written and AI-Generated Source Codes
authors: K.D.H. Apsara, R.A.H.M. Rupasingha, B.T.G.S. Kumara
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: The rapid growth of AI applications capable of writing or rewriting Java software has posed significant problems to teachers and software development divisions, as they now cannot tell whether the code was actually written by a human or generated by an AI. As a solution for this, the objective of the study is to come up with a robust classifier to recognize AI-generated code in Java. The data sample was balanced, comprising of twenty thousand samples of the GPTGCJ Java dataset, which included both human generated and AI generated programs. Following the preprocessing and preparation of the data, four feature extraction methods were used to represent various properties of the code. These were Abstract Syntax Tree features, token-based features, TF-IDF representations and code embeddings. All of the feature types were subsequently tested with three models: Artificial Neural Network (ANN), Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), and XGBoost. To guarantee sound and reliable results, all twelve models were tested using percentage split and k -fold crossvalidation. Among all combinations that were tried, the CNN model that used code embedding features demonstrated the best accuracy of 94.97 % and was the most balanced. The findings demonstrate the need to choose the appropriate feature extraction method and model in the analysis of AI-generated code. The suggested methodology is a practical and efficient way of detecting AI-generated Java code both in the academic and the professional world.

title: A Lightweight Frame-Based Wireless Intrusion Detection System for Resource-Constrained Networks
authors: Sudhakaran M, Sachin SB, R. Mohandas, R. Pandimeena, Vinodhini M, Pradhipa A
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Rapidly increasing attacks in local wireless networks, such as deauthentication floods, beacon spoofing, MAC spoofing, and Evil Twin access points, which lead to modification of 802.11 packet structures to disturb connectivity and steal user information. This research works on lightweight Wireless-Intrusion-Detection System (WIDS) that use Packet-sniffing, active network scanning to identify unusual wireless behaviors in real time. Our present work utilizes the network security analysis tools including Wireshark, Nmap, Tcpdump, Aircrack-ng, and Scapy are used to capture and analyze frame level information, which includes source MAC patterns, frame frequency, signal variations. Since the proposed WIDS is centered on the management and Control-Frames of IEEE 802.11 and not the upper layer protocols, the proposed WIDS is able to identify attacks in an early stage. Because of the light weight design and rules-based design, the proposed WIDS is able to have a lower computational overhead while keeping the accurate rate steady. Because of the listed variables above, the WIDS is able to fit in a variety of environments including small, IoT, and academic networks. The system detects wireless attacks and alerts the user before attack occurs, making it suitable for low cost IDS environments. The present study reports on a put forth of a light weight, frame level Wireless-Intrusion Detection System that which does not base itself on TCP/IP or machine learning models. We have reported that we are successful in the detection of deauthentication flooding, beacon spoofing, and MAC spoofing attacks which the system reports at an accuracy of over 97 % and a latency of less than 1 second in simulated environments.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Music Genre Classification via Tree-based Ensemble Learning Algorithms
authors: Chenghao Xu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Music Genre Classification (MGC) is a fundamental task in music information retrieval, aiming to automatically assign a genre label to an audio track based on its audio characteristics. This paper presents a comparative study on MGC using three Ensemble Learning Algorithms including Random Forests (RF), Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM) and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) by employing various segmental information of music. Based on the simulation experiments, the effectiveness and efficiency of Ensemble Learning Algorithms in classifying music genres using extracted various duration audio features of GTZAN dataset has been investigated and summarized. Simulation experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of RF (80.50% with 30 - sec MFCC, the highest among the three algorithms), the robustness of XGBoost (a 5.94% margin improvement higher than LightGBM from 3 - sec MFCC to 30 - sec MFCC), and the efficiency of LightGBM (73.11% with 3 - sec MFCC, higher than that of XGBoost) when deployed on the GTZAN dataset.

title: Privacy-preserving Recommendation based on Non-interactive Encrypted CNN
authors: Qin Yi, Ruiqi Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: To address the problems of frequent interaction and high communication overhead in existing privacy-preserving recommendation schemes, we propose a privacy-preserving sequential embedding recommendation based on a non-interactive encrypted one-dimension convolution neural network by using the Pegasus homomorphic ciphertext conversion framework. In our encrypted CNN scheme, the proposed homomorphic convolutional architecture employs complementary horizontal and vertical kernels to capture both local and global sequential patterns, reducing computational complexity from O(n<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) to O(n + d). Leveraging the Pegasus framework’s dynamic switching capability between CKKS and FHEW schemes, our solution optimally handles linear operations through SIMD-parallelized CKKS and nonlinear computations via FHEW-LUT combinations. Experimental results demonstrate substantially reduced communication overhead compared to conventional approaches without compromising recommendation quality.

title: Research on Positioning Method Based on Path Recognition in Visual Navigation of Mobile Robots
authors: Libo Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computing, Robotics and System Sciences (ICRSS)
abstract: In the autonomous navigation task of mobile robots, in order to solve the matching instability and trajectory drift caused by occlusion interference, illumination changes and path distortion, a path re-identification and positioning algorithm framework integrating deep visual semantics is constructed. Experimental results show that this method performs well in a variety of public datasets and self-built scenes. The matching accuracy rate reaches 91.7 %, and the mismatch rate is only 5.8 %; the path offset error is controlled within 0.44 meters, and the closed-loop detection success rate is as high as 87.3 %. The path continuity rate can still be maintained at 89.3 % in the case of occlusion and dynamic interference, and it has strong environmental adaptability and fault tolerance. This study can effectively improve the spatial recognition accuracy and temporal continuity of the visual navigation system, and provide theoretical and experimental support for the long-term autonomous operation of mobile robots in complex scenes.

title: AI Powered Underground Cable Fault Detection Using IoT and Machine Learning
authors: Murugesan K S, Kanishka R, Asvika J. T, Dhanasri K
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Underground Cables are becoming more common as utility companies enter the 21st century and begin to rethink how they distribute electricity. Today's underground cables have improved safety and reliability, so they'll protect utilities from environmental and mechanical risks. However, finding faults in underground cable systems is a challenge for many reasons on the insulation surrounding the conductor, the way cable was installed, and the effects of third parties on the cable through activities, such as digging. Therefore, this paper presents a cost-effective underground cable fault detection method using ESP32 Microcontrollers, voltage divider sensors, and intelligent algorithms to monitor voltage variation in real-time and to identify both open and short-circuit faults in cables. This system combines real-time monitoring of voltage data and identifying the location of failed conductors. Each fault discovered will be displayed on an LCD and sent wirelessly to the Blynk cloud using the IoT for real-time remote monitoring. Using machine learning algorithms, the type and severity of each fault are identified, which will allow utilities to implement predictive maintenance and decrease power outages with real time remote monitoring capability.

title: Research on inspection method of blade interior based on combination of machine vision and LiDAR
authors: Guangtao Xian, Tong Tong, Yi Gao, Liyu Chen, Yong Zhang, Jiawei Lan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Due to the structural concealment and complex morphology of internal defects in wind turbine blades, a single detection method is insufficient to fully characterize them. This paper proposes an internal defect inspection scheme for wind turbine blades based on the combination of machine vision and LiDAR. The scheme takes the JDF wind turbine blade endoscopic inspection robot as the carrier, integrating a high-definition camera and Hesai JT128 LiDAR. After enhancement preprocessing of visual data, the YOLOv8 algorithm is adopted for the location and recognition of targets such as whitening, lightning protection wires, and wrinkles. For LiDAR point cloud data, after statistical filtering, radius filtering denoising, and voxel grid downsampling, the PointNet++ network is used to realize 3D recognition and segmentation of three-dimensional defects and foreign objects. Experimental results demonstrate that the visual detection achieves a maximum mean Average Precision (mAP) of 98.9%, while the point cloud detection attains an overall F1-score of 96.5%. This scheme balances detection accuracy and efficiency, providing reliable technical support for the preventive inspection of internal defects in wind turbine blades.

title: Research on a High-altitude Curtain Wall Cleaning Robot with Multimodal Motion and Intelligent Perception
authors: Kaixu Wang, Tonghui Ren, Sidie Jia, Jingqi Ma, Shaohan Hu, Ze Jiang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: This paper proposes a high-altitude curtain wall cleaning robot with multimodal motion and intelligent perception. It integrates a three-fold bionic suction cup and a climbing track mechanism to achieve stable adsorption and obstacle-crossing capability in complex environments. Key components are optimized via ANSYS Workbench static simulation to ensure reliability. Optical and piezoelectric sensors enable real-time stain detection and adaptive cleaning adjustment. Experiments show the robot cleans vertical surfaces stably and efficiently, achieving 3–5 times the efficiency of manual cleaning with integrated safety mechanisms, demonstrating practical engineering value.

title: A Replacement Complementary Approach Using Monte Carlo and Greedy Algorithms for Dynamic Resource Allocation
authors: Haoyu Zhao, Chengzhi Yuan, Tianci Xue
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Technology, Communication Ecosystem and Management (ITCEM)
abstract: In this paper, we propose a novel ReplacementComplementary Optimization Algorithm designed to optimize resource allocation in dynamic environments, with a focus on agricultural crop yield optimization. By combining the global exploration capabilities of Monte Carlo methods with the rapid local convergence of Greedy algorithms, the proposed method efficiently balances exploration and exploitation. Through a dynamic switching mechanism and adaptive adjustment, the algorithm optimizes the use of resources such as water and fertilizer, while maximizing crop yield under varying environmental conditions. We evaluate the performance of the proposed algorithm using three agricultural datasets that capture weather, soil quality, and crop growth data. The results show that our algorithm consistently outperforms state-of-the-art online optimization algorithms, including Online Gradient Descent, Online Linear Programming, and Multiplicative Weights Update, in terms of resource efficiency, yield maximization, and cost-effectiveness. These findings highlight the algorithm's ability to dynamically adapt to changing conditions in real-time and provide a robust solution for agricultural optimization problems.

title: Research on Online Supervision of Power Construction Site Based on Ultra-Dense Network
authors: Junxiang Duan, Hangxiang Cui, Yan Zhang, Lihua Gong, Hao Nan-Feng, Jingchang Hao
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: Digital technologies are revolutionizing power engineering supervision through enhanced efficiency, while remote monitoring and BIM applications reduce the need for on-site inspections. Integrated with IoT, AI, and big data, online supervision achieves precise oversight. Ultra-Dense Network (UDN) technology provides high-bandwidth, low-latency connectivity for real-time monitoring through dense deployment of low-power devices. This study establishes a four-tier architecture: Perception Layer, Edge Processing Layer, Cloud Decision Layer, and Application Display Layer, encompassing real-time video transmission, smart device remote measurement, AI-driven risk alerts, automated task management, and multi-party collaboration platforms. UDN significantly improves supervision timeliness and reliability, paving the way for 6G and AI integration. This advancement drives digital transformation in the supervision sector, accelerates intelligent development, and elevates service quality and operational efficiency in power engineering supervision.

title: Enhanced Deep Learning-Based Channel Estimation for 5G NR System Using Lightweight U-Net Architecture
authors: Kamruzzaman Mahedi, Mostafa Zaman Chowdhury, Yeong Min Jang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Channel estimation is critical for reliable communication in 5 G New Radio (NR) systems, where increased bandwidth and mobility introduce significant challenges. Traditional methods like Least Squares (LS) and Minimum Mean Square Error (MMSE) face challenges with computational complexity and requirement of channel statistics, while recent deep learning approaches using cascaded networks suffer from error propagation and inefficiency. This paper presents a novel lightweight U-Net architecture that jointly performs super-resolution and denoising in a single stage, achieving superior performance with significantly reduced complexity. By treating the time-frequency channel response as a 2 D image, our approach employs a single-stage encoder-decoder network with skip connections for joint super-resolution and denoising. The proposed mixed-SNR training strategy enables a single model to operate across the entire SNR range (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0-30 ~\text{dB}$</tex>), eliminating the need for multiple model instances. Extensive simulations on 5G NR TDL channel models demonstrate that the proposed method achieves NMSE improvement over the state-of-the-art cascaded SRCNN+DnCNN approach, while reducing computational complexity, and inference time.

title: Preventing Dementia in Senior Citizens' Sleep Patterns Using Machine Learning
authors: T. Kavitha, Indujha N, Jayaseelan S, Jeyamirthula Pon
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Dementia is a progressive brain condition that seriously affects the health and independence of older adults. Catching it early is very important for managing the disease, slowing it down, and improving life for both patients and their caregivers. Traditional methods usually find dementia only after symptoms appear, based on clinical exams and cognitive tests. This study aims to change that by using machine learning to create a model that can predict who is at high risk of dementia, using behavioral and health data. The model was built using the XGBoost classifier and trained on data including age, education, Clinical Dementia Rating (CDR), executive function scores, processing speed, and medical history like diabetes and high blood pressure. After careful data preparation, the model reached an accuracy of 94.31 %. Tests like the confusion matrix, classification report, and feature importance showed the model is reliable. These results show how machine learning could help doctors spot dementia early, allowing for timely care and better planning for seniors at risk.

title: Seeing the Unseen: Geometry-Aware Test-Time Adaptation for LiDAR Segmentation
authors: Yewon Song, Soonmin Hwang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: LiDAR semantic segmentation suffers substantial degradation when deployed in unseen environments, where geometric distortions arise from both extrinsic misalignment and intrinsic sensor characteristics. We show that these overlooked factors significantly alter the representation of LiDAR measurements and are major contributors to real-world performance failures. Although domain adaptation has made progress in narrowing domain gaps, existing approaches still struggle when encountering entirely unseen scenes without labels. While testtime adaptation has recently emerged as a promising alternative to address label-free deployment in 2D vision, its application to 3D LiDAR perception remains underexplored, leaving current models vulnerable to distribution shifts. To address this gap we introduce two geometry-aware TTA modules tailored to LiDAR's properties, designed to compensate for one of these key variation sources. Our approach improves mIoU from 16.27 to 18.44 (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+13.34 \%$</tex>), demonstrating substantial gains in robustness under unseen sensor configurations and environments.

title: Ambiguity-Free DOA Estimation for Linear Arrays via Sparse Bayesian Phase Error Self-Calibration
authors: Zhexian Liu, Shuai Shao, Hongwei Liu, Tao Su
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: Array imperfections such as gain and phase errors significantly degrade the accuracy of direction-of-arrival (DOA) estimation, particularly in linear arrays where phase errors and DOA parameters are inherently coupled, leading to multi-solution ambiguity. To address this issue, this paper investigates the multi-solution characteristics between linear phase errors and DOA estimation and proposes a self-calibration method based on statistical prior modeling of phase errors. By introducing a prior constraint to describe the statistical characteristics of phase inconsistencies, the proposed approach effectively restricts the solution space and resolves the ambiguity between phase errors and DOA estimation. Furthermore, a joint gain–phase error correction framework is developed within a sparse Bayesian learning paradigm to improve calibration accuracy. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm achieves superior DOA estimation performance and robust self-calibration compared with existing methods, particularly under low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) and limited snapshot conditions. These findings confirm the effectiveness and practical applicability of the proposed approach for high-precision linear array signal processing.

title: An Interpretable Machine Learning Approach for Heart Disease Detection Using Explainable AI Techniques
authors: Babby E, K. Rajakumari, Karthik V
publish: 2025 15th International Conference on Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Computer Science and Statistics (MACS)
abstract: Considering the shortcomings in ongoing monitoring of patients and diagnostic precision in conventional healthcare systems, early diagnosis and mortality rate reduction depend heavily on accurate heart disease prediction. In order to diagnose cardiac illness early, this study investigates the application of machine learning methods, particularly XGBoost and Naive Bayes. In order to determine which features were most pertinent for prediction, three distinct feature subsets (SF-1, SF-2, and SF-3) were chosen using chisquare, ANOVA, and mutual information techniques. Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique, or SMOTE, was used in preprocessing to address data imbalance. In addition to a variety of cross-validation techniques, both public and private datasets were used to assess the algorithms' performance. According to experimental data, the SF-2 feature subset in conjunction with the XGBoost algorithm produced the best results, with 99.57% accuracy, 96.61% sensitivity, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 0. 4 8 \%}$</tex> specificity, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5. 0 0 \%}$</tex> precision, a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 2. 6 8 \%}$</tex> F1-score, and a 98% AUC. XGBoost turned out to be the best model for predicting heart disease, while Naive Bayes performed quite moderately. We interpreted feature importance and model behavior using SHAP to provide transparency in model decisions.

title: A Venous Vessel Blood Flow Detection System Based on STM32
authors: Kunquan Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: In this era, medical technology is constantly advancing and improving. Against this backdrop, precise monitoring of venous blood rheology has become increasingly crucial for disease diagnosis, evaluation of treatment effects, and monitoring of conditions. Traditional manual detection methods have many problems. They require a lot of time and effort. To provide continuous and real-time monitoring that is indispensable for the accuracy of modern medical care, it is currently impossible to achieve. This article will introduce a system to you. This system is used for real-time and highprecision venous blood flow monitoring. It adopts a microcontroller-based system. It integrates photodiodes, organic light-emitting diode (OLED) displays and lighting modules. This system integrates hardware components for blood flow detection and display, as well as software components to ensure smooth operation of functions and data processing. After actual testing, it was found that the performance of this system is particularly good. The error rate of light control and blood flow detection has always remained below 1 %, achieving the goal set at the very beginning of the design. This system enhances the accuracy of blood flow monitoring and improves the effectiveness of real-time monitoring, thereby increasing its reliability in medical applications. The design of this system has been optimized, and advancements in both hardware and software are beneficial to its stability, portability, and antiinterference capabilities. This provides some valuable perspectives and ideas for the application and research of medical electronic technology in the future.

title: Evaluating the Temporal Generalization of TGAT for Link Prediction on Dynamic Wikipedia Graph
authors: Shaochen Cui, Shida Yan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Temporal link prediction in dynamic heterogeneous graphs plays a vital role in modeling evolving real-world systems such as web navigation and social networks. However, existing methods—especially static GNNs—struggle to capture temporal dependencies and adapt to structural changes over time, motivating the need to evaluate time-aware models like TGAT in realistic settings. This study applies TGAT to a series of monthly Wikipedia clickstream snapshots, using negative sampling and temporal encoding to construct a supervised link prediction pipeline. Experimental results show that TGAT achieves strong predictive performance in early snapshots (e.g., AUC > 0.90), but its effectiveness degrades significantly in later periods due to concept drift and label sparsity. Our findings provide empirical evidence on both the strengths and limitations of TGAT, offering valuable insights into the robustness of temporal GNNs in real-world dynamic environments.

title: A Novel Non-Isolated Semi-Quadratic Boost Converter and It's Control
authors: Akash Roy Choudhury, Padmagirisan Paramasivam, Piyush Kant, Anindita Jamatia
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: The renewable energy integration, electric vehicle-ecosystem, power converters for heavy machinery, etc. are applications which call for need of DC-DC converters having higher voltage conversion ratios with less bulky structure i.e. less number of switches as well as passive elements. The desired high voltage gain with limited number of circuit elements can be obtained by a semi-quadratic boost converter. This work proposes a novel three-switch semi-quadratic boost converter topology to yield high voltage gain. The circuit operation, modeling and voltage regulation strategy are presented for the proposed converter. Voltage regulation using current programmed mode control is performed and its performance parameters are analyzed. Simulation results are presented for validation.

title: A Deep Learning Framework for Renal Disease Classification with Real-Time Web Deployment
authors: Rajendran V G, Radhakrishnan S, Baskaran S, Thalaimalaichamy M
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: RenalSense is an online tool that facilitates quick and early diagnosis and classification of renal disorders using deep learning models. Chronic kidney disease is on the rise across the globe due to lack of food and water intake. Chronic kidney disease (CKD) is becoming increasingly prevalent around the world due to inadequate food and water consumption in today's life. RenalSense uses VGG16 deep learning model to solve these issues. The ability of this model to read CT scans helps classify the health of the kidneys. Therefore, RenalSense assists in the early detection. It can help healthcare professionals and users as well. The model can distinguish stone, cyst, tumor and normal kidney scans from the Kaggle dataset Five different models used for classification and these are CNN, AlexNet, Inception V 3, ResNet50(Residual Network) and custom VGG16, all are Deep-learning based architecture. Among the various classifier models, the modified VGG16 model achieved maximum classification accuracy of 99.84%. The article explains how the AWS operation of RenalSense was designed enhanced and deployed for a secure scalable and reliable operational capability. A calibrated model, tested extensively, classifies healthy and diseased kidneys with 99.84% accuracy. The system available online allows uploading of CT images and gives an immediate report. This architecture is built on AWS components using Elastic Container Registry, Elastic Compute Cloud, and Identity and Access Management.

title: Technology Predictions 2026
authors: Christof Ebert, Izzat El Hajj, Eitan Frachtenberg, Albert Lysko, Dejan Milojicic, Roxana Saint Nom, Saurabh Sinha, Julio Toro
publish: Computer
abstract: IEEE Computer Society technology experts have unveiled 26 breakthrough technologies set to redefine industries and shape the future of our world for decades to come.

title: Lightweight CRC Verification Algorithm for IoT
authors: Yuyang Xu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: The goal of this research was to design a lightweight CRC verification algorithm that could balance good error detection with low resource use. This paper implements the algorithm in C, and it mainly uses three methods: polynomial optimization, table lookup compression, and block pipeline computing. For testing, a hybrid experimental environment was established, using the NS-3 network simulator for error modeling and Python tools for data analysis. The algorithm was also deployed and validated on real STM32 hardware. Finally, this paper evaluates its performance from four angles: error detection rate, computational time, memory footprint, and power consumption. The results show that the algorithm is very reliable. Even with a 1 % bit error rate, the error detection rate stayed at 100 %. It's also much faster: for 16-byte short messages, it was 66.9 % faster than traditional CRC16. For messages over 32 bytes, the speed-up was between 68 % and 69.1 %. On top of that, this solution cut Flash memory usage by 40 % and reduced dynamic power consumption by 20 %. It's also compatible with both ARM Cortex-M and RISC-V platforms. Overall, this algorithm provides a reliable solution that solves the old problem of traditional CRC, which couldn't balance high reliability with low overhead in these limited environments. It proved to be a great fit for the short-message applications (under 256 bytes) common in smart home and industrial IoT sensors. This work fills a gap by providing a practical, high-performance verification method that's genuinely useful for these low-power terminals.

title: Swin-WNet: Boundary-Aware Semantic Segmentation for Oral Squamous Cell Carcinoma
authors: Zhenfei Wang, Zhenyang Zhu, Kunio Yoshizawa, Masahiro Toyoura, Naoki Oishi, Xiaoyang Mao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyberworlds (CW）
abstract: Oral squamous cell carcinoma (OSCC) poses a significant threat to public health due to its severity and the laborintensive process of image analysis required by physicians. Intercellular bridges are bridge-like structures that connect adjacent cells and indicate the differentiation level of a cancer. Although intercellular bridges are known to disappear as differentiation decreases, pathologists and clinicians evaluate the presence of intercellular bridges to assess the degree of differentiation of cancer. While state-of-the-art (SOTA) deep learning methods, such as U-Net and its variants, perform well on uniform and clearly delineated objects (e.g., cell, lung, etc.), accurately segmenting intricate objects (e.g., intercellular bridge, retinal vessel) remains challenging due to their complex topologies, fine branches, and irregular morphological changes. This paper aims to propose a method that effectively utilize boundary information, particularly targeting intricate objects. Our approach, inspired by Swin-UNet, employs the Swin Transformer, comprising a feature encoder, two decoders (semantic decoder and boundary decoder) and an attention-guided fusion module to enhance the model's ability to segment intricate objects. By applying the constraints of the boundary decoder, the feature encoder's ability to encode structural information is enhanced without compromising semantic information extraction and representation. Furthermore, fusing the outputs of the boundary decoder and semantic decoder further strengthens the detail of structural information. To validate the generalizability of our method, we conducted comparative experiments on one private and one public dataset. The results demonstrate that our method outperforms SOTA methods.

title: SSFGraphRAG: Semantic-Structure Fusion Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Bingbing Liu, Mingjie Wang, Jun Wang, Bo Xu, Jianhou Gan, Xiaoyu Han
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) excel in natural language processing but suffer from hallucinations and outdated knowledge in knowledge-intensive tasks. Knowledge Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation (KG-RAG) incurporates structured knowledge to address these issues. However, existing methods fail to deeply integrate semantic information with graph structural features, leading to a “semantic-structural disconnect”. This paper proposes a Semantic-Structure Fusion Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation (SSFGraphRAG), which introduces a multi-head cross-attention mechanism and multi-scale feature fusion to achieve semantic-structure synergy. The method dynamically combines semantic embeddings with structural propagation via bidirectional diffusion and gating, enabling more precise retrieval. Experiments show SSFGraphRAG outperforms traditional methods in complex multi-hop reasoning tasks.

title: A File Backup Scheduling Method Based on Improved Dung Beetle Algorithm in Cloud-Edge-End Environment
authors: Wenjue Xu, Lei Kong, Chong Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: In the current era of pervasive computing, the seamless fusion of centralized cloud resources, peripheral edge nodes, and end-user devices has birthed a collaborative framework essential for distributed service delivery, the cloud-edge-end collaborative architecture has become a key infrastructure supporting modern distributed applications. The file backup and scheduling strategy in this architecture directly affects system reliability, response speed and resource utilization efficiency. Traditional backup scheduling methods are mostly based on static rules or heuristic strategies, which are difficult to adapt to the dynamic and heterogeneous cloud-edge-end environment, and have a tendency to be trapped in local optimum, resulting in high scheduling costs and large response delays. In the past few years, swarm intelligence algorithms have found wide application in complex optimization problems such as resource scheduling and path planning due to their good capabilities of global search and strong adaptability. As an emerging metaheuristic algorithm, the Dung Beetle Optimizer (DBO) takes inspiration from the biological life cycle of dung beetles and approach the mathematically models behaviors such as dung ball displacement, reproductive cycles, and searching for sustenance and other behaviors of dung beetles, showing excellent optimization performance. However, when dealing with high-dimensional and multi-constrained backup scheduling problems, DBO still exhibits drawbacks such as slow convergence rate and susceptibility to premature convergence. To this end, this paper develops a file backup scheduling method founded on the improved Dung Beetle Optimization Algorithm (IDBO). By introducing Logistic-Tent mapping initialization, golden sine strategy and spiral search strategy, the scheduling performance of the algorithm in the cloud-edge-end environment is significantly improved. Benchmark function simulation and cloud-edge-end scenario experiments demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms original DBO, Grey Wolf Optimization (GWO) and other algorithms with respect to convergence speed, optimization accuracy, average transmission time and load balancing degree, and effectively solves the problems of low efficiency and poor adaptability of file backup scheduling in the cloud-edge-end collaborative environment.

title: An Intelligent Energy-Efficient Computing Framework Using Machine Learning-Driven Optimization for Sustainable
authors: A. Surendar, M. Kavitha, Rajan. C
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: The fast emerging smart infrastructures within urban, industrial, and IoT-based settings have greatly contributed to the energy consumption at the computing infrastructures, and therefore, energy efficient operation has become a pressing concern of long-term sustainability. Traditional energy management strategies are usually founded on fixed or policy-based policies and cannot respond well to workload dynamics and workload-nonhomogeneous system properties. This paper will attempt to eliminate these weaknesses by introducing a smart energy-efficient computing system with a reinforcement learning approach to dynamic energy management toward sustainable smart systems. The proposed framework continuously monitors the state of the system, pressure of workload, and the use of resources and automatically adjusts the allocation of computational resources according to an energy saving learning strategy. A clearly spelled out rewards functionality that uses a direct measure of energy via the Joules and Watt-hours metrics appropriately direct the learning process to optimise the reducing of power use without sacrificing the system performance to an unacceptable degree. The strategy proposed can be tested using the effectiveness of various simulations in a realistic smart computing environment. The experimental findings indicate that the suggested reinforcement learning-based model can attain up to 34.4 % total energy saving relative to the traditional static and rule-based energy management systems. The findings verify that reinforcement learning can promote adaptive and sustainable optimization of energy and hence the proposed framework can be effectively applied to future generation applications of IoTenabled and intelligent smart systems.

title: Campus Rent: A Full-Stack Web Application for Item Renting in University Environments
authors: Prakhar Patel, Govind Dhakarey, Krishna Nand Pandey, Shelly Gupta, Mukesh Kumar Tripathi
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Campus Rent is a full-stack web platform that powers item rentals on university campuses in a peer-to-peer manner. It aims to replace fragmented owner-centrism with an access-based model, enabling students to rent electronics, tools, sports equipment, furniture, and more seamlessly on the portal. It combines a responsive frontend of React with a Node.js/Express- based backend and MongoDB as the database. Payment integration has been performed with the Razor pay gateway, which facilitates card-based, UPI-based, or cash-on-delivery-based transactions. The key functionalities of the site are user authentication; posting of items with categorization, real-time booking, and an admin dashboard for community moderation. It showed that rentals could be completed in under two minutes and handled hundreds of users at the same time with very little delay during testing. At the campus level, Campus Rent will advocate “rent-instead-of-owning” to ensure affordability and sustainability. In the future, further development could add functions such as blockchain contracts for convenience and trust-building.

title: Secure Your Words Before You Send: the KryptBoard Pre-Send Encryption Method
authors: Surya Pratap Singh Chauhan, Samyadeep Saha, Priyanka Biswas, Nirmalya Kar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: End-to-end encryption (E2E) has become standard for secure messaging, but its protection does not cover the moments when a message is being written or read. This leaves a significant security gap on devices where messages are composed and viewed. At these endpoints, input tools like browser-based forms or web mail clients handle all the text that is typed, and they are usually trusted without a second thought. However, these input methods can be weak points due to unclear data collection practices, security flaws, or malicious extensions. This means a user's private message, password, or financial detail is often unprotected long enough for malware, malicious scripts, or other web threats to spy on it. In this research, a generalized method for pre-send encryption is introduced, demonstrated through a proof-of-concept called KryptBoard. The core idea is a user-controlled “Encrypt Mode” that can be implemented across different platforms. It focuses on a web browser extension implementation, which uses the ChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption scheme to ensure messages are both private and authentic. The resulting ciphertext can be placed into any web input field, making it compatible with any web application and adding a “double-lock” when used with existing E2E protocols. By addressing a long-overlooked security weakness, it is shown that strong, user-controlled endpoint protection can be achieved without compromising usability

title: Design of Approximate Eight-Bit Array Multiplier for Low-Power Image Processing Applications
authors: Mausiki Kala, Sanyaee Das, Meghna Bose, Atanu Kundu, Sukumar Nandi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: This paper presents a novel energy-efficient approximate full adder circuit and its integration into an 8-bit array multiplier, optimized for low-power image blending and digital watermarking applications. The circuits are designed using transmission gate (TG) logic and are rigorously evaluated through Cadence Virtuoso simulations, utilizing a 180 nm process design kit (PDK). The authors systematically analyze key performance parameters, including transistor count, propagation delay, power consumption, and power-delay product (PDP). Using simplified logic structures and transmission gate-based implementations, the proposed designs achieve 67.89% and 26.76% PDP reductions for the approximate full adder and the corresponding 8-bit array multiplier, respectively, compared to accurate counterparts. These results demonstrate an effective trade-off between energy efficiency and computational accuracy, making the proposed architectures well-suited for energy-constrained edge devices and real-time multimedia systems where a limited range of inaccuracy is acceptable.

title: TexTKAN: Parameter-Efficient Text-Based Depression Detection Using Temporal Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks
authors: Gede Aditra Pradnyana, Wiwik Anggraeni, Mauridhi Hery Purnomo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Depression remains a significant mental health issue worldwide. One approach being explored to address this is using deep learning to automate depression detection from social media texts. Besides accuracy improvement, a primary challenge in this research area is the vast number of parameters required for deep learning, particularly in text classification tasks involving multiple sentences. Moreover, the issue of over-parameterization arises, resulting in prolonged training times and an increased risk of overfitting. To address these limitations, this study proposes TexTKAN, a modified Temporal Kolmogorov-Arnold Network inspired by the parameter efficiency of Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks. Experimental results indicate that TexTKAN achieves an accuracy of 0.816 and an F1-score of 0.802 using GloVe embeddings, outperforming conventional recurrent architectures, including RNN, LSTM, GRU, and BiLSTM, while requiring fewer trainable parameters. These results highlight that TexTKAN provides an effective trade-off between predictive performance and model efficiency, making it well-suited for resource-constrained and real-world depression detection applications. The findings further suggest that KAN-based architectures offer a promising direction for efficient text classification beyond depression detection.

title: Normalized WebsiteData: A Framework for Efficient, Portable, and Structured Website Representation
authors: Michael Zelensky, Aleksei Kolola
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Computer, Software and Modeling (ICCSM)
abstract: WebsiteData is a minimal, structured approach to building websites as portable data rather than code. It separates content, structure, and layout into simple, human-readable files-eliminating the need for complex build tools or server infrastructure. Compared to modern frameworks such as Nuxt.js or Astro, WebsiteData offers greater portability, transparency, and longevity. This paper presents its core concepts, key benefits, and suitable use cases for efficient, content-driven web publishing.

title: Data-driven Early Warning Method for Single-phase Grounding Fault in Distribution Networks
authors: Haoran Zhang, Jiandong Duan, Pengfei Yun, Zainan Li, Zhihua Zhang, Wanying Yan, Jinxuan Jiang
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: Early-stage fault signals in single-phase grounding faults are weak and difficult to identify. To further enhance the reliability of distribution network operation, this paper conducts a theoretical analysis of fault evolution processes and investigates fault waveform processing. It utilizes the intermittent “zero-rest” phenomenon exhibited in zero-sequence current waveforms as a characteristic of the evolution process and establishes a convolutional neural network early warning model for early fault warning. However, due to the limited availability of actual measured recording data, data augmentation techniques are introduced to process the samples. Finally, this paper built a 10 kV distribution network model to simulate the current waveforms of the fault evolution process, capacitor switching, feeder tripping, and permanent single-phase ground faults. A total of 2,478 simulated waveforms and measured waveforms were used to train and validate the model, which demonstrated high accuracy.

title: Multi View Mammogram Analysis with Segmented Breast Masses and Calcifications for BI-RADS Classification
authors: Didulangani Deepashika, Tirush Wickramasingha, Janindu Athukorala, Mithara Induwara, Uditha Wijewardhana
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Breast cancer is considered as the most prevalent cancer among women worldwide. Computer Aided Diagnosis (CAD) systems have the ability to obtain promising results in early detection of breast cancer using different imaging techniques. This study focuses on developing a framework to classify segmented mammograms with breast masses and calcifications into the Breast Imaging-Reporting and Data System (BI-RADS) categories using multi view mammogram analysis. The proposed method consists of a dual path fine tuned EfficientNetB3 architecture for multi view feature extraction connected to an artificial neural network as the classification head. The suggested approach was evaluated using the INBreast dataset and it achieved an accuracy of 0.88, and a precision of 0.85 which is competitive with the existing methods which only performs the classification to BI-RADS based on a single type of abnormality. According to the results obtained, it can be observed that the proposed multi view approach to classify breast masses and calcifications into BI-RADS categories has an improved accuracy compared to the baseline single view CNN model showing its potential to aid in the early detection of breast cancer accurately. Also, the proposed method can be considered more clinically valid based on the fact that it takes the presence of different types of abnormalities instead of solely considering breast masses or calcifications in mammograms for the classification.

title: Addressing Linguistic Barriers and Educational Gaps via Transformer based Cross Lingual Question Answering: Towards Inclusive Rural Education
authors: J. Jinu Sophia, Praveen Kumar S Student, Mohnish M
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: One major obstacle to inclusive education is the linguistic diversity that is mostly present in rural areas of India, where young people are primarily taught in their native tongue. Students in regional areas are at a disadvantage because most of the current digital and question-answering platforms were created for competent English speakers (e.g., Tamil firstlanguage speakers).
