By offering a multilingual solution, the proposed project “Cross-Lingual Question Answering via Transformers” seeks to lessen this barrier by allowing students to, for instance, “ask” a question in Tamil (or English) and receive an exact response in either language. Transformer-based models, such as MarianMT and BERT, will be used to construct this solution. These models have a number of capabilities, such as translation, context-specific response retrieval, and automatic majority language identification. The proposed research would improve inclusion, rights to access, and ultimately, the digital divide between urban and rural areas by providing a straightforward web user interface that enables the request for an answer to a query in-person or remotely, along with a voice option if necessary. In order to give young people nationwide fair access to digital literacy, it is also expected that a system such as this will eventually be scalable to incorporate all regional languages.

title: Trust-Enhanced Blockchain-Cloud Integration Framework for Secure and Scalable Data Management
authors: Bharath M B, Mamatha M, Ashwini S S, Kavitha G, Mala B A, Pooja Shree H R
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Blockchain integrated with cloud computing has become an important area for secure and transparent data management. Yet, existing consensus mechanisms such as PBFT and PoS often suffer from slow processing, scalability issues, and limited trust handling when the number of nodes grows. To overcome these constraints, this paper introduces a Lightweight Trust-Based Consensus (LTBC) framework designed to enhance dependability and resource efficiency in blockchain–cloud environments. The proposed system assigns dynamic trust ratings to every node after each consensus round and applies compact hashing with localized message exchange to cut down network delay and computation cost. Experimental studies reveal that LTBC achieves around 91% trust accuracy, improves scalability by nearly 30%, and reduces average transaction energy and CPU usage by about 28%. It also lowers the probability of security breaches to under 3% and minimizes storage load by nearly 18%. Hence, the LTBC framework provides a secure, energy-aware, and scalable approach suitable for real-time cloud–blockchain applications.

title: Research on the Deployment of Doctor Qualification Examination System Based on DELL Hard Disk Protection Suite
authors: Zhencan Li, Pengju Tang, Yanxia Gao, Dong Wang, Jinzhi Liang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computing, Robotics and System Sciences (ICRSS)
abstract: This paper proposes a dual-system integrated solution based on Dell hardware protection suites to address the deployment requirements of the National Physician Qualification Examination system. By innovatively leveraging the hardware protection features of Dell's disk protection kits, the study achieves secure isolation and rapid switching between the examination-specific system and daily teaching systems on a single computer. To resolve the challenge of batch deployment for examination software, an in-house developed “LAN-based Collaborative Control System” is introduced. This technology enables cross-terminal synchronous control by capturing real-time operational commands (including keyboard and mouse actions) from the master controller, transmitting them over the network, and accurately reconstructing these actions on client machines. Designed with a hierarchical architecture, the system enhances software installation efficiency by 93% while ensuring data security, enabling parallel deployment of all terminals within an entire network segment in a single operation. Practical applications demonstrate that the solution not only meets the stringent environmental requirements mandated by the National Medical Examination Center but also maintains the routine operation of teaching systems, showcasing significant potential for widespread adoption.

title: Distributed Privacy-Conscious Threat Analysis in Wireless Sensor Networks Using Federated Machine Learning
authors: K. Nithya, B.R.Tapas Bapu, R. Govindarajan, S. Manodhiya
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: This study introduces a framework that preserves privacy while allowing collaboration, utilizing Federated Learning (FL) to identify and categorize anomalies and jamming attacks in Cyber-Physical Systems (CPSs) that are supported by Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs). Conventional methods for detecting anomalies in CPSs often face issues like privacy violations, ineffective data collection, and delays in responding to attacks. These methods depend on centralized models that require the transfer of raw data, which puts sensitive information at risk and heightens the chances of cyber threats. The proposed framework utilizes Federated Learning to develop anomaly detection models across various distributed edge devices, ensuring data privacy while reducing communication burdens. By combining Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for extracting features with Long ShortTerm Memory (LSTM) networks for classifying sequential anomalies, the system promises high accuracy in detection and adaptability in model performance. Experimental findings indicate a 32% enhancement in detecting attacks along with a 28 % decrease in false positive rates in comparison to traditional centralized systems, thereby improving the security and dependability of CPSs in practical settings.

title: Choatic Map Based Image Steganography Using DNA Embedding
authors: Achismita Dey, Tamanna Saha, Priyanka Biswas, Nirmalya Kar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: The combination of digital methods and biological principles has created new avenues for safe communication in the rapidly changing field of data security. This study introduces a novel steganographic technique that embeds plaintext messages in digital image pixel points by encoding them into DNA sequences. Using predetermined encoding rules, the suggested method first converts textual data into a binary format and then into synthetic DNA sequences. To reduce perceptual distortion, these DNAencoded messages are then hidden inside the least significant bits (LSBs) of specific pixels image. Combining conventional image steganography with DNA-based encoding improves robustness and data capacity.

title: Deep Learning-Based Pareto Optimization Framework for LDMOS Transistors
authors: Shinwook Kim, Geon Kim, Dongyeong Kim, Suyeon Kim, Jewon Park, Ryun Kang, Sowon Kim, Chaehyuk Lim, Hyeona Seo, Jeonghyeon Yun, Juwon Lee, Hyerin Lee, Ujin Choi, Eojin Kim, Minwoo Jeong, Myoungjin Lee
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Laterally Diffused Metal-Oxide Semiconductor (LD-MOS) devices are crucial for high-power and high-frequency applications, but their optimization requires balancing complex trade-offs among structural parameters that determine breakdown voltage (BV), on-resistance (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R_{\text {on }}$</tex>), and gate-drain charge <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(Q_{\text{gd}}\right)$</tex>. Conventional single-objective approaches often fail to identify globally optimal solutions across competing performance metrics. This study presents a deep learning-based surrogate modeling framework integrated with multi-objective Pareto optimization for efficient LDMOS design. A ResNet-1D architecture trained on TCAD simulation data accurately predicts electrical characteristics from five key parameters (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$L_{1}, N_{1}, T, N_{\text {pwell }}$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N_{\text {Sub }}$</tex>), …, achieving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}>0.99$</tex> while reducing computational cost by up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$53.3 \times$</tex> compared with TCAD (Table I). SHAP analysis revealed that <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{B V}$</tex> is most sensitive to drift thickness (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$T$</tex>) through RESURF effects, while <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R_{\text {on }}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Q_{\text {gd }}$</tex> are primarily controlled by channel doping (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N_{1}$</tex>). Pareto optimization between BFOM (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{B V}^{2} / R_{\text {on }}$</tex>) and SFOM (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Q_{\text {gd }} \times R_{\text {on }}$</tex>) quantified the inherent trade-off between conduction efficiency and switching loss, enabling systematic identification of application-specific optimal designs. The proposed framework provides a computationally efficient and physically interpretable platform for LDMOS optimization, facilitating rapid design space exploration and goal-aware device engineering for power semiconductor applications.

title: Research of Artificial Intelligence Expert System for Distribution Line Live Working Based on Large Language Model
authors: Guangkai Yu, Ting Liu, Pan Tang, Delong Cai, Ziming Su, Yabei Fan
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: To address the limitations of model applications in the field of distribution line live working (DLLW), this paper proposes a knowledge graph-based method for constructing a DLLW knowledge base and a corresponding workflow framework. Drawing upon materials such as books, training manuals, technical standards, operation guides, and application cases related to DLLW, we have developed a knowledge graph that encompasses multiple semantic layers, including fundamental principles of DLLW, practical skills, technical standards, tools and equipment, as well as application cases. On a locally deployed Dify application development platform, we established an expert question-answering system workflow for DLLW. By integrating 2,000 pairs of professional Q&A data, we fine-tuned and validated a model based on Deepseek-R1:32b. The results demonstrate that the introduction of the knowledge graph significantly enhances the overall performance of the large language model, with an average improvement rate reaching 60%. The fine-tuned model exhibits markedly improved response capabilities in specialized domains. Furthermore, the agent architecture proposed in this paper ensures consistency and reliability of output even when using models with relatively fewer parameters.

title: On the Effects of Numeric Precision in a Modified Starfish Optimization Algorithm
authors: Amelia E. Pizarro, Cristobal E. Perez-Ramirez, Philip Vasquez-Iglesias
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: Nowadays, the choice of numeric precision is often overlooked due to the performance of modern desktop hardware. However, in low-performance systems this decision remains critical. This paper investigates the impact of using single precision (32-bit) versus double precision (64-bit) in the Starfish Optimization Algorithm (SFOA), complemented with a probabilistic Conditional Opposition Local-Based Learning (COLBL) scheme aimed at enhancing the exploitation phase. Experimental results on a standard benchmark set show that the contribution of this auxiliary scheme decreases under single precision, highlighting the sensitivity of the exploitation phase to numeric precision, a factor of practical relevance for implementing metaheuristic optimization in embedded systems and other low-performance hardware.

title: IoT Device Identification Method by Using DNS Queries at Initial Phase
authors: Haruki Ishimaru, Manato Fujimoto, Shingo Ata
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: In recent years, the Internet of Things (IoT), in which various devices are connected to networks, has become widespread and is bringing benefits to society. On the other hand, there is a risk of information leakage from IoT devices and unintended behavior of devices. In order to communicate securely, it is necessary to understand the devices, and device identification technology that identifies them based on their characteristics is required. Methods using traffic data from IoT devices have been proposed so far, but they do not communicate on a daily basis, which makes it time-consuming to acquire necessary data and causes the time required for identification to be unpredictable. This paper proposes a device identification method that does not require long-term data acquisition, focusing on DNS queries issued by IoT devices immediately after connecting to the network. When they connect to a network, their initial operation involves communication with a cloud server, and the target domains are characteristic of each device. We identify devices using traffic data within a short period after connection and measure the accuracy of this identification. As a result of the identification, we demonstrate that an identification accuracy of 99% at the vendor level and 87% at the device name level can be achieved using data within 60 seconds after connection.

title: An Enhanced Sparrow Search Algorithm for Tuning Constrained MPC in Precision Actuators
authors: Chuanwen Duan, Jiayi Yang, Shangke Jiang, Peiwei Luo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Precision actuators achieve sub-micron positioning in ultra-precision machining, optical alignment, and micro-robotics, but strict constraints and uncertainties degrade tracking accuracy. Constrained model predictive control (MPC) effectively handles these limits and optimizes trajectories, yet its performance depends on manual parameter tuning, hindering practical application. This paper presents RDVASSA, an enhanced sparrow search algorithm for automatic tuning of constrained MPC in precision actuators. The method employs Tent chaotic mapping to boost population diversity, symmetric adaptive boundary shrinkage to accelerate convergence, and a dynamic composite fitness function incorporating time-decaying overshoot penalties and Monte Carlo variance weighting to improve robustness. Simulations on a piezoelectric actuator model demonstrate that RDVASSA-tuned MPC achieves an RMSE of 0.12 µm with 2.1% overshoot under nominal conditions, 0.18 µm with 3.5% overshoot under 9% Gaussian noise, and 0.15 µm with 2.8% overshoot under 20% damping disturbance. These results surpass HHO-MPC, TLBO-MPC, GWO-MPC, DE-MPC, and standard MPC in tracking precision, transient response, and disturbance rejection while strictly satisfying all constraints. RDVASSA thus provides a practical and efficient solution for high-precision real-time control.

title: AI-Based Fintech Recommendation System for Personalized Mutual Fund Investments
authors: Meenakshi Sharma, Mobin Anwar, Meera Sharma
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: In recent years, the vast number of options available in the area of mutual funds combined with the increased complexity of the financial services industry has resulted in difficulty for individual investors (in developing countries such as India) to make well-informed and personalized investment decisions. The authors propose an AIbased FinTech recommendation system to assist in providing individual investors with mutual fund recommendations that are based on their individual needs using machine learning, natural language processing and behavioural data analysis. The system's architecture will utilize real time financial information from users' profiles to provide adaptive and goal oriented mutual fund recommendations. In contrast to traditional rule-based systems used by many advisory firms, this system will utilize both supervised, unsupervised and reinforcement learning to provide users with dynamic risk assessment, goal based planning and continuous portfolio management. A comparative study of FinTech platforms globally and in India, as well as case studies, were also completed to demonstrate the benefits of utilizing AI to enable the level of personalization required in this industry including, but limited to, increased user interaction, better accuracy in making investment decisions and democratizing the ability to access professional financial advice.

title: MCC–RLS-Aided Hybrid MPC–DNN Control for a Bidirectional Buck–Boost Converter
authors: Jun Jiang, Zhiliang Liu, Kangning Sun, Wenyuan Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Bidirectional noninverting buck–boost (BiBB) converters are increasingly used in storage-coupled dc systems, where the controller must enforce tight voltage regulation and safety constraints at high switching frequencies and under non-Gaussian sensing disturbances. Hybrid schemes that couple model predictive control (MPC) with neural network (NN) surrogates have been proposed to reduce online optimization effort, but typical implementations rely on kernel or recursive least-squares identification and squared-error training, which remain sensitive to impulsive noise and parameter drift. This paper develops an MCC–RLS–aided hybrid MPC–DNN controller for a 200 kHz BiBB prototype. A maximum-correntropy (MCC)-based recursive least-squares estimator maintains an adaptive linear plant model, and the same MCC criterion is used as a robust loss for training a lightweight deep neural network (DNN) surrogate of the MPC law, yielding a unified non-Gaussian design across identification and learning. The hybrid supervisor activates the MPC layer during transients and abnormal conditions, and hands control to the DNN in steady regimes to reduce average computation. Experiments on an STM32F334-based BiBB platform compare the proposed controller against a carefully tuned PID loop and a standard hybrid MPC–DNN baseline under input- and load-step disturbances. Results show that the MCC–RLS–aided design preserves the nominal operating points, efficiency, and steady-state ripple of the baseline while significantly attenuating spike-like voltage excursions, all within a 5 µs-per-sample timing budget.

title: Trust Based Selection of Cluster and Sector Heads in Integrated 6G VANET Networks
authors: U. S. Premarathne
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Non-terrestrial networks assist to expand and sustain seamless connectivity for intelligent transport systems (ITSs) within the 6G network architecture in dense urban, rural as well as in areas with challenging terrain conditions. Cluster-based approach is adopted in VANETs for efficient communications within the ITSs. Conventional VANETs rely solely on the terrestrial communication networks. However, the traditional physical node selection criteria becomes less feasible when the RSUs are not reacheable due to unreliable terrestrial network coverage. In such scenarios, non-terrestrial networks can provide connectivity to maintain seamless communications. To ensure more secure and efficient communications among the vehicles with the support of non-terrestrial networks sectors are introduced in addition to the conventional clusters. This paper proposes a trust based ranking method to compute the sector heads and cluster heads in a VANET. Based on the computation results it is evident that under high density and mobility variation scenarios the proposed method performs better compared to mobility-based clustering mechanisms.

title: Multi-Modal Supervised 3D Reconstruction Based on Multi-Resolution Neural SDF
authors: Huimin Liu, Yanna Yuan, Bo Li
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: Aiming at the challenges in existing 3D reconstruction technologies, such as background interference, difficulty in multi-modal data fusion, and sparse viewpoints with single texture, this paper proposes a multi-modal supervised 3D reconstruction method based on the multi-resolution Neural Signed Distance Function (SDF). Firstly, the SAM2 model is adopted to achieve accurate segmentation of the reconstruction target, effectively suppressing background interference. Secondly, a neural SDF representation based on multi-resolution hash encoding is constructed to realize deep fusion of RGB and RGB-D multi-modal data. Finally, by jointly optimizing color loss, gradient loss, curvature loss, and depth loss, the geometric accuracy and surface smoothness of the 3D model are improved. Experimental results show that this method can generate high-fidelity 3D geometric models and output photo-realistic rendered images, which have important application value in fields such as virtual reality, industrial design, and cultural heritage protection.

title: A Middleware-Based Architecture for Service Request and Work Order Management in Enterprise Asset Management Systems
authors: B. Lalithadevi, B. Vignesh Sai Raghav
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Enterprise Asset Management (EAM) software helps organizations manage physical assets, maintenance processes, and service delivery. IBM Maximo is a popular EAM solution available in the market that encompasses service request and work order management. However, to meet the specific needs of organizations on a large scale requires heavy customization and integrations with third-party tools. In the proposed paper, we introduce a service-oriented architecture using middleware to enhance service request and work order management of IBM Maximo. The proposed framework allows data transfer, messaging, and building business flow logic, thus building a bridge between IBM Maximo and other applications such as IoT and AI/ML. The proposed framework comprises aspects that include automated workflow processing, dynamic request routing, standard API communication, and improved reporting and monitoring functionality. The proposed framework brings forth Maximo's business logic and makes processes less complex, faster, and more transparent. The experimental results obtained during the prototyping of this proposed framework produced suitable results in its operations, decreased risk in integration, and improved scalability, and thus is practical to apply in varying organizations that utilize IBM Maximo software as their enterprise resource management.

title: Safelora: A Real-Time Multimodal Multi-Agent Advisory System for Context-Aware and Safety-Compliant Plant Diseases Management
authors: Chamath Anjula Munasinghe, Banu Athuraliya
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Plant disease management requires timely, accurate, and safe decision-making, yet existing advisory systems often rely on static knowledge and single-agent reasoning, limiting their ability to adapt to real-time field conditions and prevent unsafe recommendations. To address this gap, Safelora presents a real-time multimodal multi-agent advisory system for context-aware and regulation-compliant plant disease management. The system integrates a CNN-based Image Agent for visual disease diagnosis, a Context Agent for iterative knowledge retrieval, a Safety Agent for regulatory validation, and a fine-tuned Large Language Model as the Generative Agent, while incorporating real-time factors such as local weather conditions and crop growth stage information. Experimental evaluation demonstrates strong disease recognition performance and high semantic quality of generated recommendations based on BERTScore, with system-level assessment using RAGAS showing high retrieval accuracy and factual consistency. These results indicate that the proposed system can support reliable, safe, and field-aware decision-making in real agricultural environments. By combining multimodal perception, iterative retrieval, and deterministic safety validation within a unified multi-agent framework, Safelora offers a distinct and practical approach to plant disease advisory generation beyond existing static and single-agent solutions.

title: A Survey on Deep Learning-Based Image Compression: Methods, Efficiency, and Open Challenges
authors: Ayalneh Bitew Wondmagegn, Ton That Tam Dinh, Thwe Thwe Win, Dongwook Won, Sungrae Cho
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: The rapid growth of image data in communication, storage, and multimedia applications has created an urgent demand for efficient and adaptive compression techniques. While conventional codecs rely on handcrafted transforms and fixed statistical models, deep learning-based image compression (DLIC) has emerged as a powerful alternative by jointly optimizing feature extraction, quantization, and entropy modeling in an end-to-end manner. This survey presents a structured overview of recent advances in DLIC, covering major architectural paradigms such as autoencoder-based models, hyperprior and autoregressive entropy models, attention- and transformer-based designs, as well as variable-rate and slimmable frameworks for adaptive bitrate and complexity control. Key challenges related to entropy modeling, computational efficiency, perception-distortion trade-offs, and practical deployment are discussed, along with a comparative analysis of representative methods and a brief review of standardization efforts and real-world deployment considerations. Finally, open research directions are outlined to guide the development of scalable and deployment-ready learned image compression systems.

title: CAUNet: Cross-Attention Unet for Image Steganography
authors: Bingxin Wei, Haewoon Nam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Image steganography is an important branch in the field of information hiding, aiming to covertly embed secret information into the cover image. Existing UNet-based steganographic methods demonstrate strong feature extraction capability, but the simple skip connection performed between the encoder and decoder often leads to low feature fusion efficiency and easily introduces redundant information. To solve this problem, this paper proposes a new network structure called CAUNet (Cross-Attention UNet) for image steganography. The core contribution lies in designing a lightweight cross-attention module and embedding it into the skip connection of UNet. This module dynamically learns the interdependence between encoder and decoder features to generate an attention map for weighting and refining the encoder features, thereby achieving more effective and targeted feature fusion. Experimental results show that, compared with the conventional UNet-based steganography model, CAUNet significantly improves the visual quality of the reconstructed stego images, demonstrating that the proposed cross-attention mechanism effectively enhances the performance of steganographic networks.

title: Dynamic Harmonic Analysis and Adaptive Active Filter Control for Power Electronic Systems with Load Uncertainty
authors: Liping Liu
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: To address the problems of multi-source harmonic coupling, time-varying propagation, and performance degradation of traditional control methods under dynamic conditions caused by load uncertainty in power electronic systems, this study proposes a comprehensive solution. This research presents a unified theoretical and control framework integrating dynamic harmonic modeling, load uncertainty propagation analysis, and parameter-adaptive active filter control. First, a dynamic superposition model of multi-source harmonics is constructed under the assumption of weak nonlinearity. Simultaneously, this study achieves dynamic compression and structure preservation of the harmonic time series through adaptive piecewise average approximation (Adaptive PAA). By introducing interval vectors to describe load parameter uncertainty, a harmonic propagation model based on interval arithmetic extension is established. This innovation can characterize the range evolution characteristics of harmonic amplitude and spectrum under uncertain parameter disturbances. Then, a fusion distance metric is constructed by combining dynamic time warping (DTW) and morphological distance to improve the accuracy of harmonic time series matching and model prediction. At the control level, this study designs a parameter-adaptive generalized proportional resonance (GPR) active filter control strategy. This strategy can achieve accurate multi-frequency compensation for the multi-order harmonics and online self-tuning for load abrupt changes. On an experimental platform based on a 380 V, 20 kVA three-phase four-wire low-voltage distribution system, comparative verification was conducted under various operating conditions, including steady state, multi-source harmonic superposition, load abrupt changes, and random parameter disturbances. The findings demonstrate that the proposed strategy lowers the current's total harmonic distortion (THD) from roughly 24% without correction to less than 2.1 % under typical rectifierinductive load settings. The suppression impact is enhanced by roughly 55 % and 31 %, respectively, as compared to PI-type and fixed-parameter PR control techniques. The steady-state error is lowered to less than 1.6 % and the system current recovery time is decreased to within 0.8 fundamental cycles with an abrupt change in load equivalent impedance of about 32%. Compared to conventional approaches, the THD fluctuation amplitude is reduced by about 40% under ± 18% random load parameter fluctuations. Regarding the compensation accuracy for higher harmonics (11th and above), the suppression rate remains above 92%.

title: An AI-Driven Adaptive Interview Simulator Using Transformer-Based Semantic Analysis
authors: R. Divya, S. Karthika, T. Praveena, M. Madhumitha, R. K. Priyadharshini
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Interview preparation and assessment systems currently suffer from several limitations that restrict effective skill evaluation. Existing solutions largely follow static interview structures, resulting in uniform question delivery irrespective of response quality or candidate performance variation. Such rigidity limits focused assessment, causes redundant questioning, and reduces engagement. Additionally, conventional systems often struggle with evaluating open-ended responses, as semantic depth, contextual relevance, and articulation quality remain inadequately assessed. Feedback mechanisms are typically generic and fail to provide actionable insights, thereby limiting meaningful performance improvement. Scalability and consistency further remain challenging due to dependence on manual or semi-automated evaluation processes. To address these difficulties, an AI-driven adaptive interview simulator is proposed to enable dynamic, performance-aware interview assessment. The methodology emphasizes semantic understanding of responses and real-time adaptability of interview flow. Response evaluation is performed at a contextual level, allowing accurate measurement of relevance, coherence, and conceptual clarity. Based on continuous performance assessment, interview progression is dynamically adjusted by modifying question difficulty and topic focus. This adaptive behavior ensures targeted evaluation, reduces unnecessary repetition, and improves efficiency. Key features of the proposed system include dynamic interview progression, semantic response evaluation, interpretable performance feedback, and scalable automated assessment. The framework supports multiple interview domains and accommodates diverse skill levels without manual intervention. Quantitative analysis demonstrates consistent improvement in response quality and assessment reliability compared to non-adaptive interview frameworks. The proposed approach enhances realism in interview simulation and provides an effective solution for intelligent, personalized, and scalable interview assessment in academic and professional environments.

title: Intelligent Pet Health Monitoring and Adoption Recommendation Platform
authors: Jeba Malar M, Jaffron Shalom Kennedy R, Harish M
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: In recent years, the demand for streamlined and datadriven. This paper proposes an Intelligent Pet Health Monitoring and Adoption Recommendation Platform to improve the welfare of companion animals by providing data-driven decision support. The proposed system incorporates a wearable smart collar that can capture vital signs such as temperature, heart rate, and activity level, which are then transmitted to the cloud for constant evaluation. A machine learning-based model predicts probable health abnormalities and allows for personalized recommendations on diet or care. An adoption recommendation engine will also evaluate compatibility based on behavior, age, and energy patterns between the prospective adopters and available pets. A real-time Power BI dashboard integrates owner and veterinarian views on real-time vitals, alerts, vaccination reminders, and adoption trends. Experimental results achieved 95% accuracy in prediction, with manual effort reduced in monitoring. The platform demonstrates strong potential for scalable, intelligent pet care management and improved outcomes in adoption. The main goals of this research are to make continuous real-time pet health monitoring possible, increase the effectiveness of early disease prediction through machine learning, and enable rational pet adoption decisions through intelligent recommendations. Experimental results show that the proposed system reaches 95 % health prediction accuracy, maintains endto- end latency below four seconds, and improves adoption compatibility outcomes through data- driven recommendations.

title: InfluesCO: Recommending the Right Sri Lankan YouTube Influencer for Your Marketing Needs
authors: Dineth Randula, Shanil Praveen, Sehan Dissanayake, Sandareka Wickramanayake
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Influencer marketing is rapidly expanding worldwide, yet data-driven and localised creator recommendation systems remain limited, particularly in regions such as Sri Lanka. This paper introduces InfluesCO, an analytical platform that recommends Sri Lankan YouTube content creators for brand collaborations using large-scale metadata, semantic representations, and multi-modal clustering. The system integrates channellevel and video-level features with multilingual embeddings to capture creator identity and content themes. A clustering pipeline groups creators into coherent content categories, enabling direct influencer recommendations and similar-creator discovery. Experimental results indicate that InfluesCO generates contextually relevant recommendations and well-structured creator clusters, demonstrating its effectiveness as a localised influencer analytics platform. A user study based on the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) demonstrated strong acceptance of the system among 25 participants, with all TAM hypotheses supported. These findings indicate that InfluesCO is both effective and useraligned, offering a practical decision-support tool for influencer selection in emerging digital markets.

title: Bridging Linguistic Gaps: Neural Machine Translation Between Hindi and Malayalam
authors: Vivek M R, Lekshmi Jyothish, Sana C Mohamed, Yadhu Krishnan T N, Priya Chandran
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Neural machine translation has seen significant advances through large-scale multilingual models, which have pushed the boundaries of translation quality across diverse language pairs. However, these models often prioritise high-resource languages and require substantial computational infrastructure, making them less accessible and sustainable for low-resource linguistic scenarios, such as Indian language translation. In particular, bilingual pairs such as Hindi-Malayalam have received limited attention in mainstream multilingual training regimes, despite their linguistic significance. Addressing these limitations, this article proposes a lightweight bilingual machine translation framework tailored for Hindi-Malayalam translation, using a transformer-based architecture. Employing subword-level tokenization and trained on carefully curated parallel corpora, the models prioritise semantic alignment and syntactic generalisation over brute-force scale. The work aims to fill the gap left by large models in underrepresented language pairs and demonstrate the feasibility of compact translation systems in such contexts. The work also includes creating a new test benchmark (MHM dataset) that contains parallel data for Hindi-Malayalam pairs, curated from textbooks to include sentences of varying complexity levels. Extensive evaluations across test datasets show that prototype models deliver competitive performance, particularly on COMET and chrF2++ metrics, where they rival results from significantly larger models. These findings accentuate the potential of efficient bilingual models to deliver high-quality translation in lowresource settings. Machine translation in diverse languages can significantly improve equitable access to educational and informational materials worldwide, mainly for linguistically marginalised communities. The work aligns with the goals of SDG 4, thus contributing towards a responsible and sustainable world.

title: Securing IoT Devices Using Federated Learning and Anomaly Detection for Cyber Threat Prevention
authors: Kavitha N, Shivakumar. E, Moorthy Agoramoorthy, K. V. Kanimozhi, Lavanya. A, K. Vijaya Bhaskar
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The growing adoption of Internet of Things (IoT) devices across industrial facilities, medical institutions, intelligent homes, and urban systems has proportionally increased the risk of cyberattacks and unauthorized access. Traditional, centrally controlled security systems often struggle to manage the substantial quantity, variable types, and sensitive nature of information produced by IoT networks, especially while striving to protect user privacy and maintain operational efficiency. This research presents an IoT security model based on federated learning. This model utilizes distributed machine learning, combined with real-time anomaly detection, to strengthen protection against digital threats. Individual IoT devices autonomously refine anomaly detection models utilizing data generated locally. Importantly, only model updates, rather than the original data, are transmitted to a central server. This enables the creation of a comprehensive threat detection model while preserving data confidentiality. The proposed system promotes data protection, swift threat detection, and adaptable learning within distributed IoT networks. Experimental simulations have confirmed the model's efficacy, demonstrating its ability to accurately identify atypical device behavior, reduce false positives, and significantly decrease communication demands compared to conventional, centralized learning methods. The suggested strategy offers a robust, privacy-centric, and adaptable solution for securing IoT environments. It facilitates proactive responses to evolving digital threats and enhances trust in critical IoT applications across medical, industrial, and smart city environments.

title: HistoCNN: A Light Weight Convolutional Neural Network for Automated Lung Cancer Detection Using Histopathological Images
authors: Rudrava Tripathi, Aniruddha Bera, Pradipta Chakrabarty, Sukanya Mitra, Sayanjit Singha Roy, Soumya Chatterjee
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: This article presents a novel method for automated detection of lung cancer using histopathological images. To this end, the histopathological images of both benign and lung adenocarcinoma are utilized from an online benchmark database. The acquired images are suitably preprocessed using contrast-limited adaptive histogram equalization (CLAHE) algorithm to enhance the resolution and image quality. The CLAHE-enhanced images are resized and fed to a customized lightweight convolutional neural network (CNN) model for classification of benign and adenocarcinoma histopathological images. Investigations revealed that the proposed CNN could classify adenocarcinoma and benign tissues with 99.81% accuracy. The fully customized proposed model is lightweight, and it is in need of less computational time compared to existing popular benchmark pre-trained models. The proposed CNN model is deployed to develop an interactive lung cancer detection software. The proposed method can guide the automated lung cancer detection method in real life with excellent accuracy.

title: An Efficient Method Calculating the Inter-Tap Short Circuit Faults in Converter Transformers
authors: Qidi Zhong, Yajun Lu, Zheng Zhao, Shaowei Wang, Shuai Wang
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: The on-load tap changer (OLTC) is an essential component for voltage regulation in converter transformers. In recent years, inter-tap short circuit faults in OLTC have attracted widespread attention. However, the lack of accurate calculation methods for converter transformer inter-tap short circuit fault in EMTP software has hindered the validation of relay protection systems and the commissioning of transformers in practical engineering applications. To address this issue, this paper develops an efficient calculation approach for calculating inter-tap faults within EMTP software. The inductance parameters between each regulating winding and both the grid-side and valve-side windings are derived from finite element (FE) calculations. Furthermore, the Mayr model is employed to determine the nonlinear arc resistance for incorporation into the fault model. Modeling and Analysis of inter-tap short circuit faults for a typical 415 MVA/ 447.4 kV converter transformer are presented in this paper. The results indicate that circulating currents under such faults can reach tens of kiloamperes. Finally, proposed method is compared with FE method, this approach achieves accuracy in EMTP software, significantly reducing computational costs for transformer fault analysis and relay protection device calibration.

title: Day Similarity Learning in EV Charging Demand Using Siamese Neural Networks for Improved Load Forecasting
authors: Rahul Sagwal, Abhinav Sharma, Janakarajan Ramkumar, Sri Niwas Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: Accurate forecasting of electric vehicle (EV) charging demand is crucial for electrical grid balancing, dynamic pricing, and infrastructure planning. The traditional forecasting approaches often oversight the hidden similarities of load data patterns and they treat each day independently. On the contrary, identification of these similarities in daily EV charging demand patterns that arise from weekday-weekend effects, seasonal similarities or special occasions, can lead to much accurate predictions. This paper proposes a novel framework based on Siamese Neural Network (SNN) for learning similarities between daily EV charging load profiles using real-world charging session data from Palo Alto. Daily level features were aggregated and transformed into embeddings through SNN trained with contrastive loss. Positive & negative day-pairs were formulated based on day type and load similarity to guide the training. Study demonstrates incorporation of SNN embeddings into forecasting models significantly improved forecasting performance, reducing mean absolute error (MAE) and root mean squared error (RMSE) compared to raw features alone. The proposed framework demonstrates effectiveness of day similarity learning by SNN for EV charging load forecasting, offering practical benefits for day-ahead grid forecasting, tariff design, and EV charging station scheduling.

title: Deep Learning-Based Nonlinearity Compensation for Ghost Target Suppression in PMCW Radar Systems
authors: Hyunbin Kim, Soyoon Park, Seongwook Lee
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: This study proposes a deep-learning-based restoration method that suppresses ghost targets generated by hardware nonlinearity in a phase-modulated continuous-wave (PMCW) radar system. In a PMCW radar system that uses <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$m$</tex>-sequences, nonlinearity produces cross-terms from signal components with different delays, and the cyclic-shift property of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$m$</tex>-sequence causes these cross-terms to appear as ghost targets in the range-Doppler (RD) map. To address this problem, we propose a restoration method based on a two-dimensional residual network architecture. The proposed method suppresses ghost targets in an RD map caused by hardware nonlinearity, so that the output RD map contains only the actual targets. The performance of the method is evaluated under various signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) levels, and the results show that the method provides consistent restoration performance. The peak signal-to-noise ratio and the signal-to-clutter ratio increase by approximately 14.9 dB and 2.5 dB, respectively, at an SNR of 10 dB. These improvements confirm that the proposed method effectively suppresses ghost targets.

title: Prompt Injection Detection via Key Feature Integration in Large Language Models
authors: Seyeon Won, Wooguil Pak
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: This study proposes Total-Layer Detection (TLD), a novel framework that integrally utilizes all layers of a Large Language Model to detect prompt injection attacks effectively. To address the computational inefficiency arising from high-dimensional data, we selectively utilized only the core features decisive for attack identification. Experiments demonstrated that TLD achieves higher accuracy and superior efficiency compared to single-layer approaches by efficiently reducing the feature dimensionality from the concatenated total-layer vector while enhancing detection precision.

title: Research on Photovoltaic Power Generation Power Prediction Based on LSTM-CNN
authors: Jiaqi Pan, Jiahui Chi, Siyu Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: With the development of photovoltaic power generation, predicting photovoltaic power generation capacity is crucial for market economic allocation. With regard to its bidding mechanism, constructing a reasonable power prediction model can effectively reduce the company's economic losses. Therefore, this paper proposes an LSTM-CNN prediction model based on Kriging interpolation combined with NWP information. First, this paper proposes a fusion model based on LSTM and CNN and conduct a preliminary evaluation of the prediction results. Second, this paper combines the LSTM-CNN model with the Kriging interpolation method and provide a scene segmentation scheme. Finally, this paper changes the input of the integrated model from general information to NWP information and verify its superiority in prediction accuracy compared to the previous two methods. Compared with the first two methods, the LSTM-CNN-Krigin model improved prediction accuracy by 80 % and 44.5 %, respectively. Given that the average annual photovoltaic power generation in Qinghai is approximately 30 billion kilowatt-hours, this model can reduce penalty costs by approximately 105 million yuan and 20 million yuan, respectively, and achieve carbon dioxide emission reductions of approximately 1.7 million tons and 330,000 tons, respectively. The experimental results prove that NWP spatial downscaling information can refine spatial resolution, obtaining more accurate meteorological data further improves prediction accuracy, thereby reducing economic losses for businesses to a certain extent.

title: Assessment of Phantom Energy in Digital Politeness: Sentiment Effects and Environmental Impact of Low-Information Messages
authors: Monji M. Zaidi, Imen R. Bouazzi
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Conversational artificial intelligence (AI) systems, such as ChatGPT, have become a dominant interface for human-machine interaction, handling billions of user queries each month. While effective, these systems also consume significant computational resources. This study identifies an overlooked source of inefficiency: trivial user interactions such as greetings or polite acknowledgments (”Hello,” “Hi,” “Thank you”). Although individually negligible, these low-information messages trigger full inference pipelines, causing measurable energy waste at scale. Using a conservative estimate of 0.5 Wh per trivial message and 100 million such messages per month, the annual energy loss is projected at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$600,000 \text{kWh}$</tex> which is equivalent to the consumption of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0}$</tex> U.S. households and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\sim} \mathbf{2 5 0}$</tex> metric tons of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex> emissions. The methodology incorporates energy breakdown models and sensitivity analysis of user behavior. Results demonstrate that even minor inefficiencies accumulate into substantial environmental burdens, highlighting the need for lightweight inference strategies and user awareness. The findings provide actionable insights for AI developers, policymakers, and end users toward reducing the hidden energy cost of digital politeness.

title: Development of a HIL-Real Time Simulator for Single-Phase Line-Commutated AC-DC Converters
authors: Jhordan Pila, Edgar Haro, Alberto Sánchez, Marcelo Pozo
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: This work details the design and implementation of a Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) real-time simulator for AC-DC converters, fully implemented on a Zynq-7000 System-on-Chip (SoC). Accurate converter models are implemented in the FPGA fabric, while the ARM-based Processing System runs a custom embedded Linux OS, created with Petalinux, to host a web server to allow for RT monitoring and configuration. This architecture allows users to remotely control simulation parameters and visualize real-time output waveforms via a standard web browser or through an oscilloscope. The performance of the system was validated against theoretical models and physical measurements, demonstrating accuracy and its effectiveness as a low-cost, accessible platform for power electronics education.

title: Depression Severity Prediction Among Higher Education Students Using Neural Network Model
authors: Kanchi Agrawal, Kavitha R, Dalvin Vinoth Kumar
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Depression significantly affects students' mental health and academic performance, highlighting the need for effective early detection methods. This study investigates machine learning approaches for automated classification of depression severity using responses from the Patient Health Questionnaire-9 (PHQ-9). Deep Neural Network (DNN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and hybrid models combining structured PHQ-9 scores with descriptive text responses were evaluated. The experimental results show that the LSTM model achieved the highest classification precision (90%), demonstrating its ability to capture sequential relationships between items in PHQ-9. The findings indicate that sequence-based models are well suited to assess the severity of depression in student populations. Integrating such predictive models into digital mental health screening systems may support the early identification of at-risk students and enable timely, data-driven interventions in academic settings.

title: Application of the Network Anomaly Behavior Detection Algorithm Based on LSTM-ATT
authors: Dishuai Wang, Yang Cao, Xudong Huo, Yixin Zhu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: To address the need for detecting high-dimensional network anomalies with strong temporal dependencies, this paper proposes a corresponding detection method and develops an engineering application process. The method utilizes temporal segmentation and windowed modeling, employing stacked LSTM to encode traffic feature sequences. An attention mechanism is used to adaptively weight key time steps, generating a discriminative contextual representation for multi-class classification and outputting attention weights to support interpretable analysis. Experiments using UNSW-NB15 as a public benchmark unify the input protocol and training strategy. The model demonstrates stable performance compared to baselines such as SVM, RF, 1D-CNN, LSTM, and lightweight Transformer. Furthermore, confusion matrix, embedding visualization, and ablation experiments confirm the model's effectiveness in inter-class separability and key component contributions. Based on the deployment architecture provided in the online detection pipeline, its practical application value is evaluated considering throughput, latency, and resource consumption. Robustness analysis shows that its performance exhibits a relatively smooth decline under noise interference and packet loss, making it suitable for continuous observation applications.

title: NeuroMedia-Sense: A CNN-Transformer Fusion Model for Evaluating Responsible Suicide Reporting and Its Influence on Public Perception
authors: C.Renuga, Anju Mohan, S.Thangamayan
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Suicide reporting in mainstream and digital media plays a critical role in shaping public attitudes and behavioral responses. Inappropriate reporting—marked by sensationalism, explicit method descriptions, or stigmatizing language—can lead to harmful emotional contagion or copycat incidents. This study introduces NeuroMedia-Sense, a novel hybrid deep learning framework that integrates Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Transformer-based models to assess the ethical compliance of suicide-related media content and its influence on public sentiment. The model fuses visual features from associated media with deep contextual embeddings from textual data and evaluates alignment with the World Health Organization (WHO) guidelines for responsible suicide reporting. This framework incorporates rule-based feature extraction, cross-modal attention fusion, and a BiGRU-Attention module for sentiment impact estimation based on audience reactions. Evaluated on a custom multi-source dataset containing annotated articles, posts, images, and public comments, NeuroMedia-Sense achieved an accuracy of 94.25%, precision of 92.80%, recall of 95.10%, and an F1-score of 93.94%, significantly outperforming single-modality baselines and existing models like VisualBERT, RoBERTa, and GRU-LSTM architectures. The model also demonstrated strong generalization across platforms including news articles, Reddit, YouTube transcripts, and Twitter posts. Furthermore, a quantitative sentiment correlation analysis revealed that higher compliance scores led to positive public reactions, while ethically non-compliant content triggered negative emotional responses. The research provides a powerful tool for media stakeholders, policy-makers, and researchers to ensure ethical reporting and mitigate suicide contagion risks. Future work will expand domain adaptation and deploy explainable AI modules for real-time editorial guidance.

title: Application of Information and Communication Technologies in Public Management
authors: Jose Proaño-Bernaola, Sandra Meza, Wilver Auccahuasi, Kitty Urbano, Percy Castro, Luis Peña-Romero
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Information and communication technologies are providing support in the perception of quality in services provided by state institutions, with a greater emphasis on public management. This article is developed from the perspective of the Peruvian reality, where there is a greater concentration of the population in coastal cities, with Lima being the main city with the largest share of the Peruvian population. Therefore, if we analyze the Peruvian reality, Lima presents deficiencies in Public Management, focused on the services provided to citizens. The problem is more concerning in other cities of the country. As a methodology, the technical considerations that must be taken into account to improve the perception of quality in the services provided to citizens are presented. As a result, we present a list of areas where technological solutions can be implemented to help manage public services for the benefit of the population.

title: DCFM: A Dynamic Cross-Modal Fusion Mechanism Based on Adaptive Semantic Gating for Visual Question Answering
authors: Jiahui Dong, Yonghua Zhu, Mingfu Luo, Haolu Yu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Visual Question Answering (VQA) represents a fundamental challenge in multimodal artificial intelligence, requiring a fine-grained understanding of both visual scenes and natural language queries. Existing approaches, particularly those relying on static attention mechanisms, often suffer from attention dispersion when dealing with complex visual backgrounds or fail to align multimodal features effectively in high-dimensional spaces. To address these persistent challenges, this paper proposes DCFM, a novel Transformer-based Dynamic Cross-Modal Fusion Mechanism. We introduce an Adaptive Semantic Gating (ASG) module that utilizes question embeddings to dynamically filter visual noise, ensuring that the model strictly focuses on question- relevant regions. Furthermore, we incorporate a fine-grained image-text contrastive loss to explicitly align visual and textual representations in a shared semantic space. Experimental results on the VQA v2.0 dataset demonstrate that our method achieves an overall accuracy of 77.85%, significantly outperforming state- of-the-art models such as BLIP and LXMERT. Extensive ablation studies and visualizations confirm the effectiveness of our proposed modules in enhancing reasoning capabilities.

title: A Framework for Detecting Plant Diseases and Nutritional Defects Based on Deep Learning
authors: Kumaraswamy Kankala, T. Thanigasalam, V. Ganesan
publish: 2025 15th International Conference on Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Computer Science and Statistics (MACS)
abstract: India's economy depends heavily on agriculture, which accounts for 18 % of the country's GDP. Incorporating technological innovations into the agricultural industry offers the potential to increase output while reducing labor, time, and money waste. Healthy plant development depends on nutrients, and any shortage can have a major effect on crop yield and plant growth. This study suggests an artificial neural network (ANN) model that uses leaf attributes to identify and categorize tomato plant nutrient shortages. Leaf color and form variations are essential for a precise diagnosis since they frequently indicate certain nutritional deficiencies. Farmers can maximize the delivery of nutrients by taking prompt corrective action if these shortfalls are detected early. The study also investigates how well various picture segmentation techniques—particularly hue-based and threshold-based approaches—affect the classification system's performance. The study also assesses the impact of various activation functions on the accuracy and efficiency of the neural network. According to experimental data, the suggested ANN model effectively and very precisely detects nutrient shortages, demonstrating its usefulness in assisting farmers in making well-informed decisions regarding long-term crop management.

title: Integrating Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Deep Learning, and IoT in Remote Patient Monitoring
authors: Hussana Johar, Mayur S, Darshan JE, Kushal Kumar, Karan P
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Stress plays a significant role in determining overall health, and prolonged exposure to stressors can negatively influence physiological stability and cognitive performance. Conventional stress evaluation techniques, such as self-reports and occasional clinical checkups, fail to provide continuous insight into rapidly changing physiological conditions. This highlights the need for automated and real-time monitoring approaches that can support early risk recognition and timely intervention.In this study, a multi-level AI framework is introduced to assess stress from multiple time perspectives using continuously acquired vital measurements, including heart rate, blood pressure, oxygen saturation (SpO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>), temperature, and respiration rate. The architecture consists of three coordinated analytical components: a Random Forest classifier for immediate stress categorization, a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural network for short-term stress variation interpretation, and a TimesNet-based model for predicting long-duration stress behavior. A simulated real-time data environment along with a visualization dashboard is developed to demonstrate seamless system operation and dynamic stress tracking.Performance evaluation confirms that the integrated system produces dependable results, especially in extended forecasting where TimesNet achieves up to 99% accuracy. By combining fast decision-making with temporal reasoning and future trend anticipation, the proposed approach lays the groundwork for intelligent stress management solutions suitable for wearable IoT healthcare applications and personalized monitoring scenarios.

title: Research on Dynamic Allocation Information System of Basic Education Resources
authors: Song Tang, Chuojie Zeng, Yixiang Liu, Fucheng Guo, Xiaoshan Cai
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computing, Robotics and System Sciences (ICRSS)
abstract: This system, developed with scientific resource allocation principles as its foundation and optimized resource allocation models as its core framework, utilizes government data and feedback from students and teachers. By integrating DeepSeek and other intelligent AI technologies, the system automatically generates analytical optimization solutions. Through practical evaluation, it develops scientifically sound resource allocation strategies to support local educational planning.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Adaptive Cruise Control in Connected Autonomous Vehicles
authors: H. Azath, K. Ganesan, R. Jagadeesh Kannan, N. Mohankumar, S Sujatha, Sathishkumar V E
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) has emerged as a viable methodology for the development of Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) systems in Connected Autonomous Vehicles (CAVs), providing improved flexibility and efficiency. This work investigates the use of DRL in ACC, highlighting the enhancement of speed regulation and the preservation of safe inter-vehicle spacing in fluctuating traffic conditions. A DRL agent, informed by synthesizing simulated and empirical data, can modulate throttle and brake responses to optimize safety, comfort, and fuel economy. The quantitative findings indicate the efficacy of the proposed method using DRL-based ACC system realizes a 15% decrease in fuel consumption relative to conventional rule-based ACC systems, concurrently achieving a 20% enhancement in tracking accuracy at target following distances. The device decreases sudden acceleration and deceleration occurrences by 30%, improving passenger comfort. In safety-critical situations, such as abrupt halts, the DRL-based ACC demonstrates a 25% quicker reaction time, significantly reducing accident hazards. These findings underscore the capability of DRL to develop more adaptable, efficient, and secure ACC systems, facilitating the development of more intelligent and dependable autonomous driving technologies.

title: A Tradeoff-Based Sizing Methodology for Low-Power ULV RF LNTAs Using LUT Approach
authors: Vanessa M. da F. Botinelly, Filipe F. Caetano, Osamu Saotome, Lucas Compassi-Severo
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: Due to the desire to further improve the energy efficiency and performance of ultra-low power (ULP) radio frequency (RF) circuits. In recent years, the growing demand for Internet of Things (IoT) devices has required efficient design methodologies. However, confronting the trade-off becomes difficult. Therefore, this paper proposes a systematic sizing methodology for low-noise transconductance amplifiers (LNTAs) based on normalized biasing metrics and pre-calculated lookup tables. This approach aims to optimize the inherent tradeoffs between gain, noise, linearity, and power consumption. The methodology was applied to design and compare six variations of a 5.8 GHz LNTA implemented in 65 nm CMOS technology. The comparative analysis demonstrates that the normalized biasing metric offers the best compromise, achieving a Figure of Merit (FoM) of 180.8 GHz with a power consumption of only 147.5 µW. This approach provides a structured design flow that allows designers to quickly identify an optimal operating point and thus accelerate the development of efficient RF front-ends for power-constrained IoT applications.

title: Contrastive Multimodal Graph Vision Transformers for Context-Aware Visual Representation and Relational Reasoning
authors: Vineetha Batchu, Nithin Reddy Kumbham, Raghavendra Reddy, Amulya Mallepalli, Venkata Gopi Siva Sai Nallapati
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Vision transformers have recently advanced fast vision recognition operations, yet have low capacity to capture relational dependency and contextual semantics which impedes higher level reasoning. In the quest to overcome these shortcomings, this paper introduces CMG-ViT (Contrastive Multimodal Graph Vision Transformer) - a single framework that integrates the transformer-based learning of global features, graph-based relational reasoning, and contrastive multimodal alignment to the context-based visual understanding. This is done by using the input images and encoding them into patch-level representations and constructing a scene graph whereby objects, properties and binary relationships are represented. The heterogeneous representations are then added with the assistance of a graphconscience self-attention model, and it is fed into the transformer with topological priors to become superior models of relational features. Two view contrastive learning is a form of learning that combines visual and graph representations to a shared latent representation to allow semantic consistency source. Moreover, topology is held together with a regularization term, which provides the structural smoothness, which guarantees the integrity of relational graph of the latent representation. Massive analyses on several benchmarks such as Visual Genome, GQA, RefCOCOg, and HICO-DET, show that CMG-ViT is much higher than the state-of-the-art baselines, with significant improvements in recall, accuracy, and mean average precision. The findings indicate that adding graph reasoning and contrastive alignment to transformer systems is effective and the future of multimodal vision systems with the ability to perform deep semantic perception, situational reasoning, and interpretable visual inference is within reach.

title: EEG Signal Analysis Using Machine Learning and Deep Learning for Brain-Controlled Smart Environments
authors: Divya K S, Akshaya Jayaraj, Amal Anish, Abhijith Gopi, Adithya K Shenoy, Aravind Balaji K J
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive survey and implementation analysis of Electroencephalography (EEG)-based Brain–Computer Interface (BCI) systems for assistive communication and smart home automation. The study reviews twenty state-of-the-art models addressing EEG-driven character recognition, imagined speech decoding, and device control. Conventional methods employing Support Vector Machines (SVM), k-Nearest Neighbors (k-NN), and Decision Trees have demonstrated accuracies up to 95%, while advanced deep learning architectures—such as CNN–LSTM hybrids, ensemble CNNs, and transformer-based frameworks—achieve superior performance exceeding 97%. The survey highlights key advancements in signal acquisition, spatial–temporal feature extraction, and real-time EEG-to-text translation using models like Random Forest, SVM, and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN). Furthermore, integration with Internet of Things (IoT) platforms via micro-controllers and MQTT protocols enables seamless control of home appliances, offering a hands-free, dignified interface for individuals with severe mobility impairments. Despite promising outcomes, challenges such as cross-subject variability, data scarcity, and real-time adaptability persist. The paper concludes that future research should focus on multimodal fusion, adaptive deep learning models, and lightweight embedded deployment to develop scalable, accessible, and user-centric BCI systems for enhanced quality of life.

title: Research on Algorithm Optimization of Intelligent Editing System for Improving Editing Efficiency
authors: Wen Sun
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Technology, Communication Ecosystem and Management (ITCEM)
abstract: The development of intelligent editing technology has provided a new direction for solving the problem of low efficiency in traditional text processing. Based on this, an intelligent editing system integrating deep learning and natural language processing technologies has been developed. The system incorporates intelligent correction, automatic layout, and content recommendation functions, and has been verified through rigorous experimental design to have significant advantages in improving editing efficiency. The data shows that the system has an accuracy rate of 95%, a processing speed increase of 68%, and a resource occupancy reduction of 46.7%. In user feedback, 89% of participants believed that the system effectively improved their work efficiency. This research achievement provides a new technological solution and practical reference for the field of intelligent editing.

title: Automated Detection of Web Application Vulnerabilities
authors: M. Dhilsath Fathima, N. Jithendra, N. Yasaswini
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Web applications are primary component of digital services but are increasingly susceptible to security threats, such as SQL Injection, Cross Site Scripting, and Cross Site Request Forgery. Manual vulnerability detection is a difficult task and vulnerable to errors, needing automation and scalable approaches. The proposed model develops an Automated Web Application Vulnerability Detection system, which uses web crawling, intelligent fuzzing, and Adaptive Random Forest (ARF) classifier to detect and prioritize vulnerabilities in real time. This model extracts features such as payload length, frequency of special character, HTTP response parameters, and response variability, and computes severity scores to guide response reduction. This system utilizes an input dataset has HTTP requests and responses, including benign and malicious payloads targeting OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities, and develops a web application, allowing user to view the output. Experimental evaluation on test dataset shows increased model performance over baseline machine learning models, achieving enhanced accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. The framework adapts to obfuscated and zero-day payloads, ensuring robust detection of evolving threats. By combining automation, feature-driven ARF classification, and severity- based reporting, the proposed model provides an efficient, and actionable solution for securing modern web applications.

title:
A
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    K Net: A Lightweight Knowledge Distillation Framework for Efficient Image Enhancement
authors: M. Padma Usha, G. Kannan, Akshara Nithyasri M, Ashrath Nisha A, Keerthana L
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: Image deblurring is a major challenge in computer vision. Motion blur, defocus, and camera shake lower image quality and obstruct important uses like surveillance, autonomous navigation, medical imaging, and consumer photography. This work introduces a framework for image sharpening based on knowledge distillation (KD). In this setup, a deep convolutional neural network (CNN) teacher model shares learned representations with a lightweight student network for efficient image restoration. The models are trained and evaluated on 1,100 pairs of blurred and sharp images from the GoPro dataset. This process helps the student network match the teacher’s performance while reducing computational demands. The student model uses a hybrid loss function made up of 70% pixel-wise reconstruction loss and 30% feature distillation loss. This approach ensures a balance between image quality and efficiency. Using PyTorch with GPU support shows positive results, achieving a high Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) and decreased inference time. This makes the system suitable for real-time use on mobile or embedded devices. Experimental visualization and PSNR analysis prove that the proposed method preserves image quality while using fewer resources. This highlights how knowledge distillation can be useful for practical image deblurring in the real world.

title: Anomaly Detection in Network Management Based on Artificial Intelligence: Algorithms and Applications
authors: Dongyan Xu, Zheng Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Effective anomaly detection is crucial to contemporary network management for effective protection against malicious activities. This research work aims at exploring the possibility of using artificial intelligence (AI) algorithms to detect network anomalies. The work focuses on the supervised, unsupervised, and semi-supervised techniques for detecting anomalies in vehicle traffic, delay, and packet loss. The various AI techniques covered in the paper include deep learning, clustering, and statistical modeling. To evaluate the effectiveness of the suggested methods, experiments were conducted with real and synthetic datasets and network settings. Detection efficiency, false positive rate, and computational complexity were used to assess the performance. The findings reveal that the AI-based algorithms are more efficient than the rule-based models in detecting anomalies with enhanced precision and lower delays. Also, the practical aspects of the proposed system are described, such as real-time control, prevention of equipment failure, and adaptive resource utilization. This research also identifies some limitations like the difficulty of the model's scalability and the interpretability of algorithms. The future research direction aims at combining AI with edge computing for distributed detection and improving the resilience to dynamic threat landscapes in the network.

title: Sliding Mode Variable Structure Control of Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor Speed Control System
authors: Xinyan Li, Rutong Zhang, Yi Zhang, Yuanjun Hou
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)
abstract: A new sliding mode control (SMC) permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) speed control system is proposed to solve the problems of disturbance, obvious overshooting and slow response. A new approach law (NRL) sliding mode velocity controller is designed. The concept of system state variable and terminal attractor is introduced to suppress sliding mode jitter and solve the serious problems of overshooting and slow convergence. The load torque extended sliding mode observer is used to compensate the observed value feedforward to deal with the load disturbance. The closed-loop stability is analyzed by Lyapunov stability function, and the effectiveness of the control strategy is verified by simulation.

title: Leader-Following Consensus of Multi-Agent Systems under Markovian Switching: An Event-Triggered Observer Approach
authors: Lizhou Qian, Xingyu Gu, Tao Dong
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: This paper investigates the observer-based consensus problem for leader-following nonlinear multi-agent systems (MASs) with Markovian switching topologies. An event-triggered observer framework is proposed to estimate agent states while reducing inter-agent communication frequency. Under this framework, data transmission occurs only when specific event-triggering conditions are met at switching instants. Based on the estimated states, a novel control protocol is designed.
