Sufficient conditions for mean-square consensus are derived using Lyapunov functionals and weak infinitesimal operations. Numerical simulations validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: RISC-V Area-Optimized ASIC Design with a Multi-Height 7nm FinFET Standard Cell Library
authors: Matheus Lemos, Clayton R. Farias, Paulo F. Butzen, José Rodrigo Azambuja
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: The continuous scaling of semiconductor technology drives the demand for integrated circuits with higher performance and smaller area. In this context, standard cell libraries play a key role in optimizing digital designs. This paper presents the ASIC implementation of the PicoRV32 RISC-V CPU using a 4.5-Tracks multi-height standard cell library tailored for 7nm FinFET technology. While multi-height libraries are established, the novelty of this work lies in the practical demonstration of a complete back-end implementation, including logic synthesis, placement, routing, and area optimization. The utilization of the proposed 4.5-Tracks library achieves a 29% reduction in overall core area compared to a conventional 6-Tracks library, with DFFR and XNOR2 cells contributing most to this reduction. These results highlight the effectiveness of multi-height cells in real-world designs and provide practical guidance for future area optimized RISC-V ASIC developments.

title: A Visual and Comprehensive Dataset of Indian Currencies: Denominations Ranging from One to Five Hundred Rupees
authors: Sushil Kumar, K Selvakumar, Sahil Khan, Nisha Dhakad, Vaibhav Gupta, Aditi Sharma
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: This paper provides a dataset on Indian currency notes that was gathered to support study in areas like as machine learning, security, and financial technology. Rs.1, Rs.2, Rs.5, Rs.10, Rs.20, Rs.50, Rs.100, Rs.200, and Rs.500 notes for both earlier and newer series of Indian money are included in the dataset. Every note has been categorized and meticulously scanned. We have recorded key information such as the note's design, serial number, and security features like watermarks, security threads, and small printed text. To construct this dataset, we collected, verified, and labelled each note in a methodical manner. Making it a trustworthy tool for recognizing various currencies, detecting counterfeit notes, and automating currency recognition is our primary goal. All of the actions we performed and the quality tests we employed to ensure data consistency are clearly outlined in this documentation. This paper is all about the dataset (Indian Currencies) was created to support researchers, data scientists, and developers working on projects involving Indian rupees.Additionally, the dataset provides a solid basis for novel concepts and developments in the fields of security and finance.

title: Design and Evaluation of a Trace-Driven Cache Simulator Supporting Set-Associative and V-Way Cache Architectures
authors: Saravanan Matheswaran, Posa Venkata Durga Vamsi, Myndhala Dhanush Chowdary, Udayakumar Allimuthu, C. Edwin Singh
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Modern processors increasingly suffer from cache due to conflict misses arising from the rigid placement constraints imposed. By conventional set-associative cache architectures. Although Advanced designs such as the V-Way cache improve place ment flexibility through decoupled tag-data storage and in increased effective associativity, practical tools for studying their Alternative behaviors are considerably limited in their application. This paper presents a Light weight, open-source, trace-driven cache simulator supporting both classical setassociative caches as well as the V-Way cache architecture. Tracedriven simulation is utilized to allow for deterministic, reproducible analysis of cache placement and replacement behavior without full-system complexity or cycle-accurate modeling. Unlike traditional set-local LRU Implement replacement; the V-Way cache uses reuse-based global victim selection with configurable Tag Duplication Ratio (TDR) to by reducing conflict misses and replacement pressures. The simulator offers detailed performance data, such as miss rates, WriteBacks, victim numbers, and victim distance analysis, enabling f refined architectural assessment. Experimental evaluation with “”. The traces of memory access of V-Way show There is a significant reduction in conflict misses and dirty eviction due to caching. compared with setassociative architectures, with further enhancements increases as TDR increases. The proposed simulator is a research-ready platform for cache architecture exploration and educational use. The results show that increased placement <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{f}$</tex> lexibility is beneficial to replacement efficiency as well as reducing conflict. misses. These observations validate the applicability of trace-driven simulation. process simulation for evaluating placement-flexible cache architectures.

title: Seatify: A Real-Time Restaurant/Cafe Occupancy Monitoring System Using CCTV Feed
authors: Sadeepa Ranasinghe, Vihanga Thalangedara, Amitha Isuru, Akarshani Amarasinghe, Randima Dinalankara
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Restaurants and cafes often face customer dissatisfaction due to the lack of real-time seat availability information, especially during peak hours or group visits. This study presents Seatify, a real-time cafe and restaurant occupancy monitoring system that uses existing CCTV footage and computer vision techniques to detect seat availability without additional sensors. Seatify detects occupied and available seats real-time, identifies and maps the dynamic seating layouts and then display the most up-to-date visual seating layout to customers in real-time via a web page. Additionally, hourly and weekly demand forecasting analytics are provided to cafe owners. Custom trained YOLOv8x object detection model and ByteTrack were used to track people, chairs and tables. Identifying pose of detected persons is done using YOLOv8x pose estimation model. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach offers accurate seat detection, adapts to dynamic layouts, and provides a cost-effective alternative to hardware-based systems. Finally, Seatify is a comprehensive, AI-powered seating management system that enhances customer experience and optimizes seating efficiency.

title: A Multimodal Adaptive Active Balancing Topology for Series-Connected Battery Packs
authors: Yang Chen, Zisheng Lian, Rui Cao, Zongfa Weng, Yinuo Xiao, Tao Zou
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: This paper presents a multimodal adaptive active equalization topology to improve battery balancing performance and extend the lifespan of energy storage systems. The proposed topology utilizes a single inductor as the energy storage component, enabling direct energy transfer between arbitrary cells in a battery pack under various imbalanced conditions, offering greater flexibility and faster balancing speeds. Then, an equivalent model of the topology is developed, and its balancing performance including power and efficiency is analyzed across different imbalanced scenarios. To further optimize the balancing process, a deep reinforcement learning-based is integrated, dynamically determining the optimal balancing path and adjusting the duty cycle in real time. Simulation results indicate that the proposed DQN-based strategy achieves the shortest balancing time among the compared methods under multiple operating conditions. Finally, closed-loop validation in a hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) environment on a four-cell SW18650-26HPA ternary lithium battery pack confirms the method’s effectiveness, demonstrating flexible cell switching and fast, efficient equalization.

title: Multisharp-Turn Path Following Method via Rotating Reference Frame Adaptive Law for Unmanned Surface Vehicle
authors: Chaofang Hu, Yiyi Xiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: In general, a rotating reference frame moving along the reference path is established in the unmanned surface vehicle (USV) path following method based on line-of-sight (LOS) guidance, and good following performance is achieved for the paths with small curvature. However, this method often results in significant deviations at multiple sharp turns due to underactuation. Accordingly, a new path following method via the rotating reference frame is proposed in this article. First, it is proven that there must exists an optimal rotating frame angle, by which USV can follow the reference path precisely. Second, a novel rotating reference frame adaptive law is designed to track this optimal angle, by which the frame angle rotation rate can be regulated for adapting to variation of path curvature and environmental disturbances. Stability analysis proves that the proposed method can follow multisharp-turn reference path in presence of disturbances. In addition, the desired constrained conditions determining the bounds of desired speed and reference path curvature under thruster saturation are developed. Path following can be achieved only if the desired speed and path curvature remain within these bounds. Finally, both simulation and experiment results validate effectiveness of the proposed method by comparing with the existing LOS guidance methods.

title: Superconducting Echo State Network for High-Speed SQUID Readout
authors: Beyza Zeynep Ucpinar, Sasan Razmkhah, Massoud Pedram
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: Echo State Networks (ESNs), a type of reservoir computing that utilizes a recurrent neural network with a sparsely connected hidden layer, enable efficient temporal data processing with minimal training requirements. Conventional CMOS-based ESNs are limited in terms of speed and energy efficiency, which motivates the development of alternative implementations. We present a superconducting ESN that features ultra-low power, high-speed operation, and the nonlinear dynamics of Josephson Junctions to reconstruct waveforms from a digital Superconducting Quantum Interference Device (SQUID) magnetometer. The superconducting reservoir exhibits rich, re current dynamics, providing short-term memory and nonlinear transformation capabilities suitable for temporal pattern recognition. Experiments show that the superconducting ESN achieves high accuracy in reconstructing SQUID magnetic waveforms at GHz frequencies, producing real-time voltage output. The intrinsic dynamics of superconductivity enable low-power, high speed processing, outperforming conventional readout methods, and allowing direct in situ integration with sensors. These results establish superconducting ESNs as a scalable, noise-tolerant, and energy-efficient platform for high-speed SQUID readout and neuromorphic processing in cryogenic quantum systems.

title: A Cryo-CMOS 4–5.9-GHz Fractional-N Cascaded PLL Achieving 36.9-fsrms Integrated Jitter and −69.1-dBc Fractional Spur
authors: Wenqiang Huang, Xuanyan Liu, Gangxu Gu, Tiefu Li, Wensong Wang, Yuanjin Zheng, Zhihua Wang, Yanshu Guo, Hanjun Jiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: This article presents a cryogenic fractional-N 4–5.9-GHz cascaded phase-locked loop (PLL) operating down to 4 K for a transmon control system. The main PLL stage employs a supply-boost constant charging current sampling phase detector (SBCC-SPD) to achieve ultralow rms jitter and fractional spur from 300 to 4 K. The first-stage PLL operates in integer-N mode employing an inverter-based sampling PD and a Class-F voltage-controlled oscillator (VCO) to achieve low output phase noise, with a frequency-tuning range of 11.8–15.9 GHz. The second-stage PLL operates in fractional-N mode using double-multimodule divider (MMD) with shared delta-sigma-modulator (DSM) architecture. It incorporates an SBCC-SPD and Class-B VCO, with a frequency-tuning range of 4–5.9 GHz for transmon control application. The reference signal of SBCC-SPD is from the divided signal of the first-stage PLL, which is modulated by the same DSM used in the feedback path of the second-stage PLL to compensate for the quantization error. Fabricated in a 28-nm Bulk CMOS process, the PLL achieves an rms jitter of 58.8 fs at 300 K and 36.9 fs at 4 K, with a fractional spur of −71.8 dBc at 300 K and −69.1 dBc at 4 K. The chip consumes a power consumption of 24.8 mW at 300 K and 14.2 mW at 4K corresponding to a figure of merit (FOM) of −257.1 dB. This chip occupies an area of 0.344 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>.

title: Adaptive Collision Detection via Impulse-Momentum Theorem for Safe Sensorless Physical Human-Robot Interaction_supp1-3678832.mp4
authors: Weiyang LIN
publish: N/A
abstract: Collision detection is critical for safe physical human-robot interaction (pHRI). The generalized momentum observer (GMO) is a prevalent sensorless method, estimating momentum deviations to detect collisions. However, its reliance on iterative solutions and static thresholds severely limits operational efficiency and dynamic adaptability, leading to reduced sensitivity and accuracy in complex collision scenarios. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes an impulse-based dynamic threshold generalized momentum observer (IDT-GMO)for adaptive sensorless collision detection. Key innovations include: (i) An impulse-based dynamic threshold mechanism, leveraging the impulse-momentum theorem, enabling adaptive collision detection with enhanced sensitivity and accuracy; (ii) A closed-form analytical solution, formulated through Lagrangian mechanics and Lie group theory, eliminating iterative computation and achieving 45% faster processing than conventional GMO. Experimental validation across soft contact, rigid impact, and multi-contact scenarios confirms that IDT-GMO achieves superior detection sensitivity and accuracy compared to existing methods. Thus, IDT-GMO exhibits significant potential for applications ranging from delicate contact tasks to collision-prone environments.

title: Joint Beamforming, RIS Configuration, and Antenna Positioning for Active RIS-Assisted ISAC with Movable Antenna Arrays
authors: Sudip Biswas, Keshav Singh, Ravi Patel, Cunhua Pan, Chih-Peng Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: We propose a novel integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) framework that combines active reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RIS) with a movable antenna (MA) array at the base station to jointly enhance the communication throughput and the radar sensing resolution. Unlike conventional architectures that employ passive RIS or fixed antenna arrays, the proposed framework leverages dual-domain reconfigurability, electromagnetic and geometric, by jointly optimizing transmit beamforming, RIS reflection coefficients with amplification constraints, and the spatial positions of the mobile antennas. The system is modeled under a practical RIS noise amplification model and subject to the constraints of stringent signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR), radar beampattern, and transmission power. A unified optimization problem is formulated and decomposed into tractable subproblems using an alternating optimization approach based on semidefinite relaxation (SDR), successive convex approximation (SCA), and convex programming. Numerical results confirm that the proposed design significantly outperforms conventional passive RIS and fixed array systems in terms of both radar and communication metrics, particularly under dynamic channel conditions and constrained power budgets.

title: Budget-Aware Edge-First Collaboration for Robust Inference in Sensor–Cloud Networks
authors: Malka N. Halgamuge, Ibrahim Khalil, Iqbal Gondal, Alireza Jolfaei, Narayan Srinivasa
publish: IEEE Network
abstract: Edge-enabled sensing applications in digital health, industrial Internet of Things (IIoT), and extended reality (XR) face a persistent trilemma: lightweight edge models meet strict latency, bandwidth, and energy budgets but degrade under noise and distribution shift; cloud models improve robustness and calibration but can violate tail-latency and privacy constraints; and naïve split inference reduces edge compute at the cost of inflated network usage and unstable tails. We present a deployable, edge-first blueprint for budget-aware collaborative training and co-inference between large cloud models and lightweight edge models in sensor–cloud networks. The design has three pillars: (i) a collaborative training workflow that transfers robustness and calibrated confidence from a cloud teacher to an edge student so most inference completes locally; (ii) a confidence-gated, budget-aware router that selects among EDGE, EDGE+CORRECTION, and CLOUD actions using calibrated uncertainty and short-horizon link telemetry while explicitly enforcing P95 latency, bandwidth, and energy budgets; and (iii) privacy scaffolding via trusted execution environments (and optional differential privacy), with all correction or offload overheads accounted end-to-end when invoked. Compact case studies on inertial and physiological sensing workloads demonstrate edge-dominant operation with tight latency tails, small and predictable bandwidth, bounded energy consumption, and improved robustness over non-collaborative baselines. We conclude with practical guidance on system design, scheduling, measurement, and deployment trade-offs for real-world sensor–cloud intelligence.

title: Towards a System-of-Systems Approach for Product, Factory and Spatial Planning: A Methodological Convergence Framework Based on a Systematic Literature Review
authors: Mehmet Demir, Matthias Schmidt
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In the face of turbulent market environments and shortening innovation cycles, the competitiveness of manufacturing companies increasingly depends on the synergetic planning of products, production processes, and spatial structures. However, traditional planning approaches remain largely isolated, resulting in inefficiencies, information gaps, and inconsistent decision-making known as ‘engineering over the wall’. This paper addresses these challenges by conducting a systematic literature review to evaluate the current state of research regarding isolated and integrated planning methodologies. The analysis reveals that while domain-specific methods are mature, a holistic framework effectively integrating product, factory, and spatial planning is lacking. To bridge this gap, this paper proposes a System-of-Systems approach that conceptualizes the three domains as autonomous yet tightly coupled planning systems. A methodological convergence framework is introduced to align these systems across three abstraction levels: objective setting and establishment of the project basis, concept planning, and detailed planning. Furthermore, the concept suggests employing formal integration artifacts, such as Interface Control Documents and a Common Data Environment, to systematically manage complex interdependencies and information flows. This comprehensive framework aims to enhance transparency, enable scenario-based decision-making, and support continuous adaptation throughout the planning processes.

title: Performance Characterization and Degradation Modeling of Series-Connected NCR18650GA Battery Modules for Electric Refuse Compactor Vehicles: EIS-Based Thermal Prediction, Weibull Reliability, and BMS Validation
authors: Tsung-Hsun Wu
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive characterization and degradation modeling framework for 12-series (12S) and 14-series (14S) NCR18650GA lithium-ion battery modules deployed in electric refuse compactor vehicle (e-RCV) applications. Electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) is employed to extract equivalent circuit parameters, which are subsequently used to derive heat generation rates and construct a lumped thermal model. The thermal model, validated against experimental discharge data, achieves root-mean-square errors (RMSE) of 1.3°C and 1.4°C for 12S and 14S modules respectively. Cycle life testing of seven individual cells, analyzed via Weibull reliability statistics with maximum likelihood estimation incorporating right-censored data, yields a shape parameter β = 12.7 and scale parameter η = 204 cycles, indicating a wear-out failure mode with low cell-to-cell variability. The B10 reliability life is determined to be 171 cycles under accelerated 1C/1C conditions. Open-circuit voltage (OCV) sensitivity analysis confirms that the NCA chemistry maintains dOCV/dSOC above 4.5 mV/%SOC across the full state-of-charge range, providing a favorable basis for model-based SOC estimation. Module-level discharge characteristics, including BMS state-of-charge accuracy and thermal behavior, are compared between the two configurations. The integrated characterization pipeline—from single-cell EIS to module-level thermal prediction and statistical reliability—provides actionable design metrics for battery management system development, module warranty assessment, and e-RCV fleet management.

title: The COMMON-WEARS Project: Towards Community Oriented Wearable Computing Systems
authors: Giancarlo Fortino, Mirko Viroli, Emiliano Schena, Ferruccio Damiani, Antonio Liotta
publish: IEEE Systems, Man, and Cybernetics Magazine
abstract: According to Kings Research [1] the global Wearable Market is projected to reach US$ 192.14 billion by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 15.8% from 2023 to 2030, with an increasing growth caused by the COVID-19. The industry and public sector are then pushing for innovative WCS solutions with high levels of dependability and trustworthiness that can efficiently operate in increasingly complex scenarios. Great strides have been made to realize WCS for the 24/7 monitoring of single users based on 3-tier architectures involving wearables, edge, and cloud systems. However, new requirements targeting cooperative multiuser scenarios demand radically new approaches, as promoted by the community-oriented WCS (CO-WCS). The COMMON-WEARS project is developing novel models and architectures for next-generation CO-WCS, defining a rigorous engineering methodology, with associated formal verification and simulation tools, to drive the development lifecycle of CO-WCS and creating a pilot in a real instrumented environment to support activities of surgical teams in operating rooms. COMMON-WEARS strives to move the research front by developing CO-WCS featuring multi-user collectives of smart wearables and body sensor networks (BSN), with applications, e.g., in domestic, urban, manufacturing, emergency, and working environments. This is achieved by combining aggregate computing, collective opportunistic IoT, machine learning, and WCS/BSN architectures. Herein, we discuss lessons learned from prototyping new concepts in collaborative wearable computing, in the contest of challenging use cases, including healthcare, emergency response and pandemic management, which are strongly impacted by smart wearables.

title: Comparative Analysis of Text Extraction Methods from PDF Files for Subsequent Data Processing
authors: Vladislav A. Myasoedov, Julia A. Vishnevskaya
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: The PDF format is widely used for storing and sharing documents, yet extracting text from PDFs remains challenging due to its structural complexity. This paper presents a comparative analysis of text extraction methods: traditional, statistical, and intelligent. Key evaluation criteria include accuracy, structural preservation, formatting retention, performance, and usability. The methods were tested on a diverse set of PDF documents. Based on the results, practical recommendations are provided for selecting the appropriate method to automate the analysis of large document volumes.

title: Design of Intelligent Vacuum Cleaner Control System
authors: Zihao Zhuang, Hanhong Tan, Yanfe Teng, Zhiyuan Hu, Yiming Zhong
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computing, Robotics and System Sciences (ICRSS)
abstract: The purpose of this paper is to design and implement a smart vacuum cleaner based on STM32. By elaborating on the hardware architecture of the system, including motor drive circuits, sensor modules, power management circuits, etc., it shows how they work together to ensure the stable operation of the vacuum cleaner. At the software level, a software system including path planning algorithms, intelligent obstacle avoidance programs, and remote control functions was developed using the powerful processing power of STM32. The experimental results show that the smart vacuum cleaner can complete the cleaning task efficiently, and has intelligent features such as autonomous obstacle avoidance, path planning and remote control, which significantly improves the cleaning efficiency and user experience, and provides a new idea and practical reference for the development of smart home equipment.

title: MotionLens: Enhancing Assessment of Elderly Motion Status by Visual Analysis
authors: Zhengwei Xie, Hao Zeng, Yonghong Hu, Shujie Li, Yong Wang, Zhenya Zhang, Ye Yu, Qiang Lu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyberworlds (CW）
abstract: Human motion assessment (e.g., elderly motion assessment) refers to the process of systematically assessing and analyzing human motion posture, balance, and stability to uncover risks in motion, standardize human motion, and guide human motion. However, current assessment methods for human locomotor status are often based on joint angle kinematics analysis of skeletal joints and gait analysis based on gait parameters. These methods focus on a single type of parameter analysis, making it difficult to reflect both gait information and engineering risks simultaneously. This limits the ability to provide comprehensive motion status assessments. In this work, we focus on motion state assessment for the elderly. Through collaboration with domain experts, we identified three challenges: (a) integrating skeletal joint angles and gait parameters for motion state assessment analysis; (b) providing a correlated and intuitive visual representation of the changes in motion state parameters; and (c) offering customizable and configurable assessment criteria adaptable to different scenarios. To address these challenges, we propose MotionLens, an interactive visual analysis system designed to reveal the association between motion states, skeletal joint angles, and gait parameters. Our two case studies based on real datasets, along with expert interviews, demonstrate the effectiveness of MotionLens in assessing elderly motion states and identifying potential risks.

title: An Open-Source Analysis Methodology for Battery Supported Islanded Operation in Residential Distribution Networks
authors: Rabia Eda Patoglu, Mustafa Alparslan Zehir
publish: 2026 Open Source Modelling and Simulation of Energy Systems (OSMSES)
abstract: The rising incidence of natural disasters poses a major risk to the resilience of residential distribution networks, frequently leading to extended power outages. In order to address this challenge, this paper presents an open-source analysis framework for battery-supported islanded operation. The approach couples OpenDSS with a Python-based control interface to model residential feeders derived from the IEEE 13-bus test system, including photovoltaic (PV) generation and mobile battery energy storage systems (MBESS). The methodology prioritizes utilization of open-source tools to relieve from licensed software constraints and create a flexible environment for simulating locational outages, switching actions, and recovery scenarios.Single-line outages are represented via event-triggered switching operations to examine islanded operation enabled by mobile batteries. System behavior is assessed using bus voltage profiles, power flow redistribution, and the time evolution of battery State of Charge (SoC). The simulations show that coordinated control of MBESS and PV systems substantially prolongs the islanded operating duration, stabilizes SoC, and maintains supply to critical loads. With PV generation, battery discharge is slowed, extending support during outages. Nonetheless, voltage decline as the battery nears its reserve threshold underlines the need for proper battery sizing and reserve management.By solely using open-source software, the proposed framework delivers a scalable, low-cost, and reproducible platform for resilience-focused distribution system studies and enables proactive disaster recovery planning through configurable scenarios and control strategies.

title: VILLASnode API Gateway demonstrated for FMU and OpenDSS Co-Simulation of IEEE13bus System
authors: Alexandra Bach, Jitpanu Maneerapongsuk, Antonello Monti
publish: 2026 Open Source Modelling and Simulation of Energy Systems (OSMSES)
abstract: An Application Programming Interface (API) gateway has several functionalities. We concentrate on the translation of one API type to another. This paper provides an implementation of an API gateway in the open-source coupling tool VILLASnode to connect simulation tools. The gateway supports the translation of the HyperText Transfer Protocol (HTTP) methods implemented as Representational State Transfer (REST) API into Google Remote Procedure Call (gRPC). An FMU-OpenDSS co-simulation of the IEE13bus system demonstrates the functionality of the gateway. To provide alternatives to the API gateway co-simulation, FMU and OpenDSS node-types are implemented in VILLASnode as well. A comparison with the classical socket-based data exchange for distributed co-simulation and a local co-simulation is conducted. We quantified the delay contributions to show the impacts on additional delay and integration efficiency. Although the gateway introduces more overhead, it allows for more flexible integration of simulation tools, hence improving the automation and interoperability of the co-simulation process.

title: Emotion Classification in Adults Using Transformer Networks with Multi Stage Preprocessing and Token Optimization
authors: Murthuja Pinjara, Lakshmi Sruthi Boya, Rizwana Pattan, Lohitha Dommala, Chennakeshavulu Talari
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: This project is describing a new form of emotion classification system multimodal which integrates processing speech, text, and image to identify human emotions in a proper way. One of the systems employs the application of machine learning and deep learning algorithms to isolate emotional cues in various modalities of communication. In order to accomplish emotion recognition using audio, it extracts the audio feature such as Mel-frequency cepstral coefficients (MFCC) and then subject them to the Convolutional neural network (CNN) to determine them accurately. The text domain system employs Multistage preprocessing and Transformer networks to decode the feelings of written text, and token optimization to enhance the system. Regarding image-based emotion recognition, the facial expression is detected with the help of MobileNet and Explainable AI (XAI) in order to ensure the transparency and comprehensibility of the decision-making process. The explanation of AI-incorporation may be utilized to understand the model predictions better and improve the practical use of the model in the real-world and its adoption.

title: A Self-Reflective Multi-Agent Physician Recommendation Framework
authors: Shuai Mao, Yuqing Yang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Accurately analyzing complex, multi-dimensional physician information to recommend and match patients with the most suitable authoritative physicians remains a significant challenge. However, existing physician recommendation systems face significant challenges: commercial rating platforms often lack objectivity, while traditional recommendation algorithms struggle to process the unstructured, multi-dimensional text data (e.g., clinical notes and academic publications) that embodies professional expertise. To address these limitations, we propose SR-MAPR (Self-Reflective Multi-Agent Physician Recommendation), a novel framework that simulates the workflow of a human expert committee. SR-MAPR integrates Large Language Models (LLMs) with a multi-agent architecture to automate criteria generation, preliminary screening, and comprehensive assessment. A core innovation of this framework is the integration of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a dual-memory mechanism comprising both case-based reasoning and heuristic rules, which mitigates hallucinations and enhances interpretability. Furthermore, we introduce a Reflection Agent that enables the system to learn from errors and iteratively refine its rule base, achieving a self-evolving evaluation process. Extensive experiments on a dataset of 7,362 physician profiles demonstrate that SR-MAPR significantly outperforms baselines, including BERT and standard LLM prompting strategies (Zero-shot and Chain-of-Thought). Our method achieves an accuracy of 97.82% and an F1-score of 0.9075, with superior ranking performance (NDCG@10 of 0.9004). Overall, SR-MAPR establishes a dynamic, interpretable, and self-optimizing paradigm for professional evaluation, empowering patients to optimize physician selection.

title: Intelligent Real-Time Human Activity Monitoring and Unsafe Behavior System
authors: L. Sai Ramesh, Jerophin D R, Keshav S
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Human activity monitoring has become increasingly critical at homes, hospitals, offices, public spaces, and industrial facilities, where timely identification of unsafe or abnormal behaviour can reduce the incidence of accidents and greatly improve general safety. The paper proposes an Intelligent Real-Time Human Activity Monitoring and Unsafe Behaviour Detection System using computer vision, deep learning, and artificial intelligence to recognize human activities and detect behaviour that may present safety risks. This system makes use of the YOLOv8 object detection framework integrated with OpenCV for the accurate recognition of activities such as walking, sitting, falling, running, or entering restricted or sensitive areas. Real-time video streams are processed on a frame-by-frame basis in order to achieve low latency during monitoring and rapid response. This automatically initiates alerts upon the detection of unsafe or anomalous behaviour while recording all events at the central data repository for further analysis. DeepFace-based face recognition can allow the identification of authorized personnel in controlled access scenarios. A dedicated interface for live monitoring with React offers activity analytics, incident histories, and behaviour trends in this regard via intuitive visualizations. On the backend, Flask/ FastAPI ensures efficient data management, scalability for multi-camera setups, and optional cloud connectivity. By marrying modular architecture, real-time processing, and AI-driven decision support, the proposed system comes out to be robust and adaptable for enhancing safety, situational awareness, and proactive behaviour monitoring across multiple domains.

title: Autonomous Multi-Agent Collaboration in Dynamic Environments
authors: Supriya Kumari, Yukti, Suresh Kaswan
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Autonomous multi-agent systems (MAS) are growing as a foundation in addressing complex, real-world challenges that demand scattered intelligence, adaptability, and collaboration. In active environments, agents must operate under conditions of vagueness, incomplete information, and rapidly changing constraints, while maintaining collective performance and robustness. This paper explores the design and coordination strategies for autonomous multi-agent collaboration in such environments, focusing on decentralized policymaking, actual communication, and adaptive learning mechanisms. We review existing backgrounds, highlight the role of reinforcement learning and game-theoretic approaches in enabling cooperation, and propose a hybrid architecture that balances independence with collaborative efficiency. Experimental simulations reveal the potential of the proposed approach to improve scalability, resilience, and task efficiency in applications such as disaster response, intelligent transportation, and swarm robotics.

title: Genre Classification of Sinhala Songs Using Machine Learning Based on Audio Features
authors: S. M. K. N. Nawamini Karunathilake, Dulan S. Dias, Samitha A. Nanayakkara
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Music genre classification plays a significant role in modern audio analysis and music retrieval systems, particularly in organizing and preserving culturally diverse musical traditions. Despite the growing use of automated genre classification techniques worldwide, Sinhala music remains largely underrepresented in computational music analysis, with limited publicly available datasets and genre-focused classification studies. In this context, this study presents a machine learning approach for classifying Sinhala songs into five genres: Pop, Rock, Hip Hop, Classical, and Folk. Due to the absence of public datasets and prior computational research on Sinhala music, a balanced dataset was curated and key audio features such as MFCCs, spectral descriptors, tempo, and rhythm were extracted. Four base models were evaluated: Support Vector Machine, k-Nearest Neighbors, Random Forest, and a Neural Network. The k-Nearest Neighbors model achieved an accuracy of 81.57 %, followed by the Neural Network with an accuracy of 80.86 % and Random Forest with an accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 0. 6 8 \%}$</tex>. A Soft Voting Ensemble combining the Neural Network and Random Forest achieved an accuracy of 81.93 %, offering improved genre-wise balance. These findings demonstrate the effectiveness of both individual and ensemble learning methods for Sinhala music classification and support applications in music retrieval and cultural preservation.

title: A Study on Intelligent Road Damage Detection from UAV Imagery Based on the YOLOv10 Architecture
authors: Yufei Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: With the accelerating pace of urbanization, road infrastructure is facing increasingly severe traffic loads, leading to frequent occurrences of pavement damage that significantly impact traffic safety and operational efficiency. Traditional manual inspection methods are inefficient and offer limited coverage, making it imperative to adopt intelligent approaches for accurate road defect detection. This paper proposes a lightweight object detection model, YOLO-MCG, based on an improved YOLOv10 architecture, targeting the challenges posed by UAV aerial images-such as weak textures, scale variation, blurred edges, and strong background interference in road damage detection. Built upon the backbone of YOLOv10, the proposed YOLO-MCG integrates a Context Guided Block (CG block) to enhance the modeling of local and contextual features, and incorporates a Multi-Scale Dilated Attention (MSDA) mechanism to improve the perceptual representation of fine-grained and multi-scale targets. Extensive experiments conducted on the high-resolution UAV-PDD2023 dataset demonstrate that YOLO-MCG achieves improvements of 14.9 % and 23.2 % in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP} {@} 0.5$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP} {@} 0.5: 0.95$</tex>, respectively, compared to the baseline YOLOv10 model. Comparative and ablation studies further confirm the effectiveness of the CG block and MSDA module in enhancing detection accuracy, robustness, and generalization under complex backgrounds. This research provides a practical and efficient model framework to support intelligent road damage detection in real-world applications.

title: TAGT: A Time-Aware Graph Transformer for Securing Time-Sensitive Networking
authors: Liang Qi, Wushao Wen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: The precision of time synchronization is the cornerstone of safety-critical applications in Automotive Ethernet and Time-Sensitive Networking (TSN). The IEEE 802.1AS protocol (gPTP), while widely adopted, lacks built-in security mechanisms, making it vulnerable to sophisticated delay attacks and packet manipulation. Existing Deep Learning-based Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) often treat network traffic as discrete sequences, ignoring the continuous physical time dynamics and the complex spatial topological dependencies inherent in synchronization protocols.To address these limitations, this study proposes TAGT, a novel Time-Aware Graph Transformer framework for robust gPTP attack detection. Unlike standard Transformers that rely on discrete positional encodings, TAGT incorporates a Learnable Continuous Time-Encoding (LCTE) mechanism. LCTE maps irregular packet arrival intervals into high-dimensional frequency representations, enabling the model to capture microsecond-level clock jitter and drift anomalies. Furthermore, we integrate Graph Attention Networks (GAT) to extract spatial features from the network topology, fusing them with temporal embeddings to learn a unified spatio-temporal representation. Extensive experiments on high-fidelity OMNeT++ simulations demonstrate that TAGT achieves an F1-score of 95.65%, outperforming existing baseline models (e.g., LSTM, GCN) by a significant margin. The results validate that our approach is highly effective in detecting low-frequency attacks and is robust to topological changes.

title: AI-Optimized Adaptive Beamforming for Mm-Wave and THZ Communication
authors: Saveetha T, R.Ramya
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: This paper introduces a streamlined deep learning-enhanced Visual SLAM framework for dependable drone navigation in GPS-denied and cluttered settings. The system combines a CNN-Transformer feature extractor with a monocular depth-prediction network to improve visual odometry and make it easier to see obstacles. A local planner that uses reinforcement learning creates safe paths by combining predicted depth with real-time SLAM map updates. Tests on the EuRoC MAV, TUM RGB-D, and custom outdoor datasets show that this method works better than traditional SLAM pipelines for both localisation and avoiding obstacles. The main new thing is how well deep perceptual cues work with geometric SLAM and the dual-mode obstacle-avoidance strategy that is best for embedded UAV platforms

title: A Novel Federated Learning using Deformable Convolutional GRU Framework for Smart Irrigation in Next-Gen Agriculture with Encryption
authors: Wilson Anandaraj A, S. Maruthupermal
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Smart irrigation plays a crucial role in farming, where real-time sensor information and smart decision-making technology improve the sustainability and accuracy. Within this framework, Federated Learning (FL) provides a robust solution to enhance irrigation strategies collaboratively without revealing their confidential data. This strategy guarantees that the agricultural information stays secure, both during processing and transmission, when paired with secure encryption methods, which promote the ethical and safe digital advancement in the agricultural sector. However, the intelligent irrigation is hindered by various drawbacks associated with current centralized methods. These systems analyze large volumes of raw data in a central location, which heightens the chances of unauthorized access and breaches of farmers' trust. Hence, in this work, a novel FL-based network is introduced for smart irrigation in next-gen agriculture. In the beginning, the required data from the Internet of Things (IoT) sensors is collected and then subjected to Fully Homomorphic Encryption with Polynomial Arithmetic (FHE-PA) for encrypting and preserving the privacy of the data. Further, the encrypted data are subjected to Federated Learning using Deformable Convolutional Gated Recurrent Unit (FL-DCGRU) for decision making in the irrigation process. In the end, the developed framework is compared with the conventional models to ensure its effectiveness.

title: Frequency-Limited Gramian Framework and Model Reduction Using Square-Root Truncated Realization
authors: Deepak Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a novel configuration of frequency-limited controllability and observability Gramians to establish a new square-root truncated realization-based model reduction approach, which minimizes errors within designated frequency regions. The proposed method produces stable, simpler models and offers improved approximations compared to current methodologies. Two numerical examples are employed to evaluate the efficacy of the presented method, demonstrating through error plots and comparisons that the suggested reduced models adequately approximate the actual models within the designated frequency band.

title: Hybrid Tree-Kernel Learning for Material-Family Recognition From Differential and Curve-Level Supercapacitor Signatures
authors: Lance Dominic C. Raquel, Elmer Peramo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: We investigate whether nine supercapacitor electrode material families can be discriminated using only a small, physically interpretable set of electrochemical descriptors from a dataset shared with us by researchers in University of Tennessee-Knoxville through private communication and from an unpublished research. Using their dataset, we first reconstructed the three-descriptor setup (current-voltage, charge-voltage, and power-voltage derivatives). With a tuned stacked hybrid model on 8,985 samples and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$80 / 20$</tex> stratified split, we achieved 89.4 % test accuracy. This confirms that the three differential signatures alone already contain meaningful class information. We then derived three more physically interpretable, curve-level descriptors from the cyclic-voltammetry samples (area, peak, and spread), combined them with the original three to form six-dimensional feature vectors, and fed these into a stacked hybrid model composed of five heterogeneous base learners and a meta-learner. On the same held-out set of 1,797 samples, the single extra-trees model and the stacked hybrid achieved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 8. 4 - 9 8. 6 \%}$</tex> accuracy, with macro-F1 scores close to 0.99. Only 26 samples were misclassified; these misclassifications occurred between chemically similar class pairs (for example, confusing MnCoFe with MnCo, or classifying commercial Mn-graphene as Fe). This shows that nine-class material-family recognition from a small set of electrochemical descriptors is achievable on this dataset once curve-level information is included.

title: Optimization and Application of Intelligent Decision-Making Algorithm for Work Tickets Based on Deep Learning
authors: Xiaohong Lu, Jiehui Chen, Keju Shen, Kaiwen Yang, Yifeng Sun
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: In the context of digital transformation in industries such as electricity and energy, traditional work ticket processing methods are difficult to meet the needs of efficient and accurate decision-making. This paper proposes an intelligent decision-making algorithm for work tickets based on a hybrid attention mechanism, which integrates a multi-head self-attention mechanism to mine the semantic association of work ticket fields, uses a temporal attention mechanism to capture the timing characteristics of jobs, and corrects the decision results through a rule engine. The experiment constructed a data set containing 120,000 real data, and compared it with LSTM, GRU and decision tree algorithms using accuracy, recall and F1 values as evaluation indicators. The results show that the accuracy of the algorithm in this paper reached 94.2 %, an increase of 13.7 % over the LSTM algorithm; the recall rate was 92.5 %, an increase of 16.1 % over the GRU algorithm; the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex> value reached 93.3 %, which was significantly better than the comparison algorithm in all indicators. In the simulated business scenario test, the algorithm increased the efficiency of work ticket processing by 40 % and reduced the error rate to 5.8 %, verifying its effectiveness and innovation in practical applications, and providing a new solution for intelligent decision-making of work tickets.

title: Research on the Impact of Investor Sentiment on IPO Pricing Efficiency Based on Double Machine Learning
authors: Jintai Ye, Yunshi Chen, Yile Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Technology, Communication Ecosystem and Management (ITCEM)
abstract: Against the backdrop of deepening registrationbased reform and increasing volatility in capital market sentiment, research on the mechanisms affecting IPO pricing efficiency holds both significant theoretical value and practical relevance. Drawing on behavioral finance and information economics, this paper employs the Double Machine Learning (DML) framework and Generalized Random Forests (GRF) to conduct a systematic empirical analysis of A-share IPOs from 2019 to 2023. The findings indicate that investor sentiment exerts a significant positive effect on IPO underpricing, and this conclusion remains robust across a series of sensitivity tests. Further moderation analysis reveals that issuance size negatively moderates the sentiment-underpricing relationship through liquidity dilution and investor structure optimization, while daily price fluctuation limits amplify the effect of sentiment due to delayed price discovery. The heterogeneity analysis further demonstrates that issuance size plays a dominant moderating role: smaller offerings are more susceptible to sentiment-driven mispricing, whereas larger issues exhibit greater pricing stability. Issuance P/E ratio presents an inverted-U effect, with sentiment impact peaking at moderate valuation levels. Earnings per share displays a nonlinear pattern, where low-profit or loss-making firms are more sentiment-sensitive, while stronger profitability weakens the marginal influence of sentiment. Overall, this study uncovers the complexity and nonlinearity of sentiment effects on IPO pricing efficiency and provides solid theoretical and empirical evidence for enhancing pricing mechanisms under the registration-based system.

title: Leveraging Vision-Language Models for Referring Video Shadow Detection
authors: Chenjun Liang, Hai Lu, Luping Shan, Yunxun Liu, Yan Xu, Jiabao Chen, Qinglian Xue, Danying Xu, Lei Zhu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyberworlds (CW）
abstract: Referring Video Shadow Detection (RVSD) is an emerging vision-language task that segments specific shadow regions in videos based on natural language descriptions, combining the flexibility of language-guided localization with the temporal dynamics of video understanding. While initial approaches have demonstrated promising results, existing methods remain limited in their ability to fully leverage the rich semantic relationships between textual prompts and visual shadow features, particularly in complex scenes with multiple shadows or dynamically changing lighting conditions. This paper presents ShadowVLM, a novel vision-language model framework that leverages the crossmodal representation capabilities of large-scale vision-language models. By integrating multiscale image perception and multiscale video perception mechanisms, ShadowVLM achieves robust performance in complex multimodal understanding tasks. Our approach introduces three key innovations: (1) A hierarchical visual understanding module that extracts and interprets image features from multiple spatial perspectives, enhancing the model's visual perception. This design enables comprehensive image understanding at global, local, and fine-grained levels, improving the model's ability to capture diverse visual cues; (2) A query propagation mechanism for temporal consistency, which extends the model's temporal awareness via a specially designed memory bank. This mechanism ensures stable target tracking across frames, even in complex and dynamic environments; (3) The use of a large-scale vision-language model's cross-modal representation capability to align textual features with shadowed visual inputs. This alignment allows the model to acquire prior knowledge about shadowed objects, thereby improving its target recognition ability under challenging visual conditions. Extensive experiments demonstrate that ShadowVLM significantly outperforms previous state-of-the-art methods.

title: Application of Patch Antennas in Process Technology: A Survey
authors: Shridhar Modak, Aman Gupta, Vaishali Gongane, R. G. Kaduskar, Y. B. Thakare
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: Microstrip Patch Antennas (MPAs) are emerging as key components in wireless sensing for process technology due to their compactness, passive operation, and ease of integration. This survey summarizes their role in applications such as temperature sensing, structural health monitoring, moisture detection, biomedical applications, and chemical/material analysis. It also examines interrogation mechanisms such as backscattering, RFID, impedance matching, and amplitude modulation.

title: QoS- Driven Wireless Resource Management for Network Slicing in Heterogeneous Cellular Networks
authors: Yongheng Wang, Jing Zhao, Zhiqi Ren, Rui Zhou, Kuan Shen
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: With the development of the fifth-generation and future networks, network slicing, as a key technology to meet varying QoS demands, faces significant challenges in managing diverse services within heterogeneous networks. To solve this issue, this paper proposes a QoS-oriented wireless resource management framework. The network is modeled as a graph, and QoS, energy, and resource constraints are integrated into a unified system model. A multi-time-scale resource allocation algorithm is designed. A graph neural network enables topology-aware power and resource block allocation in the macro phase. In the micro phase, a dynamic weight-priority mechanism adjusts resources in real time. Then, a cross-period coordination strategy is executed to achieve a trade-off between energy consumption and QoS. Simulation results show that the proposed approach can significantly improve QoS and reduce interference in small-cell-only scenarios. And in macro-micro heterogeneous networks, optimized access increases the total QoS by over 200%, while energy efficiency optimization reduces the system energy consumption with only a 0.48% QoS loss, providing an efficient solution for network slicing in heterogeneous cellular networks.

title: Intelligent Transportation Management using Hyperledger Fabric and Metaheuristic Route Optimization
authors: Sachin Kumar Sangwan, Arun Mishra
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: In the race to achieve efficacy in the logistics operations, the growing dependency on Intelligent Transportation systems has become a common norm for Smart Logistics. It has become utmost important and foundational for any organisation to excel in the competing environment of global supply chains. Traditional logistics systems, often centralized and reliant on static planning, exhibit significant vulnerabilities in terms of security, transparency, and adaptability, leading to inefficiencies that can compromise operational effectiveness. The impact of new technologies and new world operating conditions has made critical for Supply chains organization to consider the new prevailing factors in their optimization of routine tasks and operational functionality. This research proposes a novel, integrated framework that addresses these critical shortcomings by synergizing the immutable, permissioned distributed ledger technology of Hyperledger Fabric with a deterministic route optimization engine using the Off chain Computation techniques. The optimization component utilizes Metaheuristic algorithm as a foundational element within a broader Vehicle Routing Problem (VRP) solving methodology to manage large-scale supply chain movements specifically to transportation. The Hyperledger Fabric layer provides a secure, tamper-proof, and auditable platform for all logistical transactions, ensuring data integrity and empowering trust among disparate organizational units, suppliers, and partners. Further the proposed model also provides the dynamic re-routing to counter the various unforeseen circumstances at the ground level. In a simulated operational environment characterized by dynamic order requisitions and network disruptions, the proposed framework demonstrated a significant improvement in route efficiency and a substantial reduction in average expenditure in terms of security, clock time, resources allocations leading to seamless sustainable green operations as compared to traditional heuristic-based planning models. Furthermore, the underlying blockchain network sustained a high throughput with low latency for critical updates, validating its suitability for near-real-time operational demands. This paper presents the complete architecture, implementation details, and comprehensive performance evaluation of the framework, establishing its potential to create a more resilient, intelligent, and secure enterprise logistics ecosystem.

title: Research on a Multi-Stage Multi-Attention Fusion Approach for Rice Pest Detection
authors: Yuxin Wang, Yuting Wang, Zhanfeng Zhang, Xiaofeng An
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Rapid and accurate identification of rice pests is crucial for ensuring food security. Addressing the insufficient feature extraction capability of traditional YOLO in detecting small rice pests, this paper proposes a staged triple attention fusion object detection method based on the YOLOv8 framework. A triple attention module is constructed comprising Efficient Channel Attention (ECA), Simultaneous Attention Modulation (SimAM), and Global Context Blocks (GC). Adaptive weight fusion and learnable residual scaling enhance feature expression and training stability. Simultaneously, based on the semantic and spatial distribution characteristics of features at different scales, partial or complete triple attention is embedded in stages at shallow, intermediate, and deep layers. This approach highlights local textures while capturing global context, all while controlling computational overhead. Experimental results on the rice pest dataset demonstrate that the improved model significantly outperforms the original YOLOv8 in metrics such as Precision, Recall, and mAP. It effectively enhances the identification accuracy of rice pests in complex field scenarios, providing an efficient and reliable technical solution for intelligent agricultural pest monitoring systems.

title: Seeing Through Deepfakes: Explainable AI (XAI) for Image Forensics and Visual Detection
authors: Veerendra P, A V Chethana Reddy, Kashish Gupta, Radhakrishnan Gopalapillai
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Deepfake technology has made it increasingly easy to fabricate highly realistic facial videos. This creates serious challenges for individual privacy and information security. Many existing deep fake detection models based on Deep Convolutional Neural Networks (DCNNs) perform well on benchmark datasets, but their performance is not very good on real-world content, particularly for faces from underrepresented demographics. In practice, these models often misclassify Indian, South Asian, and other Asian faces with high false positive rates. To address this short coming, we design a deepfake detection framework tailored to Indian users, built on a strong pre-trained DCNN that is adapted to the target domain using parameter-efficient fine-tuning. We have created a new proprietary dataset, DF-India, consisting of diverse Indian facial identities and realistic capture conditions, including variations in device quality, resolution, compression, and common online distortions, to better reflect deployment scenarios. The framework also incorporates an Explainable AI (XAI) component based on Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad- CAM), which highlights the image regions driving the model's decisions. Experimental results indicate that the proposed approach offers improved detection accuracy, stronger cross- domain robustness, and reduced demographic disparity in error rates, leading to more trustworthy deepfake detection for South Asian populations.

title: Dynamic Cloud Load Balancing Via Hybrid Swarm-Evolutionary Bio-Inspired Optimization with Real-Time Adaptivity
authors: S. Karthick T., YogeshRajkumar R.
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Cloud computing has also emerged as a central concept in the provision of scalable and on-demand computational services but due to the imbalanced overscheduling in the virtual machines, there is degradation in performance, higher latency as well as ineffective utilisation of resources. Quality of service, scalability and energy efficiency of the dynamic cloud environments need efficient load balancing and optimization mechanisms. Conventional heuristic and non-adaptive scheduling techniques do not keep pace with very dynamic and heterogeneous cloud workloads. This paper addresses these issues by coming up with a dynamic cloud load balancing structure grounded on a hybrid swarmevolutionary bio-inspired optimization scheme coupled with real-time adaptability. The suggested solution incorporates both the worldwide search power of swarm intelligence and the convergence power of evolutionary processes to attain equilibrium in the distribution of tasks in different workload situations. Real-time adaptive control allows the model to adapt effectively to variations of resource availability as well as task arrival patterns without having to be manually reconfigured. The framework is tested with typical cloud simulation environments with various workload conditions, and the performance measure is taken on the basis of response time, makespan, resource usage, energy use, and degree of load imbalance. Compared to traditional bio-inspired and non-bioinspired methods, it is shown that the proposed hybrid methodology can always result in a more effective load distribution and stability of the system at low computational costs. The findings indicate that adaptive hybrid bio-inspired optimization is effective and reliable to use in dynamic and scalable cloud load balancing.

title: Detecting Periodic Patterns in Semirepetitive Video Footage With Analysis and Comparison
authors: Shahin Mehdipour Ataee
publish: Computer
abstract: This article presents the design and implementation of a software method for detecting and calculating the repetition period in semirepetitive video footage. This method leverages image similarity functions to identify visually similar frames within a video and applies time-series analysis techniques to find the repetition period.

title: Classification of Tomatoes Growth Degree from Spectrum Hue Information Measured Applying RBF-Kernel Method
authors: Yoshitsugu Nakagawa, Hiroyasu Sano, Toshiro Takata
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: In recent years, efforts have been made in the agricultural field to improve crop quality and streamline agricultural management by collecting and visualizing various data on crop growth. In this study, we focus on the “spectrum of light” as information on sunlight that is essential for crop growth and aim to capture its functions. Specifically, a spectral sensor is used to measure the reflectance of light for each wavelength. Measurement data generally contains variations, making classification difficult using machine learning based on linear separation. In this study, we attempt classification using a nonlinear separation method capable of high-dimensional analysis and report its effectiveness. Crop growth varies depending on location, lushness, etc. Traditionally, this work relied mainly on experience and subjectivity, but by quantitatively evaluating the distribution and changes in the visible light band, farmers can visually grasp the growth status of crops and determine the optimal harvest time.

title: Data-Driven and Knowledge-Enhanced Approaches for Real-Time Grid Dispatch: A Comprehensive Survey
authors: Wenlong Shi, Xiaoling Wu, Lizhen Zhang, Jianjun Zhang, Lixun Zhao, Runquan Meng
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: The Data-Knowledge Dual-Driven Artificial Intelligence method synergizes data-driven and knowledge-driven approaches, leveraging their respective strengths to form a superior integrated methodology. The paper first establishes Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) as a key technical approach to address the complexity in resource coordination. However, achieving optimal resource allocation and overall performance enhancement in large-scale power grids remains a highly valuable yet incompletely resolved open question. Knowledge-driven and data-driven methods aim to enhance the learning efficiency, decision-making capabilities, and robustness of DRL agents operating within complex dynamic environments, potentially providing auxiliary support for addressing model interpretability issues. Finally, it summarizes and prospects the applicable scenarios of the dual knowledge-data driven methodology in power dispatch.

title: Robust Constant Voltage Control of the IPT System under Parameter Variations
authors: Qinglong Feng, Shuangxin Zhu, Yi Zhang, Yuanjun Hou, Jinbin Zhao, Zhixin Wang
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)
abstract: To address the challenge of output voltage instability resulting from parameter variations in inductive power transfer (IPT) systems, a constant voltage control framework based on the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$H_{\infty}$</tex> tracking controller with adjustable performance indexes is proposed in this paper. In terms of the generalized state-space averaging (GSSA) approach, a dynamic IPT model with varying coupling coefficient and load resistance is e stablished to capture the nonlinear behavior under parameter uncertainties first. Then, an enhanced <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$H_{\infty}$</tex> controller with a relaxable performance index is developed to balance regulation performance and implementation complexity. Finally, the series-series (SS) compensated IPT system with a primary-side Buck-Boost structure is adopted to validate the effectiveness and the feasibility of the proposed approach.

title: An Auxiliary Decision-making Algorithm for Power Supply Quality in Mountainous Power Grid Based on
authors: Kuanhong Li, Siyang Cao, Yan Zhang, Bojian Chen, Yishi Lin
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: With the large-scale integration of renewable energy sources such as wind power and photovoltaics, the structure and operational characteristics of power systems are becoming increasingly complex. Power supply quality issues are becoming increasingly prominent in mountainous power grids, particularly in areas with complex terrain and uneven distribution of power generation and consumption. This paper proposes a decision-making support method based on Large Language Model (LLM) reasoning. By integrating multi-source heterogeneous data with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) technology, the system enables intelligent fault diagnosis, reactive power optimization, and restoration strategy generation. Combining traditional optimization algorithms with a visual interface, it provides intuitive and reliable decision support for dispatchers. Practical applications have demonstrated that this system can provide dispatchers with intuitive and reliable decision support that adapts to complex environments, improving the operational management of mountainous power grids.

title: Adaptive Disturbance Compensation for a Control Moment Gyroscope System with Different Control Delays
authors: Cong Vinh Tu, Natalia Dudarenko
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: The Control Moment Gyroscope (CMG) system is a nonlinear multivariable system, widely used in satellites, spacecraft, unmanned vehicles, and marine vehicles as an actuator for attitude control or system stabilization. This paper presents a solution to address the disturbance compensation problem for the CMG system with different control delays at the input, where the external disturbances are in the form of multi-harmonic signals with unknown parameters. To solve this problem, the linearized model of the CMG is used. From this, a linear state feedback control law based on the classical Falb-Wolovich method is applied to eliminate the cross-coupling between the CMG control channels. Finally, the control law along with an adaptive algorithm using memory regressor extension is developed. Simulation results are carried out using Matlab Simulink to verify the effectiveness of the proposed control scheme.

title: HDA-vmunet: High-order Dynamic Adaptive Vision Mamba Unet for Medical Image Segmentation
authors: Yijun Li, Haoran Wang, Limin Sun
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: In the field of medical image segmentation, various model frameworks based on Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Transformers have been systematically developed and thoroughly investigated. However, the limited receptive field of convolutional operations makes it difficult for CNNs to effectively capture global contextual information, while the high computational overhead of Transformers restricts their deployment in resource-constrained clinical settings. Recently, Mamba, a selective state space model, has received widespread attention for its efficient sequence modeling capabilities and linear computational complexity. In this study, we propose a novel model named High-order Dynamic Adaptive Vision Mamba Unet (HDA-vmunet), which integrates a high-order spatial interaction algorithm with the Dynamic Adaptive Scan (DAS) mechanism to enhance representation and modeling flexibility. In addition, we design a Channel and Window Attention Bridge (CWAB) module that effectively improves the model’s ability to fuse multi-scale features. Experimental results on public medical imaging datasets such as ISIC17, ISIC18, and Kvasir-SEG demonstrate that HDA-vmunet achieves superior segmentation performance with a lightweight model architecture, offering a favorable balance between efficiency and accuracy and showing strong potential for practical application and competitive advantage.

title: Telemedicine Cart with AI-Symptom Chatbot
authors: Devi P. P, Vechham Sai Yaswin, Kallamadi Prashanth Kumar Reddy, Kapala Pradyumna
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: The web application developed is a Health Assistance Web Application that efficiently offers healthcare guidance to make healthcare more accessible, trustworthy and user friendly. The process starts with a secure signed up and logged in procedure where the user provided their details such as name, contact number, email address, and password to register. The user also provides email and password during sign-in for authentication. Upon signing in to an individualized user interface (UI) user is guided through the process of entering its location in sequential steps, first selecting the state, on further clicking it follows with the district for user profile purposes and to provide a “Hospitals Near Me” feature. After completing the location setting the user is lead to interact with an AIempowered Symptom Chatbot that takes the symptoms as input and predicts possible diseases, using integrated machine learning and AI APIs such as Google Gemini. The chatbot is thoughtfully designed and takes the user interaction a step further by presenting the results in the form of clickable bubbles such as Precautions and Medicines which allows the user to refer to safety measures or common medical advice more readily. The user is also provided with a list of nearby hospitals, comprising of their name, contact number, and location; to help users reach out for professional healthcare services when needed. The aim of the system is to act as an early medical advice application and not as a replacement for physicians. Its purpose is to assist you in identifying possible health concerns, possible delays in taking actions, and present efficient routes to access the hospital if needed. The project allows you to develop a secured user authenticated web application, predicted health status through AI models, interactive interface, and suggestions for assistance if not able to reach the hospital in an appropriate time. The project acts as a digital health partner that allows and enables individuals to take ownership of their health and navigate health issues if needed.

title: Research on the Evaluation Method for the Adjustable Load Potential of Electrochemical Energy Storage Systems under Extreme Weather Conditions
authors: Wentao Chang, Kaiji Liao, Xiang Zhang, Xiaojun Li, Yifei Zhou
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: With the frequent occurrence of extreme weather events posing severe challenges to the stable operation of power grids, the potential assessment of electrochemical energy storage-based adjustable load (EES-AL) - a key resource for enhancing power grid resilience - has become an urgent issue to be addressed. This paper proposes an EES-AL potential assessment method based on component decomposition and variable correction, which deconstructs the EES-AL load characteristics into three levels: base load component, adjustable reserve component, and emergency support component. Meanwhile, correction variables and slack variables are introduced to reflect the impact of extreme weather on system performance. The effectiveness of the proposed method is verified through the analysis of actual operation data from 318 energy storage users in a southwest province of China. The results show that under extreme low temperature ($-19^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$), the battery energy storage system (BESS) capacity degrades by 30%, and the base load component increases to 5%; under extreme high temperature ($40^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$), the photovoltaic output efficiency decreases by 25%, and the BESS round-trip efficiency drops to 75-80%. The proposed assessment method can accurately quantify the regulation potential of EES-AL under different extreme weather scenarios, providing a scientific basis for power grid dispatching decisions.

title: Application of Fuzzy Adaptive Pid Algorithm in Steering Control of Hydraulic-Mechanical Dual-Flow Transmission Vehicles
authors: Fan Bai, Zhaofei Tai, Honglei Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computing, Robotics and System Sciences (ICRSS)
abstract: Tracked vehicles adopting the hydraulic-mechanical dual-flow transmission system have significantly improved mobility compared to those with a single-flow transmission system. However, due to the adoption of the hydraulic-mechanical transmission system, its precise control has an important impact on vehicle steering and the exertion of mobility. This paper focuses on tracked vehicles with hydraulic-mechanical dual-flow transmission. It analyzes the hydraulic pump-motor mechanism, discusses the requirements of steering control for algorithms, the characteristics and advantages of the fuzzy adaptive PID algorithms, studies the application of the fuzzy adaptive PID algorithm in steering control, and verifies the feasibility of the algorithm through computer simulation.

title: A Node Selection Scheme for Target Localization in Passive Radar Networks
authors: Weiqi Xiao, Junkun Yan, Peng Zhang, Chang Gao, Tianyi Jia, Hongwei Liu
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: This paper proposes a node selection scheme for passive radar networks that minimizes the number of passive radars required for target localization while maintaining a desired level of location accuracy. Specifically, we formulate an integer optimization model that incorporates the Cramér-Rao Lower Bound of localization error as a constraint, with the objective of minimizing the number of passive radar nodes. To efficiently solve this problem, we design a heuristic optimization method that incrementally increases the number of selected passive radars and employs an early stopping mechanism to determine the final node configuration. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed scheme effectively reduces usage of passive radars while satisfying localization accuracy requirements.

title: Data-Driven Optimization of Intelligent Identification Model for Metamorphic Buried-Hill Reservoir Classification
authors: Yan Shi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Aiming at the problem of low classification accuracy of metamorphic buried-hill reservoir, this paper proposes a data-driven intelligent identification model optimization method.
