Attention mechanism and multi-scale feature fusion strategy are introduced based on convolutional neural network, and genetic algorithm is used to optimize hyperparameters. The experiment uses 1500 sets of reservoir data actually collected from an oil field, which are divided into training set, validation set and test set in a ratio of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7: 2: 1$</tex>. The results show that the optimized model has an accuracy of 93.6 %, a recall rate of 92.1 %, an F 1 value of 92.8 %, and an AUC of 0.945 on the test set, compared with the traditional support vector machine method, the accuracy is increased by 23.4 %, and the F1 value is increased by 15.3 % compared with the original convolutional neural network model. At the same time, when 10% noise data is added, the model accuracy remains at 88.7 %, showing good robustness. Visual analysis shows that the model can effectively capture the key characteristics of the reservoir. The study confirmed that the optimization model significantly improved the classification performance of metamorphic buried-hill reservoirs and provided accurate data support for oil and gas exploration.

title: Data-Driven Monitoring of Power Distribution Systems Using Machine Learning
authors: Sakshi Kumar, Chhavi Jhari, Subho Paul
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: Power distribution systems require robust monitoring to ensure stability and efficiency. This paper presents a data-driven approach for monitoring the IEEE 33-bus system using machine learning (ML). The system is modeled in OpenDSS, and a Python framework is used to generate synthetic datasets via Monte Carlo simulation. Five machine learning (ML) models like Random Forest (RF), k-Nearest Neighbors (k-NN), Artificial Neural Networks (ANN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) are trained to predict bus voltages and line losses. Detailed sensitivity analysis and hyperparameter tuning are conducted, with performance evaluated using metrics such as root mean square error (RMSE) and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. Results indicate that ANN achieves the highest accuracy, with a test R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.956673, averaged across three different prediction tasks. This work demonstrates the feasibility of ML-based monitoring for power distribution systems, with ANN and LSTM models showing particular promise for practical implementations.

title: AI-Driven Web Portal for T-Cell and Biomarker Analysis in Breast Cancer Diagnostics
authors: Anushree Raj, Shylesh B C, Varuna Gangadhar, Khadersab Adamsab
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Significantly, Breast cancer remains a leading global health burden, demanding early and accurate diagnostics to improve patient outcomes [1]. However, current protocols rely on separate analyses of imaging and molecular assays, which can be invasive, time-consuming, and subject to variability. We propose a novel cloud-based diagnostic platform that synergistically integrates T-cell–rich histopathological images and clinical biomarker profiles using artificial intelligence. Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) analyze high-resolution T-cell morphology from digitized histology slides, while models of machine learning process biomarker data (HER2, ER, PR), combining these modalities into a unified prediction. The system generates rapid, interpretable reports via a secure web portal, making advanced diagnostics accessible in diverse clinical settings. By merging morphological and molecular information, the hybrid model aims to improve sensitivity and specificity beyond single-modality systems [2], [3]. From what we have observed initially combined method together seems to improve classification and reduce false negatives compared to image-only or biomarker- only models. Crucially, the portal emphasizes user-centered design and data security, enabling remote pathology consultation without requiring local expert pathologists. Overall, our work illustrates the promise of integrating multi-dimensional data through AI, paving the way for more precise and equitable breast cancer diagnosis. The paper highlights technical challenges in multi-modal integration and outlines future work to incorporate additional biomarkers and validate the system in clinical studies.

title: Autoencoder Based Real-Time Anomalous Human Activity Detection in High-Security and Public Environments Using Spatiotemporal Ai
authors: GASSA Dissanayake, BVKI Vidanage, B Hettige
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Traditional cloud-based video surveillance systems have recurring issues of latency and privacy of data. This work addresses these challenges by proposing a complete system of real-time and privacy-aware anomalous human activity detection that can be fully run on a limited resource edge device. We use unsupervised learning approach, training a 3D Convolutional Autoencoder (3D-CAE) on the complex spatiotemporal patterns of normal behavior. Using NVIDIA Jetson Nano, the system detects anomalies by classifying disparate conditions as that which constitutes a significant deviation of the norms of these learned ones based on the reconstruction error. A key aspect of this work was to get beyond the computational bottleneck to real-time performance. We have been able to speed up the deep learning model through a rigorous process of model optimization in the TensorRT framework provided by NVIDIA to suit the real-time processing needs of live video. This bold move has facilitated real-time throughput with a very negligible sacrifice in the accuracy of the underlying detection of the system. The deployed system runs efficiently with power- and memory-constrained edge hardware. The research presented in this paper has contributed to a holistically designed and tested resolution, which shows the feasibility of implementing advanced, privacy-sensitive deep learning models through intelligent surveillance at the edge by the network.

title: Influence of Augmented Reality-Based Virtual Content on Desire to Participate in Real-World Traditional Cultural Experiences
authors: Yuya Ieiri, Osamu Yoshie
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Computer, Software and Modeling (ICCSM)
abstract: In recent years, the amount of virtual tourism content available online has increased significantly. However, types of virtual tourism content that effectively promote the desire to engage in real tourism experiences have not been identified yet. In this study, we investigated augmented reality (AR)-based traditional cultural experiences and examined their impact on users' desire to participate in corresponding realworld experiences. To this end, a survey was performed on 42 participants, revealing that AR-based traditional cultural experiences positively influenced the willingness of male participants to engage in real-life experiences. On the other hand, although female participants reported more positive attitudes toward ARbased traditional cultural experiences, this did not necessarily translate into greater desire to engage in real-life experiences. We believe that our findings will help the design of marketing strategies involving AR technology, helping to target potential tourists more effectively.

title: Intelligent Multi-Disease Prediction Using Advanced Deep Learning
authors: Deepa G, Asha Mary R, Murugan K, Yuvaraj N, Kaviya M, Nafeez Ahmed L
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Deep learning used by the medical system would focus on predicting the specific diseases such as brain tumor, lung cancer, chronic kidney disease, and Parkinson's disease. To overcome the above said disadvantage the proposed work aims at a single system multiclass disease prediction using state, of, art deep learning models and web based prediction platform built with the help of Streamlit. The proposed work employs TensorFlow to train the deep learning models for specific diseases, the trained model parameters are stored with help of Python pickling and are reclaimed during prediction with the help of unpickling. Based on the selected disease the user enters either the clinical data or images which is passed through the optimized deep learning model and produces the prediction. The experimental result shows that the proposed framework is very effective and outperforms compared with the other deep learning methods with the accuracy of 99.89 % for Mask R, CNN when compared with 95.56 % and 92.47 % accuracy of FCN and LSTM respectively. It also provides integrated platform for early detection, timely treatment and effective decision making to relieve the burden on doctors. The framework proposed helps in predicting multiple disease and makes real, time prediction from a single platform.

title: GPU-Based Fast Near-Field Imaging Scheme for Rectangular-Boundary MIMO Array
authors: Shaohong Xue, Jiangtao Liu, Ze Hu, Tao Su, Jianzhong Chen
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: With the growing demand for rapid and non-contact security screening in crowded public areas, achieving high-quality millimeter-wave 3-D imaging with high computational efficiency remains a major challenge. This paper presents a Graphics Processing Unit (GPU)-based near-field imaging scheme for rectangular-boundary MIMO array. By exploiting the characteristics of the array measurement signals and the Fourier-based imaging framework, a parallel imaging architecture is developed to fully leverage the GPU massive parallel computing capability. Furthermore, an additional optimization strategy is introduced, which is tightly coupled with the GPU architecture to perform efficient transformation from spatial data to the frequency domain. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed GPU-based scheme achieves an acceleration of approximately 252 $\times$ compared with the conventional CPU implementation, while the additional optimization strategy further improves efficiency by an average of 39%, confirming the proposed method’s strong engineering practicality and real-time potential.

title: Adaptive Emotion Recognition from EEG Signals Using Multi-Domain Neural Features
authors: Ruihan Zhang, Li Zhang, Jiyuan Miao, Ziyao Zhang, Lingyan Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Emotion recognition based on electroencephalography (EEG) is an important research topic in affective computing and brain–computer interface (BCI) systems. To mitigate the limited recognition accuracy, a machine-learning-based EEG emotion recognition method is developed by integrating multi-feature representation and supervised classification. Firstly, EEG signals acquired from the frontal-pole electrodes FP1, FP2, and Fz are preprocessed using low-pass filtering, wavelet reconstruction, and α-band extraction to enhance emotion-related components. Subsequently, multidimensional features, including wavelet packet decomposition (WPD), power spectral density (PSD), and sample entropy (SampEn), are extracted to construct discriminative feature vectors. On this basis, a Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier is employed to model the emotion classification task, with k-fold cross-validation and grid-search strategies applied for parameter optimization and generalization performance evaluation. Experiments conducted on EEG data collected from 56 participants achieve an average recognition accuracy of 82.3% across two emotional classification valence and arousal. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed machine-learning-based method can effectively exploit emotion-discriminative information in EEG signals, providing a reliable solution for intelligent affective analysis and human–machine interaction applications.

title: Leveraging Artificial Intelligence for Data-Driven Decision-Making in Business Organizations
authors: Rajesh Ravindran, R. Vettriselvan, Palanivel Rathinasabapathi Velmurugan, Shiammala P N, M. Sakthivanitha
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has precipitated a profound reconfiguration of strategic management and operational efficacy by endowing contemporary business organizations with the capacity for data-driven decision making (DDDM). With a combination of high-tech analytics, machine-learning (ML) algorithms, and thought-processing, AI provides the executive leaders with the means to consume complex data, extract actionable data, and, consequently, enhance the quality of decisions made by the managers. This paper presents an empirical study of AI technologies, considering their strength to enhance evidence-based decision-making, enhance governance structures, and develop adaptive learning in organizations. Based on empirical research and theoretical definitions, the analysis outlines the provisions of AI in complementing managerial judgments, enabling real-time responsiveness, and improving the accuracy of the strategic planning process. The discussion argumentatively challenges the technological, behavioural, and ethical aspects of AI implementation, highlighting the centrality of data-driven intelligence in increasing the organizational culture and leadership behaviour. Based on the paradigms of human-AI symbiosis and predictive decision making, the manuscript states a conceptual framework that proves how AI has the potential to boost managerial thinking and reduce information asymmetries. The study highlights the inherent worth of a human-AI collaboration by stating that although AI can expedite the accuracy of the decisions made, its effectiveness in the long term is determined by accountable integration, algorithm transparency, and effective mechanisms of continuous learning. Finally, it is proposed that <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{A I}$</tex> is not only the computational device, but an inseparable strategic business partner, which can support the inclusive, well-regulated, and evidence-based management practice.

title: Distributed Photovoltaic-Storage Collaborative Planning Model Based on Improved Whale Optimization Algorithm
authors: Xin Chen, Zheling Zhou, Ling Wang, Hongshi Chen, Ming Ma, Weisheng Xu
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)
abstract: With the accelerated integration of high-penetration renewable energy into distribution network, the intermittency of distributed photovoltaic (PV) generation has become a critical bottleneck constraining the secure and stable operation of newtype power systems. Therefore, this paper proposed a distributed photovoltaic-storage collaborative planning model based on an improved whale optimization algorithm (IWOA). Considering the temporal sequence of load and distributed PV, the distributed photovoltaic-storage collaborative planning model is constructed by optimizing the temporal and spatial complementary characteristics of energy storage system (ESS) and distributed PV. The proposed model is solved using IWOA to determine the optimal siting and sizing of PV and ESS. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves network loss reduction and voltage stability enhancement while maintaining economic efficiency.

title: Multimodal Cost-Aware DRL for Beam Realignment in mmWave V2X Communications
authors: Nuri Choi, Taesik Nam, Han-Shin Jo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Millimeter-wave (mmWave) bands offer large bandwidth but suffer from blockage, making rapid beam re-alignment critical for V2X communication. Conventional beam management relies on exhaustive codebook search, causing high time-domain overhead. Vision-aided studies reduce this cost by predicting the best beam index, yet they still overlook when re-alignment should occur. To address this gap, we propose a DRL-based policy that learns optimal re-alignment timing using sector probability, temporal stability, link fluctuations, and switching rate. The vision-aided model shows stronger proactive behavior than nonvision baselines, demonstrating the importance of visual cues for early degradation detection and efficient beam management.

title: Research on Real-Time and Robust MU-MIMO Scheduling Under O-RAN Architecture
authors: Jaemin Kim, Dongwook Won, Donghyun Lee, Junsuk Oh, Chihyun Song, Seungchan Lee, Juyoung Kim, Sungrae Cho
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: The O-RAN architecture's disaggregation enables flexible PHY/MAC control via standardized non-RT and near-RT loops, but imposes a fundamental tension between the submillisecond latency requirements of the real-time (RT) control and the data-driven intelligence (e.g., for robust scheduling) envisioned for the near-real-time (near-RT) loop. This paper synthesizes two critical, complementary design philosophies-a compute-aware GPU-centric design and an uncertaintyaware chance-constrained design-contrasting their architectural choices and problem decompositions. We unify notation, state wall-clock constraints explicitly, and connect these systems to xApp-based near-RT control and robust MU-MIMO literature to extract actionable design lessons for 5G/6G O-RAN deployments.

title: Deep Learning-Based Range-Doppler Map Reconstruction for 1-bit Quantized PMCW Systems
authors: Hyunjoung Lee, Seonmin Cho, Seongwook Lee
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Reliable target detection in phase-modulated continuous-wave (PMCW) radar systems relies on the formation of high-resolution range-Doppler (RD) maps. However, the use of 1-bit analog-to-digital converters (ADCs) for high-rate sampling in PMCW radar systems retains only the sign information of the received signal, resulting in a loss of amplitude information. This quantization process introduces severe noise across the entire RD map, thereby degrading target detection performance. In this paper, we propose a U-Net-based deep learning network to reconstruct high-quality RD maps from noise-distorted RD maps obtained using 1-bit ADCs in PMCW radar systems. The network is trained using paired datasets consisting of noisedistorted RD maps and corresponding refined RD maps, where the refined maps are generated using a kurtosis-based filtering algorithm. The proposed method effectively mitigates nonlinear distortions that cannot be corrected by conventional fast Fourier transform (FFT) and windowing-based processing. Specifically, the proposed method achieves a peak sidelobe level (PSL) of -46.19 dB and a signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) of -23.14 dB, improving PSL by 31.5 dB and SINR by 13.2 dB over conventional 1-bit FFT processing. In addition, it outperforms the multi-bit ADC-based FFT processing by 18.35 dB in PSL. These results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively enhances target detection performance in PMCW radar systems using 1-bit ADCs.

title: Herbsat: Satellite Imagery for Ayurvedic Crop Detection in Hilly Regions
authors: Devaki M, Gogila Devi K, Kalai R, Kiruthika V
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: In precision agriculture, early and accurate detection of Ayurvedic crops in hill station regions using remote sensing imagery is essential for minimizing yield losses, improving crop health management, and supporting sustainable agricultural practices. This study presents an advanced, end-toend deep learning framework for Ayurvedic crop detection and prediction in hill station environments using high- resolution remote sensing images acquired from satellite and unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) platforms. The proposed multi-stage pipeline integrates advanced pre- processing, segmentation, feature extraction, and classification modules to achieve robust and reliable crop identification under diverse environmental conditions. The pre-processing stage employs Gaussian Normalization Filtering to suppress sensor noise, atmospheric interference, and illumination inconsistencies commonly found in remote sensing data, while preserving critical spatial and spectral information related to vegetation health. This normalization enhances crop-specific patterns such as discoloration, canopy variations, and texture irregularities, enabling improved discrimination between Ayurvedic crops and non-medicinal vegetation. For accurate crop region extraction, a hybrid U-Net and Mask R-CNN segmentation framework is utilized to precisely delineate Ayurvedic crop canopies and cultivation regions from complex backgrounds, including soil, shadows, overlapping vegetation, and mixed crop scenes commonly observed in hill station areas. The segmented regions are subsequently processed using a hybrid LSTM-CNN feature extraction module. The CNN component captures spatial and spectral features such as canopy color variations, leaf patterns, structural characteristics, and texture information, while the LSTM component models temporal variations across multitemporal remote sensing images, effectively learning seasonal growth patterns, environmental stress responses, and altituderelated vegetation dynamics. Final crop classification and prediction are performed using a DenseNet-based deep neural network, selected for its dense feature connectivity, efficient feature reuse, and stable gradient propagation. DenseNet demonstrates superior performance in multi-class Ayurvedic crop classification while maintaining computational efficiency suitable for large-scale agricultural and ecological monitoring systems. The proposed framework is evaluated using publicly available remote sensing datasets along with real-world field imagery, achieving an overall crop detection accuracy exceeding 98.5% and a segmentation Intersection-over-Union (IoU) score of 0.93, significantly outperforming baseline deep learning models such as ResNet50, VGG19, and MobileNet in terms of accuracy, robustness, and convergence speed. In addition to accurate crop detection, the system generates interpretable crop distribution and vegetation health maps, providing actionable visual insights for farmers, researchers, and decision-makers. The proposed approach demonstrates strong potential for large-scale, automated Ayurvedic crop monitoring in hill station regions, early resource assessment systems, and precision agriculture-driven cultivation management.

title: Analysis and Measurement of Indoor Orbital Angular Momentum Diffraction Propagation
authors: Shangling Li, Kai Wen, Xi Liao, Tao Hu
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: The spatial orthogonality of orbital angular momentum (OAM) wave provides an additional degree of freedom for wireless communications. However, research on their propagation characteristics in indoor scenarios remains limited. This paper investigates the diffraction propagation characteristics of 38 GHz OAM wave in indoor corridor environments. First, an OAM wave diffraction measurement platform was set up using a spiral phase plate (SPP) antenna. Then, diffraction propagation data under different incident angles were collected. Finally, the distribution of the diffracted received power and the diffraction gain were analyzed based on the measured data. The results show that OAM waves experience significant power attenuation, with pronounced attenuation observed near diffraction angles of 0 degrees. The proposed Bessel-modified single knife-edge diffraction model achieves mean absolute errors (MAE) below 2 dB and standard deviations (SD) under 1 dB when compared with experimental measurements.

title: Hybrid CNN-Random Forest Framework for Robust Lemon Leaf Disease Classification
authors: Taruna Sharma, Bhavna Thakur, Gaurav Sharma, Jhulan Kumar, Amandeep Kaur, Gourav Verma, Deepak Banerjee
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: The proposed study is a deep-learning based diagnosis technique with high efficiency and accuracy in diagnosing seven illnesses of lemon leaves. All the diseases are scored highly on all the four categories, accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 scores are also high on each of the classes: Alternaria leaf spot (97.09), Anthracnose (97.16), Citrus canker (97.26), Citrus leaf spot (97.25), Greasy spot (96.95), Powdery (96.84), and Zinc deficiency (97.25). The support of all classes in the dataset was fairly high, and the range of support was between approximately 1,425 ( Powdery ) and 1,645 ( Citrus canker ) with each of the classes represented very closely (nearly 13-15% ). The overall accuracy of the classes was also minimal at 99 hours, making the model reliable in terms of detecting the disease. These findings are testimony of the expertise of the suggested CNN-based method to the precise identification of citrus leaf diseases. However, it is these very high-performance measures that are especially fascinating because they will propel the real-world implementation of this solution into practice, especially enabling the detection and subsequent treatment of diseases that occur in farming. Having a high level of classification and covering all parts of the dataset, this model will enable farmers and agronomists to improve health conditions of lemon crops and prevent future losses by monitoring diseases and intervening in time.

title: Low-Complexity Spatio-Temporal Deformable Fusion for Compressed Video Quality Enhancement
authors: Gilberto Kreisler, Luis Carlos Linares, Daniel Palomino, Bruno Zatt, Attilio Fiandrotti, Guilherme Corrêa
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: The Spatio-Temporal Deformable Fusion (STDF) network has shown strong performance in video quality enhancement (VQE) by exploiting multi-frame information through spatio-temporal deformable convolutions. However, the high computational cost of STDF severely limits its applicability on mobile and embedded devices. To address this limitation, we propose the Low-Complexity Spatio-Temporal Deformable Fusion (LC-STDF) model, a novel variant of STDF designed for efficient deployment. LC-STDF incorporates a stripe-wise pruning mechanism inspired by the filter-in-filter strategy, and further optimizes the pruning process through a two-stage hyperparameter search that combines Bayesian Optimization and Binary Search. Experimental results on the MFQEv2 dataset demonstrate that LC-STDF achieves a reduction of 43.25% in GFLOPs and 56.8% in the number of parameters, with a minor decrease in quality enhancement (−0.192 dB). These results highlight LC-STDF as an effective solution for enabling real-time and energy-efficient VQE on resource-constrained devices.

title: Towards a Semantic-driven Automation of Modelling and Co-Simulation of Energy Systems
authors: Mohammadreza Taheri, Pietro Rando Mazzarino, Daniele Salvatore Schiera
publish: 2026 Open Source Modelling and Simulation of Energy Systems (OSMSES)
abstract: Co-simulation of multi-domain energy systems requires coordinating heterogeneous models with complex physical interdependencies, yet existing platforms ensure only syntactic interoperability and depend on manual semantic checks, limiting configuration to experts fluent in both domain physics and orchestration tools. We introduce an ontology-driven framework that uses formal semantic descriptions and a multi-layer validation pipeline to automate this process. The framework aligns established simulation and energy standards within a four-tier architecture comprising structural checks, ontology-derived constraint validation, semantic reasoning over knowledge graphs, and a two-stage physical assessment integrating energy-carrier compatibility with component-level feasibility. Its contributions include automatic constraint generation from ontological axioms, unified verification of electrical properties and energy-carrier semantics, interactive semantic enrichment of legacy FMUs, and persistent composite models stored as reusable graph patterns. In a microgrid case study, the framework automatically identified physically invalid connections, reduced configuration effort, and enabled domain experts to define scenarios autonomously through semantic queries over the model catalog.

title: Hazard Prejudice and Accident Prevention Intelligent Mining Surveillance with YOLOv8
authors: K Sundara Velrani, Deepak A, Deepak Raj M
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Mining industry is among the worst occupational industries whereby the employees are at the mercy of random hazards caused by the faulty machinery, poor visibility, insecure working conditions, and non-observance of safety precautions. The traditional surveillance mechanisms are more likely to be in form of manual real time monitoring on the ground but that is extremely time consuming and can be highly subject to error and is not effective in identifying any impending threat. In accordance with the challenges outlined, the proposed work will introduce a system of AI-based safety and hazard detection in the mining industry that will integrate both the advanced approaches to computer vision and intelligent alerts. The live video feeds that will be processed at varying times at the mining sites will assist in the identification of any threat with high levels of accuracy and necessary measures can be undertaken to ensure that no accident occurs. It will be integrated into a web platform based on Django to accept the incoming streams of data, document hazardous events, deliver intuitive dashboards to the supervisors. This system assists in automatic alert messages to relay messages to the safety officers on important alerts in good time. The combination of rapid visual identification and nondecentralized surveillance and automatic reporting will make certain monumental changes in the safety of mining as compared to the more conventional method offered by this proposed structure. This paper can thus substantiate the fact that AI-improved hazard detection may be an important factor in mitigating operational risks, offer improved protection to the workers and make decisions in the mining regions in accordance to data.

title: Hybrid ML Driven Multi-Cloud Service Work Load Prediction For Financial Systems
authors: Muthireddy Bhargava Sai, Sai Srinivas Vellela
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Recent studies indicate that over 70% of financial enterprises now operate across hybrid or multi-cloud environments, with global financial cloud workloads projected to surpass 350 zettabytes by 2026. However, traditional workload management and manual performance tuning approaches often fail to adapt to dynamic traffic fluctuations, causing up to 40% efficiency losses due to over-provisioning and unbalanced resource allocation. The proposed framework utilizes a Multi-Cloud Service Dataset encompassing transactional, latency, and service response parameters across distributed financial servers. The Categorical Boost based Classification and Regression Tree (CB-CART) model is then adopted to provide advanced feature extraction to utilize graduate-boosted sets of decision trees to identify service measures with significant influence on workload traffic throughput variation and Quality of Service (QoS). Subsequently, the Extra Trees CART (ET-CART) model also performs classification and regression tasks: the classification submodule makes predictions of the workload changes in three ordinal steps Low, Medium, and High, whereas the regression submodule predicts the QoS score, and thus, provides controlled scaling of the cloud resources and the continuity of the services. The CB-CART with ET-CART hybridization between CB and ET models enables the high-resolution forecasting of the workload, reduces the variability of latency and also improves the general system resilience.

title: Game-Theoretic Optimization Algorithms for Blockchain-Powered Cloud Platforms
authors: Ganji Ramanjaiah, Balajee Maram, Tummala Srinivasa Ravi Kiran, Komanduri Venkata Sesha Sai Ramakrishna
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: With the help of blockchain technology, cloud platforms enable users to establish better networks and efficiently create secured ledger management systems. Though potentially beneficial, these interactions contain important multi-agent interactions among the cloud provider, the miner/validator and the end-user, which take interdependent often conflicting decisions. The problem of integrating cloud and blockchain is modeled as an optimization problem. The phrase essentially means that the system is great for developing smart tech such as smart grid, cars and cities. We look into the non-cooperative (Nash), hierarchical (Stackelberg), and cooperative (coalition formation) as well as evolutionary (replicator dynamics) game-theoretic frameworks for resource allocation, consensus mechanism and operational costs. In an extensive experimental assessment, a blockchain-based cloud testbed has been developed to simulate a game-theoretic optimization scheme. The testbed shows that with respect to resource utilization, transaction throughput, latency, and operational cost, game-theoretic optimization performs better than any baseline and centralized models. The study demonstrates that game-theory approaches stabilize decentralized ecosystems while achieving sustainable tradeoffs among performance, cost, and trust. It opens doors to efficient and scalable blockchain-cloud platforms.

title: Gesture Controlled Contactless Switch Using Camera
authors: M. Sentamilselvi, Mohamed Salman Al Farish A, Mohammed Aathil J, Mukesh M
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Gesture-based human-machine interaction is becoming a safer, faster, and more intuitive way to control smart home systems. Traditional methods like physical switches or mobile apps require users to touch the device, which in some situations may be very inconvenient or unhygienic. In this context, we introduce a Vision-Based Smart Home Appliance Control System that allows users to operate electrical devices contactlessly using real-time hand-gesture recognition. The system uses computer vision through OpenCV, together with an ESP32 microcontroller and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4}$</tex>-channel relay module, to detect finger count gestures and execute the appropriate actions. A webcam detects the hand gesture of the user and sends the captured image to OpenCV, which counts the number of raised fingers. The recognized gesture is wirelessly sent to ESP32 by activating the right relay channel to power the appliance connected to the ESP32. This setup is particularly suited for places where touchless operation will be more vital, such as homes, hospitals, labs, and industrial settings. Tests demonstrate that gestures can be recognized quickly and accurately by the system, thus allowing appliances to respond smoothly. This approach provides a practical, effective, low-cost, and expandable means of implementing contactless smart home automation using embedded systems and computer vision.

title:
Deep Learning-Based Forecasting of Oceanic pCO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    at Station Papa: A Comparative Study of Time Series Models
authors: Gwangun Yu, Gilhan Choi, Moonseung Choi, Sun-hong Min, Junu Jang, Yonggang Kim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Predicting oceanic pCO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> is crucial for quantifying air sea carbon fluxes and enhancing projections of ocean acidification, which is essential for understanding the ocean response to ongoing climate change. This paper examines multistep forecasting of seawater pCO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> using 3-hourly marine carbon observations obtained from the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) National Centers for Environmental Information (NCEI) Accession 0100074 dataset. A unified evaluation framework is developed to compare four representative time-series forecasting architectures: LSTM, PatchTST, iTransformer, and DLinear. All models are trained using the same multivariate inputs and preprocessing pipeline, and their performance is assessed across multiple prediction horizons. The results summarize the relative prediction behavior of recurrent, transformer based, and linear approaches, providing a consistent reference for applying modern deep learning methods to marine prediction pCO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>.

title: A Distributed Learning Framework for Dynamic Skill Demand Prediction in Low-Altitude Logistics Enterprises
authors: Nanjing Chen, Jiayi Mao, Xuming Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Technology, Communication Ecosystem and Management (ITCEM)
abstract: The rapid development of the low-altitude economy has created dynamic and unpredictable demands for skilled personnel in logistics enterprises. Traditional competency modeling and training practices are limited in their responsiveness to such changes, resulting in inefficiencies in workforce development. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a distributed learning framework for dynamic skill demand prediction in low-altitude logistics enterprises. The framework integrates UAV operational data, maintenance records, and HR profiles, and employs distributed machine learning models-Random Forest and Deep Neural Network-to forecast evolving workforce competencies. A dynamic competency model is then constructed based on the predictions, which guides the design of adaptive training programs. Experimental results demonstrate that the distributed deep neural network achieves the highest performance with an F1score of 0.88, outperforming traditional baselines such as logistic regression and SVM by a significant margin. Moreover, the framework improves training coverage by 92% and enhances efficiency by 18% compared to conventional methods. These findings highlight the potential of distributed learning not only to enhance predictive accuracy but also to provide practical managerial value by optimizing training and aligning workforce competencies with operational requirements. This study extends the application of distributed learning to human resource management in emerging industries and offers a scalable solution for workforce adaptation in the fast-evolving low-altitude economy.

title: Design and Execution of Machine Learningenabled Wearable Sensor System for Real-Time Physiological Health Monitoring
authors: Sandeep Mishra, Ankita Sharma, Jawed Akhtar, Tamanna Sethi, Surender Kumar, Irfan Ahmad Pindoo
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: In the present scenario Physiological health monitoring became a major challenge for every scientist, researchers and doctors. Through this paper an attempt has been made to overcome and focus on this issue. To achieve the same from basic principles to major technologies has been used. First of all, a deep research has been done on different sensor technologies, wearable devices, different parameters, materials, human health's, environmental response and factors etc. Later a very strong and modern age technology called Machine Learning has been used to enabled the diverse wearable devices in healthcare monitoring-based issues. Initially, the basic studies of sensors and different parameters has been done in COMSOL tool later after understanding the entire basic concept machine learning concept has been combined. In this paper a comprehensive approach has been implemented for the machine learning based wearable devices which helps to monitor the human health like body temperature, ECZ, heart rate in real time. The main purpose is to execute and develop a system which is able to provide the effortless targeted outputs. As a main advantage of this paper is as an outcome there are several enhancements like almost exact and improved usability, accuracy, and responsiveness as compare to existing and conventional methods. Finally, it has a potential use for the personal as well as clinical point of view, which makes the point of research attraction and center of reputation. This research is recommended for smart cities as well as smart clinical operations.

title: Simulation and Optimization of Adaptive Control for Multi-Axis CNC Tool Paths Based on Deep Learning
authors: Rongli Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: In order to meet the demanding requirements of real time and accuracy for complex trajectory control in multi-axes CNC processing (CNC), an adaptive tool path control system based on deep learning was developed. Model the structure of the control system and coordinate transformation, design the error forecast structure of the neural network, construct the high fidelity simulation platform, and carry on the typical surface processing tasks, show that the model can reduce the average trajectory error by 53.5%, 49.0% and 48.6% in the X, Y and Z axes. The system response time is reduced by 27.5%, and the overshoot is reduced by 58.7%. Resource assessment shows that the average model inference time is 3.82 ms, while the GPU utilization remains below 60%, meeting the industrial real time control requirements. This method is more accurate, responsive, and robust than traditional control strategies.

title: A Version-Aware Metric for Measuring Innovation in Software Code Patterns
authors: A.M. Rinas, B.M.G.S.T.S.K. Wickramasinghe
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Software repositories evolve through continuous sequences of changes, ranging from minor edits to major refactorings and feature additions, yet distinguishing genuinely innovative modifications from routine updates remains challenging. This study introduces a version-aware novelty metric that quantifies code innovation by integrating structural dissimilarity with pattern rarity, formalizing the principle that each version's contribution is proportional to the magnitude of its changes. Structural divergence is measured using normalized edit distance against all previously observed patterns, while pattern rarity emphasizes uncommon code structures. The metric produces bounded, interpretable values reflecting both uniqueness and structural innovation. We define three version-aware measures: global novelty, capturing a function's distinctiveness relative to repository history; initial drift, quantifying cumulative deviation from the first implementation; and local change, representing immediate modifications between consecutive versions. Together, these measures form an evolution trace vector, enabling precise monitoring of incremental updates, major refactorings, and innovation peaks. The methodology is language-agnostic, computationally efficient, compatible with standard Git workflows, and demonstrates sensitivity to both substantial rewrites and fine-grained edits, providing actionable insights for automated innovation tracking, risk-aware code review, and AI-assisted development.

title: Post Quantum Signature for Blockchain
authors: Rohit Razdan, Manisha J. Nene
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Advancements in quantum processing technology threaten the core security mechanisms that protect contemporary distributed ledger platforms. Hyperledger Fabric, an enterprise- focused, permissioned ledger developed under the Linux Foundation’s open-source umbrella, caters to organizational priorities including data seclusion, expansion capabilities, and regulated user involvement. In contrast to decentralized public networks such as Bitcoin and Ethereum, Fabric incorporates verified entities, flexible validation protocols, and streamlined verification routines. Despite these strengths, its dependence on the Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm (ECDSA) exposes it to vulnerabilities from Shor’s computational method, which efficiently reconstructs confidential keys from exposed counterparts, thereby jeopardizing transaction validity, genuineness, and irrefutability. This study advocates for the incorporation of quantum-secure cryptographic techniques (PQC), particularly the CRYSTALS-Dilithium authentication protocol, into Hyperledger Fabric employing a merged authentication paradigm that fuses ECDSA with Dilithium. This blended strategy yields stratified defenses, comparable to redundant safety systems in vehicles, delivering endurance to quantum incursions while preserving synergy with established infrastructures. Initial testing demonstrates negligible impacts on operational efficiency coupled with notable bolstering of protective measures, facilitating the evolution of fortified, quantum-immune commercial ledgers that sustain enduring credibility and informational steadfastness. Keywords—Hyperledger Fabric, Permissioned Ledger, Quantum-Safe Cryptography, CRYSTALS-Dilithium, ECDSA, Quantum Safeguard, Ledger Steadfastness

title: Reducing Delay and Network Load through Adaptive Threshold-Based Rate Control in IoT Systems
authors: Pushpendra Singh, Somnath Sinha, Binayak Dutta
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Some of the major challenges in managing IoT networks, which are normally resource-constrained, come from restricted bandwidth, processing power, and energy supplies. Traditional random transmission usually leads to network overload, increased packet delays, and inefficient use of resources. This paper reviews smart rate control mechanisms designed for IoT networks that have limited resources. We analyze and contrast random baseline transmission against threshold-based adaptive control methods by way of extensive simulation runs under realistic network scenarios via the Contiki-Cooja framework. Our experimental results have shown that threshold-based rate control can achieve as much as 31% reduction in average packet delay and 62% reduction in network load when compared with traditional random transmission techniques. Threshold-based rate control represents a deployable and practical solution that properly balances the tradeoff between performance enhancement and computational ease and thus is a good match for real-world IoT deployments over actual resource-constrained networks. Hybrid machine learning, multiobjective optimization, federated learning, and context-aware mechanisms might be potential avenues of future research toward enhancing the performance of IoT systems.

title: Ensemble Enhanced Residual Network Based Non-Contact Blood Pressure Estimation Using CW Radar
authors: Vuong Tri Tiep, Hoang Thi Yen, Van-Phuc Hoang, Nguyen Huu Son, Chu Anh My, Guanghao Sun
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Continuous and unobtrusive blood pressure (BP) monitoring is essential for early detection and management of cardiovascular disorders. However, traditional solutions still rely on contact-based sensors or intermittent cuff measurements. This study proposes a deep learning framework for estimating systolic blood pressure (SBP) and diastolic blood pressure (DBP) using non-contact continuous-wave radar signals recorded under resting conditions. The method integrates a rigorous preprocessing pipeline, including artifact removal, phase reconstruction, feature normalization, and physiologically informed window extraction, with a data augmentation strategy designed to increase robustness against variability in radar micro-motion signals. Proposed one-dimensional Ensemble Enhanced Residual Network (EnE-ResNet) is developed to jointly regress SBP and DBP from four radar-derived feature channels (I, Q, phase, and magnitude). Evaluation on a publicly available resting-state radar dataset of 30 individuals demonstrates that the proposed approach achieves accurate BP estimation, with SBP and DBP mean absolute errors of 5.28 mmHg and 4.52 mmHg, respectively, and strong correlations with reference measurements. These findings highlight the potential of integrating radar sensing with deep learning to enable unobtrusive BP monitoring in future Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) applications.

title: Interpretable Deep Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic Truck Dispatching in Open-Pit Mining
authors: Francisco Rosales, Angelo Diaz
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Dynamic resource allocation under uncertainty remains a central challenge in operations research, particularly in capital-intensive industries such as open-pit mining, where inefficient dispatching decisions can lead to substantial productivity losses and increased operational costs. This paper presents a Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) framework for the dynamic truck assignment problem in openpit mining, with a dual emphasis on performance optimization and policy interpretability. The problem is modeled as a Markov Decision Process and implemented within a discrete-event simulation environment that captures the stochastic behavior of truck-shovel interactions. A Deep Q-Learning approach with neural network function approximation is employed to learn adaptive dispatching policies directly from simulated experience. To promote transparency and industrial applicability, Explainable Artificial Intelligence techniques based on Shapley values are applied to interpret and validate the learned decision strategies. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework substantially outperforms heuristic dispatching methods while providing interpretable insights that support trust and deployment in real mining operations.

title: Quantitative Analysis of Academic Procrastination and Its Impact on University Students' Performance
authors: Qurat Ul Ain Zam Zam Mirza, Rimsha Kanwal, Humaira Ashraf, NZ. Jhanjhi, Anggy Pradiftha Junfithana
publish: 2025 15th International Conference on Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Computer Science and Statistics (MACS)
abstract: The growing rate of procrastination among university students is of great challenge in terms of academic performance and well-being. The present study investigates the connection between academic performance and procrastination behavior based on quantitative and cross-sectional study design. A structured online questionnaire was used to gather data on 42 student participants who included 25 undergraduate students and 17 postgraduate students who participated in the study and passed the online questionnaire. The scale contained seven Likert scale measures of procrastination behaviors and a selfreported cumulative grade point average (CGPA) of academic performance. The analysis data were performed with the help of Python (Pandas, NumPY, and SciPy) with descriptive and inferential statistics. Pearson correlation test showed that the negative correlation between procrastination and CGPA is strong and statistically significant (r = −0.863, p; ¡ 0.001), which means that an increase of the level of procrastination correlates with a decrease in academic achievement. This correlation was also supported by group analysis in which there were lower CGPA scores among those students that had a high procrastination rate. The findings emphasize the issue of procrastination as one of the determinants of academic performance. The research paper suggests adopting time-management workshops, academic mentoring and computerized tracking tools to prevent the problems of procrastination and increase performance of higher education students.

title: IoT-Enabled Heart Disease Prediction Using CatBoost and Hyperledger Fabric
authors: Bhavesh Kumar, Ashutosh Shukla, Tanisha Gupta, J Sathish Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: Cardiovascular disease (CVD) is the major cause of deaths in the world, killing millions of people every year. The idea of detecting in time is critical to enhancing the results of the treatment, and the new technologies in the sphere of the Internet of Things (IoT) have provided the opportunities to monitor the health continuously and in real-time. This article presents a combined model of IoT-based sensing, machine learning, and blockchain technology to obtain precise prediction of heart disease. Heart rate, blood pressure, ECG parameters are constantly monitored with the help of wearable IoT devices, which makes it possible to predictively monitor cardiac conditions. Collected data are then processed with CatBoost machine learning algorithm, with the performance of the algorithm optimized with the use of the Optuna library of hyperparameter optimization. The model has a remarkable prediction accuracy of 978 percent, which is better than traditional classifiers and demonstrates the effectiveness of gradient improvement in the evaluation of cardiovascular risk. In addition, permissioned blockchain platform, Hyperledger Fabric is in use that guarantees safe and regulated access to confidential health records.

title: Real-Time ETL for EAV-Modeled Clinical Data: A CDC-Based Approach Using OpenMRS
authors: Weerakutti G.D.W. C., Chathumini K.G. L., Kumara B.T.G. S., Bacher I.
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Electronic Medical Record systems widely use the Entity-Attribute-Value data model for representing heteroge-neous clinical data, but attribute-centered queries execute 3-5 times slower than relational queries. Existing batch-based Extract-Transform-Load solutions introduce latency unsuitable for real-time dashboards, alerts, and clinical decision support. This paper presents a real-time ETL framework integrating Change Data Capture, stream processing, and metadata-driven transformation to continuously flatten EAV data. Implemented as a Spring Boot application orchestrating Apache Flink jobs with declarative YAML configurations, the system uses the Flink MySQL CDC Connector and a concept-based horizontal expansion algorithm for EAV pivoting. Evaluation using OpenMRS demonstrated processing of 1,000 CDC events per second with sub-second latency, reducing data staleness compared to batch tools while enabling automatic schema adaptation, providing a practical, scalable solution for real-time clinical analytics in resource-constrained health information systems.

title: Cross-Lingual Transfer Learning for Swahili Automatic Speech Recognition Using Parameterefficient Fine-Tuning and Data Augmentation
authors: Silas Wataki, Lawrence Nderu, Jason Githeko
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) technologies have fundamentally reshaped human-computer interaction, yet the benefits remain disproportionately concentrated in high-resource languages. This study addresses the critical dual challenges of data scarcity and high computational barriers that hinder the development of robust ASR systems for Swahili, a language spoken by approximately 200 million people. We establish a resource-efficient framework that leverages cross-lingual transfer learning by adapting the multilingual openai/whisper-small end-to-end model. To circumvent the prohibitive hardware demands of full-model fine-tuning, the methodology employs ParameterEfficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) via Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) and integrates a rigorous data augmentation pipeline comprising Gaussian noise, pitch shifting, and time stretching. Trained on 13.5 hours of validated Swahili speech from the Mozilla Common Voice corpus using a single graphics processing unit (GPU), the model achieves a Word Error Rate (WER) of 13.32 % on the test set. This performance quantitatively surpasses existing benchmarks trained on significantly larger datasets or using different architectures. These results demonstrate that the synergistic application of multilingual pretraining, PEFT-LoRA, and synthetic data augmentation provides a scalable, cost-effective pathway for extending state-of-the-art speech technologies to underresourced languages under constrained computational settings.

title: Improving Cloud Load Management with an Enhanced Lion-Based Metaheuristic Approach
authors: T. Manigandan, T. Thanigasalam, V. Ganesan
publish: 2025 15th International Conference on Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Computer Science and Statistics (MACS)
abstract: Load balancing is an important aspect of cloud computing which ensures better utilization of resources, fault tolerance, reduced response time, and more throughput. Among the drawbacks of the conventional and even some modern load balancing algorithms, including various bio-inspired algorithms, one would find inefficient job relocation, poor response time, reduced throughput, and local optima. To eliminate these concerns and problems, this paper proposes a new Bio-Inspired Enhanced Lion optimization Meta-Heuristic Method to effectively balance loads in cloud-based systems. The proposed approach enhances the primary Lion Optimization Algorithm by introducing adaptive features that increase the exploitation and exploration capabilities ensuring that more overworked units will be found and that early convergence will be avoided. The strategy is implemented by the CloudSim simulator and the effectiveness is evaluated against the accepted benchmarks in literature. In comparison to the old and the existing bio-inspired algorithms, the experimental results indicate that the proposed model is more successful in turnaround time, productivity, job relocation efficiency and fault tolerance. This piece of work contributes to the development of more reliable and scalable load balancing methodology to support the modern cloud computing environment.

title: FastAPI-Based Multilingual Interface for DenseNet121-Driven Diabetic Retinopathy Classification
authors: Ruthwick Sai P, Shanmuga Prabha P
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is a leading cause of vision loss worldwide, making early and accurate detection essential. This paper presents a deep learning–based three-class DR classification system using DenseNet121 trained on 5,000 retinal fundus images. The model effectively distinguishes No DR, Mild or Moderate DR, and Severe DR, achieving high diagnostic accuracy. A key contribution of this work is the deployment of the trained model through a FastAPI framework integrated with a multilingual conversational interface enabling real-time, user-friendly interaction in multiple languages. This is the first study to combine a DenseNet121 based three-class DR classifier with FastAPI deployment and multilingual conversational support within a unified workflow. The proposed system provides not only strong classification performance but also an accessible and scalable platform suitable for tele-ophthalmology applications. The novelty of this work lies in integrating DR image classification, real-time system deployment, and multilingual patient communication into a single, fully deployable diagnostic tool.

title: An IoT-Integrated ESP32 Smart IR Remote with Dynamic Learning and User-Centric Customization
authors: Vaishnavi Khare, Akash Chaturvedi, Tushant Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: This paper introduces UniMote, an IoT-enabled universal IR remote system designed to solve the problem of managing multiple remote controls. The system is built around an ESP32 microcontroller, which handles all IR communication using integrated transmitter and receiver modules. A companion mobile app provides a straightforward interface for users to add devices, customize buttons, and change modes. Its key feature, Learning Mode, allows the system to acquire new IR codes in real-time, ensuring it works with a wide variety of appliances. By using Firebase for cloud storage and a RESTful API, the system offers both scalability and reliability. The result is a simple, cost-effective solution that allows a single smartphone to control any IR-based device, making modern environments smarter and more user-friendly.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning Assisted Task Offloading and Resource Allocation in Satellite Edge Computing
authors: Zhaodong Ding, Haixia Cui
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: Space based communication system is an important component of space-air-ground integrated network (SAGIN), which complements ground networks to achieve a wider range of network functions and meet a growing demand for network functionality performance. This paper presents a novel deep reinforcement learning (DRL) based strategy for task offloading and resource allocation within a three-layer satellite network architecture. This strategy aims to allocate tasks and resources reasonably in satellite network, combining DRL with low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite priority offloading and optimizing latency/energy consumption while ensuring the corresponding success rate. Our proposed strategy demonstrates significant improvements in latency, energy consumption, and task success rate across all key metrics compared to baseline algorithms.

title: Understanding Sustainable Growth in Industrial Open Source Software Through Collaboration Networks
authors: Yuming Shi, Ruokun Xu, Fanyu Han, Yingjie Wang, Xiaoya Xia, Shengyu Zhao, Qinyue Zhu
publish: Computer
abstract: This article examines the evolutionary dynamics of the FreeCAD ecosystem by combining longitudinal OpenRank measurements with multidimensional collaboration network analysis, revealing a co-evolutionary pattern that supports sustained ecosystem growth.

title: Hyperspectral Unmixing via Nonconvex Low-Rank and Weighted Sparsity Constraints
authors: Jiaqiang Zhou, Fan Li, Shaoquan Zhang, Hongyu Zhang, Yuyang Liu, Chengzhi Deng, Shengqian Wang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: The pervasive mixed-pixel problem in hyperspectral imaging has spurred significant research interest in sparse unmixing (SU) techniques, which offer a powerful solution to this challenge. In hyperspectral unmixing, the low-rankness of the abundance matrix is generally approximated via the nuclear norm in a convex manner. However, the nuclear norm tends to over-penalize large singular values, limiting its ability to adequately capture the global spatial correlations. A novel algorithm termed dual-weighted nonconvex low rank Sparse Unmixing (DWNLSU) is proposed to meet this challenge. This approach makes two primary contributions: (i) the introduction of dual weighting mechanisms to jointly promote row-wise sparsity in the abundance matrix and spatial sparsity within individual endmember abundance maps; and (ii) the adoption of a more flexible weighted Schatten p-norm (WSNM), replacing the nuclear norm as the nonconvex low-rank regularizer. This model not only approximates the rank function more effectively but also accounts for the importance of different singular values, thereby improving the characterization of global spatial correlations. To enhance unmixing performance, a nonconvex optimization framework is established, along with a fast algorithm based on the Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM). In synthetic hyperspectral experiments under high noise levels, the method exhibits remarkable robustness.

title: Implementation and Experimental Evaluation of a Frog-Chorus-Based Autonomous Decentralized Collision Avoidance Method for LoRa Networks
authors: Dai Kojima, Hiroyuki Yasuda, Song-Ju Kim, Maki Arai, Jin Nakazato, Mikio Hasegawa
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Low-power wide-area (LPWA) technologies for the Internet of Things (IoT) enable long-range and long-term data acquisition with minimal energy consumption. However, as the number of devices transmitting within the same coverage area increases, packet collisions become more frequent, leading to degraded communication stability and efficiency. Conventional collision avoidance schemes are not well suited to high-density and dynamically changing IoT environments, and often fail to meet the low-power requirements of battery-operated nodes. To address this issue, we previously proposed an autonomous and decentralized collision avoidance scheme based on the Kuramoto model with smallest-phase-difference intermittent inhibitory coupling, and demonstrated its ability to reduce the packet error rate (PER) on LoRa devices. Nevertheless, the Kuramoto model exhibits only a small phase response for small phase differences, resulting in slow separation of transmission timings and prolonged collision occurrences. In this study, we propose an autonomous decentralized collision avoidance method based on the frog chorus model, whose phase response is strong for small phase differences and weak for large phase differences. The contributions of this paper are threefold: (i) we design and formulate a frog-chorus-based inhibitory coupling rule that accelerates the separation of transmission timings in dense networks, (ii) we conduct a fair simulation-based comparison using normalized coupling strengths and show substantially faster convergence than the Kuramoto-based approach, and (iii) we implement the proposed algorithm on 50 LoRa devices and validate its effectiveness through PER measurements, demonstrating its applicability to large-scale IoT networks.

title: COMPAREIT - An Intelligent Multi-Platform Price Comparison and Sentiment-Driven Product Recommendation System
authors: Aishwarya S, Anupriya V M, Veronica Philo Vimal J, Saranya M, Swarna Gowsalya P, Kalaivani V
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: In the era of digital e-commerce, Customers rely more and more on price comparisons and online reviews to make informed selections about what to buy. The rapid rise of e-commerce sites such as Amazon and Flipkart has generated large numbers of product data and product reviews, making it challenging to draw significant conclusions. This study presents an intelligent system that automates price comparison and sentiment-driven product suggestions. Using web scraping techniques, the suggested framework finds the two cheapest products, collects product prices from Flipkart and Amazon, and then examines user reviews to choose the best option. For sentiment analysis SVM, LSTM & RoBERTa are utilized. A comparative analysis of these models demonstrates that RoBERTa outperforms SVM and LSTM in terms of Precision, Recall, F1-score & improve recommendation accuracy by capturing complex perspectives in user reviews and using its strong contextual understanding to provide more accurate recommendations. In order to assist users in making wellinformed purchasing decisions, the system also integrates future price prediction utilizing the Prophet model to predict possible pricing trends. In order to ensure prompt response, an automated email alert system notifies users in real time when product prices fall below a predetermined threshold. Results from experiments show how well the system works to improve user experience across e-commerce platforms, optimize purchases, and support customer decision-making.

title: Research on Harmonic Mitigation Methods Considering the Governance Potential of Grid-Connected Inverters
authors: Guanqiao Li, Hao Bai, Hongliang Peng, Ruotian Yao, Shui Liu, Pengda Yan
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)
abstract: With the advancement of the ‘Dual Carbon’ goals, the integration of large-scale renewable energy has exacerbated the harmonic issues in distribution networks. Although some scholars have studied the controllable harmonic mitigation capabilities of grid-connected inverters, challenges remain in scenarios involving the coordinated mitigation of harmonics by multiple inverters. These challenges include the lack of diverse evaluation dimensions for mitigation potential and insufficient coordination optimization mechanisms. To address these issues, this paper proposes a harmonic coordination optimization method based on multi-dimensional mitigation potential assessment. First, the harmonic mitigation potential of grid-connected inverters is clearly defined as the harmonic compensation capacity that the inverter's remaining capacity can provide, under the premise of meeting active power output demands. This capacity must meet four criteria: technical feasibility, economic viability, safety margin, and reliability. Based on this, an evaluation system is constructed, including six quantifiable parameters, overcoming the limitations of traditional single-dimensional assessments. Next, a dynamic evaluation model based on game theory and combined weighting is proposed. By integrating the subjective experience weights from the Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP) and the objective data weights from the entropy weight method, a TOPSIS-RID comprehensive evaluation model is developed for the collaborative optimization of multi-inverter harmonic mitigation potential. This model enables the full-dimensional, quantifiable, and graded assessment of harmonic mitigation potential for multiple inverters. On this basis, a multi-objective optimization model is established, aiming at meeting the harmonic voltage distortion rate standard and minimizing mitigation costs. These costs primarily refer to the harmonic mitigation service fees paid by photovoltaic power stations or energy storage systems. Additionally, safety constraints such as voltage deviation and current harmonic content are set. The improved particle swarm optimization algorithm is used to optimize the collaborative compensation capacity of multiple inverters. Finally, the proposed algorithm is validated in an IEEE 33-bus system. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed model can coordinate multiple high-potential grid-connected inverters to mitigate harmonics during instances of harmonic overstandard, achieving harmonic mitigation while minimizing mitigation costs. This effectively verifies the model's superiority in improving both the power supply quality and operational economy of the distribution network.

title: RivetNet-GAN: A SPR Rivet Clutter Removal and Weak Signal Reconstruction Model Based on Generative Adversarial Networks
authors: Jiale Si, Peng Zhou, Hongyuan Jing, Fangqi Chen
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: Rivet clutter is the main source of interference in Surface Penetrating Radar (SPR) detection, as its strong reflections mask defect signals and severely compromise interpretation accuracy. Traditional methods exhibit limited generalization, while supervised deep learning requires paired data that is impractical to obtain. This paper presents RivetNetGAN, an unsupervised generative adversarial network that reformulates clutter removal as a domain adaptation task, translating data from the rivet-clutter domain to the clutter-free domain. By integrating adversarial training with a local contrastive loss, the model enforces structural consistency without needing paired examples. The lightweight generator incorporates residual blocks and hybrid attention mechanisms to enhance feature learning. Experiments on simulated data demonstrate that RivetNet-GAN effectively suppresses clutter and reconstructs weak signals, achieving superior structural fidelity (SSIM $\gt96.5 \%$) and outperforming state-of-the-art methods like Cycle-GAN and CUT. This provides a robust, data-efficient solution for intelligent SPR processing, showing high promise for practical applications.

title: Boosting Mars Image Classification with Limited Labels: A Swin Transformer to ResNet Semi-Supervised Distillation Approach
authors: Weiming Lin, Ting Zhang, Xianxian Luo, Wenxi Liu, Yiyou Guo
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: This study focuses on high-precision classification of Martian surface images under the constraint of limited annotated data. To address the challenge of difficult image annotation, we propose a semi-supervised teacher-student learning method that facilitates knowledge transfer from a Swin Transformer Tiny model as the teacher to a ResNet-18 model as the student. The approach incorporates an energy-based dynamic filtering mechanism to select reliable pseudo-labels, along with a dual data augmentation strategy combining weak and strong transformations to enhance robustness under label scarcity. Experiments on the HiRISE Mars surface image dataset demonstrate that using only $20 \%$ of the labeled data, the method achieves up to $94.92 \%$ accuracy ($94.44 \%$ on average) and a macro F1-score up to $83.06 \%(81.07 \%$ on average), significantly outperforming the fully supervised baseline while maintaining high efficiency. These results validate the efficacy of the proposed semi-supervised distillation framework for Mars image classification and offer a practical tool for Mars geological analysis.

title: AI Driven Email Triage and Action Bot for Smarter Email- to-Action Conversion
authors: Blessy Abidha B S, Vijayetha S T, Bhuvaneswaran B
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: In today's world, professionals are receiving a huge number of emails, which are mostly urgent, time-sensitive, or task- based. It takes too much time to manually sort, prioritize, and answer emails, and there is a risk of missing out on such important communication. To solve this problem, the project introduces an AI-Driven Email Triage and Action Bot that is created with UiPath and is powered by Natural Language Understanding (NLU) and Agentic AI. The bot moves beyond the convention of keyword-based filters and understands the context of the emails by evaluating the content, urgency, intent, sender, and subject relevance. The emails are automatically sorted in folders like High Priority, Finance, Meetings, and Projects. The main features to come out of the gate are Email Summarization through which long email threads get summarized in brief highlights, and Email-to- Audio, thus enabling professionals to listen to the emails while doing other things. The local bot, through Context-Aware Action Execution, is able to send a response automatically, forward, flag, or add a reminder, thereby facilitating the response cycles to speed up without any manual intervention. Its adaptive learning is always getting better as it keeps changing according to the user's preference over a period of time. The intelligent, automated tool that is available at the moment lessens the burden on the brain, provides for swift attention to priority tasks, and improves productivity, hence it can be considered as a substantial advancement in the field of smart communication and inbox management.

title: Study on Circulating Current Characteristics and Influencing Factors of Metal Sheath in 110 kV SingleCore Power Cables
authors: Ke Wu, Yanli Wang, Yecheng Yin, Yunsong Qiao, Baiqiao Zhao, Jian Wu, Qianglin Fu, Tianyun Chen
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: This Aiming at the loss and power grid safety issues caused by the circulating current of the metal sheath of $110 k v$ high-voltage single-core cables, this study constructed a circulating current model based on the theory of electromagnetic induction, developed a circulating current calculation interface using VB language, and through comparative analysis of the influence of horizontal, right-angle, triangular and mixed connection arrangement methods, as well as the uniformity of crossinterconnection segments on the circulating current, the following conclusions were drawn: The triangular pattern has the smallest total circulation in a single arrangement, while the mixed arrangement can reduce the total circulation by optimizing the phase spacing. Uneven segmentation leads to a sharp increase in single-phase circulation, but the total circulation is nearly zero. The inter phase distance is positively correlated with the circulating current, providing important technical support for the design and operation and maintenance of high-voltage cable projects and having significant engineering application value.

title: PRIME: Progressive Retinex Guided Diffusion for Zero-Shot Low-Light Image Enhancement
authors: Zhidong Zhu, Hui Yi, Bangshu Xiong, Xing Li, Zhibo Rao, Jinhao Zhu, Shuzhen Yu, Qiaofeng Ou
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: Recent zero-shot learning low-light image enhancement methods often rely on the generative priors of pretrained diffusion models to bypass complex constraints and improve generalization. However, as these models are trained on normally exposed datasets, they struggle to recover stable structural priors under low-light conditions. To address this, we propose PRIME, a novel framework that integrates Retinex decomposition into pretrained diffusion sampling. We progressively preserve the input reflectance component throughout the diffusion sampling trajectory, ensuring that original structural features remain unaffected by misleading structural priors. Simultaneously, the illumination component serves as an external prior to guide the sampling process toward generating realistic brightness distributions, thereby achieving stable enhancement without compromising detail. Experimental results show that PRIME outperforms existing zero-shot methods in both visual quality and quantitative metrics, producing well-lit images with preserved texture and structure. Code is available at https://github.com/Archaic-Atom/PRIME.

title: Conceptual Aspects of Intelligent Systems in Production
authors: Liudmila P. Kozlova, Olga A. Kozlova
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
