abstract: Intellectualization is a necessary reality of modern production. This article analyzes the structural concepts of such systems and presents methodologies that ensure optimal operating parameters.

title: Prediction Method for Dynamic Susceptibility of Landslides Based on the ConvLSTM-Transformer Hybrid Model
authors: Shijun Wang, Xiaopeng Li, Yangzhou Feng, Qing Xu, Lu Lin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: The prediction of the dynamic susceptibility of landslides is of vital importance for the prevention and control of geological disaster risks. Most traditional models rely on static environmental factors or a single temporal analysis method, making it difficult to effectively capture the spatiotemporal dynamic evolution characteristics of landslides. To this end, this paper proposes a ConvLSTM-Transformer hybrid model, which integrates the local spatiotemporal feature extraction ability of the Convolutional Long Short-Term Memory network (ConvLSTM) with the global time-dependent modeling advantage of the Transformer. Firstly, the spatiotemporal correlations in multi-source remote sensing, topography and meteorological data are hierarchically encoded using ConvLSTM; Secondly, the long-term temporal dependencies are captured through the self-attention mechanism of the Transformer, and the weights of environmental factors are dynamically quantified. Finally, a dynamic susceptibility prediction model is constructed by combining historical landslide data. This model outperforms the comparison models in both F1-Score and AUC values, and can significantly improve the prediction timeliness of seasonal raininduced landslides. The research provides new methodological support for the dynamic assessment of regional landslide risks.

title: Xami : E x pert-Aware A daptive Compression for Mi xture-of-Experts on Sparse Tensor Cores
authors: Johnson Umeike, Bahar Asgari
publish: IEEE Computer Architecture Letters
abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models reduce active computation through sparse routing; yet their significant memory overhead due to retaining the full parameter footprint of all experts remains a deployment challenge. To address this, we present Xami, an expert-aware adaptive compression framework that exploits routing heterogeneity to apply differentiated structured sparsity across experts. By profiling per-layer activation distributions, Xami preserves frequently activated experts at higher fidelity while more aggressively compressing infrequent ones, all while remaining compatible with native 2:4 Sparse Tensor Core (SpTCs) hardware execution. Across different MoE architectures, Xami consistently improves model-level compression over uniform 2:4 sparsity with minimal accuracy loss and competitive inference throughput. Our results demonstrate that expert heterogeneity can be translated into practical memory savings, achieving up to 2.4× compression over dense compared to just 1.8× achieved by uniform 2:4, without modifying the underlying hardware execution model, providing a highly desirable path toward more efficient MoE inference.

title: ASIC-based Fetal Heart Rate Sensing System using Dry/Capacitive Electrodes
authors: Yijing Zhang, Alessandra Galli, Myrthe van der Ven, Beatrijs van der Hout-van der Jagt, Sotir Ouzounov, Mohammed Meftah, Massimo Mischi, Eugenio Cantatore, Pieter Harpe
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Engineering
abstract: Objective: Fetal heart rate (fHR) monitoring is a critical tool for assessing fetal well-being. A home-use solution that enables comfortable and long-term monitoring is of great interest. Dry and capacitive abdominal electrodes are considered promising techniques for this purpose. However, prior systems are typically based on discrete components, whereas application-specific integrated circuit (ASIC) designs enable a smaller form factor, lower power consumption, and the ability to integrate advanced motion artifact effect reduction (MAR) techniques. This work proposes an ASIC-based system to address the limitations of prior discrete component designs. Methods: A 16-channel recording ASIC with an integrated MAR technique was designed in 180nm CMOS. A prototype was developed using this IC, together with a wearable garment and an exemplary fHR extraction algorithm. The IC was electrically characterized, and the prototype was validated through both in-vitro and in-vivo experiments. Results: The chip achieves the required performance and MAR through a 10x extended dynamic range (>1 V) and saturation prevention, while occupying only 12.5 mm2 and consuming 450 μW. Validation with four pregnant volunteers using dry electrodes shows that within each 24-minute recording, at least 3 minutes of data provided sufficient quality for the detection of fetal QRS-peaks and fHR. Conclusion: The IC-based solution offers low power, small form factor, MAR, and the ability to obtain fHR in real use cases. Significance: The proposed design is the first IC-based solution for dry and potentially capacitive fHR monitoring, which proves to be a promising candidate for home monitoring applications.

title: Theoretical Performance Study for mmWave/THz ISAC Systems with Sensing-Based Beam Alignment
authors: Zi-Jie Wang, Aimin Tang, Xudong Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: To combat severe path loss, millimeter-wave and Terahertz integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) systems rely on accurate beam alignment (BA) to targets (e.g., vehicles, drones, etc.) in vehicular networks. This paper establishes a theoretical framework to analyze sensing-based BA performance and derive corresponding bounds for the high-frequency ISAC system. It leverages angle information estimated from the sensing sub-system for BA. To analyze the performance tradeoff and derive bounds, a hybrid time-bandwidth resource regulation scheme between sensing and communication (SAC) sub-systems is proposed, allowing for modeling SAC as multiple-access to ISAC channels. A critical challenge in theoretical analysis is the mutual dependency between BA and angle estimation: imperfect BA reduces estimation accuracy; inaccurate angle estimation hinders BA. Therefore, one-way analysis from estimation accuracy to BA performance is inherently inaccurate. Through beam misalignment loss derivation, closed-form angle estimation accuracy is quantified as a function of sensing time-bandwidth sharing and beam misalignment. To decouple the mutual dependency, an iterative procedure is introduced as a theoretical analysis tool, where the beam is continuously aligned to the estimated target direction. It reveals sensing-based BA limits, demonstrating how estimation accuracy ultimately affects BA over the long term. Finally, fundamental performance bounds on SAC rates are derived, capturing both direct impacts of resource regulation and its indirect influences through sensing-based BA. Both theoretical analysis and numerical results show that an ISAC system with sufficient sensing time-bandwidth resources effectively provides accurate angle estimation for BA.

title: SurfaceXR: Fusing Smartwatch IMUs and Egocentric Hand Pose for Seamless Surface Interactions
authors: Vasco Xu, Brian Chen, Eric J. Gonzalez, Andrea Colaço, Henry Hoffmann, Mar Gonzalez-Franco, Karan Ahuja
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Mid-air gestures in Extended Reality (XR) often lead to fatigue, discomfort and imprecision, limiting their suitability for extended use. Surface-based interactions offer a compelling alternative, providing improved accuracy, speed, and comfort. However, current egocentric vision-based methods struggle with reliable surface inputs due to challenges in hand tracking and surface plane estimation from oblique and occluded viewing angles. To this extent, we introduce SurfaceXR, a novel sensor fusion approach that combines headset based hand tracking with micro-vibration data sampled from commodity smartwatch IMUs to enable precise and robust inputs on everyday surfaces. Our system is designed with flexibility in mind — it can function using only hand tracking, only IMU sensing, or optimally with both modalities combined, and remains robust even without explicit surface calibration. Our key insight is that these modalities are complementary — hand tracking provides 3D positional data of hand joints, whereas IMUs supply high-frequency wrist/hand motion data. Our user study across 21 participants validates SurfaceXR's effectiveness in augmenting surface touch tracking and 8 class hand-surface gesture recognition, demonstrating significant improvements over single-modality approaches. Enabled by SurfaceXR, we demonstrate a series of interactive apps for both AR and VR, ranging from on-surface sketching, text entry and gesture-based navigation.

title: Look2React: Making VR NPCs Come Alive with Dynamic Vision-Guided Reactions
authors: Ritik Vatsal, Xincheng Huang, Robert Xiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: A central promise of virtual reality (VR) games is the increased control players have over their character through pose and body language. However, many non-player character (NPC) systems fail to respond convincingly to these poses, user intent, and situational context, limiting immersion. We present Look2React, an interaction system that captures what NPCs see, using a vision-based reasoning model to select pose and text responses. Look2React endows NPCs with the ability to react dynamically and appropriately to player interactions. Through a gaze and proximity-based detection system inspired by stealth games, we trigger our system intuitively and only when intended, while also reducing resource costs. We invited 20 participants to play two versions of an RPG game: one with NPCs based on contemporary games and the other with Look2React NPCs. Our results demonstrate that Look2React increases engagement, leading to more frequent and repeated interactions with NPCs. Participants reported more satisfactory play sessions, significantly increased feelings of social presence, and felt that the dynamic reactions gave the NPCs more depth and personality — ultimately making them feel more human.

title: Distribution Network Optimization for Low-Carbon Energy Supply in Data Centers: A Boundary-Interpretable Feasible Region Method
authors: Weihao Yin, Tiance Zhang, Gengyin Li, Jianxiao Wang, Ming Zhou, Qiang Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: With the proliferation of lightweight generative artificial intelligence, data centers (DCs) are increasingly deployed within distribution networks (DNs). Their highly stochastic and high-power demands pose significant challenges to achieving low-carbon and secure power supply. Thus, coordinating the operation of DNs and DCs is critical for ensuring reliable low-carbon electricity delivery. However, significant barriers persist due to the integration of numerous distributed energy resources (DERs) and operational privacy requirements among stakeholders. To enable secure coordination while safeguarding model confidentiality, a boundary-interpretable feasible region (BIFR) method is proposed in this paper. This method can capture the intrinsic relationship between the controllable variables and the boundary responses that affect both carbon emissions and system safety performance. Based on the BIFR, a bidirectional coordinated optimization mechanism for DC-DN systems is established. The mechanism determines optimization strategies for low-carbon collaborative operation according to the coupled BIFR by iterative bidirectional adjustments until convergence. This mechanism achieves efficient DC-DN coordination through minimal information exchange while preventing privacy breaches. Case studies on modified IEEE 33-bus and Caracas 141-bus distribution networks validate the effectiveness and efficiency of the proposed method.

title: When Effort Becomes Visible: Facet-Level Shifts in Evaluation and Workload during VR Teamwork
authors: Zheng Wei, Hyeonmin Lee, Yawei Zhao, Junxiang Liao, Hao Li, Xian Xu, Hayoung Oh, Lik-Hang Lee, Wai Tong, Pan Hui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Surfacing peers' workload and speed can reshape collaboration in VR, yet prior work often conflates social cues with environmental load. We isolate the social channel with TRACE-VR, which holds geometry, physics, rules, and timing constant while independently manipulating effort identifiability (traceable vs. anonymous) and peer effort (pace) (high vs. low). In a 2×2 within-subjects study (n = 32), each participant worked with nine scripted co-actors for three minutes, transporting 16 crates along a self-chosen path between fixed pickup/drop points; co-actors followed fixed pre-authored routes. We measured intrinsic motivation, social–evaluative load, NASA–TLX, and behavioral/process outcomes. Our findings show that identifiability and higher peer effort (pace) each modestly increased completion rates, with asymmetric effects suggesting partial substitution between accountability cues and normative pace. Perceived monitoring and temporal demand depended on their combination—identifiability raised monitoring at low pace and amplified time pressure at high pace—while composite TLX and finishers' times remained comparable across conditions. Thus, these cues chiefly determine who finishes rather than how fast finishers move. Motivation did not uniformly increase under higher pace. We frame identifiability and pace as partially substitutable levers for shaping social–evaluative experience and facet-level motivation, and outline tempo-aware, accountability-aware guidance for collaborative VR.

title: An Accuracy-Preserving Multiphysics FETI-DP Framework for Electromagneto-Thermo-Mechanical Analysis
authors: Qi Qiang Liu, Jiawen Li, Hongyang Li, Shuai Zhang, Absalom E. Ezugwu, Wen-Sheng Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article presents an accuracy-preserving unified multiphysics domain decomposition framework for electromagneto-thermo-mechanical analysis, which is based on the dual–primal finite-element tearing and interconnecting (FETI-DP) method. The framework adopts a compatible mesh discretization across different physical fields over their overlapping computational regions, avoiding interpolation inconsistencies commonly reported in nonmatching multiphysics mesh coupling. Leveraging the structural properties of FETI-DP, including flexible subdomain partitioning and robust dual–primal interface constraints, the method establishes a consistent multiphysics decomposition structure without requiring monolithic global system assembly and factorization. Multiphysics coupling is executed at the subdomain level with unified interface constraint operators, enabling consistent construction of cross-physics source terms and interface conditions within each coupled subdomain. Within this framework, vector and scalar physical fields are discretized using operator-compatible high-order approximation spaces, with field-dependent discretization orders to support mixed vector–scalar and mixed-operator accuracy requirements while preserving interface compatibility. Numerical experiments show strong agreement with reference solutions and demonstrate the robustness and applicability of the proposed framework for large-scale radio frequency (RF) multiphysics simulations.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Resource Sharing and UAV Trajectory Optimization in Multi-Operator UAV-Assisted Wireless Networks
authors: Atefeh Hajijamali Arani, Xavier Fernando, Omar Alhussein, Yeying Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Multi-operator unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV)-assisted wireless networks introduce new challenges in coordinating aerial base stations, ensuring fair resource sharing, and dynamically adapting to user mobility. Most existing studies either focus on single-operator scenarios or do not account for fairness in inter-operator load exchange. To address these limitations, we propose a hierarchical deep reinforcement learning framework that jointly optimizes inter-operator resource sharing and UAV trajectory design. At the operator level, we introduce a novel load exchange balance metric to ensure equitable cooperation and prevent disproportionate benefits. At the UAV level, deep reinforcement learning enables autonomous three-dimensional (3D) trajectory control to enhance throughput and reduce outages. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed framework significantly outperforms benchmark schemes in terms of fairness, user rate, throughput, and outage, thereby validating its effectiveness in complex multi-operator environments.

title: Integrated Fault Location Method Using Feeder-Centric Graph Neural Networks for Renewable-Penetrated DC Distribution Networks
authors: Lai Wei, Kai Liao, Bo Li, Jianwei Yang, Zhengyou He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid
abstract: This paper proposes a fault location method for DC distribution networks (DCDNs) based on graph neural networks (GNNs), which integrates the fault line selection (FLS) and fault distance estimation (FDE) that are conventionally handled independently. The proposed method focuses on the analysis of feeders, including FLS of multiple feeders and FDE of a single feeder. Specifically, a feeder-as-node graph is constructed, where synchronous measurement data are extracted as node features, ensuring consistent dimensionality and enhanced learning efficiency. Moreover, the proposed method explicitly embeds the logical interdependencies between FLS and FDE into the structural design and parameter updating mechanism. An output processing module is designed to estimate the fault distance by analyzing the FLS results, ensuring the model utilizes the data of the DCDN system-level information rather than a single feeder. Furthermore, a two-stage pre-training strategy is introduced to improve stability and generalization, in which partial parameters are frozen. The effectiveness and generalization of the proposed method are verified by hardware-in-the-loop experiments.

title: S-MDMA: Sensitivity-Aware Model Division Multiple Access for Satellite-Ground Semantic Communication
authors: Hui Cao, Rui Meng, Shujun Han, Song Gao, Xiaodong Xu, Ping Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Satellite-ground semantic communication (SemCom) is expected to play a pivotal role in convergence of communication and AI (ComAI), particularly in enabling intelligent and efficient multi-user data transmission. However, the inherent bandwidth constraints and user interference in satellite-ground systems pose significant challenges to semantic fidelity and transmission robustness. To address these issues, we propose a sensitivity-aware model division multiple access (S-MDMA) framework tailored for bandwidth-limited multi-user scenarios. The proposed framework first performs semantic extraction and merging based on the MDMA architecture to consolidate redundant information. To further improve transmission efficiency, a semantic sensitivity sorting algorithm is presented, which can selectively retain key semantic features. In addition, to mitigate inter-user interference, the framework incorporates orthogonal embedding of semantic features and introduces a multi-user reconstruction loss function to guide joint optimization. Experimental results on open-source datasets demonstrate that S-MDMA consistently outperforms existing methods, achieving robust and high-fidelity reconstruction across diverse signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) conditions and user configurations.

title: ConfIC-RCA: Statistically Grounded Efficient Estimation of Segmentation Quality
authors: Matias Cosarinsky, Ramiro Billot, Lucas Mansilla, Gabriel Jimenez, Nicolás Gaggion, Guanghui Fu, Tom Tirer, Enzo Ferrante
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Imaging
abstract: Assessing the quality of automatic image segmentation is crucial in clinical practice, but often very challenging due to the limited availability of ground truth annotations. Reverse Classification Accuracy (RCA) is an approach that estimates the quality of new predictions on unseen samples by training a segmenter on those predictions, and then evaluating it against existing annotated images. In this work we introduce ConfIC-RCA (Conformal In-Context RCA), a novel method for automatically estimating segmentation quality with statistical guarantees in the absence of ground-truth annotations, which consists of two main innovations. First, In-Context RCA, which leverages recent in-context learning models for image segmentation and incorporates retrieval-augmentation techniques to select the most relevant reference images. This approach enables efficient quality estimation with minimal reference data while avoiding the need of training additional models. Second, we introduce Conformal RCA, which extends both the original RCA framework and In-Context RCA to go beyond point estimation. Using tools from split conformal prediction, Conformal RCA produces prediction intervals for segmentation quality providing statistical guarantees that the true score lies within the estimated interval with a user-specified probability. Validated across 10 different medical imaging tasks in various organs and modalities, our methods demonstrate robust performance and computational efficiency, offering a promising solution for automated quality control in clinical workflows, where fast and reliable segmentation assessment is essential. The code is available at https://github.com/mcosarinsky/Conformal-In-Context-RCA.

title: A Lightweight Wrist Exoskeleton with Equivalent-Input-Disturbance-Based Control for Pathological Tremor Suppression
authors: Mingyuan Xie, Jian Huang, Jinhua She, Yan Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: Developing a wearable exoskeleton that effectively suppresses pathological tremor while remaining lightweight for daily use is a significant challenge. Furthermore, the complex and time-varying nature of wrist dynamics often limits the performance of conventional model-based control strategies. To address this, this study proposes a lightweight (less than 200 g) wrist exoskeleton. By employing a compact transmission architecture to amplify the torque of a miniature motor, the design achieves the high-fidelity force transmission required for active suppression while minimizing physical burden. Additionally, ergonomic sponge-lined fixtures are integrated to ensure user comfort and adaptability. To tackle the challenge of unmodeled wrist dynamics and time-varying tremor, an Equivalent-Input-Disturbance (EID) control strategy is developed. This method treats complex nonlinearities and tremor torque as a lumped disturbance, estimating and compensating for them in real-time without requiring precise patient-specific modeling. Pilot clinical validation with five patients (four with PD and one with ET) demonstrated significant tremor attenuation, with a power suppression ratio ranging from 89.37% to 96.37%. Furthermore, the root-mean-square error during voluntary motion tracking was reduced by 21.3%. These preliminary findings suggest the feasibility and potential efficacy of the proposed system.

title: A Highly Integrated Resonant Coupled Brushless Electrically Excited Synchronous Motor – Based on Winding Reuse and Dual-Frequency Coupling
authors: Yuqi Zhang, Beibei Song, Shumei Cui, Shuo Zhang, Shuai Ren, Hongwen Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: Brushless excitation overcomes the speed limitations and low reliability of electrically excited synchronous motors (EESMs). However, existing brushless schemes still face challenges in simultaneously achieving high integration and high efficiency. This paper presents a highly integrated Resonant Coupled Brushless Electrically Excited Synchronous Motor (RCBESM) with an internally embedded brushless excitation structure. By reusing the stator winding and injecting dual-frequency currents, the fundamental component generates torque, while the high-frequency component resonantly transfers excitation energy to the rotor. Comparing with conventional brush and harmonic excitation EESMs, the proposed RCBESM achieves higher integration without external excitation hardware while maintaining superior efficiency. The brushless excitation subsystem achieves an efficiency exceeding 80%, and the overall machine efficiency is improved by more than 0.4% compared with that of conventional harmonic excitation schemes. Finally, experimental validation under high-frequency excitation regulation, dual-frequency torque generation, and variable speed/load conditions demonstrates that the proposed motor exhibits excellent steady-state efficiency and dynamic performance.

title: Self-Organizing Edge Computing Distribution Framework for Visual SLAM
authors: Jussi Kalliola, Lauri Suomela, Sergio Moreschini, David Hästbacka
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Localization in a known environment is an essential capability for mobile robots. Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) addresses this by combining real-time tracking with computation-intensive map optimization, which can present a challenge for resource-limited robots. Edge-assisted SLAM approaches that offload heavy computation while maintaining real-time tracking onboard offer a potential solution. In this article, we propose a novel self-organizing VSLAM framework that provides a general structure for distributing existing keyframe-based VSLAM systems across a network of devices. The framework introduces a state management model for handling shared SLAM state among devices and a distribution policy for orchestrating the distribution in a self-organizing manner. To demonstrate the framework, we implemented it for monocular ORB SLAM3 using a three-layer architecture. The distributed SLAM was evaluated in both fully distributed and standalone configurations and compared against the original ORB SLAM3. The experimental results show that the proposed framework achieves comparable accuracy and resource utilization to ORB SLAM3. Moreover, the system can revert to standalone SLAM when network connectivity is lost, demonstrating effective self-organization. Our code is publicly available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/JussiKalliola/VSLAM-distribution-framework</uri>

title: Solving the WiFi Sensing Dilemma in Reality Leveraging Conformal Prediction
authors: Honglu Li, Qiufan Ji, Cong Shi, Yan Wang, Jerry Q. Cheng, Kailong Wang, Minge Xie, Yingying Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: With the extensive deployment of smart environments and IoT devices, WiFi sensing has proven its significant convenience and contact-free sensing capabilities in supporting a wide range of applications. However, designing a ubiquitous WiFi sensing system for diverse real-world scenarios presents a substantial dilemma, as the system performance deteriorates when the testing data diverges significantly from the training data due to domain variations. To address this dilemma, existing studies need extra efforts to develop new features or even retrain the original model under environmental variations. However, these approaches have not efficiently resolved the dilemma. In this study, we conduct a comprehensive study on the domain variation problem to make WiFi sensing robust and accurate in practical applications. Our definition of domains is comprehensive and includes environmental conditions, surrounding settings, user differences, user orientations, user's positions relative to WiFi sensors, and user participation time frames. We design a novel conformal prediction framework that quantifies the conformity (i.e., similarity) between the testing and training WiFi samples, then labels the testing samples with the most probable class(es). Unlike traditional conformal prediction which relies on data from a single domain, we develop a new statistical (Type I) approach to assess the conformity of the testing WiFi samples to individual training domains and aggregate the outcomes. To further improve the framework's generalization, we design a (Type II) fusion approach that utilizes the inter-relationships among domains for more accurate conformity quantification. Built upon these two methods, kernel density estimation-based and SVM-based methods are developed to compute the conformity scores for new testing samples to make conformal predictions. Extensive experiments, utilizing both self-collected and publicly available datasets show that our framework can improve prediction accuracies ranging from 20.1% to 77.1% in three of the most representative WiFi-based applications across six types of domain variations.

title: Gap Series Regulator for Spacecraft Electrical Power Systems
authors: P. Granello, L. Schirone
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Modern spacecraft increasingly demand high-voltage buses for high-power loads, yet raising Solar Array (SA) voltage drives risks and qualification costs. This paper proposes a dual- bus power architecture that provides a higher-voltage bus alongside a lower-voltage battery bus without imposing higher voltage requirements on the SA. The SA is series-connected with the battery to enable Direct Energy Transfer (DET) from the SA to the higher-voltage bus, while a single bidirectional DC/DC converter, connected in series Partial Power Processing (PPP), regulates the higher-voltage bus, and, in unregulated- bus mode, executes maximum power point tracking (MPPT). Because the higher-voltage bus is tightly regulated by the DC/DC converter, MPPT dynamics are decoupled from bus impedance during load transients, preserving bus voltage stability. Processing only a fraction of the differential power between load demand and the SA's DET contribution reduces processed power, converter rating, mass, and losses. An analytical model of the architecture is derived, and a laboratory prototype validates the model, the operating principle, and SA- bus decoupling with experimental measurements that closely match theory.

title: A Federated Deep Learning Framework for Cell-Free RSMA Networks
authors: S. Ali Mousavi, Mehdi Monemi, Reza Mohseni, Matti Latva-aho
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Next-generation wireless networks are poised to benefit significantly from the integration of three key technologies (KTs): Rate-Splitting Multiple Access (RSMA), cell-free architectures, and federated learning. Each of these technologies offers distinct advantages in terms of security, robustness, and distributed structure. In this paper, we propose a novel cell-free network architecture that incorporates RSMA and employs machine learning techniques within a federated framework. This combination leverages the strengths of each KT, creating a synergistic effect that maximizes the benefits of security, robustness, and distributed structure. We formally formulate the access point (AP) selection and precoder design for max-min rate optimization in a cell-free MIMO RSMA network. Our proposed solution scheme involves a three-block procedure. The first block trains deep reinforcement learning (DRL) neural networks to obtain RSMA precoders, assuming full connectivity between APs and user equipments (UEs). The second block uses these precoders and principal component analysis (PCA) to assign APs to UEs by removing a subset of AP-UE connections. The final block fine-tunes the RSMA precoders by incorporating the associated APs into a second DRL network. To leverage the distributed nature of the cell-free network, this process is implemented in a Federated Deep Reinforcement Learning (FDRL) structure operating through the cooperation of APs and a central processing unit (CPU). Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed FDRL approach performs comparably to a benchmark centralized DRL scheme. Our FDRL approach, provides a balanced trade-off, maintaining high performance with enhanced security and reduced processing demands.

title: An Adaptive Denoising Framework for TECIS Full Waveform LiDAR Based on Bayesian Optimization and Structure-Aware Filtering
authors: Dejun Wang, Yanqiu Xing, Yuanxin Li, Yifei Hou
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: The Terrestrial Ecosystem Carbon Inventory Satellite (TECIS) provides highly dense full waveform LiDAR data for global carbon monitoring. However, in forest environments, the complex canopy structure and terrain introduce significant background noise, making effective signal extraction challenging. To address this issue, an adaptive waveform denoising framework is proposed in this study. Bayesian optimization (BO) is first employed to adaptively determine the effective signal threshold for each waveform by minimizing structural volatility within background noise segments, thereby achieving reliable separation between effective signals and background noise. Subsequently, a structure-aware adaptive Gaussian filtering (SAAF) method is developed. Residual noise in the effective signal is suppressed by dynamically adjusting the Gaussian kernel width based on structural factors, including notch density, flat peak characteristics, peak skewness, and multipeak structures. The results indicate that, compared to the official TECIS parameter extraction (TPE) method, the BO method removes an additional 5.14% of redundant background noise energy, yielding a cleaner, more physically realistic effective signal. Furthermore, relative to manually annotated effective signal start and end points, the localization accuracy of the extracted start and end points is improved by 77.5% and 78.3%, respectively. For effective signal smoothing, SAAF maintains high precision across varying slopes and forest types, with peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) consistently exceeding 44 dB and the correlation coefficient (CC) remaining above 0.96. This framework provides reliable, structurally complete waveform data for subsequent inversion of forest structure parameters.

title: A 24–30-GHz Sideband Reuse Transmitter With High IRR and High LOFT Suppression in 180-nm CMOS
authors: Qiuxi Jiang, Yizhu Shen, Yifan Wang, Kaibo Zhang, Sanming Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: This paper presents a 24-30 GHz sideband reuse transmitter with high image rejection ratio (IRR) and high local oscillator feedthrough (LOFT) suppression for 5G millimeter wave communication. Based on the Hartley heterodyne structure, the proposed sideband reuse scheme significantly reduces the required LO bandwidth for covering the 24–30 GHz RF output range. The LO bandwidth is narrowed to 2 GHz from the 6 GHz required in conventional heterodyne transmitters. As a result, excellent IRR and LOFT suppression are achieved across the operating band using a narrowband and simple-structure I/Q LO generation network. Moreover, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$g_{\text {m}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-boosting, linearity-enhanced mixer and power combining technique are employed in the transmitter to achieve high gain and output power. The proposed transmitter is fabricated in low-cost 180-nm bulk CMOS process. It delivers superior IRR greater than 37 dBc and LOFT below −41 dBc across the 24-30 GHz. The small signal 3-dB gain bandwidth spans from 24 to 30 GHz, with a peak gain of 21.7dB attained at 27.5 GHz. The complete transmitter achieves a measured saturated output power (P<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">sat</sub>) of 16.5 dBm with 11.1% power-added efficiency (PAE) and the output 1-dB compression point (OP<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\mathbf {1dB}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) of 14.8 dBm at 1.8V supply. Furthermore, with the 64-quadrature amplitude modulation (QAM) modulated signal, the proposed transmitter achieves an average output power of 8.7dBm at 27.5 GHz with the adjacent channel power ratio (ACPR) of −32.7 dBc.

title: Adaptive and Storytelling Practices of Panamanian Autism Families: Towards Culturally-Situated Digital Storytelling
authors: Elizabeth del Carmen Castillo, Debbie Alvarado Latino, Cecilia Aracelly Fonseca Sanchez, Annuska Zolyomi
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Human-Computer Interaction

title: Exploring Direction Alignment and Discrepancy Standardization for Knowledge Distillation
authors: Dingyao Chen, Xiao Teng, Xiang Zhang, Xun Yang, Long Lan

title: Securing Digital Images with AI-Augmented NonNegative Matrix Factorization for Tamper Detection
authors: N. Kurinjivendhan, T. Saravanan, D Thilagam, B. Senthilkumaran, B. Sribharathi, K. Sivakami
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: A secure hashing method and AI-enhanced Non-Negative Matrix Factorization (NMF) form the foundation of this study’s strong image authentication and tampering detection framework. Hamming distance comparisons between generated image hashes are used by the suggested system to reliably verify and effectively detect image manipulation. The framework is tested on the GenImage and CASIA datasets, focusing on challenging tasks like cross-generator image identification and degraded image classification. It undergoes testing in difficult circumstances, such as image compression, blurring, and low resolution. Using the GenImage dataset, experimental results show that the suggested method achieves exceptional classification accuracy $97.21 \%$, confirming its effectiveness in practical settings.

title: CNN-Based Weather Image Classification Using VGG16 Transfer Learning
authors: Nikita Yadav, Swati Jagtap, Harshvardhan Patil, Shrushti Shetti, Vishaka Aglave, Neha Patthe
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: This work focuses on Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), which are among the most advanced methods for recognizing complex patterns in images. Instead classifying the image one by one layer in CNN which is a time consuming and computationally heavy task, we employ transfer learning using the well-known VGG16 architecture which is a powerful CNN that has already learned to interpret a wide variety of images through training on the massive ImageNet dataset. Therefore, by using this models pretrained knowledge we can extract meaningful features even if the dataset is limited in size. These features are then passed into a custom-built classifier which identifies the image as one of four weather categories we have used in the dataset: Cloudy, Rain, Shine, or Sunrise. The results shows that the model is highly accurate and can be used in various applications like autonomous vehicles, climate tracking, and technologies for smart cities. This work shows how deep learning can lead machines to learn about the weather like humans do.

title: Optimization Configuration and Economic Analysis of Energy Storage Based on Information Gap Decision Theory
authors: Yuting Wang, Leyi Wang, Xiao Xu
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: This study proposes an optimization framework for energy storage to enhance profitability in ancillary service markets. A life-cycle cost model and a revenue model for ancillary services are developed, followed by an optimization configuration under a two-part electricity tariff that incorporates battery characteristics and industrial load profiles. Information Gap Decision Theory (IGDT) is applied to balance robustness and economic performance, yielding two scheduling strategies—risk-averse and risk—seeking-that guide system operation. A regional case study with numerical experiments validates the model’s effectiveness and compares the economic outcomes of different ancillary services, offering insights for future investment decisions.

title: Intelligent Drawing Review Model based on SDD Analysis
authors: Zeyin Song, Zutao Shen, Xuehai Xiong, Yuxiang Li, Jing Huang, Yu Wang
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: Aiming at the industry pain points such as the low efficiency of the review of the design drawings of power secondary circuit and the easy omission of inspection, this paper proposes an intelligent review model based on the Secondary circuits Digital Description file (SDD), which realizes the full-automatic compliance verification through multimodal data fusion and dynamic rule reasoning. Firstly, the system uses a hierarchical architecture design, and the model is composed of SDD analysis layer, topology modeling layer, multimodal drawing analysis layer, rule reasoning and report generation layer, forming a closed-loop process of “data analysis feature alignment rule matching feedback optimization”. Secondly, the XML structure is parsed based on the SDD parsing engine and the original data is converted into a structured format. The topological map and standardized equipment library are output by constructing the adjacency matrix. Then, in the multi-modal drawing analysis layer, Multi-Scale improved YOLOv8 (YOLOv8-MS) is introduced to detect and identify the components and text in CAD drawings, and visual features and topological features are extracted based on visual transformer. Finally, the interactive drawing audit report is generated through compliance verification and audit classification alarm. The experimental results show that under the same experimental conditions, the completeness coverage of the proposed model, The specification compliance rate and topological consistency are the largest, $98.12 \%, 95.52 \%$ and $94.12 \%$ respectively. At the same time, its audit time is the shortest, and its performance is superior to other comparison methods.

title: Keeper-Assisted Dynamic Comparator: A Low-Power and High-Speed Approach
authors: Seikh Husnine, Md Abid Ali, Sakil Ahmed, Deep Chandra Upadhyay, Ankur Beohar, Ashish Kumar Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: In modern electronics, faster circuitry is preferred and latched comparators are favored over static ones due to their use of positive feedback, which speeds up output charging. This paper introduces a dynamic latch comparator design enhanced with a sleep transistor and a keeper transistor to achieve energy efficient, higher speed and minimized power-delay-product (PDP). The keeper helps maintain charge during evaluation, preventing charge sharing at the dynamic node and reducing power by limiting unnecessary current paths. The sleep transistor acts as a switch during standby mode to cut off power and reduce leakage. Implemented using the gpdk90 library in Cadence Virtuoso, this design offers improved linearity, reliability and power efficiency compared to existing circuits.

title: Somatosensory Control Robotic Arm
authors: Chuixin Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: In recent years, technology has been constantly advancing, and somatosensory interaction technology has also developed rapidly. Robotic arms are the most common terminal for automation devices in the field of robotics, and many high-intensity and repetitive tasks can be improved in work efficiency through the use of robotic arms. At present, in the industry, the interaction between robotic arm devices and users is poor, the control efficiency is poor, and the maneuverability and scalability need to be further improved. To address these issues, a motion sensing robotic arm car has been designed based on STM32. The robotic arm is installed on the car, and the gyroscope collects hand posture data. After processing by STM32, the posture of the robotic arm device is controlled through Bluetooth. At the same time, the software upper computer controls the movement of the car through Bluetooth, and various data are displayed on the upper computer for easy user viewing. This design effectively combines the robotic arm with the trolley, which has good reference value for studying the control of robotic arms. At the same time, this design can also be applied to field exploration and explosive discharge scenarios, and has good research value.

title: Sports Action Teaching Correction System Combined with Intelligent Pose Recognition Technology
authors: Long Zhou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computing, Robotics and System Sciences (ICRSS)
abstract: In order to improve the intelligent correction effect of physical education teaching and facilitate students to carry out self-help training, this paper proposes a sports action teaching correction system combined with intelligent pose recognition technology. This paper proposes a lightweight human pose estimation network Lite-CCA-HRNet, which introduces cascade channel attention. By designing a lightweight and cheap deep convolution change module to reconstruct the high-resolution human pose estimation network, the amount of model parameters and floating-point calculations are greatly reduced. At the same time, multi-scale feature fusion module is used to enhance the fusion effect between different scale features and improve the robustness of human pose estimation network. Finally, cascade channel attention is introduced to learn the importance of each channel feature, which helps the model pay more attention to key information and enhances the feature expression ability of the model. Combined with experimental research, the effectiveness of the model is verified, and the correction effect of sports action teaching is further improved.

title: Implementation of Solar Powered Polyhouse with Automated Irrigation, Environmental Monitoring and Control
authors: Edward Jerome, Abhishek C R, Anand V Kurian, Rohith V R, Ginu Ann George, Tomson Thomas
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Computing and Applications (AICAPS)
abstract: The usage of energy and automated farming can easily results in more efficient cultivation of basic crops. This paper primarily focuses on the implementation of a polyhouse house which runs completely from the solar power where a good growing condition for the crops is maintained inside this polyhouse. As the system completely uses solar energy an overall environment can be reduced to a large extent. In this polyhouse, temperature, humidity, light are controlled in such a way to get the optimal plant growth. This method of polyhouse is very much efficient as compared with the traditional methods. When it is implemented in a large scale a good yield from the crops can be effortlessly achieved. The major advantage of the system is the usage of automated irrigation system. As a result of this, water can saved which makes this system a primary choice in areas facing water scarcity. Moreover, this sensible way gives this polyhouse a unique appeal. A cleaner environment with minimal emmisions can attained with the correct installation and monitoring of this type of sytems. The mainteinance expenditure will be very low because it needs only basic sensors to operate.In the long term it is so much beneficial to the farmers since the cost of production is minimum.

title: Visual Analytics of Personalized Meal Recommendations Using Deep Neural Models
authors: P. Parthiban, M. Varsha, C. Ardhra. Hari, M. Senthilkumar, S Indhumathi, B. Karthikeyan
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: With more users following diverse dietary preferences, nutritional needs, and cultural practices, personalized meal recommendation is becoming increasingly important in today's large scale catering environments. The traditional rule based and collaborative filtering approaches usually cannot capture these heterogeneous requirements. This research presents DeepCater Net, a Hybrid Deep Meal Recommendation Network (HDMRN), which integrates user features, nutritional attributes, ingredient semantics, and contexts such as time and season. The model maintains three distinct embedding networks for users, meals, and context, followed by a dense fusion layer to learn joint interactions among them. Extensive experiments on real world catering data demonstrate that DeepCater Net attains Precision@5 of 0.72 and Recall@10 of 0.63, outperforming the benchmark classical CF, matrix factorization, and neural collaborative filtering by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10 - 25 \%$</tex>. Our ablation results reveal that the contextual embeddings provide an additional 8% improvement in ranking metrics. The training-validation loss curves confirm stable convergence and very negligible overfitting. These results establish DeepCater Net as a precise, scalable, and context aware framework that serves well for next generation personalized catering systems.

title: Design of Textile-Integrated Patch Antennas using Various Fabrics Substrates for ISM Band Applications
authors: V. Femina, M. Thiruveni
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The Substrate material in Microstrip Patch Textile Antenna (MPTA) plays a crucial role in ensuring the antenna performance. It decides mechanical stability, operating frequency, efficiency, size, and bandwidth and also balances impedance values. Hence it is necessary to focus in choosing suitable substrate material for designing MPTA. The electrical properties such as relative permittivity, loss tangent and thickness of the substrate decides the characteristics of the substrates. The electrical properties are not similar even for same fabrics. For instance, the electrical properties of one Jeans (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$J n_{1}$</tex>) fabric substrates may differ from electrical properties of another Jeans (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$/ n_{2}$</tex>) fabric substrate. Each configuration (ex. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$J n_{1}$</tex> or <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$J n_{2}$</tex>) of fabric substrates exhibit unique radiation characteristics like return loss, gain etc. of a textile antenna, since the performance of MPTA is decided by the substrates. So it is necessary to analysis various configurations of textile substrates suitable for targeted applications. Mostly textile antennas are used for wearable applications, hence it is expected to operate in ISM band. This article focuses in analysing three different textile substrates such as Cotton, Jeans and Felt when it is used in designing textile antenna that operates in ISM band. The electrical properties of various configurations in each fabric are retrieved from existing research articles. Initially, the MPTA was designed to operate in ISM using FR4 Substrate. Then the MPTA are designed using cotton fabrics substrates having different electrical properties namely (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$C t_{1}, C t_{2}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$C t_{3}$</tex>), Jeans fabrics substrates <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(J n_{1}, J n_{2}\right.$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left.J n_{3}\right)$</tex> and Felt fabrics substrates <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(F t_{1}, F t_{2}, F t_{3}, F t_{4}\right.$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F t_{5}$</tex>). Their operating range, resonant frequency, return loss and bandwidth are simulated and compared. From the comparison, among three different fabric substrates, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$M C t_{1}, M J n_{1}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$M F t_{1}$</tex> exhibit better results in terms of operating range, resonant frequency, return loss and bandwidth while operating in ISM band.

title: Establishing Minimum Elements for Effective Vulnerability Management in AI Software
authors: Mohamad Fazelnia, Sara Moshtari, Mehdi Mirakhorli
publish: Computer
abstract: This article discusses minimum elements for artificial intelligence (AI) vulnerability management and the establishment of an artificial intelligence vulnerability database supported by an AI Bill of Materials for structured model documentation as well as highlighting challenges and gaps in AI vulnerability management.

title: Lightweight Multi-Key Homomorphic Encryption for Scalable Federated Learning
authors: Himani Bhatt, Saurabh Rana, Mandeep Mittal
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Homomorphic Encryption (HE) is a cryptosystem that allows computation over the encrypted data. Multi-key Homomorphic Encryption (MKHE) is a notion of HE which may perform arithmetic operations on ciphertexts that have been encrypted with multiple keys. To allow secure computation across multiple data sources, this notion uses asymptotically optimum approaches. This paper proposes a MKHE scheme based upon BFV which has wide range application in secure computation between multiple data providers. Specifically, the proposed protocol guarantees the confidentiality of the plaintext data of multiple parties during computation. The experiment is conducted with CNN model and shows efficiency in communication and computation costs.

title: Sybil Attack Detection in Industrial Internet of Thing Network Using Hybrid Model
authors: Adnan Nadeem, Amir Mehmood, Mohammad Zubair Khan, Muhammad Ashraf, Syed Saood Zia, Shakir Karim Baksh, Hani Almooamari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: The Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) describes to the incorporation of the Internet of Things (IoT) with artificial intelligence (AI) in automating processes in sophisticated industrial things and supply chains. IIoT facilitates smart manufacturing, asset tracking, supply chain optimization, energy management, and remote monitoring and control. However, these IIoT applications are vulnerable to various cyberattacks. This paper focuses on the sybil attack, in which an attacker within a distributed system uses multiple fake identities to achieve their objectives. To study this, we meticulously generated a dataset of 100 node attribute instances by simulating an IIoT environment. We then performed preprocessing, including applying the SMOTE technique to address class imbalance. Initially, we implemented four baseline models and compared their performance with our proposed hybrid model combining GNN and transformer models. The simulation results demonstrate high accuracy of proposed model in detecting the sybil attacks.

title: An Edge AI-Powered Health Monitoring System for Dairy Cattle
authors: Devi P.P, Nuka Vamshi Krishna, Nalla Venkata Soma Prudhvi, Samineni Dileep Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: The health and well-being of cattle are very important to dairy farming and the farming industry as a whole, where early disease detection can enhance animal welfare while limiting financial loss. In this research paper's low-cost and scalable Internet of Things-based system, an Arduino UNO, ESP8266 microcontroller, and some sensors are combined to monitor cows' vital parameters such as temperature, humidity, heart rate, and respiratory rate continuously. For monitoring live animal conditions, real-time data is forwarded to the ThingSpeak cloud platform and visualized through ThingView. Python's Pickle serialization is employed for inserting a machine learning-based predictive model trained on both realtime and synthetic data into a Streamlit web application. The model predicts with an accuracy of 80% to 95% in a controlled test environment, categorizing the cattle's health status as Normal, Fever, Heat Stress, or Respiratory Infection. The proposed solution caters to small and large-scale livestock farms with realtime, remote, and smart diagnostics for cattle health, thus meeting the limitations of manual monitoring. The term Edge AI refers to performing preliminary data analysis and health inference directly on local microcontrollers (Arduino and ESP8266), thereby reducing cloud dependency and enabling faster, on-farm decision-making.

title: Investigation of Deep Learning Based Beam Steering for Metamaterial Antennas Integrated in UAVs for Enhanced Inter-Drone Communication
authors: M P Harikrishnan, Ajay Kumar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Computing and Applications (AICAPS)
abstract: The proliferation of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) for surveillance, logistics, and disaster relief requires high capacity, low-latency, and directional inter-drone communication links. Conventional codebook-based beam-forming techniques are limited by slow reconfiguration and poor adaptation to mobility. This paper proposes a deep learning-enabled beam steering framework for a $16 \times 16$ reconfigurable metamaterial antenna integrated on UAV platforms. The framework couples open source electromagnetic (EM) solvers-openEMS and Meep-with Python-based deep learning libraries such as PyTorch and TensorFlow to jointly model, train, and validate antenna configu rations. Supervised convolutional neural networks (CNNs), deep reinforcement learning (DRL), and hybrid CNN+DRL strategies are evaluated to predict optimal phase states of metasurface unit cells in real time. Simulation results indicate sub-3◦ beam pointing error and $\mathbf{5}-\mathbf{1 0} \mathrm{ms}$ reconfiguration latency per update, yielding $20-30$ The proposed method paves the way for adaptive, scalable, and low-cost aerial communication networks suitable for Beyond 5G and 6G scenarios. Index Terms-Metamaterial antennas, beam steering, deep learning, reconfigurable intelligent surface, UAV communications, reinforcement learning, opensource electromagnetic simulation

title: Performance Analysis of Semi-Synchronous SAR ADC Using DLL
authors: Vikas Tiwari, Aswathi Krishnan, R. K. Nagaria
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: This manuscript presents a SAR ADC topology that enhances the distribution of conversion time by amalgamating semi-synchronous timing and a delay-locked loop (DLL) to generate clocks. The versatile nature of SAR ADC design permits its utilization with various forms of digital-to-analog converters (DACs). Our methodology entails the assessment of SAR ADC performance with the integration of DLL and the inclusion of a DAC at the output signal using a capacitor. By eliminating timing dependencies on analog delays, our approach facilitates improved conversion speed compared to asynchronous ADCs. The proposed methodology's functionality was verified through transistor-level circuit simulations using a 16 nm TSMC process technology node in cadence virtuoso. Additionally, the reliability evaluations of the proposed circuit encompassing power consumption, noise analysis comprising Signal Noise Ratio (SNR), Spurious Free Dynamic Range (SFDR), and Signal-to-Noise plus Distortion Ratio (SNDR).

title: Time Crystal-Based Scheduling for High-Frequency Financial Data Pipelines
authors: Udala Matta
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: The high frequency financial systems need to process large amounts of market data with very hard time requirements, where delays in the order of microseconds can undermine trading and system performance. Scheduling of tasks which allow predictability of execution, effective use of resources and ability to withstand abrupt changes in work loads is one of the main challenges in such environment. Traditional scheduling policies generally respond reactively when workload changes have already taken place, which increases latency and jitter in execution, as well as deteriorating performance when the market has gone hot. The paper will suggest a new time crystal scheduling system that can be used in high-frequency financial pipelines. Traditionally, the time crystals cycle periodically and in a stable manner, which led to the framework in which the execution of the tasks is based on fixed, repeating micro-time cycles. Time slots are deterministically allocated tasks based on urgency, latency sensitivity and computational demand to allow very predictable execution. Minimal adaptive adjustments are introduced so as to provide transient workload surges without degrading the underlying periodic structure. Simulation-based experimental analysis of high-frequency financial workloads reveals that the suggested scheduler can greatly minimize the end-to-end latency, enhance meeting deadlines, and limit jitter execution with respect to traditional scheduling strategies. These findings suggest that time crystal-based scheduling is a solid and effective technology to use in time-sensitive financial applications that need high predictability and stability in constantly changing operating environment.

title: Beyond the Accuracy: Why Data Scientists Must Speak Business
authors: Norita Ahmad
publish: Computer
abstract: As data science becomes more embedded in business decision-making, the most effective data scientists are those who can communicate clearly, collaborate across functions, and connect models to real-world outcomes.

title: A Deep Learning-Based Predictive Approach for Early Detection of Chronic Kidney Disease Using Machine Learning Technique
authors: J. Adilakshmi, S. Viharika, Katukojwala Shiva Sai, Addepalli Karthikeya, Vadla Harshitha, Suryadevara Purandhar
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Chronic Kidney Disease has emerged as a significant public-health problem in up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 0} - \text{1 5} \%$</tex> of the population around the world. Because it slowly develops and early signs may not be present, many patients are diagnosed when the disease has already progressed. Historically, the assessment of CKD is dependent on labor-intensive review of laboratory results - an approach that might miss subtle early markers of the disease. In this paper, explore the possibility of using machine learning for early detection of CKD. Here introduce an Artificial Neural Network model and we train our model using the UCI CKD dataset in order to capture complex patterns and nonlinear dependencies among multiple clinical features. When the model was evaluated using standard performance measure (accuracy, precision response, and F1-score),observed that ANN model worked better than conventional classification methods. These results showed that ANN model had the ability to improve early CKD diagnosis, so that medical staff could make intervention in time and reduce burden on society caused by early missed diagnosis of disease. This approach supports early clinical decision-making and timely medical intervention by reducing the risk of delayed diagnosis. Overall, the proposed model contributes to efficient and reliable CKD prediction in healthcare systems.

title: Proximity-Gated Wake-Word Spotting on Low-Power Embedded Hardware Using Quantized CNNs
authors: Swara Kadam, Tirth Upadhyay, Vaidehi Tiwari, Prakul Singh, Prafull Pandey, Vitthal S. Gutte
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cognitive and Intelligent Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Wake Word Spotting (WWS) refers to the usage of short voice commands to enable hands-free interaction with smart devices. While many WWS systems already exist, they rely on cloud connectivity or high-performance processors, resulting in increased latency, higher energy consumption, and privacy concerns. This restricts the suitability of these systems for embedded and offline IoT applications. In this paper, we present a Wake Word Spotting system that is fully offline, and designed for low power microcontroller-based devices. This system employs an 8-bit quantized convolutional network (CNN) trained using a modified subset of the Google Speech Commands Dataset (v2), and recognizes the wake word “hello”. We integrate an ultrasonic proximity sensor that activates the processes of audio capture, feature extraction, and neural inference only when a user is detected within a 30 cm range of the device. This further improves energy efficiency, as it avoids unnecessary continuous audio processing. The complete pipeline runs entirely on the ESP32 microcontroller, and uses the TensorFlow Lite for Microcontrollers (TFLM) Library, ensuring that user audio is not transmitted anywhere outside of the device. An OLED display and status LED are integrated to provide real-time feedback during the device operation. The trained CNN model achieves over 90 % classification accuracy, and has a low false-alarm rate on offline evaluation performed. When deployed on the ESP32, the system demonstrates sub-10 ms inference latency, and runs in the power range of low tens of milliwatts. In addition to quantitative evaluation of the model performance, the integrated system was qualitatively validated in a real-world classroom environment containing background noise. The results show that TinyML-based Wake Word Spotting can deliver low-latency and accurate results on low-resource hardware when combined with techniques like proximity-based activation.

title: Real-Time Cyber Incident Feed System for Defending Against Threats
authors: Dhivya D, Dineshkumar C, V.R. Sadasivam
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The continuous and exponential growth of unstructured Open-Source Intelligence (OSINT) poses a significant challenge to modern Cyber Threat Intelligence (CTI) systems, primarily due to inherent data noise and the critical need for mandatory ethical compliance. Conventional CTI approaches are reactive and inherently flawed, suffering from slow analyst triage times and failing to enforce pre-persistence privacy protection for Personally Identifiable Information (PII). This paper presents the Real-Time Cyber Incident Feed System (R-CIFS), a novel, three-layered architecture that resolves this trade-off. The core innovation lies in the tight integration of the Mandatory Redaction Module (MRM), a specialized BERT-based Named Entity Recognition (NER) pipeline, which enforces a 100% PII-redaction guarantee concurrently with data ingestion. This redacted data then enters the Hierarchical Threat Scoring (HTS) module, which employs a mathematically formulated ensemble of Random Forest and Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifiers. Evaluation confirms the system's robustness, demonstrating a validated 91.2% classification accuracy and achieving a substantial 73% reduction in Mean Time to Triage (MTTT) compared to manual methods. R-CIFS thus provides a resilient, high-throughput, and ethically compliant framework for automated CTI processing.

title: Construction of Sensor Data Fusion Model for Old Building Monitoring Platform
authors: Haijun Liu, Yongyuan Cai, Xuejun Wang, Mengxiao Dong
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: This paper proposes a sensor data fusion algorithm based on spatiotemporal attention mechanism and graph convolution to construct a fusion model suitable for old building monitoring platform. The algorithm dynamically captures the time series changes and spatial correlation characteristics of data through the spatiotemporal attention mechanism, and uses the graph convolution network to mine the sensor topological relationship. In the experimental simulation, the simulated old building monitoring data set and the real scene data set are used. The mean square error (MSE), mean absolute error (MAE) and correlation coefficient (CC) are used as evaluation indicators to compare with the traditional weighted average fusion algorithm and the fusion algorithm based on Kalman filter. The results show that on the simulated data set, the MSE of this algorithm reaches 0.35, which is 40 % lower than the traditional weighted average algorithm, the MAE is 0.16, which is 35 % lower than the Kalman filter algorithm, and the CC reaches 0.93, on the real data set, the MSE is stable at 0.43, the MAE is 0.20, and the CC is 0.90. Experiments show that the algorithm significantly improves the accuracy and reliability of data fusion, and provides an efficient data processing solution for the safety monitoring of old buildings.

title:
Experimental Demonstration of a Temperature-Sensitive VO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Memristor-Based Spiking Circuit in Stochastic Bursting Regime
authors: Léopold Van Brandt, Noémie Bidoul, Thomas Ratier, Xi Zeng, Jean-Charles Delvenne, Denis Flandre
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: Most recent implementations of sensitive neurons based on resistive-switching materials operate in a always-spiking regime, i.e. as relaxation oscillators whose frequency encodes the stimulus. Envisioning future low-energy event-based neuromorphic sensors, we have implemented a temperature-sensitive neuron with a memristor made of vanadium dioxide (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{VO}_{2}$</tex>), a phase-change material, capable of exhibiting a stochastic bursting behaviour when biased in subthreshold regime by a MOS transistor. We experimentally demonstrate this functionality by recording intermittent bursts of spikes separated by quiescent periods of time, a behaviour reminiscent of excitable systems. Distinct bursts are unambiguously identified through a rigorous interquartile range method. Temperature is encoded into the temporal characteristics, i.e. the full statistical distribution of these bursts thanks to the temperature dependence of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{VO}_{2}$</tex> transition parameters. The bursts duration and number of spikes per burst are experimentally found to follow an exponential distribution, in agreement with the general theory of Markovian bistable systems. The observed phenomenon could be explained by a noise-induced transient escape from a stable equilibrium to a limit cycle.

title: A FHD@30fps Hardware Design for WSiLU Activation Function for Neural Video Coding
authors: Vanessa Aldrighi, Denis Maass, Ruhan Conceição, Wen-Hsiao Peng, Marcelo Porto, Luciano Agostini
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: Neural video codecs, such as DCVC-RT, represent the state-of-the-art in real-time neural video compression, achieving high coding efficiency. However, their deployment on resource-constrained hardware is limited by their high computational complexity, particularly in activation functions like the Weighted Sigmoid Linear Unit (WSiLU). To address this challenge, a hardware-friendly polynomial approximation of the WSiLU function is proposed, for which a dedicated high-throughput hardware architecture was also designed. This approximation reduces by almost 10 times the number of arithmetic operations with a minor impact on the DCVC-RT coding efficiency of 0.49 % in BD-Rate. Synthesis results targeting a 40 nm technology demonstrate that the designed hardware can process Full High Definition (FHD - <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 9 2 0} \boldsymbol{\times} \mathbf{1 0 8 0}$</tex> pixels) at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0}$</tex> frames per second (30 fps) video in real time, with an area of 209.81 kG ates and a power dissipation of 29.97 mW. To the best of the authors' knowledge, this is the first work in the literature proposing a hardware design for the WSiLU activation function.

title: A ource-Storage Cooperative Control Considering Current Limiting and Voltage Supporting Transient Response of Inverters
authors: Jiazheng Sun, Shanshan Wang, Hengyu Liu, Zhi Zhang, Dezhao Ouyang, Jia Hou
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: The new electricity system with a high percentage of renewable energy sources faces the problems of insufficient voltage support capability and unstable power dynamic response. Virtual synchronous generators can effectively enhance the frequency and voltage stability of power systems by simulating the inertia and damping characteristics of synchronous generators. However, the power supplied to demand side by the generators fluctuates significantly due to multi-dimensional control limitation. For this reason, this paper proposes a new source-storage cooperative control strategy with the capability of anti-drop in power performance. Firstly, the system model is derived through the traveling waves (TWs) based on real-time fault information acquisition. Secondly, VSG stabilizes the power angle and performs current limitation in cooperation with energy storage system (ESS), which compensates the drop power according to feedback information. Then, while the system is in out of service (O/S) state, VSG provides voltage support meanwhile ESS compensates the power drop caused by current decrease. This control strategy ensures balanced power supply particularly when a large number of generators are linked to demand side. Finally, the proposed control strategy is confirmed in MATLAB/Simulink environment.

title: Medical Disease Detection Using Respiratory Impedance and Forced Oscillation Techniques
authors: Muskan.J. Momin, Sandhya.S. Waghere
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: This paper talks about lung diseases, particularly lung cancer, pose significant threats to the respiratory system and remain one of the leading causes of death globally. Early detection greatly enhances survival rates, with computed tomography CT) Scans are working better than conventional X-rays. However, combining many imaging modalities is frequently necessary for a thorough diagnosis. The creation and assessment of a deep neural network intended to accurately identify lung cancer from medical photos is presented in this research. The system will use artificial intelligence to quickly review computerized tomographs (CT scans), spot serious problems such as cancer and identify other diseases such as pneumonia. Help doctors better understand different types of patients when analyzing images related to infections and emergencies. In this study, a method called the Forced Oscillation Technique is used to combine deep learning with our lung function. Special computer models, called LSTM models, process data from the photos to study airflow and pressure in the lungs. This helps measure how the lungs respond, which is important for understanding diseases. The results show that stacked LSTM models can detect changes in lung tissue and accurately monitor lung function. By combining FOT with deep learning, doctors now have a better non-invasive way to diagnose lung cancer and see how well treatment, especially radiation therapy, is working. Overall, this new method helps improve the diagnosis and treatment of lung cancer by using advanced technology.
